"Women of course are deceivers, even occupied with selfishness. Let them be condemned! 
Here, I am not mentioning of all women but of Bharata's mother only."

verses.sanskrit:
अनर्थभावेऽ र्थपरे नृशंसे |

ममानुतापाय निविष्टभावे |

किमप्रियम् पश्यसि मन्निमित्तम् |

हितानुकारिण्यथवापि रामे || २-१२-१०३
verses.translation:
"Oh, cruel woman of evil intent; given to pursuit of your selfish ends, you have a settled 
disposition to bring grief to me. What a mischief do you expect through me or through Rama, who is always 
doing benefit to you?"

verses.sanskrit:
परित्यजेयुः पितरो हि पुत्रान् |

भार्याः वतींश्चापि कृतानुरागाः |

कृत्स्नम् हि सर्वम् कुपितम् जगत्स्या |

द्दृष्ट्वे रानन् व्तसबे बुनग्बन् || २-१२-१०४
verses.translation:
"On seeing Rama plunged in adversity fathers leave of their sons and wives too their 
husbands. Not, even the entire world gets exasperated."

verses.sanskrit:
अहम् पुनर्देवकुमाररूप |

मलकृतम् तम् सुतमाव्रजन्तम् |

नन्दामि पश्यन्नपि दर्शनेन |

भवामि दृष्ट्वा च पुनर्युवेव || २-१२-१०५
verses.wordMeaning:
105. aham punaH = I for one; randaami = rejoice; pashyan 
= by seeing; tam sutam = that son; devakumaara ruupam = in the form of adivine boy;
alamkR^itam = and adorned with ornaments; aavrajantam = coming; darshanenaapi 
= in proximity to look at; dR^ishhTvaa = By seeing; punaH = again; bhavaamicha 
= I also become; yuveva = like youth.
verses.translation:
"I, for one, rejoice by seeing that son Rama in the form of a divine boy, adorned with 
ornaments coming in proximity to me. By seeing him again and again, I get rejuvenated."

verses.sanskrit:
विनापि सूर्येण भवेत्प्रवृत्ति |

रवर्ष्ता वज्रधरेण वापि |

रामम् तु गच्छन्तमितः समीक्ष्य |

जीवेन्न कश्चित्त्विति चेतना मे || २-१२-१०६
verses.translation:
"Active life may not be possible even without sun or even Indra (the wielder of thunder 
bolt) not pouring rain. But, my opinion is that not even one will survive, by seeing Rama departing 
from here."

verses.sanskrit:
विनाशकामामहिताममित्रा |

मावासयम् मृत्युमिवात्मनस्त्वम् |

चिरम् बताङ्केन धृतासि सर्पी |

महाविष तेन हतोऽस्मि मोहात् || २-१२-१०७
verses.wordMeaning:
107. aavaasayam = I lodged in my house; tvaam = you; vinaashkaamaam 
= who seek my destruction; mR^tyumiva = as death; aatmanaH = one's own; amitraam 
= enemy; bata = Alas!; tena = on account of; mohaat = ignorance; mahaavishhaa 
= a highly venomous; serpii = a female serpant; dhR^itaasi = has been held; aNkena 
= on my lap; chiram = so long; hataHasmi = I am undone!
verses.translation:
"I lodged in my house, as one would one's own mortal enemy, you, who seek my destruction 
and are unfriendly. Alas, due to ignorance, a highly venomous female serpent has been held on my lap 
so long and therefore I am undone."

verses.sanskrit:
मया च रामेण सलक्ष्मणेन |

प्रशास्तु हीनो भरतस्त्वया सह |

पुरम् च राष्ट्रम् च निहत्य बान्धवान् |

ममाहितानाम् च भवाभिहर्षिणी || २-१२-१०८
verses.translation:
"Bereft of me as also Rama and Lakshmana, let Bharata along with you, rule over the city 
and the state. After killing your relatives, bring delight to my enemies."

verses.sanskrit:
नृशंसवृत्ते व्यसनप्रहारिणि |

प्रसह्य वाक्यम् यदिहाद्य भाषसे |

न नाम ते केन मुखात्पतन्त्यधो |

विशीर्यमाणा दशना स्सहस्रधा || २-१२-१०९
verses.wordMeaning:
109. nR^ishamsavR^itte = Oh; cruel natured!; vyasana prahaariNi 
= One who has struck a blow in adversity!; yat = which; vaakyam = words; bhaashhase 
= you utter; prasahya = violently; adya = now ; kena = why; dashanaa 
= the teeth; te mukhaat = from your mouth; na naama patanti = have not fallen; 
athaH = down; vishiiryamaaNaaH = breaking into; sahasradhaa = thousands of pieces.
verses.translation:
"Oh, cruel natured! One who has struck a blow in adversity! When you violently utter 
such words now, why the teeth from your mouth have not fallen down, breaking into thousands of pieces?"

verses.sanskrit:
न किंचिदाहाहितमप्रियम् वचो |

न वेत्ति रामः परुशाणि Bहाषितुम् |

कथन्नु रामे ह्यभिरामवादिनि |

ब्रवीषि दोषान् गुणनित्यसम्मते || २-१२-११०
verses.wordMeaning:
110. raamaH = Rama; na aaha = does not speak; vachaH 
= a word; kimchit = even a little; ahitam = which is malevolent or; priyam 
= unkindly or; na vetti = does not know how to; bhashhItum = say; parushhaaNi 
= harsh words. katham nu = How indeed; bravishhi = are you telling; doshhaan = faults;
raame = of Rama; abhiraama vaadini = who talks beautifully; guNa nityasammate 
= who is ever respected for his virtues.
verses.translation:
"Rama does not speak a word which is even a little malevolent or unkindly. He does not 
know how to utter harsh words. How indeed are you recounting the faults of Rama, who talks beautifully 
and who is always admired for his virtues"

verses.sanskrit:
प्रताम्य वा प्रज्वल वा प्रणश्य वा |

सहस्रशो वा स्फुटिता महीम् व्रज |

न ते करिष्यमि वचः सुदारुणम् |

ममाहितम् केकयराजपांसनि || २-१२-१११
verses.translation:
"Oh, Kaikeyi, the black guard of Keka dynasty! You may faint away or flare up or perish 
or enter the earth split up into thousands of cracks! I will not act on your word which is very cruel 
and inimical to me."

verses.sanskrit:
क्षुरोपमाम् नित्यमसत्प्रियम्वदाम् |

प्रदुष्टभावाम् स्वकुलोपघातिनीम् |

न जीवितुम् त्वाम् विषहेऽमनोरमाम् |

दिधक्षमाणाम् हृदयम् सबन्धनम् || २-१२-११२
verses.translation:
"I do not wish the survival of you, who are destructive like a razor, always speaking 
falsely pleasing words, are of evil natured, disastrous to the family, intent upon burning my heart 
along with vitals and repellent to my mind."

verses.sanskrit:
न जीवितम् मेऽस्ति पुनः कुतः सुखम् |

विनात्मजेनात्मवतः कुतो रतिः |

ममाहितम् देवि न क् कर्तुमर्हसि |

स्पृशामि पादावपि ते प्रसीद मे || २-१२-११३
verses.translation:
"There is no life to me without my son. How can there be happiness moreover? From whom 
else can there be joy, while I survive? Oh, queen! You ought not to do an unfriendly act to me. I even 
touch your feet. Be gracious to me."

verses.sanskrit:
स भूमिपलो विलपन्ननाथवत् |

स्त्रीया गृहीतो हृदयेऽतिमात्रया |

पपात देव्याश्चरणौ प्रसारिता |

पुभावसम्प्राप्य यथातुरस्तथा || २-१२-११४
verses.wordMeaning:
114. bhuumipaalaH = King; gR^ihiitaH = who has been gripped;
hR^idaye = in the heart; striyaa = by wife; atimaatrayaa = who transgressed 
all bounds of decorum; vilapan = wailing; anaathavat = like a forlorn child; asamprayaa 
= not reaching; davyaaH = Kaikeyi's; ubhon charaNou = both the feet; prasaaritou 
= which were spread along; papaata = sank down; yathaa tathaa = like; aaturaH 
= an ailing man.

sargas.title: Sarga 13
sargas.introduction:
In this chapter, Sage Valmiki describes the bitter agony of Dasaratha on hearing to
the adamant Kaikeyi's words.

verses.sanskrit:
अतदर्हं महाराजं शयानम् सयानमतथोचितम् |

ययातिमिव पुण्यान्ते देवलोकात्परिच्युतम् || २-१३-१

अनर्थरूपा सिद्धार्था ह्यभीता भयदर्शिनी |

पुनराकारयामास तमेव वरमङ्गना || २-१३-२

त्वं कत्थसे महाराज सत्यवादी दृढव्रतः |

मम चेदं वरं कस्माद्विधारयितुमिच्छसि || २-१३-३
verses.translation:
"Oh, king! You always boast yourself, saying "I speak truth. I am persisted in my
promise." Now, why are you objecting to my boon?"

verses.sanskrit:
एवमुक्तस्तु कैकेय्या राजा दशरथस्तदा |

प्रत्युवाच ततः क्रुद्धो मुहूर्तं विह्वलन्निव || २-१३-४

मृते मयि गते रामे वनं मनुजपुंगवे |

हन्त अनार्ये मम अमित्रे सकाम सुखिनी भव || २-१३-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. anaarye = Oh; the vulgar one! mama amitre = you; my
enemy!; bhava = become; sukhinii = happy; sakaamaa = after your desire
is fulfilled; manuja puN^gave raame vanamgate = when Rama the best among men leaves for
forest; mayi mR^ite = and when I die; hanta = alas!
verses.translation:
"Oh, the vulgar one! You, my enemy! You want to be happy after your desire is
fulfilled when Rama the best among men leaves for the forest and when I die thereafter. Alas!"

verses.sanskrit:
स्वर्गेऽपि खलु रामस्य कुशलं दैवतैरहम् |

प्रत्यादेशादभिहितं धारयिष्ये कथं बत || २-१३-६
verses.translation:
"When celestials ask for me about Rama's well-being after I reach heaven, how can I
indeed convince them that he is well obscuringly the actual fact?"

verses.sanskrit:
कैकेय्याः प्रियकामेन रामः प्रव्राजितो मया |

यदि सत्यं ब्रवीम्येतत्तस्त्यं भविष्यति || २-१३-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. braviimiyadi = If I tell ; etat = this; satyam =
truth; raamaH = Rama; pravaajitaH = was sent; vanam = to forest;
mayaa = by me; priya kaamena = desirous of showing kindness; kaikeyyaa =
to Kaikeyi; tat = that; bhavishhyati = will become; asatyam =
untruth.
verses.translation:
"If I tell without obscuring the fact that I sent Rama to exile desirous of showing
kindness to Kaikeyi, nobody will believe me."

verses.sanskrit:
अपुत्रेण मया पुत्रः श्रमेण महता महान् |

रामो लब्धो महाबाहुः स कथं त्यज्यते मया || २-१३-८
verses.translation:
"With much effort I, the childless begot the great and powerful Rama as my son. How
can I abandon such Rama?"

verses.sanskrit:
शूरश्च कृतविद्यश्च जितक्रोधः क्षमापरः |

कथं कमलपत्राक्षो मया रामो विवास्यते || २-१३-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. katham = how; raama = Rama; shuuraH = who is
valiant; krita vidyashcha = who is knoledgeable; jitu krodhaH = whose anger has
been defeated; kshhamaaparaH = who is solely devoted to patience; kamala
patraakshhaH = who has eyes like lotus petals ; vivasyate = be exiled; mayaa
= by me?
verses.translation:
"How can I send away Rama who is valiant and knowledgeable, who has subdued anger,
who has forbearance and who has eyes like lotus-petals".

verses.sanskrit:
कथमिन्दीवरश्यामं दीर्घबाहुं महाबलम् |

अभिराममहं रामं प्रेषयिष्यामि दण्डकान् || २-१३-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. katham = How; aham = I; preshhayishhyaami = can
send; raamam = Rama; indiivarashyaamam = who is dark blue in colour like a blue
lotus; diirghabahuum = who is long-armed; mahaabalam = who is very strong;
abhiraamam = who is graceful; daNDakaan = to Dandaka forest?
verses.translation:
"How I can I send Rama, who is dark blue in colour like a blue lotus, who is
long-armed , who is very strong and graceful, to Dandaka forest?"

verses.sanskrit:
सुखानामुचितस्यैव दुःखैरनुचितस्य च |

दुःखं नामानुपश्येयं कथं रामस्य धीमतः || २-१३-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. uchitasya = one who is accostomed to; sukhaanaam =
comforts; anuchitaasya = who is not habituated to; duHkhaiH = difficulties;
dhiimatataH = who is sensible; katham naama = How; anupashyeyam = can I
see; raamasya = (such)Rama's ; duHkham = trouble ?
verses.translation:
"Rama is accustomed to comforts. He is not accustomed to difficulties. How can I
imagine such sensible Rama in a troublesome situation?"

verses.sanskrit:
यदि दुःखमकृत्वाद्य मम संक्रमणं भवेत् |

अदुःखार्हस्य रामस्य ततः सुखमवाप्नुयाम् || २-१३-१२

नृशंसे पापसंकल्पे रामं स्त्यपराक्रमम् |

किं विप्रियेण कैकेयि प्रियं योजयसे मम || २-१३-१३

अकीर्तिरतुला लोके ध्रुवः परिभवश्च मे |
verses.wordMeaning:
13. nR^ishamse = Oh; cruel one! paapasankalpi = one with
sinful thoughts! kaikeyii = Oh; Kaikeyi! kim = why; yojayase = are you
instigating; vipriyeNa = offence; raamam = to Rama; priyam = who is
beloved; mama = to me; satyaparaakramam = who is truly heroic ? atulaa =
uneaqualled; akiirtiH = ill-fame; paraabhavashcha = and humiliation;
dhR^ivaH = is certain; me = to me; loke = in the world.
verses.translation:
"Oh, cruel one! One with sinful thoughts! Oh, kaikeyi! why are you planning to do
harm to Rama who is beloved to me and who is truly heroic? I shall certainly get unequalled ill
fame and humiliation in this world".

verses.sanskrit:
तथा विलपतस्तस्य परिभ्रमित चेतसः || २-१३-१४

अस्तमभ्यगमत्सूर्यो रजनी चाभ्यवर्तत |

सा त्रियामा तथार्तस्य चन्द्रमण्डलमण्डिता || २-१३-१५

राज्ञो विलपमानस्य न व्यभासत शर्वरी |

तथैवोष्णम् विनिःश्वस्य वृद्धो दसरथो नृपः || २-१३-१६

विललापार्तवद्दुःखं गगनासक्तलोचनः |
verses.wordMeaning:
16. vR^iddhaH = The aged; dasarathaH nR^ipaH = king
Dasaratha; vilalaapa = was lamenting; duHkham = painfully; aartavaat =
as afflicted with disease; vinishshvasya = sighed; ushhNam = hotly;
gaganaasakta lochanaH = having his eyes fixed on sky.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रभातं त्वयेच्छामि निशे नक्षत्रभूषणे || २-१३-१७

क्रियतां मे दया भद्रे मयायं रचितोऽञ्जलिः |
verses.translation:
"Oh, auspicious night, decorated with stars! do not wish you to do break into a
dawn. Have mercy on me. I pray with folded hands."

verses.sanskrit:
अथवा गम्यतां शीघ्रं नाहमिच्छामि निर्घृणाम् || २-१३-१८

नृशंसां कैकेयीं द्रष्टुं यत्कृते व्यसनं महत् |
verses.translation:
"Otherwise, Oh, Night! Leave away quickly. I do not went to see Kaikeyi, who is
shameless, cruel and for whose reason this great calamity occurred"

verses.sanskrit:
एवमुक्त्वा ततो राजा कैकेयीं संयताञ्जलिः || २-१३-१९

प्रसादयामास पुनः कैकेयीं चेदमब्रवीत् |
verses.wordMeaning:
19. raajaa = king; uktvaa = spoke; evam = this;
tataH = thereafter; prasa dayaamaasa = beseeched; kaikeyiim = to
Kaikeyi; samyataaNjaliH = with folded hands; punaH = again; abraviit =
spoke; idam cha = this word also; Kaikeyiim = to Kaikeyi

verses.sanskrit:
साधुवृत्तस्य दीनस्य त्वद्गतस्य गतायुषः | २-१३-२०

प्रसादः क्रियतां देवि भद्रे राज्ञो विशेषतः |
verses.translation:
"Oh, auspicious queen! I am a well conducted man. I am dejected and seeking refuse
in you. I am an old man and especially a king. Be kind to me."

verses.sanskrit:
शून्ये न खलु सुश्रोणि मयेदं समुदाहृतम् || २-१३-२१

कुरु साधु प्रसादं मे बाले सहृदया ह्यसि |
verses.wordMeaning:
21. sushroNi = Oh; the well hipped!; idam = All this; na
khalusamudaatiR^itum = is not indeed addressed to ; shuunye = vacuum!(the sky);
baale = Oh; young woman!; kuru = do; prasaadam = favour; me =
to me; saadhu = well; asihi = you are indeed; sahR^idayaa = good
hearted!
verses.translation:
"Oh, well-hipped one! I hope that whatever I told, has not merged in the sky. Oh!
young woman, be kind to me . You are good-hearted."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रसीद देवि रामो मे त्वद्धत्तं राज्यमव्ययम् || २-१३-२२

लभतामसितापाङ्गे यशः परमवाप्नुहि |
verses.wordMeaning:
22. devi = Oh; queen!; asitaapaaN^ge = with dark outer
corner of eyes; prasiida = Be kind; raamaH labhataam = Let Rama obtain; me
raajyam = my kingdom; tvaddattam = given by you; avaapnuti = obtain;
param = great; yashaH = fame.
verses.translation:
"Oh, queen with dark outer corner of eyes be kind. You your self give my kingdom to
Rama. Thus, you will obtain great fame."

verses.sanskrit:
मम रामस्य लोकस्य गुरूणां भरतस्य च || २-१३-२३

प्रियमेतद्गुरुश्रोणि कुरु चारुमुखेक्षणे |
verses.wordMeaning:
23. gurushroNe = Oh; the broad hipped; charumukhekshhaNaa =
with beautiful face and eyes; kuru = do; etat = this; priyam = which
will be pleasing; mama = to me; raamasya = to Rama; lokasya = to the
world; guruuNaam = to priests bharatasyacha = and to Bharata.
verses.translation:
"Oh, broad hipped, with beautiful face and eyes! do this. It will be pleasing to me,
to Rama, to the world, to priests and to Bharata."

verses.sanskrit:
विशुद्धभावस्य सु ुष्टभावा |

दीनस्य ताम्राश्रुकलस्य राज्ञः |

श्रुत्वा विचित्रं करुणं विलापं |

भर्तुर्नृशंसा न चकार वाक्यम् || २-१३-२४

ततः स राजा पुनरेव मूर्छितः |

प्रियामतुष्टां प्रतिकूलभाषिणीम् |

समीक्ष्य पुत्रस्य विवासनम् प्रति |

क्षितौ विसंज्ञो निपपात दुःखितः || २-१३-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. samiikshha = seeing; priyaam = wife; pratikuula
bhashhiniim = who is talking unpleasantly; putrasya vivaasanam prati = about sending
the son to forest; atushhtaam = and who is dissatisfied; saH raajaa = that king;
duHkhitaH = was distressed; tataH = and therafter; muurchchhitaH =
fainted; punareva = again; visamGyaH = losing consciousness; nipapaata =
fell down; kshhitau = on floor.

verses.sanskrit:
इतीव राज्ञो व्यथितस्य सा निशा |

जगाम घोरं श्वसतो मनस्विनः |

विबोध्यमानः प्रतिबोधनं तदा |

निवारयामास स राजसत्तमः || २-१३-२६

sargas.title: Sarga 14
sargas.introduction:
This chapter starts with Kaikeyi's entreaties to the king followed by Dasaratha
disowning her as wife. The night passes and Sumantra comes to wake up the King. Ordered by Kaikeyi
to bring Sri Rama to the king, he goes to Sri Rama to fetch Him.

verses.sanskrit:
पुत्र शोक अर्दितम् पापा विसम्ज्ञम् पतितम् भुवि |

विवेष्टमानम् उदीक्ष्य सा ऐक्ष्वाकम् इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-१४-१

पापम् क्ऱ्त्वा इव किम् इदम् मम संश्रुत्य संश्रवम् |

शेषे क्षिति तले सन्नः स्थित्याम् स्थातुम् त्वम् अर्हसि || २-१४-२
verses.translation:
"What is this? After hearing the promise given to me, you are lying on the floor
dejected as though you had committed a great sin. You ought to keep yourself within bounds of
ethics".

verses.sanskrit:
आहुः सत्यम् हि परमम् धर्मम् धर्मविदो जनाः |

सत्यम् आश्रित्य हि मया त्वम् च धर्मम् प्रचोदितः ||२-१४-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. janaaH = People; dharma vidaH = who know what is right;
aahuH hi = indeed speak; satyam = of truthfulness; paramam dharmam = as
highest virtue; tvam = you; prabodhitaH = are made aware of; dharmam =
(your)duty; mayaacha = by me; aashritya = who has taken refuge; satyam =
in truth.
verses.translation:
"People who know what is right, indeed speak of truthfulness as highest virtue. I
too have taken refuge in truth and made you aware of your duty."

verses.sanskrit:
संश्रुत्य शैब्यः श्येनाय स्वाम् तनुम् जगती पतिः |

प्रदाय पक्षिणो राजन् जगाम गतिम् उत्तमाम् || २-१४-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. raajaa = Oh; king! samshrutya = having promised;
svaam = his own; tanum = body; shyenaaya pakshhiNe = to a bird called
hawk; shaibyaH = Saibya; jagatiipatiH = the lord of the world; jagaama =
obtained; uttamaam = the highest; gatim = destiny.
verses.translation:
"Oh, king! Having made a promise to a hawk and offering his body to the bird , king
Saibya, the ruler of world obtained the greatest destiny".

verses.sanskrit:
तथ हि अलर्कः तेजस्वी ब्राह्मणे वेद पारगे |

याचमाने स्वके नेत्रेउद्ध्ऱ्त्य अविमना ददौ || २-१४-५
verses.translation:
"In that manner, Alarka the glorious man plucking his own eyes, indeed gave
remorselessly, to a brahmana skilled in the Veda, When asked for them."

verses.sanskrit:
सरिताम् तु पतिः स्वल्पाम् मर्यादाम् सत्यम् अन्वितः |

सत्य अनुरोधात् समये वेलाम् खाम् न अतिवर्तते || २-१४-६
verses.translation:
"Following ruthfulness, ocean the lord of rivers even at the time of flow-tide, does
not transgress even to a small extent its boundary because of its compliance to truth."

verses.sanskrit:
स्त्यमेकपदं ब्रह्मे सत्ये धर्मः प्रतिष्ठतः|

सत्यमेवाक्षया वेदाः सत्येनै वाप्यते परम् || २-१४-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. satyam = Truth; ekapadam = in the word; brahma =
(is)Brahma; satye = on truth; dharmaH = is righteousness; pratishhTitaH
= eshtablished; satyameva = truth indeed; vedaaH = is knowledge;
akshhayaaH = which is imperishable; satyaineva = by truth alone; param =
the supreme being; aapyate = is obtained.
verses.translation:
"Truth is one word and is Brahma. On truth is righteousness established. Truth
indeed is the knowledge imperishable. By truth alone, the supreme being is obtained".

verses.sanskrit:
सत्यं समनुवर्त्स्व यदि धर्मे धृता मतिः |

सफलः स वरो मेऽस्तु वरदो ह्यसि सत्तम || २-१४-८
verses.translation:
"O the best among men! firmly adhere to truth, if your mind is fixed on piety. Since
you are a bestower of boons, let my aforesaid prayer be granted.

verses.sanskrit:
धर्मस्येहाभिकामार्थं मम चैवाचिचोदनात् |

प्रव्राजय सुतं रामम् त्रिः खलु त्वां ब्रवीम्यहम् || २-१४-९
verses.translation:
"In the matter, yearning righteousness and to concede to my request, send Rama the
son to exile. I am telling to you three times".

verses.sanskrit:
समयम् च मम आर्य इमम् यदि त्वम् न करिष्यसि |

अग्रतः ते परित्यक्ता परित्यक्ष्यामि जीवितम् || २-१४-१०
verses.translation:
"Oh, the venerable man! If you do not implement our agreement, it means you have
abandoned me. Hence I will give up my life here in front of you".

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् प्रचोदितः राजा कैकेय्या निर्विशन्कया |

न अशकत् पाशम् उन्मोक्तुम् बलिर् इन्द्र क्ऱ्तम् यथा || २-१४-११

उद्भ्रान्त ह्ऱ्दयः च अपि विवर्ण वनदो अभवत् |

स धुर्यो वै परिस्पन्दन् युग चक्र अन्तरम् यथा|| २-१४-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. dhuryaH yathaa = Like a bullock; parispandan =
throbbing; yuga chakraantaram = between yoke and the wheel; saH = Dasaratha;
udbhraanta hR^idayaH = got agitated at heart; abhavat = became; vivarNa
vadanaH chaapi = pale in his face.

verses.sanskrit:
विह्वलाभ्याम् च नेत्राभ्याम् अपश्यन्न् इव भूमिपः |

क्ऱ्च्च्रात् धैर्येण संस्तभ्य कैकेयीम् इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-१४-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. bhuupatiH = The king; vihvalaabhyaam netrabhyaam = with
bedimmed eyes; apashyanniva = was unable; as it were to see; samstabhya = stood
firm; dhairyeNa = with courage; kR^ichchhraat = and with difficulty;
abraviit = spoke; idam = these words; kaikeyiim = to Kaikeyi.

verses.sanskrit:
यः ते मन्त्र क्ऱ्तः पाणिर् अग्नौ पापे मया ध्ऱ्तः |

तम् त्यजामि स्वजम् चैव तव पुत्रम् सह त्वया || २-१४-१४
verses.translation:
"Oh, wicked woman! I abandon your hand, which was clasped by me in presence of
nuptial fire, when it was consecrated by sacred recitations as also your son begotten by me along
with yourself".

verses.sanskrit:
प्रयाता रजनी देवि सूर्यस्योदयनं प्रति |

अभिषेकं गुरुजन्स्त्वरयीष्यति मां ध्रुवम् || २-१४-१५

रामाभिषेकसम्भारैस्तदर्थमुपकल्पितैः |
verses.wordMeaning:
15. devi = Oh; queen! rajanii = the night; prayataa
= has gone by; suuryasya udayanam prati = with return of rising sun; gurujanaH =
elderly people; dhruvam = certainly; tvarayishhyati = will quicken; mama
= me; abhishhekam = for installation; raamaabhishheka sambhaaraiH = with material
to be used for Rama's coranation; upakalpitaiH = procured; tadartham = for the
purpose.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Queen! the night has gone by, with the return pf rising sun. Elderly people
will certainly quicken me for installation of Rama with the sacred materials procured for the
purpose".

verses.sanskrit:
रामः कारयितव्यो मे मृतस्य सलिलक्रियाम् || २-१४-१६

त्वया सपुत्त्रया नैव कर्तव्या सलिलक्रिया |

व्याहन्तास्यशुभाचारे यदि रामाभिषेचन्म् || २-१४-१७
verses.translation:
"Oh, woman of vicious conduct! If you obstruct Rama's installation, you including
your son cannot offer me water. Rama will be made to do the offering of water to me, after
death.

verses.sanskrit:
न च शक्नोम्यहं द्रषुं पूर्वं तथा सुखम् |

हतहर्षं निरानन्दं पुनर्जनमवाङ्मुखम् || २-१४-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. dR^ishhTvaa = Having seen; janam = people; tathaa
sukham = with that joy; puurvam = before; aham = I; na shaknomicha
= cannot; drashhTum = see; punaH = again; hata harshham = their
happiness ended; niraanandam = without joy; avaaNmukham = having face turned
downwards.
verses.translation:
"Having seen people with that joy before, I cannot see them with there happiness
ended, without any merriment and having their faces turned downward in grief".

verses.sanskrit:
तां तथा ब्रुवत्स्तस्य भूमिपन्य महात्मनः |

प्रभाता शर्वरी पुण्या चन्द्रनक्षत्रश्रालिनी || २-१४-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. tasya bhuumipasya = That king; mahaatmanaH = the great
souled; bruvataH = speaking; taam = to her; tathaa = as aforesaid;
puNyaa = the holy; sharvarii = night; chandra nakshhatra shaalinii =
endowed with moon and stars; prabhaataa = began to become clear into a dawn.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः पाप समाचारा कैकेयी पार्थिवम् पुनः |

उवाच परुषम् वाक्यम् वाक्यज्ञा रोष मूर्चिता ||२-१४-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. tataH = Afterwards; kaikeyii = K aikeyi; paapa
samaachaaraa = of vicious conduct; vaakyaG^yaa = of skillful talk; roshha
puuritaa = filled with anger; punaH = again; uvacha = spoke;
parushham = harsh; vaakyam = words; parthivam = to the king.

verses.sanskrit:
किम् इदम् भाषसे राजन् वाक्यम् गर रुज उपमम् |

आनाययितुम् अक्लिष्टम् पुत्रम् रामम् इह अर्हसि || २-१४-२१
verses.translation:
" Oh, king ! You are uttering words, which cannot be swallowed easily like in a
painful throat disease. You ought to summon your son Rama here without any delay."

verses.sanskrit:
स्थाप्य राज्ये मम सुतम् क्ऱ्त्वा रामम् वने चरम् |

निह्सपत्नाम् च माम् क्ऱ्त्वा क्ऱ्त क्ऱ्त्यो भविष्यसि || २-१४-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. kR^ita kR^ityaH bhavishhyasi = You will be one who discharged
duty; shthaapya = by installing ; mama sutam = my son; raajye = to
kingdom kR^itvaa = by making; raamam = Rama; vane charam = wander in woods;
kR^itvaa = by making; maam = me; niH sapatnaam = devoid of enemies.
verses.translation:
"You will be the one who discharges duty, by installing my son to this kingdom, by
making Rama to wander in woods and rid me of enemies."

verses.sanskrit:
स नुन्नैव तीक्षेन प्रतोदेन हय उत्तमः |

राजा प्रदोचितः अभीक्ष्णम् कैकेयीम् इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-१४-२३

धर्म बन्धेन बद्धो अस्मि नष्टा च मम चेतना |

ज्येष्ठम् पुत्रम् प्रियम् रामम् द्रष्टुम् इच्चामि धार्मिकम् || २-१४-२४
verses.translation:
"I am bound by the ties of morality. I lost my judgement. I wish to see the pious
Rama, my beloved elder son."

verses.sanskrit:
ततः प्रभातां र्जनीमुदिते च दिवाकरे |

पुण्ये नक्षत्रयोगे चे मुहूर्ते च समाहिते || २-१४-२५

वसिष्ठो गुणसंपन्नः शिष्येः परिवृतस्तदा |

उपगृह्याशु संभारान् [रविवेश पुरोत्तमम् || २-१४-२६

सिक्तसंमार्जितपथां पताकोत्तमभूषिताम् |

विचित्रकुसुमाकीर्णां नानास्रग्भिर्विराजिताम् || २-१४-२७

संहृष्टमनुजोपेतां समृद्धविपणापणाम् |

महोत्सवसमाकीर्णां राघवार्थे समुस्त्सुकाम् || २-१४-२८

चन्दनागुरुधूपैश्च सर्वतः प्रतिधूपिताम् |

तां पुरीम् समतिक्रम्य पुरन्दरपुरोपमाम् || २-१४-२९

ददर्शान्तः पुरश्रेष्ठं नानाद्विजगणायुतम् |

पौरजानपदाकिर्र्र्णं ब्राह्मणैरुपशोभितम् || २-१४-३०

तदन्तः पुरमासाद्य व्यतिचक्राम तम् जनम् || २-१४-३१

वसिष्ठः परमप्रीतः परमर्षिर्विवेश च |
verses.wordMeaning:
31. paramarshhiH = The sage; vasishhTaH = Vasista;
paramapriitaH = bei ng well pleased; aasaadya = reaching; tat antaHpuram that
gynaeceum; vyatichakraama = got past; janam = those people; viveshcha =
and entered it.

verses.sanskrit:
स त्वपश्यद्विनिष्क्रान्तं सुमन्त्रं नाम सारथिम् |

द्वारे मनुजसिंहस्य सचिवं प्रियदर्शन्म् || २-१४-३२
verses.wordMeaning:
32. saH tu = That sage Vasista; apasyat = saw; sumantram
naama = Sumantra by name; sachivam = who was minister; saarathim = and
charioteer; priya darshanam = who had a pleasing appearance; dvaare = at the
gate; manujasimhasya = of Dasaratha; the best of men; vinishhkraantam = coming
out.

verses.sanskrit:
तमुवाच महातेजाः सूतपुत्रं विशारदम् || २-१४-३३

वसिष्ठः क्षिप्रमाचक्ष्व नृपते र्मामिहागतम् |

इमे गङ्गोदकघटाः सागरेभ्यश्च काञ्चनाः || २-१४-३४

औदुम्बरं भद्रपीठमभिषेकार्थमागतम् |

सर्वबीजानि गन्धाश्च रत्नानि विविधानि च || २-१४-३५

क्षौद्रम् दधि घृतं लाजा दर्भाः सुमनसः पयः |

अष्टौ च कन्या रुचिरा मत्तश्छ वरवारणः | २-१४-३६

चतुरश्वो रथः श्रीमान् निस्त्रिंशो धनुरुत्तमम् |

वाहनं नरसंयुक्तं चत्रं च शशिपन्निभम् || २-१४-३७

श्वेते च वालव्यजने भृङ्गारुश्छ हिरण्मयः |

हेमदामपिनद्धश्च किकुद्मान् पाण्डुरो वृषः || २-१४-३८

केसरी च चतुर्दंष्ट्रो हि श्रेष्ठो महाबलः |

सिंहानस्नं व्याघ्रतनुः समिद्धश्छ हुताशनः || २-१४-३९

सर्ववादित्रसंघाश्च वेश्याश्छालंकृताः स्त्रियः |

आचार्या ब्राह्मणा गावः पुण्यश्च मृगपक्षिणः || २-१४-४०

पौरजानपदश्रेष्ठा नैगमाश्च गणैः सह |

एते चान्ये च बहवो नीयमानाः प्रियम्वदाः || २-१४-४१

अभिषेकाय रामस्य सह तिष्ठन्ति पार्थिवैः |
verses.translation:
"These pitchers filled with holy waters of ganga , gold pitchers with sea water and
an excellent seat with Udambara wood came for the purpose of Rama's installation ceremony. All
types of seeds, fragrant substances and precious stones of various kinds, honey, curd, clarified
butter, parched grains of paddy, blades of Darbha grass, flowers, milk, eight beautiful virgins ,
an elephant in rut, a glorious chariot with four horses, a special kind of sword called Nistrimsa,
an excellent bow , a palanquin with bearers an umbrella resembling the moon , a pair of white
chowries, a golden jug, a white bull with large , humo on its back, an excellent lion with
beautiful mane with large canine teeth, a throne, a tiger skin, fire with ignition , all varieties
of musical instruments,courtesans, women decked with ornaments, teachers, brahmanas, cows, animals
and birds which are sacred, the best of citizens and men of country side, merchants along with
their followers, all these and many other retainers speaking kind words, along with kings stand
waiting for Rama's coronation.

verses.sanskrit:
त्वरयस्व महाराजं यथा समुदितेऽहनि || २-१४-४२

पुण्ये नक्षत्रयोगे च रामो राज्यमवाप्नुयात् |

इति तस्य वचः श्रुत्वा सूतपुत्रो महात्मनः || २-१४-४३

स्तुवन्नृपतिशार्धूलं प्रविवेश निवेशनम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
43. suute putraH = Sumantra; shrutvaaa = hearing;
iti = th ese; vachaH = words; tasya mahaatmanaH = of Vasista; the great
souled; pravivesha = entered; niveshanam = the gynaeceum; stuvan =
enlogging; nR^ipati shaarduulam = Dasaratha; that tiger among kings.

verses.sanskrit:
तं तु पूर्वोदितं वृद्धं द्वारस्था राजसम्मतम् || २-१४-४४

न शेकुरभिसंरोद्धुं राज्ञः प्रयचिकीर्ष्वः |
verses.wordMeaning:
44. dvaarasthaaH = The doormen; priyachikiirshhavaH = who
were wishing to do kindness ; raajJNaH = to king; na shekuH = could not;
abhisamroddum = obstruct; tamtu = him; p uurvoditam = who came before
hand; vR^iddham = who was senior; raaja sammatam = who was esteemed by the
king.

verses.sanskrit:
स सवीपस्थितो राज्ञ्स्तामवस्थामजज्ञीवान् || २-१४-४५

वाग्भिः परमतुष्टाभिरभिष्टोतुं प्रचक्रमे |

ततः सूतो यथाकालं पार्थिवस्य निवेशने || २-१४-४६

सुमन्त्रः प्राञ्जलिर्भूत्वा तुष्टाव जगतीपतिम् |

यथा नन्दति तेजस्वी सागरो भास्करोदये |

प्रीतह् प्रीतेन मनसा तथानन्दघनः स्वतः || २-१४-४७
verses.wordMeaning:
47. yatha = How; tejasvii = the splendid ; saagaraH
= ocean; nandati = gladdens; bhaskar odaye = at the time of sunrise;
tathaa = so; aananda ghanaH = you filled with delight; svataH = by
nature; priitaH = gladden (us); manasaa = with your mind; priitena =
delighted.
verses.translation:
"How the splendid ocean gladdens at the time of sunrise, so do you by nature filled
with delight, gladden us with your delighted mind."

verses.sanskrit:
इन्द्रमस्यां तु वेळायामभितुष्टाव मातलिः || २-१४-४८

सोऽजयद्धानवान्सर्वांस्तथा त्वां बोधयाम्यहम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
48. asyaam veLaayaam = At this very hour; maataliH =
Matali(Indra's charioteer)abhitushhTaava = praised; indram = Indra; saH = that
Indra; ajayat = conquered ; sarvaan = all; daanavaan = the demons;
tathaa = this; aham = I; bodhayaami = am awakening; tvaam =
you.
verses.translation:
"At the very hour of sunrise, Matali (Indra's charioteer)praised Indra and the
latter conquered all the demons. In the same manner, I am awakening you."

verses.sanskrit:
वेदाः सहाङ्गविद्याश्छ यथाह्यात्मभुवम् विभुम् || २-१४-४९

ब्रह्माणम् बोधयन्त्यद्य तथा त्वां बोधयाम्यहम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
49. tathaa = So; aham = I; bodhayaami = am
awakening; tvaam = you; adya = now; yathaa = as ; vedaaH =
vedas; sahaaNga vidyaaH = along with the knowledge of limbs of the body;
bodhayanti = guide** vibhum = Lord; brahmaaNam = Brahma; aatma ; bhuvam
= self born
verses.translation:
"As Vedas along with the knowledge of the limbs of the body guide Lord Brahma (the
creator) who is self born, so am I awakening you now."
verses.commentary:
Brahma derives his knowledge of objects to be evolved at the beginning of
creation from the Vedas.

verses.sanskrit:
आदित्यः सह चन्द्रेण यथा भूतधरां शुभाम् || २-१४-५०

बोधयत्यद्य पृथिवीं तथा त्वाम् बोधयाम्यहम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
50. yathaa = Even as aadityaH = sun; sahachandreNa = along
with moon ; bodhayati = awaken; shubhaam = the beautiful; pR^ithiviim =
earth; bhuutadharaam = which sustains the ; living beings; tathaa = so;
aham = I; bodhayaami = am awakening ; tvaam = you."
verses.translation:
"Even as the sun along with the moon awaken the beautiful earth which sustains the
beings so, am I awakening you."

verses.sanskrit:
उत्तिष्ठाशु महाराज कृतकौतुकमङ्गळः || २-१४-५१

विराजमानो वपुषा मेरोरिव दिवाकरः |
verses.wordMeaning:
51. mahaaraaja = Oh; Majesty!; u ttishhThaH = raise up;
divaakaraH iva = like a sun ; ; meroH = from mount Meru; vapushhaa =
with body; viraajamaanaH = shining brilliantly; kR^ita kantuka ; maN^gaLaH = and
dressed suitably for the auspicious ceremony.
verses.translation:
"Having dressed suitably for the auspicious ceremony and shining brightly with you
personality, raise up. Oh, Majesty, like the sun from mount Meru."

verses.sanskrit:
सोमसूर्यौ च काकुत्थ्स शिववैश्रवणावपि || २-१४-५२

वरुणाश्छग्निरिन्द्रश्च विजयं प्रदिश्न्तु ते |
verses.translation:
"Oh, Dasaratha born in Kakutsa dynasty! May the Gods -Sun and the Moon Shiva and
Kubera, Varuna, Agni and Indra bestow you victory!"

verses.sanskrit:
गता भगवती रात्रिः कृतकृत्य मिदं तव || २-१४-५३

बुद्ध्यस्व सृपशार्दूल कुरु कार्यमनन्तरम् |

उदतिष्ठत रामस्य समग्रमभिषेचन्म् || २-१४-५४
verses.wordMeaning:
58. mahiipatiH = King Dasaratha; iti = thus;
shrutvaa = hearing; tasya vachaH = his words; arthavat = which were
meaningful; saantva puurvamiva = and which were very soothing ; abhyakiiryata =
was surrounded ; shokena = with grief; bhuumya eva = once more.
verses.translation:
"Oh, the best of the kings ! The holy night has gone by. Know what has been done and
do what has to be done. All the requirements for Rama's coronation is kept ready" .

verses.sanskrit:
पौरजानपदैश्चापि नैगमैश्च कृताञ्जलिः |

स्वयं वसिष्ठो भगवान् ब्राह्मणैः सह तिष्ठति || २-१४-५५
verses.wordMeaning:
55. bhagavaan = The venerable; vasishhThaH = Vasishta;
svayam = hi mself ; tishhThati = stands; bhaahmaNaiH scha = along with
brahmanas; kR^itaaJNjaliH = being saluted with joined palms by; paura
jaanapadaishchaapi = citizens and village folks; naigamaishcha = and merchants.
verses.translation:
"The venerable Vashishta himself stands waiting at the gate along with brahmanas,
being saluted saluted with joined palms by citizens, village folk and merchants."

verses.sanskrit:
क्षिप्रमाज्ञ्प्यतां राजन् राघवस्याभिषेचन्म् |

यथा ह्यपालाः पशवो यथा सेना ह्यानायका || २-१४-५६

यथा च्न्द्रं विना रात्रिर्यथा गावो विना वृषम् |

एवं हि भविता राष्ट्रं यत्र राजा न दृश्यते || २-१४-५७
verses.translation:
"Oh king! Give orders quickly for Rama's coronation ceremony. A kingdom without a
king is like cattle without a herdsman, army without a commander, night without the moon and cows
without a bull."

verses.sanskrit:
इति तस्य वचः श्रुत्वा सान्त्वपूर्वमिवार्थवत् |

अभ्यकीर्यत शोकेन भूय एव महीपतिः || २-१४-५८
verses.wordMeaning:
58. mahiipatiH = King Dasaratha; iti = thus;
shrutvaa = hearing; tasya vachaH = = his words; arthavat = which were
meaningful; saantva puurvamiva = and which were very soothing ; abhyakiiryata =
was surrounded ; shokena = with grief; bhuumya eva = once more. 59. saH raajaa = That king ; dhaarmikaH = who was pious;
shriimaan = and glorious; sannaharshha = having lost joy; sutam prati =
about his son; udvuikshhya = looked up; shoka raktekshhaNaH = with burning red
eyes ; uvaacha = spoke; tam suutam = to that charioteer; nikR^intasi =
"You are chopping off; mama = my; marmaaNi = vitals; bhuuyaH eva =
further; vaakyaiH = with words"

verses.sanskrit:
सुमन्त्रः करुणम् श्रुत्वा द्ऱ्ष्ट्वा दीनम् च पार्थिवम् || २-१४-६०

प्रग्ऱ्हीत अन्जलिः किंचित् तस्मात् देशात् अपाक्रमन् |
verses.wordMeaning:
60. sumantraH = Sumantra; srutva a = hearing;
karuNam = mournful words; dR^ishhTvaacha = and seeing ; diinam =
depressed; paarthivam = king; pragR^i hiitaaJNjaliH = taken upon himself the
joined palms; apaakramat = slipped away; kiNchit = a little; tasmaat
deshaat = from that place.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा वक्तुम् स्वयम् दैन्यान् न शशाक मही पतिः || २-१४-६१

तदा सुमन्त्रम् मन्त्रज्ञा कैकेयी प्रत्युवाच ह |
verses.wordMeaning:
61. yadaa = when; mahiipati H = the king; na
shashaaka = could not; vaktum = say; svayam = himself; dainyaat =
due to depression; tadaa = then; kaikeyi = Kaikeyi; mantrajJNaa = who is
experienced in fore-thoughts; pratyuvaachaha = replied; suman tram = to
Sumantra.

verses.sanskrit:
सुमन्त्र राजा रजनीं रामहर्षसमुत्सुकः || २-१४-६२

प्रजागरपरिश्रान्तो निद्रावशमुपेयुवान् |
verses.translation:
"Oh Sumantra! The king, being smitten by emotional joy in relation to Rama, who
tired of awakening the whole night and got subjected to sleep"

verses.sanskrit:
तद्गच्छ त्वरितं सूत राजपुत्रं यशस्विनम् || २-१४-६३

राममानय भद्रं ते नात्र कार्या विचारणा |
verses.translation:
"Oh, Sumantra! Hence, quickly go and bring the glorious prince Rama. Blessedness to
you! Do not have any hesitation in this matter. "

verses.sanskrit:
स मन्यमानः कल्याणम् हृदयेन नन्नन्ध च || २-१४-६४

निर्जगाम च संप्रीत्या त्वरितो राजशासनात् |
verses.wordMeaning:
64. saH = He; manyamaanaH = was thinking; kalyaanam
= of auspicious occasion; hR^idayena = in heart; nanandacha = and rejoiced;
sampriityaa = With pleasure; nirjagaama = he started; tvaritaH =
hurriedly; raaja shaasanaat = by the orders of the king.
verses.translation:
"He was thinking of that auspicious occasion in heart and rejoiced. With pleasure,
he set out, by the orders of the king."

verses.sanskrit:
सुमन्त्रश्चिन्तयामास त्वरितं चोदितस्तया || २-१४-६५

व्यक्तं रामोऽभिषेकार्थमिहायास्यति धर्मवित् |

इति सूतो मतिं कृत्वा हर्षेण महता वृतः || २-१४-६६

निर्जगाम महाबाहो राघवस्य दिदृक्षया |
verses.wordMeaning:
66. suutaH = Sumantra; iti = thus; matimkR^itvaa =
thinking ; mahataa harshheNa = in great joy; nirjagaama = set out;
didR^ikshhayaa = with desire to see; raaghavasya = Rama; mahaabaaho =
the long armed

verses.sanskrit:
सागरह्रदसंकाशात्सुमन्त्रोऽन्तःपुराच्छुभात् || २-१४-६७

निष्क्रम्य जनसंबाधं ददर्श द्वारमग्रतः |
verses.wordMeaning:
67. sumantraH = Sumantra; nishhkramya = coming out;
antaHpuraat = of gynaeceum; shubhaat = splendid; saagrahrada sankaashaat
= like a pool in the sea; dadarsha = saw; dvaaram = the gate; jana
sambaadhaam = looking congested; with men; agrahataH = in front.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः पुरस्तत्सासा विनिर्गतो |

महीपतीन् द्वारगतो विलोकयन् |

ददर्श पौरान् विविधान्महाधना |

नुपस्थितान् द्वारमुपेत्य विष्ठतान् || २-१४-६८

sargas.title: Sarga 15
sargas.introduction:
In this chapter, Sumantra enters the royal court and observes all the arrangements made 
for the coronation of Sri Rama. Ordered by Dasaratha to fetch Sri Rama, he enters the mighty building 
of Sri Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
ते तु ताम् रजनीम् उष्य ब्राह्मणा वेद पारगाः |

उपतस्थुर् उपस्थानम् सह राज पुरोहिताः || २-१५-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. te = these; brahmaNaaH = brahmanas; vedapaaragaaH 
= skilled in vedas; ushhya = stayed; taam rajiniim = for whole night; upatasthuH 
= and arrived; upasthaanam = at nearby place; saha raaja purohitaaH = along with royal 
priest; Vasista.

verses.sanskrit:
अमात्या बल मुख्याः च मुख्या ये निगमस्य च |

राघवस्य अभिषेक अर्थे प्रीयमाणाः तु सम्गताः || २-१५-२

उदिते विमले सूर्ये पुष्ये च अभ्यागते अहनि |

अभिषेकाय रामस्य द्विज इन्द्रैः उपकल्पितम् || २-१५-३

कान्चना जल कुम्भाः च भद्र पीठम् स्वलम्क्ऱ्तम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
3. raamasya = Rama's; abhishhekaaya = coronation; upakalpitam 
= arranged by; dvijendraiH = the best of brahmanas; udite = after rising; suurye 
= of sun; vimale = clearly; abhyaagate = on arrival of ; pushhyecha = pushyami 
star; praapte = and after arrival of; raamasya = Rama's; janma = birth;
sthite = position; karkatakalagne = of auspicious time of cancer.

verses.sanskrit:
काञ्चना जलकुमाभश्च भद्रपीठं स्वलङ्कृतम् || २-१५-४

रथश्च सम्यगा स्तीर्णोभास्वता व्याग्रचर्मणा |
verses.wordMeaning:
4. kaaJNchanaa = golden; jala kumbhaasheha = water pots; 
svalaNkR^itam = well decorated; bhadrapiitham = throne; rathashcha = and the 
chariot; aastiirNaH = covered by; bhaaasvataa = shining; vyaaghracharmaNaa 
= tiger-skin.

verses.sanskrit:
गङ्गायमुनयोः पुण्यात्सङ्गमादाहृतं जलम् || २-१५-५

याश्चान्याः सरितः पुण्या ह्रदाः कूपाः सरांसि च |

प्राग्वाहाश्चोर्ध्ववाहाश्च तिर्यग्वाहा स्समाहिताः || २-१५-६

ताभ्यश्चैवाहृतं तो यं समुद्रेभ्यश्च सर्वशः |

सलाजाः क्षीरिभिश्छन्ना घटाः काञ्चनराजताः || २-१५-७

पद्मोत्पलयुता भान्ति पूर्णाः परमवारिणा |

क्षौद्रं दधि घृतं लाजा दर्भाः सुमनसः पयः || २-१५-८

वेश्याश्चैव शुभाचाराः सर्वाभरणभूषिताः |
verses.wordMeaning:
8. kshaudram = honey; dadhi = curd; ghR^itam = clarified 
butter; laajaaH = popped grain; darbhaaH = sacred grass; sumanasaH = flowers;
payaH = milk(were glimmering); veshyaaHcha eva = as also harlots; shubhaachaaraaH 
= with good customs;sarvaabhara Na bhuushhitaaH = and adorned with all types of ornaments.

verses.sanskrit:
चन्द्रांशुविकचप्रख्यं काञ्चनं रत्नभुषितम् ||२-१५-९

सज्जं तिष्ठति रामस्य वालव्यजनमुत्तमम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
9. raamasya = Rama's; uttamam = excellent; vaalavyajanam 
= fly-flapper; chandraanshu vikachaprakhyam = like spread-out rays of moon; kaanchanam - made 
with gold; ratna bhuushhitam = adorned with diamonds; tishhTati = was kept; sajjam 
= ready.

verses.sanskrit:
चन्द्रमण्डलसम्काशमातपत्रं च पाण्डुरम् || २-१५-१०

सज्जं द्युतिकरं श्रीमदभिषेकपुरस्कृतम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
10. paaN^Duram = white; aatapatramcha = umbrella also; chandramN^dalasankaasham 
= equal to circular moon dyutikaram = which was shining; shriimat = which was beautifl; 
abhishhekapuraskR^itam = which was kept in advance for the occasion of coronation; sajjam 
= was ready.

verses.sanskrit:
पाण्डुरश्च वृषः सज्जः पाण्डुरोऽस्वश्च सुस्थितः || २-१५-११

प्रसृतश्च गजः श्रीमानौपवाह्यः प्रतीक्षते |

अष्टौ च कन्या माङ्गल्याः सर्वाभरणभूषिताः || २-१५-१२

वादित्राणि च सर्वाणि वन्दिनश्च तथापरे |
verses.wordMeaning:
12. ashhTau = eight; maaN^galyaaH = auspicious; kanyaaH 
= virgins; sarvaabharaNa bhuushhitaaH = adorned with all types of ornaments; sarvaaNi 
= all types of; vaaditraaNi = musical instruments; vandinaH cha = panegyrishts;
tathaa = and; pare = others; (were waiting)

verses.sanskrit:
इक्ष्वाकूणां यथा राज्ये संभ्रियेताभिषेचनम् || २-१५-१३

तथाजातीयमादाय राजपुत्राभिषेचन्म् |

ते राजवचनात्तत्र समवेता महीपतिम् || २-१५-१४

अपश्यन्तोऽब्रुवन् को बु राज्ञोनः प्रतिपादयेत् |

न पश्यामश्च राजानमुदितश्च दिवाकरः || २-१५-१५

यौवराज्याभिषेकश्च सज्जो रामस्य धीमतः |
verses.wordMeaning:
15. nacha pashyaamaH = (we are ) not seeing; raajaanam = the 
king; divaakaraH cha = the sun also; uditahH = has risen; dhiimataH = the 
wise; raamasya = Rama's yauvaraajyaabhishhekaH cha = crowing ceremony also; sajjaH - is ready.
verses.translation:
" The king is not being seen. The sun has risen. Everything is ready for the coronation 
of Rama, the wise".

verses.sanskrit:
इति तेषु ब्रुवाणेषु सार्वभौमान् महीपतीन् || २-१५-१६

अब्रवीत्तानिदं सर्वान्सुमन्त्रो राजसत्कृतः |
verses.wordMeaning:
16. teshhu bruvaaNeshu = while they were speaking; iti = thus;
sumantraH = sumantra; raaja satkR^itaH = who is well respected by king; abraviit 
= spoke; idam = these words; taan mahiipatiin = to those kings; sarvaan saarvaabhaumaan 
= who belong to all countries entirely.

verses.sanskrit:
रामं राज्ञो नियोगेन त्वरया प्रस्थितोऽस्म्यहम् || २-१५-१७

पूज्या राज्ञो भवन्तस्तु रामस्य च विशेषतः |
verses.translation:
"I am going to Rama quickly as per king's directions. But, you are all worthy of worship 
by Dasaratha and by Rama in particular."

verses.sanskrit:
अयं पृच्छामि वचनात् सुखमायुष्मतामहम् || २-१५-१८

राज्ञः संप्रतिबुद्धस्य चानागमनकारणम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
18. aayam aham = this I; pR^ichchhaami = shall ask; vachanaat 
= as word; aayushhmataam = if you who are long- lived; sukham = about well being;
raajJNaH = of king; sam pratibuddhasya = who is now awake; anaagamana kaaraaNam 
cha = and about the reason of not coming.
verses.translation:
"I shall ask as your word about the well-being of king who is now awake and also about 
the reason of his not coming here."

verses.sanskrit:
इत्युक्त्वान्तःपुरद्वारमाजगाम पुराणवित् || २-१५-१९

सदासक्तं च तद्वेश्म सुमन्त्रः प्रविवेश ह |

तुष्टावास्य तदा वंशं प्रविश्य स विशां पतेः || २-१५-२०

शयनीयं नरेन्ध्रस्य तदसाद्य व्यतिष्ठत |

सोऽत्यासाद्य तु तद्वेश्म तिरस्करिणि मन्त्रा || २-१५-२१

आशीर्भिर्गुणयुक्ताभिरभितुष्टाव राघवम् |

सोमसूर्यौ च काकुत्स्थ शिववैश्रवणावपि || २-१५-२२

वरुणश्चग्निरिन्द्रश्च विजयम् प्रदिशन्तु ते |
verses.wordMeaning:
22. kaakutthsa = oh; king Dasaratha! soma suuryau = moon and 
sund; shivavyashravaNau api = shiva and kubera; varuNaH cha = Varuna; agniH 
= Agni; indraH cha = Indra; pradishantute = grant; vijayam = victory; 
te = to you.
verses.translation:
"Oh, king Dasaratha! Let sun and the moon, guardians of the world like Shiva and Kubera, 
Varuna, Agni and Indra grant victory to you!"

verses.sanskrit:
गता भगवती रात्रिरः शिवमुपस्थितम् || २-१५-२३

बुद्ध्यस्व नृपशार्दूल कुरु कार्यमनन्तरम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
23. nR^ipashaarduula = oh; the best of kings! bhagavatii = the 
venerable; raatriH = night; gataa = has gone; shivam = the auspicious;
ahaH = day; upasthitam = has come; buddhyasya = wake up; kuru = 
do; kaaryam = whatever to be done; anantaram = thereafter.
verses.translation:
"Oh, the best of kings! The venerable night has gone. The auspicious day has come. Wake 
up and do whatever needs to be done thereafter."

verses.sanskrit:
ब्राह्मणा बलमुख्याश्च नैगमाश्चागता नृप || २-१५-२४

दर्शनम् प्रतिकाङ्क्षन्ते प्रतिबुद्ध्यस्व राघव |
verses.wordMeaning:
24. raaghava nR^ipa = oh; king born in Raghu dynasty ! braahmaNaaH 
cha = chiefs of armed forces; nygamaaH cha = and traders; aagataaH = have come;
pratikaaN^kshhante = willing; darshanam = to see; paratibuddhyasya = wake 
up!
verses.translation:
"Oh, king Dasaratha ! Brahmanas, chiefs of armed forces and traders are waiting to see 
you. Please wake up."

verses.sanskrit:
स्तुवन्तं तम् तदा सूतं सुमन्त्रं मन्त्रकोविदम् || २-१५-२५

प्रतिबुद्ध्य ततो राजा इदं वचनमब्रवीत् |

राममानय सूतेति यदस्यभिहितो/अनया || २-१५-२६

किमिदं कारणम् येन ममाज्ञा प्रतिहन्यते |
verses.wordMeaning:
26. suuta = oh; the charioteer! kim = what is; idam kaaraNam 
= this reason; yena by which; mama aajJNaa = my order; pratihamyate = is being rejected;
yat abhihitaH api = eventhough you were told; anaya = to bring; raamam = 
Rama.
verses.translation: "Why have you not followed my orders even though you were asked by her to bring Rama."

verses.sanskrit:
न चैव सम्प्रसुप्तोऽहमानयेहाशु राघवम् || २-१५-२७

इति राजा दशरथः सूतं तत्रान्वशात्पुनः |

स राजवचनं श्रुत्वा शिरसा प्रतिपूज्य तम् || २-१५-२८

निर्जगम नृपावासान्मन्यमानः प्रियं महत् |

प्रसन्नो राजमार्गं च पताकाध्वजशोभितम् || २-१५-२९

हृष्टः प्रमुदितः सूतो जगामाशु विलोकयन् |
verses.wordMeaning:
29. suutaH = the charioteer placid; prasannaH = became placeid;
jagaama = went; hR^ishhTaH = rejoiced; pramuditaH = with pleasure; vilokayan 
= seeing; raajamaargamcha = royal roads; pataakaadhvaja shobhitam = adorned with flags 
and banners.

verses.sanskrit:
स सूतस्तत्र शुश्राव रामाधिकरणाः कथाः || २-१५-३०

अभिषेचनसंयुक्तास्सर्वलोकस्य हृष्टवत् |

ततो ददर्श रुचिरं कैलासशिखरप्रभम् || २-१५-३१

रामवेश्म सुमन्त्रस्तु शक्रवेश्मसमप्रभम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
31. tataH = then; sumantraH tu = Sumantra; dadarshha 
= saw; raamaveshma = Rama's palace; ruchiram = which ws lovely; kailaasa sikharaprabham 
= shining like top of a mountain; shakra veshma samaprabham = radiant like Indra's palace.

verses.sanskrit:
महाकवाटपिहितं वितर्दिशतशोभितम् || २-१५-३२

काञ्चनप्रतिमैकाग्रं मणिविद्रुमतोरणम् |

शारदाभ्रघनप्रख्यं दीप्तं मेरुगुहोपमम् || २-१५-३३

मणिभिर्वरमाल्यानां सुमहद्भिरलंकृतम् |

मुक्तामणिभिराकीर्णं चन्धनागुरुभूषितम् || २-१५-३४

गन्धान्मनोज्ञान् विसृजद्धार्दुरं शिखरं यथा |

सारसैश्च मयूरैश्च विनदद्भिर्विराजितम् || २-१५-३५

सुकृतेहामृगाकीर्णं सुकीर्णं भक्तिभिस्तथा |

मन्श्चक्षुश्च भूतानामाददत्तिग्मतेजसा || २-१५-३६

चन्द्रभास्करसंकाशम् कुबेरभवनोपमम् |

महेन्द्रधामप्रतिमं नानापक्षिसमाकुलम् || २-१५-३७

मेरुशृङ्गसमम् सूतो रामवेश्म ददर्श ह |

उपस्थितैः समाकीर्णम् जनैरञ्जलिकारिभिः || २-१५-३८

उपादाय समाक्रान्तैस्तथा जानपदैर्जनैः |

रामाभिषेकसुमुखैरुन्मुखैः समलम्कृतम् || २-१५-३९

महामेघसमप्रख्यमुदग्रं सुविभूषितम् |

नानारत्नसमाकीर्णं कुब्जकैरातकावृतम् || २-१५-४०

स वाजियुक्तेन रथेन सारथि |

र्नराकुलं राजकुलम् विराजयन् |

वरूथिना रामगृहाभिपातिना |

पुरस्य सर्वस्य मनांसि हर्शयन् || २-१५-४१

ततस्समासाद्य महाधनं महत् |

प्रहृष्टरोमा स बभूव सारथिः |

मृगैर्मयूरैश्च समाकुलोल्बणं |

गृहं वरार्हस्य शचीपतेरिव || २-१५-४२

स तत्र कैलासनिभाः स्वलंकृताः |

प्रविश्य कक्ष्यास्त्रिदशालयोपमाः |

प्रियान् वरान् राममते स्थितान् बहून् |

व्यपोह्य शुद्धांतमुपस्थितो रथी || २-१५-४३

स तत्र शुश्राव च हर्षयुक्ता |

रामाभिषेकार्थकृता जनानां |

नरेंद्रसूनोरभिमंगळार्थाः |

सर्वस्य लोकस्य गिरः प्रहृष्टः || २-१५-४४

महेंद्रसद्मप्रतिमं तु वेश्म |

रामस्य रम्यं मृगपक्षिजुष्टं |

ददर्श मेरोरिव शृंगमुच्चं | 

विभ्राजमानं प्रभया सुमम्त्रः || २-१५-४५

उपस्थितै रञ्जलिकारिभिश्च |

सोपायनैर्जानपदैर्जनैश्च |

कोट्या परार्धैश्च विमुक्तयानैः |

समाकुलं द्वारपदम् ददर्श || २-१५-४६
verses.wordMeaning:
46. dadarsha = saw; dvaarapadam = area of entrance; samaakulam 
= filled with; kotyaa = crores; parardhaishcha = and millions of; jaanapadaiH 
= of rural folk; janaishcha = and people; upasthitaaH = who approached; anjalikaaribhisheha 
= with folded hands; sopaayanaiH = with gifts; vimuktayaanaiH = who left their respective 
vehicles.

verses.sanskrit:
ततो महामेघमहीधराभं |

प्रभिन्नमत्यङ्कुशमत्यसह्यम् |

रामोपवाह्याम् रुचिरम् ददर्श |

शत्रुम्जयं नागमुदग्रकायम् || २-१५-४७
verses.wordMeaning:
47. tataH = there after; dadarsha = saw; ruchiram = 
beautiful; naagam = elephant; shatrunjayam = called satrunjayam; udagrakaayam 
= with highly elevated; raamopavaahyam = Rama's royal vehichle; mahaamegha mahiidharaabham 
= like great cloud and mountain;prabhinnam = intoxicated; atyaN^kusham = uncontrollabel;
asahyam = intolerable.

verses.sanskrit:
स्वलंकृतान् सास्वरथान् सकुम्जरा |

नमात्यमुखयांश्च ददर्श वल्लभान् |

व्यपोह्य सूतः सहितान्समंततः |

समृद्धमंतःपुर माविवेश ह || २-१५-४८
verses.wordMeaning:
48. dadarsha = saw; amaatya mukhyaamshaha = the ministers in 
chief; vallabhaan = who were beloved to the king; svalankR^itaan = who were well-adorned;
saashvarathaan = with horse carriages sakunjaraan = the people; gathered; tataH = 
on all sides; aviveshaH = entered; samR^iddham = rich; antaHpuram = inner 
apartment.

verses.sanskrit:
ततोऽद्रिकूटाचलमेघसन्नि भं |

महाविमानोपमवेश्मसंयुतम् |

अवार्यमाणः प्रविवेश सारथिः |

प्रभूतरत्नं मकरो यथार्णवम् || २-१५-४९
verses.wordMeaning:
49. tataH = then; saarathiH = the charioter; pravivesha 
= entered; mahaavimaanopama veshma samyutam = that Rama's palace containing houses equal to 
excellent divine cars; adrikuutaachala magha sannibham = similar to top of mountain; and immoveable 
cloud; makaro yathaa = like crocodile(entering) arNavam = ocean; prabhuutaratnam = 
containg; a number of precious stones; avaaryamaaNaH = without being obstructed by anyone.

sargas.title: Sarga 16
sargas.introduction:
In this chapter, Sumantra arrives at Rama's palace to fetch him to royal court. Rama
starts to the royal court to meet his father. On the way Rama observes the jubiliant city.

verses.sanskrit:
स तदंतःपुरद्वारं समतीत्य जनाकुलम् |

प्रविविक्तां ततः कक्ष्यामाससाद पुराणवित् || २-१६-१

प्रास कार्मुक बिभ्रद्भिर् युवभिर् मृष्ट कुण्डलैः |

अप्रमादिभिर् एक अग्रैः स्वनुरक्तैः अधिष्ठिताम् || २-१६-२

तत्र काषायिणो वृद्धान् वेत्रपाणीन् स्वमंकृतान् |

ददर्शविष्ठितान् द्वारि स्त्रध्यक्षान् सुसमाहितान् || २-१६-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. tatra = there; dadarsha = (he)saw; vR^iddhaan =
aged; stryadhyakshhaan = palace supervisors; kaashhayiNaH = with orange coloured
clothes; svalankR^itaan = well-adorned themselves; vetrapaaNiin = having vanes in
their hands; susamaahitaan = well composed; vishhTitaan = being posted;
dvaari = in door-ways.

verses.sanskrit:
ते समीक्ष्य समायान्तं रामप्रियचिकीर्षवः |

सहसोत्पतिताः सर्वे ह्यसनेभ्यः ससम्भ्रमाः || २-१६-४

तानुवाच विनीतात्मा सूतपुत्रः प्ररक्षिणः |

क्षिप्रमाख्यात रामाय सुमन्त्रे द्वारि तिष्ठति || २-१६-५

ते राममुपसंगम्य भर्तुः प्रियचिकीर्षवः |

सहभार्याय रामाय क्षिप्रमेवाचचक्षिरे || २-१६-६

प्रतिवेदितमाज्ञाय सूतमभ्यन्तरं पितुः |

तत्रैवानाययामास राघवः पियकाम्यया || २-१६-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. aN^yaaya = knowing; suutam = Sumantra;
abhyantaram = who was intimate; pituH = to father; prativeditam = being
informed by them ; raagahava = Rama; priyakaamyayaa = who desired to show
kindness; anaayayamaasa = called him in ; tatraiva = there only.

verses.sanskrit:
तं वैश्रवणसंकाशमुपविष्टुं स्वलंकृतम् |

ददर्श सूतः पर्यङ्गे ससैवर्णे सोत्तरच्छदे || २-१६-८

वराहरुघिराभेण शुचिना च सुगन्धिना |

अनुलिप्तं पराघर्येन चन्दनेन परंतपम् || २-१२-९

स्थितया पार्श्वतश्चापि वालव्यजनहस्तयास्थितया पार्श्वतश्चापि |

उपेतं सीतया भूयः चित्रया शशिनं यथा || २-१६-१०

तं तपन्तमिवादित्यम्पपन्नं स्वतेजसा |

ववन्दे वरदम् बन्दी नियमज्ञो विनीतवत् || २-१६-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. vandi = Sumantra; vinayajJNH = who knew pokite conduct;
vavande = respectfully saluted; tam = that Rama; vimiitavat = who was
refined; upapannam = endowed with; svateja sa = hi own brilliance;tapantam =
shining; aadityam iva = like sun; varadam = granting wishes.

verses.sanskrit:
प्राञ्जलिः ससुमुखं दृष्ट्वा विहारशयनासने |

राजपुत्रमुवाचेदं सुमन्त्रो राजसत्कृतः || २-१६-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. sumantraH = Sumantra; raajasatkR^itaH = who was treated
with respect by Rama; vuaacha = spoke; idam = these words; praN^jaliH =
with folded hands; raajaputram = to that prince; sumukham = with beautiful face;
vihaarashayanaasane = and who was seated on a swinging sofa.

verses.sanskrit:
कौसल्या सुप्रभा राम पिता त्वं द्रष्टुमिच्छति |

महिष्यापि हि कैकेय्या गम्यताम् तत्र माचिरम् || २-१६-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. raamaH = Rama; kausalyaa suprajaa = the good offspring
of Kausalya! pitaa = your father; mahishhyaa kaikeyyaasaha = along with Queen Kaikeyi;
ichchhati = wish; drasTum = to see; tvaam = you; gamyataam =
let you go; tatra = there; maachiram = wtihout delay.
verses.translation:
"Oh Rama, the good offspring of Kausalya! Your father and the Queen Kaikeyi want to
see you. Go there soon."

verses.sanskrit:
एवमुक्तस्तु संहृष्टो नरसिंहो महाद्युतिः |

ततः सम्मानयाम् सीतामिदमुवाच ह || २-१६-१४

देवि देवश्च देवी च समागम्य मदंतरे |

मन्त्रेयेते ध्रुवं किंचित् अभिषेचन संहितम् || २-१६-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. devi = Oh; Sita! devashcha = the king; deviicha
= and the queen; samaagamya = together; mantrayate = are thinking;
kinchit = of something; madantare = about me; abhishhachana samhitam =
relating to coronation; dhR^ivam = certain.
verses.translation:
"Oh Sita! The king and the queen together are thinking of something about me,
relating to coronation. It is certain."

verses.sanskrit:
लक्षयित्वा ह्यभिप्रायं प्रियकामा सुदक्षिणा |

संचोदयति राजानं मदर्थ मसितेक्षणा || २-१६-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. asitekshhaNaa = the black-eyed Kaikeyi; sudakshhiNaa =
who is dexterous; priyakaamaa = friendly disposed; lakshhayitva = perceived;
abhipraayam = the opinion; sanchodayati = inciting; raajaanam = the
king; madartham = for my sake.
verses.translation:
"Then black-eyed Kaikeyi who is dexterous and friendly disposed, has perceived the
king's opinion and is inciting him for my sake"

verses.sanskrit:
सा प्रहृष्टा महाराजं हितकामानुवर्तिनी |

जननी चार्थकामा मे केकयाधिपतेस्सुता || २-१६-१७
verses.translation:
"Kaikeyi, my mother is following the king's intentions with great pleasure, desirous
of my benefit and prosperity"

verses.sanskrit:
दिष्ट्या खलु महाराजो महिष्या प्रियया सह |

सुमन्त्रं प्रहिणोद् दूतमर्थकामकरं मम || २-१६-१८
verses.translation:
"It is certainly our luck that king and queen have sent Sumantra, who fulfils my
desires and wishes, as messenger".

verses.sanskrit:
यादृशी परिषत् तत्र तादृशो दूताअगतः |

ध्रुवमधैव मां राजा यौवराज्येऽभिषेक्ष्यति || २-१६-१९
verses.translation:
"A worthy messenger has come who is truly representing the assembly there. It is
certain that today itself the king will anoint me for the right of succession to kingdom".

verses.sanskrit:
हन्त शिघ्रमितो जत्वा द्रक्ष्यामि च महिपतिम् |

सह त्वं परिवारेण सुखमास्स्व रमस्व च || २-१६-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. hanta = Alas! gatvaa = (I shall) go; shiighram =
immediately; itaH = from here; drakshhyaami cha = and see; mahipatim =
the king; tvam = you; aasva = stay; sukham = happily; parivaareNa
saha = with the circle of friends; ramasva cha = and enjoy yourself.
verses.translation:
"I shall go immediately and see the king. You stay happily with the circle of
friends and enjoy yourself."

verses.sanskrit:
पतिसम्मानिता सीता भर्तारम्असितक्षणा |

आ द्वारमनुवव्राज मङ्गलान्यभिदध्युषी || २-१६-२१
verses.wordMeaning:
21. siitaa = Sita; patisammaanitaa = treated with respect by
husband; asitekshhaNaa = having black eyes; anuvavraaja = followed;
bhartaaram = husband; aa dvaaram = upto door; abhidadhyushhii =
thinking; mangalaani = of auspicious things.

verses.sanskrit:
राज्यं द्विजतिभीर्जुष्टं राजसूयभिषेचनम् |

कर्तुमर्हति ते राजा वासवस्येव लोककृत् || २-१६-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. raajaa = king Dasaratha; te = to you; raajyam =
kingdom; jushhTam = being served; dvijaatibhiH = by brahmanas;
vasavasyeva = like to Devendra; lokakR^it = by Brahma; arhati = to
perform; raajasuuyaabhishhehanam = great sacrifice at coronation of king.
verses.translation:
"King Dasaratha will give you kingdom, being served by brahmanas, which will invest
you with eligibility to perform the great sacrifice at the coronation ceremony like Brahma to
Devendra."

verses.sanskrit:
दीक्षितं व्रतसम्पन्नं वराजिनधरं शुचिम् |

कुरङ्गशृङ्गपाणिं च पश्यन्ती त्वां भजाम्यहम् || २-१६-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. aham = I; bhajaami = shall enjoy; pashyantii =
seeing; tvaam = you; diikshhtam = initiated; vratasampannam = for
perfect religious act of austerity; varaajinadharam = wearing excellent antelope-hide;
shuchim = being pure; kuraN^ga shR^iN^gapaaNimcha = and bearing a deer-horn in
hand.
verses.translation:
"I shall be delighted to see you, initiated for the perfect religious act of
austerity, wearing excellent antelope-hide, being pure and bearing a deer-horn in hand".

verses.sanskrit:
पुर्वां दिशं वज्रधरो दक्षिणां पातु ते यमः |

वरुणः पाश्चिमामाशां धनेशस्तुत्तरं दिशम् || २-१६-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. te = your; puurvam disham = eastern direction;
vajradharaH = Indra the god of rain; dakshhiNaam = south; yamaH = yama
the god of death; pashchimaamashaam = western direction; varunaH = varuna the
regent of ocean; uttaraam disham tu = northern direction; dhaneshaH = Kubera; the
god of riches; paatu = protect.
verses.translation:
"May Indra the god of rain in the east, Yama the god of death in the south, Varuna
the regent of the ocean in the west and Kubera the god of riches and treasure in the north, protect
you".

verses.sanskrit:
अथ सीतामनुज्ञाप्य कृतकौतुकमगळः |

निश्चक्राम सुमन्त्रेण सह रामो निवेशनात् || २-१६-२५

पर्वतादिव निष्क्रम्य सिंहो गिरिगुहाशयः |

लक्ष्मणंद्वारि सोऽपश्यत् प्रह्वञ्जलिपुटं स्थितम् || २-१६-२६

अथ मध्यमकक्ष्यायां समागच्छत् सुहृज्जनैः |

स सर्वानर्थिनो दृष्ट्वा समेत्य प्रतिनन्द्य च || २-१६-२७

ततः पावकसंकाशमारुरोह रथोत्तमम् |

वैयाघ्रं पुरुष्व्याघो राजितं राजनंदनः || २-१६-२८

मेघनादमसम्बाधं मणिहेमविभूशितम् |

मुष्णन्तम् इव चक्षूंषि प्रभया मेरुवर्चसम् || २-१६-२९

करेणुशिशुकल्पैश्च युक्तं परमवाजिभिः |

हरियुक्तं सहस्राक्षो रथमिन्द्र इवाशुगम् || २-१६-३०

प्रययौ तूरमास्थाय राघवो ज्वलितः श्रिया |

स पर्जन्य इवाकाशे स्वनवानभिनादयन् || २-१६-३१

निकेतान्निर्ययौ श्रिमान् महाभ्रादिव चन्द्रमाः |
verses.wordMeaning:
31. shriimaan saH = that splendid chariot; svanavaan =
having sound; parjanya iva = like rain cloud; aakaashe = in the sky;
abhinaadayan = making sound; niryayau = started; niketaat = from the
house; chandramaa iva = like moon; mahaabhraat = from mighty cloud.

verses.sanskrit:
चित्रचामरपाणिस्तु लक्ष्मणो राघवानुजः ||२-१६-३२

जुगोप भ्रातरं भ्राता रथमास्थाय पृष्ठतः |
verses.wordMeaning:
32. lakshhmaNaH = Lakshmana; raaghavaanujaH = Rama's
younger; bhraataa = brother; aasthaaya = mounted; ratham = the chariot;
pR^ishhTataH = in the back-side; jugopa = protected; bhraataram =
brother; chhatra chaamara paaNiH = having umbrella and chamara fan in hand.

verses.sanskrit:
ततो हलहलाशब्दस्तुमुलह् समजायत ||२-१६-३३

तस्य निष्क्रममाणस्य जनौघस्य समन्ततः |
verses.wordMeaning:
33. tataH = then; tasya = those; janaughasya =
crowds; nishhkramamaaNasya = dispersing; samantataH = from all sides;
samajaayata = generated; tumulaH = tumultuous; halahalaashabdaH =
hallooing sound.

verses.sanskrit:
ततो हयवरा मुख्या नागाश्च गिरिसन्निभाः || २-१६-३४

अनुजग्मुस्तदा रामं शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः |
verses.wordMeaning:
34. tataH = thereupon; shatashaH = hundreds;
sahasrashaH = and thousands of ; mukhyaaH = importatn; hayavaraaH =
excellent horses; nagaashcha = and elephants; girisannibhaaH = looking like
mountains; tadaa = then; anujagmuH = followed; raamam = Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
अग्रतश्चास्य सन्नद्धाश्चन्दनागुरुभूषिताः || २-१६-३५

खड्गचापधराः शूरा जग्मुराशंसवो जनाः |
verses.wordMeaning:
35. shuraaH janaaH = valiant persons; sannaddhaaH = dressed
in armour; chandanaaguru bhushhitaaH = adorned with sandal and aloe perfumes;
khadgachaapadharaaH = wearing swords and bows; aashamsavaH = declaring arrival of
Rama; jagmuH = went; agrataH = in front; asya = of Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
ततो वादित्रशब्दाश्च स्तुतिशब्दाश्च वन्दिनाम् ||२-१६-३६

सिंहनादाश्च शूराणां तदा शुश्रुविरे पथि |
verses.wordMeaning:
36. tataH = thereafter; tadaa = then; vaaditra
shabdaashcha = sounds of musical instruments; stutishabdaashcha = sounds of praising
hymns; vandinaam = of panegyrists; simha naadaashcha = and lion like roars;
shuuraaNaa = of valiant perosons; shushruvare = were heard; pathi = on
the way.

verses.sanskrit:
हर्म्यवातायनस्थाभिर्भूषिताभिः समन्ततः || २-१६-३७

कीर्यमाणः सुपुष्पौघैर्ययौ स्त्रीभिररिंदमः |

रामं सर्वानवद्याण्ग्यो रामपिप्रीषया ततः || २-१६-३८

वचोभिरग्र्यैर्हर्म्यस्थाः क्षितिस्थाश्च ववन्दिरे |
verses.wordMeaning:
38. tataH then; sarvaanavadyaaN^gyaa = women of equisitely beautiful
form; harmyasthaah = standing in palaces; kshhitisthaashcha = and on ground
vavandhire = were offering salutations; raamam = to Rama; agryaiH vachobhiH =
with the best words; raamapipriishhayaa = with desire to please Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
नूनं नन्दति ते माता कौसल्या मातृनन्दन || २-१६-३९

पश्यन्ती सिद्धयात्रं त्वं पित्र्यं राज्यमुपस्थितम् |
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama, who creates happiness to mother! Have successful journey! Your mother
Kausalya will be certainly delighted to see you obtaining the paternal kingdom."

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वसीमन्तिनीभ्यश्च सीतां सीमन्तिनीं वराम् || २-१६-४०

अमन्यन्त हि ता नार्यो रामस्य हृदयप्रियाम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
40. taaH = those; naaryaH = women; amanyantahi =
thought; siitaam = Sita; raamasya hR^idayapriyaam = who is dear to Rama's heart;
varaam = is better; siimantiim = woman; sarvasiimantiibhyaH = than all
women.

verses.sanskrit:
तया सुचरितं देव्या पुरा नूनं महत्तपः || २-१६-४१

रोहिणीव शशाङ्केन रामसंयोगमाप या |
verses.translation:
"As Rohini star stays together with the moon, Sita having obtained Rama as husband
must have certainly performed a great penance in former times."

verses.sanskrit:
इति प्रासादशृङ्गेषु प्रमदाभिर्नरोत्तमः || २-१६-४२

शुश्राव राजमार्गस्थः प्रिया वाच उदाहृताः |

स राघवस्त्रत्र तदा प्रलापान् |

शुश्राव लोकस्य समागतस्य |

आत्माधिकारा विविधाश्च वाचः |

प्रहृष्टरूपस्य पुरे जनस्य || २-१६-४३

एष श्रियं गच्छति राघवोऽद्य|

राजप्रसादातद् विपुलं गमिष्यन् |

एते वयम् सर्वसमृद्धकामा|

योषामयं नो भविता प्रशास्ता || २-१६-४४
verses.translation:
"By the king's propitiousness, this Rama today is going to obtain this earth and the
wealthy kingdom. All our desires are going to be fulfilled because Rama will become the ruler."

verses.sanskrit:
लाभो जनस्यास्य यदेष सर्वं|

प्रपत्स्यते राष्ट्रमिदं चिराय |

न ह्यप्रियं किंचन जातु कश्चित् |

पश्येन्न कुःखं मनुजादिपेऽस्मिन् || २-१६-४५
verses.translation:
"If Rama rules this state for a long time, it will be a great benefit to the people.
If he becomes a king none can see dislike and sorrow at any time."

verses.sanskrit:
स घोषवद्भिश्च हयैः सनागैः |

पुरस्सरैः स्वस्तिकसूतमागधैः |

महीयमानः प्रवरैश्च वादकैः |

अभिष्टुतो वैश्रवणो यथा ययौ|| २-१६-४६
verses.wordMeaning:
46. suutamaagadhaiH = bards and panegyrists; svastika =
invoking blessings; purassaraiH = moving in front; mahiiyamaanaH = praising with
great respect; abhishhTutaH = eulogised; pravaraiH vaadyakaiH = by the most
excellent instrumentalists; saH = that Rama; vaishraavaNotatha = like Kubera the
god of weatlh; yayau = went; hayaiH = with horses; sanaagaiH = together
with elephants; ghoshhavadbhiH = having resounds.

verses.sanskrit:
करेणुमात्ङ्गरथाश्वसंकुलं |

महाजनौघैः परिपूर्णचत्वरम् |

प्रभूतरत्नं बहुपण्यसंचयं |

ददर्श रामो विमलं महापथम् || २-१६-४७

sargas.title: Sarga 17
sargas.introduction:
This chapter further describes journey towards the royal palace. On the way Rama hears 
blessings and well meaning words of his friends and various people gathered there.

verses.sanskrit:
स रमो रथमास्थाय सम्प्रहृष्टसुहृञनः |

पताकाध्वजसंपन्नं महार्हगुरुधूपितम् || २-१७-१

अपश्यन्नगरं श्रीमान् नानाजनसमाकुलम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
1. raamaH = Rama; sriimaan the glourious man; samprahR^ishhTa suhR^ijjanaH 
= having joyful friends; aasthaaya = ascended ; ratham = the chariot; apashyat 
= saw; nagaram = the city; pataakaadhavaja sampannam = rich with flags and banners;
mahaarhaguru dhuupitam = perfumed with excellent befitting wood; naanaajanasamanvitam 
= endowed with varied types of people.

verses.sanskrit:
स गृहैरभ्रसंकाशैः पांडुरैरुपशोभितम् || २-१७-२

राजमार्गं ययौ रामो मध्येनगरुधूपितम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
2. saH raamaH = that Rama; yayau = went; raajamaargam madhyena 
= through; middle of royal = passage; agarudhuupitam = which was incensed with aloe 
wood; upashobhitam = splendid; paN^duraiH grihaiH = with white houses; abhrasaN^kaashaiH 
= resembling like clouds.

verses.sanskrit:
चन्दनानाम् च मुख्यानामगुरूणाम् च संचयैः || २-१७-३

उत्तमानां च गन्धानां क्षौमकौशाम्बरस्य च |

अविद्धाभिश्च मुक्ताभिरुत्तमैः स्फाटिकैरपि || २-१७-४

शोभमानसम्बाधं तं राजपथमुत्तमम् |

संवृतं विविधैः पुष्पैभ्रुक्ष्यैरुच्चावचैरपि || २-१७-५

ददर्श तं राजपथं दिवि देवपतिर्यथा |

दध्यक्षतहविर्लाजैर्धूपैरगुरुचंदनैः || २-१७-६

नानामाल्योपगंधैश्च सदाभ्यर्चितचत्वरम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
6. dadarsha = (Rama) saw; tam raajapatham = that royal passage;
devapatham yathaa = like divine passage; divi = in heaven; abhyarahita chatvaram 
= whose road junctions were worshipped; sadaa = perpetually; dadhyakshhata havirlaajaiH 
= with yoghurt; unbroken rice; clarified butter; fried grain; dhuupaiH = incense; aguruchandanaiH 
= aloe and sandal wood; naanaa maalyopagandhaishcha = and all types of garlands and perfumes.

verses.sanskrit:
आशीर्वादान् बहून् शृण्वन् सुहृद्भिः समुदीरितान्  || २-१७-७

यथार्हं चापि सम्पूज्य सर्वानेव नारान् ययौं |
verses.wordMeaning:
7. shR^iNvan = hearing; bahuun = various; aashiirvaadaan 
= blessings; samudiiritaan = spoken; suhR^idbhiH = by friends; yayau = (he) 
went; sampuujyacha = acknowledging; sarvaanena = all; naraan = people;
yathaarham = according to merit.

verses.sanskrit:
पितामहैराचरितं तथैव प्रपितामहैः || २-१७-८

अध्योपादाय तं मार्गमभिषित्तोऽनुपालय |
verses.wordMeaning:
8. abhishhiktaH = get coronated; adya = today; upaadaaya 
= take; tam maargam = that path; aacharitam = followed pitaamahaiH = by grandfathers;
tathaiva = and likewise; prapitaamahaiH = by greatgrandfathers; ampaalaya 
= and preserve(kingdom).
verses.translation:
"Get coronated today, take the path followed by your grandfathers and great grandfathers 
and rule the kingdom."

verses.sanskrit:
यथा स्म पोषिताः पित्रा यथा पूर्वैः पितामहैः ||२-१७-९

ततः सुखतरं सर्वे रामे वत्स्याम राजनि |
verses.wordMeaning:
9. raame = Rama; raajanisati = being king; vatsyaamaH 
= (we shall) dwell; sukhataram = happily further; tataH = than that; pitraa 
= by father; yathaa = in which way; poshhitaasma = we were ruled; yathaa 
= by which way; sarvaiH = by all; pitaamahaiH = grandfathers.
verses.translation:
"If Rama becomes king, we shall be more happy than when his father ruled and also when 
his grandfathers ruled.

verses.sanskrit:
अलमद्य हि भुक्तेन परमार्थैरलं च नः || २-१७-१०

यथा पश्याम निर्यान्तं रामं राज्ये प्रतिष्ठितम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
10. pashyaamayadi = if we can see; raamam = Rama; niryaantam 
= going forth in city; pratisThitam = being established; raajye = in kingdom; 
adya = today; alam = it is enough; bhuktena = of eating; alam = it is 
enough; paramaarthaiH = of other concerns.
verses.translation:
"If we can see Rama going forth in the city after getting coronated today, it is enough 
of eating or other activities."

verses.sanskrit:
ततो हि न प्रियतरं नान्यत्किंचिद्भविश्यति || २-१७-११

यथाभिषेको रामस्य राज्येनामिततेजसः |
verses.wordMeaning:
11. kimchit = "which anyat = other thing; nabhavishhyati = is 
not about to be; priyataram = dearer; natt = to us; yathaa = as; abhishhekaH 
= coronation; raajyena = in kingdom; raamasya = of Rama; annitachetasaH = 
of unbounded spleadour.
verses.translation: "There is no other dearer thing to us than coronation of Rama of unbounded splendour".

verses.sanskrit:
एताश्चान्याश्च शुहृदामुदासीनः सुभाः कथाः || २-१७-१२

आत्मसम्पूजनीः शृण्वन् ययौ रामो महापथम् |

नहि तस्मान्मनः कश्चिच्चक्षुषी वा नरोत्तमात् || २-१७-१३

नरः रोशवनोत्यपाक्रष्टुमातिक्रान्तोऽपि राघवे |
verses.wordMeaning:
13. raaghave atikraante api = though Rama has gone distantly; kashchit 
naraH = which man; na shaknotihi = was not able; apaakrashhTum = to turn back;
chakshhushhii = eyes; manaH = mind; tasmaat = that; narottamaat 
= best of men.

verses.sanskrit:
यश्च रामं न पश्येत्तु यं च रामो न पश्यति || २-१७-१४

निन्दतः सर्वलोकेषु स्वात्माप्येनं विगर्हते |
verses.wordMeaning:
14. yashcha = who; napashyethu = did not see; raamam 
= Rama; yamcha = and to whom; raamaH = Rama; napashyati = did not see;
ninditaH = was blamed; sarvalokeshhu = among all people; svaatmaapi = one's 
mind also; vigarhate = yould blame; enam = oneself.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वेषाम् स हि धर्मात्मा वर्णानां कुरुते दयाम् || २-१७-१५

चतुर्णां हि वयह्स्थानाम् तेन ते तमनुव्रता |
verses.wordMeaning:
15. saH = that Rama; dharmaatmaa = the virtuous; kurute dayaa 
= took pity on; sarveshhu = all men; chaturNaami = of four; varNaanaam = 
classes; vayasthaanam = towards the aged; tena = hence; te = they; anuvartaaH 
= were devoted; tam = to him.

verses.sanskrit:
चत्पुष्पथान् देवपथांश्चैत्यांश्चायतनानि च || २-१७-१६

प्रदक्षिणं परिहरन् जगाम नृपतेस्सुतः |
verses.wordMeaning:
16. nnapate sutaH = Rama; the prince jagaama = went; pariharam 
= by crossing; chatushhpathaan = road junctions; devapathaan = god's paths; chaityaan 
= sacred trees; aayatanaanicha = and temples; pradakshhiNam = keeping his; right side 
towards them.

verses.sanskrit:
स राजकुलमासाद्य मेघसंघोपमैः शुभैः || २-१७-१७

प्रासादशृङ्गैर्विविधैः कैलासशिखरोपमैः |

आवारयद्भिर्गनं विमानैरिव पाण्डुरैः || २-१७-१८

वर्धमानगृहैश्चापि रत्नजालपरिष्कृतैः |

तत्पृथिव्यां गृहवरं महेन्द्रसदनोपमम् || २-१७-१९

राजपुत्रः पितुर्वेश्म प्रविवेश श्रिया ज्वलन् |

स कक्ष्या धन्विभिर्गुप्तास्तिस्रोऽतिक्रम्य वाजिभिः || २-१७-२०

पदातिरपरे कक्ष्ये द्वे जगाम नरोत्तमः |

स सर्वाः समतिक्रम्य कक्ष्या दशरथात्मजः || २-१७-२१

संनिवर्त्य जनं सर्वं शुद्धान्तःपुरुमत्यगात् |

तस्मिन् प्रविष्टे पितुर्न्तिकं तदा |

जनः स सर्वो मुदितो नृपात्मजे |

प्रतीक्षते तस्य पुनः स्म निर्गमम् |

यथोदयं चन्द्रमसः सरित्पतिः || २-१७-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. tadaa = then; tasmin = that; nR^ipaatmaje = prince;
pravishhTe = went; antikam = nearer; pituH = to father; saH sarvaH 
= all those; janaH = people; muditaH = wer delighted; pratiikshhatesma = 
waited; punaH nirgamam = for exit again;chandramasaH udryam yathaa = as of rising of moon saritpatiH 
= the ocean.

sargas.title: Sarga 18
sargas.introduction:
In this chapter Rama arrives at the king's presence. Seeing King disturbed, Rama
becomes grieved and wants to know the cause of king's perturbance. Queen Kaikeyi describes to Rama
about the boons that Dasaratha had given earlier and also her wishes of coronating Bharatha instead
of Rama and sending Rama to Dandaka forest. On listening to these harsh words of Kaikeyi, Rama did
not become sorrowful, but Dasaratha was very much disturbed.

verses.sanskrit:
स ददर्श आसने रामः निषण्णम् पितरम् शुभे |

कैकेयी सहितम् दीनम् मुखेन परिशुष्यता || २-१८-१

स पितुः चरणौ पूर्वम् अभिवाद्य विनीतवत् |

ततः ववन्दे चरणौ कैकेय्याः सुसमाहितः || २-१८-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. saH = Rama; viniitavat = being humble;
susamaahitaH = well compsed; puurvam = first; abhivaadya = reverentially
saluted; pituH = father's charaNau = feet; tataH = then; vavande =
saluted; kaikeyyaaH = Kaikeyi's; charaNau = feet.

verses.sanskrit:
राम इति उक्त्वा च वचनम् वाष्प पर्याकुल ईक्षणः |

शशाक न्ऱ्पतिर् दीनो न ईक्षितुम् न अभिभाषितुम् || २-१८-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. nR^ipatiH = the king Dasaratha; uktvaa = spoke;
vachanam = the word; raamaiti = "Oh; Rama!" bhaashhpa paryaakulekshhaNaH
= with his eyes filled with tears; diinaH = dejected; nashashaaka = being not
able; iikshhitum = to see; na = nor able; abhibhaashhitum = to talk.

verses.sanskrit:
तत् अपूर्वम् नर पतेर् द्ऱ्ष्ट्वा रूपम् भय आवहम् |

रामः अपि भयम् आपन्नः पदा स्प्ऱ्ष्ट्वा इव पन्नगम् || २-१८-४

इन्द्रियैः अप्रह्ऱ्ष्टैअः तम् शोक सम्ताप कर्शितम् |

निह्श्वसन्तम् महा राजम् व्यथित आकुल चेतसम् || २-१८-५

ऊर्मि मालिनम् अक्षोभ्यम् क्षुभ्यन्तम् इव सागरम् |

उपप्लुतम् इव आदित्यम् उक्त अन्ऱ्तम् ऱ्षिम् यथा || २-१८-६

अचिन्त्य कल्पम् हि पितुस् तम् शोकम् उपधारयन् |

बभूव सम्रब्धतरः समुद्रैव पर्वणि || २-१८-७

चिन्तयाम् आस च तदा रामः पित्ऱ् हिते रतः |

किंस्विद् अद्य एव न्ऱ्पतिर् न माम् प्रत्यभिनन्दति || २-१८-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. chaturaH = the ingenious; raamaH = Rama; rataH =
who is fond of; pitR^ihite = father's welfare; chintayaamaasa = anxiously
thought; kimsvid = "why indeed; nR^ipatiH = king; adyaiva = just today;
napratyabhinandati = is not greeting in return; maam = to me?"

verses.sanskrit:
अन्यदा माम् पिता द्ऱ्ष्ट्वा कुपितः अपि प्रसीदति |

तस्य माम् अद्य सम्प्रेक्ष्य किम् आयासः प्रवर्तते || २-१८-९
verses.translation:
"At other times, the father, even though angry used to be gracious after seeing me
why today such a father has become weary after seeing me?"

verses.sanskrit:
स दीनैव शोक आर्तः विषण्ण वदन द्युतिः |

कैकेयीम् अभिवाद्य एव रामः वचनम् अब्रवीत् || २-१८-१०

कच्चिन् मया न अपराधम् अज्ञानात् येन मे पिता |

कुपितः तन् मम आचक्ष्व त्वम् चैव एनम् प्रसादय || २-१८-११
verses.translation:
"I hope that I have not done anything wrong through ignorance. Tell me why father is
angry with me. You alone propitiate him in my favour."

verses.sanskrit:
अप्रसन्नमनाः किम् नु सदा मां प्रति वत्सलः |

विवर्ण वदनो दीनो न हि माम् अभिभाषते || २-१८-१२
verses.translation:
"Why my father, who was always affectionate to me, has become displeased? With his
face becoming pale and dejected, he is not talking to me."

verses.sanskrit:
शारीरः मानसो वा अपि कच्चित् एनम् न बाधते |

सम्तापो वा अभितापो वा दुर्लभम् हि सदा सुखम् || २-१८-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. kachchit = I hope that; santaapova.api = either
affliction; shaariiraH = of body; abhitaapovaa = or distress; maanasaH =
of mind; na baadhate = is not troubling; evam = him; sukham = happiness;
durlabham hi = is indeed difficult to be attained; sadaa = always.
verses.translation:
"I hope that either bodily affliction or mental distress is not troubling him. The
object of happiness is indeed difficult to be attained always!"

verses.sanskrit:
कच्चिन् न किंचित् भरते कुमारे प्रिय दर्शने |

शत्रुघ्ने वा महा सत्त्वे मात्ऱ्ऱ्णाम् वा मम अशुभम् || २-१८-१४
verses.translation:
"I hope that no bad thing occurred to Bharata, the delightful man to look at or to
Shatrughna, the man of great strength or to my mother. "

verses.sanskrit:
अतोषयन् महा राजम् अकुर्वन् वा पितुर् वचः |

मुहूर्तम् अपि न इच्चेयम् जीवितुम् कुपिते न्ऱ्पे || २-१८-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. nR^ipe kupite = if our father is angry; nechchheyam = i
do not desire; jiivitum = to live; muhuurtampai = even for a moment;
atoshhayan = without satisfying; mahaarajan = the king; akurvan =
without doing; vachaH = word; pituH = of father.
verses.translation:
"If our father becomes angry, I do not desire to live even for a moment without
satisfying him or without fulfilling his command."

verses.sanskrit:
यतः मूलम् नरः पश्येत् प्रादुर्भावम् इह आत्मनः |

कथम् तस्मिन् न वर्तेत प्रत्यक्षे सति दैवते || २-१८-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. naraH = MN; iha = in this world; pashyet =
sees; yatomuulam = from what root; aatmanaH one's own; pradurbhaavam = coming
into existence; tasmin = that father; sati = being; pratyakshhe = the
visible; daivate = god; katham = how; navarteta = behaves not.
verses.translation:
"Man sees father as the visible god who is the root cause of one's own coming into
existence and how else can he behave towards him other than like that?"

verses.sanskrit:
कच्चित् ते परुषम् किंचित् अभिमानात् पिता मम |

उक्तः भवत्या कोपेन यत्र अस्य लुलितम् मनः || २-१८-१७
verses.translation:
"I hope you have not spoken anything which hurt my father's conscience with your
pride and anger."

verses.sanskrit:
एतत् आचक्ष्व मे देवि तत्त्वेन परिप्ऱ्च्चतः |

किम् निमित्तम् अपूर्वो अयम् विकारः मनुज अधिपे || २-१८-१८
verses.translation:
"I am asking you why my father got this perturbation, which was not there earlier.
Tell me the true position."

verses.sanskrit:
एवमुक्ता तु कैकेयी राघवेण महात्मना |

उवाचेदं सुनिर्लज्जा धृष्टमात्महितं वचः || २-१८-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. evam = thus; uktaa = spoken; raaghaveNa = by
Rama; mahaatmanaa = the great-souled; kaikeyii = Kaikeyi; dhR^ishhTam =
fearlessly; uvaacha = spoke; idam vachaH = these words; aatmahitam = for
her own benefit; sunirlajjaa = without any shame.

verses.sanskrit:
न राजा कुपितो राम व्यसनम् नास्य किंचन |

किंचिन्मनोगतं त्वस्य त्वद्भयान्नाभिभाषते || २-१८-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. raama = Oh; Rama! raajaa = the king; nakupitaH
= is not angry; nakinchana = there is nothing of vyasanam = distress; asya = for
him; tu = but; asya = to him; knchit = there is something manogatham =
in mind; naabhibhaashhate = which he is not telling; tvadbhayaat = from fear of
you.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! The king is not angry. There is nothing of distress for him. But he has
something in mind which he is not telling from fear."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रियम् त्वामप्रियम् वक्तुम् वाणी नास्योपपर्तते |

तदवश्यम् त्वया कार्यम् यदनेनाश्रुतम् मम || २-१८-२१
verses.wordMeaning:
21. asya = his; vaaNii = words; nopavartate = are
not coming off; vaktum = to tell; apriyam = the disagreeable; tvaam = to
you; priyam = the loveful one; yat = which; anena = by him;
ashrutam = is promised; mama = to me; tat = that; kaaryam = is
to be done; avashyam = certainly; tvayaa = by you.
verses.translation:
"To him, words are not coming off to tell disagreeable utterance to you, the dear
one. He promised me a word. It is to be certainly done by you."

verses.sanskrit:
एष मह्यम् वरम् दत्त्वा पुरा मामभिपूज्य च |

स पश्चात्तप्यते राजा यथान्यः प्राकृतस्तथा || २-१८-२२
verses.translation:
"Long ago, the king honoured me and gave me a boon. Like a common man, he is
repenting now for having given the boon."

verses.sanskrit:
अतिसृज्य ददानीति वरम् मम विशाम्पतिः |

स निरर्थं गतजले सेतुम् बन्धितुमिच्छति || २-१८-२३
verses.translation:
"This king promised me to give the boon and now he desires to build a dam wastefully
on a place where water has gone away."

verses.sanskrit:
धर्मूलमिदम् राम विदितम् च सतामपि |

तत्सत्यम् न त्यजेद्राजा कुपितस्त्वत्कृते यथा || २-१८-२४
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! It is known by even wise men, that truth is the root of righteousness.
See that the offended king does not abandon such a truth for your sake."

verses.sanskrit:
यदि तद्वक्ष्यते राजा शुभम् वा यदि वाऽशुभम् |

करिष्यसि ततः सर्वमाख्यामि पुनस्त्वहम् || २-१८-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. raajaa = the king; vakshhyate = is about to mention(to
you); tat = that truth; shubhamvaa = either auspicious; ashubhamyadivaa
= or inauspicious; aham punastu = still; I; aakhyaasyaami = shall tell;
sarvam = all that; karishhyasi yadi = if you shalldo it; tataH =
afterwards.
verses.translation:
"The king is about to mention that truth which may look auspicious or inauspicious
to you. Still, I shall tell you all that, if you shall do it afterwards."

verses.sanskrit:
यदि त्वभिहितं राज्ञा त्वयि तन्न विपत्स्यते |

ततोऽहमभिधास्यामि न ह्येष त्वयि वक्ष्यति || २-१८-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. tat = what; abhihitam = is spoken; raajJNyaa =
by the king; na vipatsyate = does not go wrong; tvayi = in you; aham =
I; abhidhaasyaami = shall tell; tatH = then; eshhaH = this king;
navakshhyatihi = whill not indeed tell; tvayi = unto you.
verses.translation:
"If what is spoken by the king does not create unfavourableness in you, I shall then
tell you. This king will not indeed tell you."

verses.sanskrit:
एतात्तु वचनं श्रुत्वा कैकेय्या समुदाहृतम् |

उवाच व्यथितो रामस्ताम् देवीम् नृपसन्निधौ || २-१८-२७

अहो धिङ्नार्हसे देवि पक्तुं मामीदृशं वचः |

अहम् हि वचनात् राज्ञः पतेयम् अपि पावके || २-१८-२८

भक्षयेयम् विषम् तीक्ष्णम् मज्जेयम् अपि च अर्णवे |

नियुक्तः गुरुणा पित्रा न्ऱ्पेण च हितेन च || २-१८-२९
verses.translation:
"Oh, queen! what a pity! Do not talk like that to me. If father tells me, I shall
jump even in fire. If father, who is a king, who wishes my welfare and who is venerable, commands
me, I shall eat fiery poison and shall sink into an ocean."
