verses.sanskrit:
तत् ब्रूहि वचनम् देवि राज्ञो यद् अभिकान्क्षितम् |

करिष्ये प्रतिजाने च रामः द्विर् न अभिभाषते || २-१८-३०
verses.translation:
"Oh queen! Tell me the words of the king as desired by him. I even promise to do it.
Rama does not speak two things."

verses.sanskrit:
तम् आर्जव समायुक्तम् अनार्या सत्य वादिनम् |

उवाच रामम् कैकेयी वचनम् भ्ऱ्श दारुणम् || २-१८-३१

पुरा देव असुरे युद्धे पित्रा ते मम राघव |

रक्षितेन वरौ दत्तौ सशल्येन महा रणे || २-१८-३२
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! During a battle and a great war between celestials and demons long ago, I
protected your father who was pierced by darts. Then, he gave me two boons."

verses.sanskrit:
तत्र मे याचितः राजा भरतस्य अभिषेचनम् |

गमनम् दण्डक अरण्ये तव च अद्य एव राघव || २-१८-३३
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! According to those boons, I asked the king for coronation of Bharata and
for your going to Dandaka forest today itself."

verses.sanskrit:
यदि सत्य प्रतिज्ञम् त्वम् पितरम् कर्तुम् इच्चसि |

आत्मानम् च नर रेष्ठ मम वाक्यम् इदम् श्ऱ्णु || २-१८-३४
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! If you want to make yourself and your father faithful to a promise,
listen to these words of mine.

verses.sanskrit:
स निदेशे पितुस् तिष्ठ यथा तेन प्रतिश्रुतम् |

त्वया अरण्यम् प्रवेष्टव्यम् नव वर्षाणि पन्च च || २-१८-३५
verses.translation:
"Abide by your father's command. As per his assurance, you have to stay in the
forest for fourteen years."

verses.sanskrit:
भरतस्त्वभिषिच्येत यदेतदभिषेचन्म् |

त्वदर्थे विहितम् राज्ञा तेन सर्वेण राघव || २-१८-३६
verses.translation:
"Bharata is to be enthroned with the use of complete collection of materials already
arranged by the king for your sake."

verses.sanskrit:
सप्त सप्त च वर्षाणि दण्डक अरण्यम् आश्रितः |

अभिषेकम् इमम् त्यक्त्वा जटा चीर धरः वस || २-१८-३७
verses.wordMeaning:
37. tyaktvaa = leaving; imam abhishhekam = this coronation
function; vasa = dwell; daNdakaaraNyam aashritaH = taking refuge in the forest of
Dandaka; sapta saptacha varshhaaNi = for fourteen years; jaTaajinadharaH =
wearing braided hari and covered with a hide.
verses.translation:
"You have to leave this coronation function and dwell in the forest of Dandaka for
fourteen years, wearing braided hair and covered with a hide."

verses.sanskrit:
भरतः कोसल पुरे प्रशास्तु वसुधाम् इमाम् |

नाना रत्न समाकीर्णम् सवाजि रथ कुन्जराम् || २-१८-३८
verses.wordMeaning:
38. bharataH prashaastu = let Bharata rule; imaam vasudhaam
= this earth; kosalapure = Ayodhya; the capital of kosala; naanaaratna
samaakiirNam = completely covered with various types of precious things;
savaajirathakuN^jaraam = filled with horses; chariots and elephants.
verses.translation:
"Let Bharata rule this earth, by residing in Ayodhya filled with various types of
precious thing together with horses, chariots and elephants."

verses.sanskrit:
एतेन त्वां नरेन्द्रोयम् कारुण्येन समाप्लुतः |

शोकसंक्लिष्टवदनो न शक्नोति निरीक्षितुम् || २-१८-३९
verses.wordMeaning:
39. etena = for this reason; ayam narendraH = this king
Dasaratha; samaaplutaH = overwhelmed; kaaruNyena = with sympathy; na
shaknoti = is not able; niriikshhitum = to see; tvaam = you; shoka
samklishhTavadanaH = with face afflicted with grief.
verses.translation:
"For this reason, king Dasaratha who is overwhelmed with sympathy towards you is not
able to see you with his face, afflicted with grief."

verses.sanskrit:
एतत्कुरु नरेन्ध्रस्य वचनं रघुनन्दन |

सत्यन महता राम तारयस्व नरेश्वरम् || २-१८-४०
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! Do as per the words of the king and liberate him by giving your father an
opportunity to maintain the truth."

verses.sanskrit:
इतीव तस्यां परुषम् वदन्त्याम् |

नचैव रामः प्रविवेश शोकम् |

प्रविव्यधे चापि महानुभावो |

राजा तु पुत्रव्यसनाभितप्तः || २-१८-४१
verses.wordMeaning:
41. tasyaam vadantyaam = while she was speaking; parushham =
harsh words; itiiva = in this way; raamaH = Rama; nachaiva pravivesha =
did not even enter; shokam = the sorrowfulness; raajaatu = but the king;
mahaanubhaavaH = the mighty; pravivyathe = become much disturbed
putravyasanaabhitaptaH = having been afflicted by calamity occured to hi son.

sargas.title: Sarga 19
sargas.introduction:
Rama on hearing the harsh and cruel words of Kaikeyi remains unruffled. He asks
Kaikeyi sorrowfuly about the reason the king did not speak to him directly about the matter. He
then immediately decides to leave for forest after duly saluting the king and Kaikeyi. On the way
to forest He desires to visit his mother and Sita and inform them of his decision.

verses.sanskrit:
तत् अप्रियम् अमित्रघ्नः वचनम् मरण उपमम् |

श्रुत्वा न विव्यथे रामः कैकेयीम् च इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-१९-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. raamaH = Rama; amitraghnaH = the annihilator of enemies;
amitraghnaH = those words; apriyam = which were harsh; maraNopamam =
equivalent to death; navivyathe = was unruffled; abrabiit = spoke; idam
= these words; kaikeyiim = to Kaikeyi.

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् अस्तु गमिष्यामि वनम् वस्तुम् अहम् तु अतः |

जटा चीर धरः राज्ञः प्रतिज्ञाम् अनुपालयन् || २-१९-२
verses.translation:
"Let it be, as you said it. I shall fulfil the king's promise, go to the forest from
here to reside there, wearing braided hair and covered with a hide."

verses.sanskrit:
इदम् तु ज्ञातुम् इच्चामि किम् अर्थम् माम् मही पतिः |

न अभिनन्दति दुर्धर्षो यथा पुरम् अरिम् दमः || २-१९-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. ichchhaami = I want; N^yaatum = to know; idam tu
= this as to; kimartham = why; mahiipatiH = the king; durdharshhaH = the
inviolable; arindamaH = the subduer of enemies; naabhinandati = is not greeting;
maam = me; yathaapuram = as befere.
verses.translation:
"But I want to know why the king, the inviolable and the subduer of enemies, is not
greeting me today as before."

verses.sanskrit:
मन्युर् न च त्वया कार्यो देवि ब्रूहि तव अग्रतः |

यास्यामि भव सुप्रीता वनम् चीर जटा धरः || २-१९-४
verses.translation:
"Oh, queen! you need not be indignant. I am telling before you that I shall go to
the forest, wearing rags and braided hair. Become delighted well."

verses.sanskrit:
हितेन गुरुणा पित्रा क्ऱ्तज्ञेन न्ऱ्पेण च |

नियुज्यमानो विश्रब्धम् किम् न कुर्यात् अहम् प्रियम् || २-१९-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. nakuryaam kim = how can i not do; visrabdhaH =
faithfully; priyam = action dear; pitraa = to father; niyujyamaanaH = as
commanded; hitena = as well = wisher; guruNaa = as person with right conduct;
nR^ipeNa cha = and as king.
verses.translation:
"How can I not do faithfully an action dear to my father, as commanded by him as
well-wisher, venerable man, as person with right conduct and as king."

verses.sanskrit:
अलीकम् मानसम् तु एकम् ह्ऱ्दयम् दहति इव मे |

स्वयम् यन् न आह माम् राजा भरतस्य अभिषेचनम् || २-१९-६
verses.translation:
"My heart is burning indeed with one sorrowful feeling that king himself has not
informed me about Bharata's coronation."

verses.sanskrit:
अहम् हि सीताम् राज्यम् च प्राणान् इष्टान् धनानि च |

ह्ऱ्ष्टः भ्रात्रे स्वयम् दद्याम् भरताय अप्रचोदितः || २-१९-७
verses.translation:
"Without being asked, I myself would have gladly offered even Sita with kingdom,
even my life, loved ones and wealth."

verses.sanskrit:
किम् पुनर् मनुज इन्द्रेण स्वयम् पित्रा प्रचोदितः |

तव च प्रिय काम अर्थम् प्रतिज्ञाम् अनुपालयन् || २-१९-८
verses.translation:
"Being directed by king, who is my father himself, how much more should I tell that
I can give everything to Bharata, duly obeying father's promise to fulfil your beloved desire."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् आश्वासय हि इमम् त्वम् किम् न्व् इदम् यन् मही पतिः |

वसुधा आसक्त नयनो मन्दम् अश्रूणि मुन्चति || २-१९-९
verses.translation:
"That is why, you console him. Why indeed the king is thus slowly shedding tears,
with eyes gazed upon the floor?"

verses.sanskrit:
गच्चन्तु च एव आनयितुम् दूताः शीघ्र जवैः हयैः |

भरतम् मातुल कुलात् अद्य एव न्ऱ्प शासनात् || २-१९-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. gachchhantu duutaaH = let messengers go; adyaiva = now
itself; hayaiH = on horses; shiighrajavaiH = with rapid speed; aanayitum
= to bring; bharatam = Bharata; maatulakulaat = from maternal uncle's house;
nR^ipashaasanaat = as per orders of king.
verses.translation:
"Let messengers go now itself on fleet horses to bring Bharata from maternal uncle's
house as per orders of the king."

verses.sanskrit:
दण्डक अरण्यम् एषो अहम् इतः गच्चामि सत्वरः |

अविचार्य पितुर् वाक्यम् समावस्तुम् चतुर् दश || २-१९-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. satvaraH = Immediately; eshhaH aham = I shall hasten;
gachchhaami = in going; vastum = to live; daNdakaarNyam = in the forest
of Dandaka; chaturdasha = for fourteen; samaaH = years; avichaarsya =
without reflecting; vaakyam = on words; pituH = of father.
verses.translation:
"Immediately, I shall go to live in forest of Dandaka for fourteen years, without
reflecting on whether my father's words are right or wrong."

verses.sanskrit:
सा ह्ऱ्ष्टा तस्य तत् वाक्यम् श्रुत्वा रामस्य कैकयी |

प्रस्थानम् श्रद्दधाना हि त्वरयाम् आस राघवम् || २-१९-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. shrutvaa = hearing; tatvaakyam = those words; tasya
raamasya = of that Rama; saa kaikayii = that Kaikeyi; hR^ishhTaa = felt
glad; shraddhadhaanaa = believing; prasthaanam = his departure;
tvarayaamaasa = hastened; raaghavam = Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् भवतु यास्यन्ति दूताः शीघ्र जवैः हयैः |

भरतम् मातुल कुलात् उपावर्तयितुम् नराः || २-१९-१३
verses.translation:
"Let it be so. Messengers can go on horses having rapid speed, to bring back Bharata
from his maternal uncle's house."

verses.sanskrit:
तव तु अहम् क्षमम् मन्ये न उत्सुकस्य विलम्बनम् |

राम तस्मात् इतः शीघ्रम् वनम् त्वम् गन्तुम् अर्हसि || २-१९-१४
verses.translation:
"But I think it is not quite appropriate for you who are enthusiastic to go to
forest, to delay further."

verses.sanskrit:
व्रीडा अन्वितः स्वयम् यच् च न्ऱ्पः त्वाम् न अभिभाषते |

न एतत् किंचिन् नर श्रेष्ठ मन्युर् एषो अपनीयताम् || २-१९-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. etat = this; nakinchit = is nothing; yat =
that; naabhibhaashhate nR^ipaH = the king is not talking; vriidaanvitaH = because
of shyness; tvaam - to you; eshhaH - this; manyuH = indignation; avaniiyataam =
be removed.
verses.translation:
"It is nothing but shyness that the king is not able to speak to you. Oh Rama, the
best of men! Do not worry about it."

verses.sanskrit:
यावत् त्वम् न वनम् यातः पुरात् अस्मात् अभित्वरन् |

पिता तावन् न ते राम स्नास्यते भोक्ष्यते अपि वा || २-१९-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. raama = Oh; Rama! te pitaa = your father; na
snaasyate = will not take bath; bhokshhyatepi vaa = nor eat a meal; yaavat
taavat = so long as; tvam = you; yaataH = do not go; abhitvaram =
immediatly; vanam = to the forest.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! Your father will neither take his bath nor eat a meal until you leave the
city for the forest immediately.

verses.sanskrit:
धिक् कष्टम् इति निह्श्वस्य राजा शोक परिप्लुतः |

मूर्चितः न्यपतत् तस्मिन् पर्यन्के हेम भूषिते || २-१९-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. raajaa = the king; iti = thus; niHshvasya =
sighed; dhik = what a pity! kashhTam = how much misery!" shokapriplutaH = was overwhlmed
with murchhitaH = fainted; nyapatat = fell; tasmin paryaN^ke = in that couch;
hemabhuushhite = adorned with gold.

verses.sanskrit:
रामः अपि उत्थाप्य राजानम् कैकेय्या अभिप्रचोदितः |

कशया इव आहतः वाजी वनम् गन्तुम् क्ऱ्त त्वरः || २-१९-१८

तत् अप्रियम् अनार्याया वचनम् दारुण उदरम् |

श्रुत्वा गत व्यथो रामः कैकेयीम् वाक्यम् अब्रवीत् || २-१९-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. raamaH = Rama; shrutvaa = hearing; anaaryaayaaH tat
vachanam = that vulgar woman's words; daaruNodayam = having cruel consequence;
apriyam = which were harsh; gatavyathaH = was unruffled; abraviit =
spoke; vaakyam = the words; kaikeyiim = to Kaikeyi.

verses.sanskrit:
न अहम् अर्थ परः देवि लोकम् आवस्तुम् उत्सहे |

विद्धि माम् ऱ्षिभिस् तुल्यम् केवलम् धर्मम् आस्थितम् || २-१९-२०
verses.translation:
"Oh queen! I am not concerned with wealth. I want to receive the world hospitable.
Know me as equal to a sage, abiding in righteousness alone."

verses.sanskrit:
यद् अत्रभवतः किंचित् शक्यम् कर्तुम् प्रियम् मया |

प्राणान् अपि परित्यज्य सर्वथा क्ऱ्तम् एव तत् || २-१९-२१
verses.translation:
"If I have to do whatever action is dearer to my revered father, that action is just
done in all respects even by renouncing life."

verses.sanskrit:
न हि अतः धर्म चरणम् किंचित् अस्ति महत्तरम् |

यथा पितरि शुश्रूषा तस्य वा वचन क्रिया || २-१९-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. naasti hi = there is not indeed; kimchit = anything;
mahattaram = of greater; dharmacharaNam = performance of duty; ataH =
than this; yathaa = as; shushruushhaa = doing service; pitari = to
father; tasya vachana kriyaavaa = or doing what he commands.
verses.translation:
"There is not indeed anything of greater performance of duty than doing service to
father or than doing what he commands."

verses.sanskrit:
अनुक्तः अपि अत्रभवता भवत्या वचनात् अहम् |

वने वत्स्यामि विजने वर्षाणि इह चतुर् दश || २-१९-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. anukto.api = even if not told; atrabhavataa = by our
reverent father; aham = I vatsyaami = shall reside; vane = in the forest;
vijane = devoid of people; iha = now; chaturdasa varshhaaNi = for
fourteen years; bhavatyaaH vachanaat = as per your word.
verses.translation:
"Even if our reverent father does not tell me, I shall reside in the forest, devoid
of people, now for fourteen years as per your word."

verses.sanskrit:
न नूनम् मयि कैकेयि किंचित् आशंससे गुणम् |

यद् राजानम् अवोचः त्वम् मम ईश्वरतरा सती || २-१९-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. kaikeyi = Oh; Kaikeyi! tvam = you; iishvarataraa
satii = even though with; mama = concerning me; yatavochaH = talked for
which reason; rajaanam = to the king; aashamsane = not seeing; kimchit =
any; guNam = merit; mayi = in me; nuunam = certain!
verses.translation:
" In the matter of coronation of Bharata, you told Dasaratha and not to me, even
though you had every authority to tell, me directly. By this, it is known that you have not seen
any merit in me. It is certain!"

verses.sanskrit:
यावन् मातरम् आप्ऱ्च्चे सीताम् च अनुनयाम्य् अहम् |

ततः अद्य एव गमिष्यामि दण्डकानाम् महद् वनम् || २-१९-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. aham = I; yaavat aapR^ichchhe = shall bid farewell;
maataram = to mother; anunayaami = shall console; siitaamcha = Sita
also; tataH = afterwards; adyaiva = today iisef; gamishhyaami = I shall
go; mahat dandakaanaam vanam = to the great forest of Dandaka.
verses.translation:
" Today itself, I shall go to the forest of Dandaka after bidding, farewell to my
mother and also after consoling Sita."

verses.sanskrit:
भरतः पालयेद् राज्यम् शुश्रूषेच् च पितुर् यथा |

तहा भवत्या कर्तव्यम् स हि धर्मः सनातनः || २-१९-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. kartavam = obligation; bhavatyaa = by you;
yathaa = how; bharataH = Bharata; paalayet = rules; raajyam =
kingdom; tathaa = so also; shushruushhecha = does service; pituH = to
father; saH = It; sanaatanaH dharamaH = is indeed an agae old practice.
verses.translation:
"While ruling the kingdom, see that Bharata serves our father well. It is indeed an
age-old practice."

verses.sanskrit:
स रामस्य वचः श्रुत्वा भ्ऱ्शम् दुह्ख हतः पिता |

शोकात् अशक्नुवन् बाष्पम् प्ररुरोद महा स्वनम् || २-१९-२७

वन्दित्वा चरणौ रामः विसम्ज्ञस्य पितुस् तदा |

कैकेय्याः च अपि अनार्याया निष्पपात महा द्युतिः || २-१९-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. raamaH = Rama; mahaadyutiH = with great brilliance;
nishhpapaata = came out; vanditvaa = offering obeisance; charaNau = to
the feet; pituH = of father; visanjJNyasya = who was fainted; tathaa =
and; kaikeyyaashchaapi = also to the feet of Kaikeyi; anaryaayaaH = who was not
honourable.

verses.sanskrit:
स रामः पितरम् क्ऱ्त्वा कैकेयीम् च प्रदक्षिणम् |

निष्क्रम्य अन्तः पुरात् तस्मात् स्वम् ददर्श सुह्ऱ्ज् जनम् || २-१९-२९

तम् बाष्प परिपूर्ण अक्षः प्ऱ्ष्ठतः अनुजगाम ह |

लक्ष्मणः परम क्रुद्धः सुमित्र आनन्द वर्धनः || २-१९-३०

आभिषेचनिकम् भाण्डम् क्ऱ्त्वा रामः प्रदक्षिणम् |

शनैः जगाम सापेक्षो द्ऱ्ष्टिम् तत्र अविचालयन् || २-१९-३१

न च अस्य महतीम् लक्ष्मीम् राज्य नाशो अपकर्षति |

लोक कान्तस्य कान्तत्वम् शीत रश्मेर् इव क्षपा || २-१९-३२
verses.wordMeaning:
32. kaantatvaat = as pleasing personality; lokakaantasya =
he was loved by people; raajyanaashaH = loss of kingdom; na apakarshhati = could
not diminish;asya = his; mahatiim = great; lakshhmiim = splendour; khhapaa
eva = as a night; siina rashme = of moon's splendour.

verses.sanskrit:
न वनम् गन्तु कामस्य त्यजतः च वसुंधराम् |

सर्व लोक अतिगस्य इव लक्ष्यते चित्त विक्रिया || २-१९-३३

प्रतिषिद्ध्य शुभम् छत्रं व्यजने च स्वलंकृते |

विसर्जयित्वा स्वजनं रथम् पौरास्तथा जन्नान् || २-१९-३४

धारयन् मनसा दुह्खम् इन्द्रियाणि निग्ऱ्ह्य च |

प्रविवेश आत्मवान् वेश्म मातुर प्रिय शंसिवान् || २-१९-३५

सर्वो ह्यभिजनः श्रीमान् श्रीमतः सत्यवादिनः |

नालक्षयत् रामस्य किंचिदाकारमानने || २-१९-३६

उचितम् च महाबाहुर्न जहौ हर्षमात्मनः |

शारदः समुदीर्णांशुश्चन्द्रस्तेज इवात्मजम् || २-१९-३७
verses.wordMeaning:
37. mahaabaahuH = Rama with great arms; najahau = did not
lose; aatmanaH = his; uchitam = usual; harshham = joy; tejaiva
= as the splendour; aatmajam = that is natural; shaaradaH = of autumnal;
chandraH = moon; diirghaamshuH = with lofty rays.

verses.sanskrit:
वाचा मधुरया रामः स्र्वं सम्मानयन् जनम् |

मातुस्समीपं धीरात्मा प्रविवेश महायशाः || २-१७-३८

तं गुणैस्समतां प्राप्तो भ्राता विपुलविक्रमः |

सौमित्रिरनुवव्राज धारयन् दुःखमात्मजम् || २-१९-३९

प्रविश्य वेश्म अतिभ्ऱ्शम् मुदा अन्वितम् |

समीक्ष्य ताम् च अर्थ विपत्तिम् आगताम् |

न चैव रामः अत्र जगाम विक्रियाम् |

सुह्ऱ्ज् जनस्य आत्म विपत्ति शन्कया || २-१९-४०

sargas.title: Sarga 20
sargas.introduction:
Rama approaches his mother Kausalya to inform her of the calamity. Kausalya on
hearing the news, faints. On recovering, she laments in the most grevious tones to Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मिंस्तु पुरुष्व्याघ्रे निष्क्रामति कृताञ्जलौ |

आर्तशच्दो महान् जज्ञे स्त्रीणाम न्तःपुरे तदा || २-२०-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. tadaa = Then; tasmin = that Rama; purushha
vyaaaghre = the best of men; nishhkraamati = While leaving; kR^itaanjalou =
after offering salutation; sriiNaam = among women; antaHpura = in the palace;
jajJNe = there was; mahaan = great aartashsbdaH = cry of pain.

verses.sanskrit:
क्ऱ्^त्येष्वचोदितः पित्रा सर्वस्यान्तः पुरस्य च |

गतिर्यः शरणम् चासीत् स रोओमोऽद्य प्रवत्स्यति || २-२०-२

कौसल्यायाम् यथा युक्तो जनन्याम् वर्तते सदा |

तथैव वर्ततेऽस्मासु जन्मप्रभृति राघवः || २-२०-३
verses.translation:
"Rama, since he was born, was showing the same attention to us, as he was showing to
his mother Kausalya."

verses.sanskrit:
न क्रुध्यत्यभिशस्तोऽपि क्रोधनीयानि वर्जयन् |

कृद्धान् प्रसादयन् सर्वान् स इतोऽद्य प्रवत्स्यति || २-२०-४
verses.translation:
"He used to avoid actions which created anger and soothed all those who were angry.
Rama was not furious with those who abused him. Such Rama is going today to exile."

verses.sanskrit:
अभुद्धिर्बत नो राजा जीवलोकम् चरत्ययम् |

यो गतिम् सर्वभूतानाम् परित्यजति राघवम् || २-२०-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. yaH = Which Dasaratha parityajati = by abonding raaghavam = Rama
gatim = who was helpful sarvabhuutaanaam = to all beings ayam = this naH rajahaa = our king
abuddhiH = who is stupid; charati = is offending jiivalokam = all beings."
verses.translation:
"Our stupid king by abandoning Rama who was helpful to all beings , is offending all
the beings."

verses.sanskrit:
इति सर्वा महिष्यस्ता विवत्सा इव धेनवः |

पतिमाचुक्रुशुश्चैव सस्वरम् चापि चुक्रुशुः || २-२०-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. iti = Thus sarvaaH = all taaH = those mahishhaH = king's consorts
chukrushuHcha api = were weaping sasvaram = with sound; dhenavaH = who lost their calves
aachukrushushcha eva = also abused patim = husband.

verses.sanskrit:
स हि चान्तः पुरे घोरमार्तशब्धम् महीपतिः |

पुत्रशोकाभिसन्तप्तः श्रुत्वा व्यालीयतासने || २-२०-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. shrutva = After hearing aarta shabdam = the distressful sounds
yhoram = which was horrible; antaHpura = in the palace mahiipatiH = king Dasaratha
vyaaliiyata = clung; aasane = to the seat putra shokaabhisantaptaH = with extreme anguish
for his son.

verses.sanskrit:
रामः तु भ्ऱ्शम् आयस्तः निह्श्वसन्न् इव कुन्जरः |

जगाम सहितः भ्रात्रा मातुर् अन्तः पुरम् वशी || २-२०-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. raamastu = Rama; be it so; bhRisham = greatly aayastaH =
hurt; niHshvasan = sighed kuN^jaraHiva = like elephant; vashii = subdued the
senses jagaama = went antaHpuram = to the palace maatuH = of mother bhraatraa sahitaH = along with
brother.

verses.sanskrit:
सो अपश्यत् पुरुषम् तत्र व्ऱ्द्धम् परम पूजितम् |

उपविष्टम् ग्ऱ्ह द्वारि तिष्ठतः च अपरान् बहून् || २-२०-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. saH = Rama apashyat = saw parama puujitam = much-worshipped
vriddham purusham = old man upavishTam = sitting gR^ihadvaari = at the gateway; bahuum =
many aparaamshcha = others tishTataH = standing tatra = there.

verses.sanskrit:
द्ऱ्^ष्ट्वै तु तदा रामं ते सर्वे सहसोत्थिताः |

जयेन जयताम् श्रेष्ठम् वर्धयन्ति स्म राघवम् || २-२०-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. tadaa = Then; dR^iTvaiva = after seeing raamam = Rama te
sarve = all of them utthitaaH = rose up sahasaa = quickly vardhayantisma = exhilarating their
spirits jayena = by uttering "Hail! Glory!" raaghavam = to Rama jayatam shreshTam = the most
triumphant man.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रविश्य प्रथमाम् कक्ष्याम् द्वितीयायाम् ददर्श सः |

ब्राह्मणान् वेद सम्पन्नान् व्ऱ्द्धान् राज्ञा अभिसत्क्ऱ्तान् || २-२०-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. saH = That Rama pravishya = entered prathamaam kakshyaam = the
first enclosure; dadarsha = saw dvitiiyaam = at the second gate vriddhaaan = old
braahmaNaan = brahmanas; veda sampannaan = perfect in Vedas abhisatkRitaan = honoured
raaGyaa = by king.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रणम्य रामः तान् व्ऱ्द्धांस् त्ऱ्तीयायाम् ददर्श सः |

स्त्रियो व्ऱ्द्धाः च बालाः च द्वार रक्षण तत्पराः || २-२०-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. raamaH = Rama pranamya = saluted taan = those vipraan =
brahmanas; dadarsha = saw vR^iddhaaH = old striyaH = women tathaa = and balaaH = girls
dvaara rakshaNa tatparaaH = who were interested in guarding the gate tR^itiiyaayaam = the third
enclosure.

verses.sanskrit:
वर्धयित्वा प्रह्ऱ्ष्टाः ताः प्रविश्य च ग्ऱ्हम् स्त्रियः |

न्यवेदयन्त त्वरिता राम मातुः प्रियम् तदा || २-२०-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. tadaa = Then taaH = those striyaH = women; prahR^ishTaaH
= having been glad; vardhayatvaa = greeted Rama with word of success; tvaritaaH =
quickly pravishya = entered gR^iham = house; nyavedayasta = informed raamamaatuH =
Kausalya; the mother of Rama priyam = the pleasing news.

verses.sanskrit:
कौसल्या अपि तदा देवी रात्रिम् स्थित्वा समाहिता |

प्रभाते तु अकरोत् पूजाम् विष्णोह् पुत्र हित एषिणी || २-२०-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. tadaa = at that time; kausalyaa = Kausalya sthitvaa =
having stayed samaahitaa = steadfast raatrim = all the night; akarot = performed pujaam =
worship vishhnoH = to Vishnu prabhaate = at dawn putrahi taishhiNii = for the welfare of the
son.

verses.sanskrit:
सा क्षौम वसना ह्ऱ्ष्टा नित्यम् व्रत परायणा |

अग्निम् जुहोति स्म तदा मन्त्रवत् क्ऱ्त मन्गला || २-२०-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. saa = Kausalya vrata paraayaNaa = who was interested to pracise
religious vows nityam = regularly; kshhouma vesanaa = was wearing white silk sari kR^ita
maN^galaa = made up of auspiciousness; hR^ishhTaa = thrilling with rapture;
juhotisma = was doing sacrificial ceremony agnim = in fire mantravat = reciting vedic
hymns.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रविश्य च तदा रामः मातुर् अन्तः पुरम् शुभम् |

ददर्श मातरम् तत्र हावयन्तीम् हुत अशनम् || २-२०-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. tadaa = Then raAmaH = Rama provishya = entered shubham =
auspicious maatuH antaHpuram = mother's apartment; dadarsha = saw maataram = mother
haavayantiim = performing sacrificial ceremony hutaashanam = in sacred fire tatra = there.

verses.sanskrit:
देवकार्यनिमित्तम् च तत्रापश्यत् समुद्यतम्|

दध्यक्षतम् घृतम् चैव मोदकान् हविषस्तदा || २-२०-१७

लाजान् माल्यानि शुक्लानि पायसम् कृसरम् तथा |

समिधः पूर्णकुम्भांश्छ ददर्श रघुनंदनः || २-२०-१८

ताम् शुक्लक्षौमसम्वीताम् व्रतयोगेन कर्शिताम् |

तर्पयन्तीम् ददर्शाद्भिः देवताम् देववर्णिनीम् || २-२०-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. dadarsha = saw taam = Kausalya shukla kshhouma samviitaam = who
was wearing white silk sari; karshitaam = became lean vratayogena = because of observance
of vow; deva varNiniim = having appearance of an angel devataam = gods abdhiH = with
water.

verses.sanskrit:
सा चिरस्य आत्मजम् द्ऱ्ष्ट्वा मात्ऱ् नन्दनम् आगतम् |

अभिचक्राम सम्ह्ऱ्ष्टा किशोरम् वडवा यथा || २-२०-२०

स मातरमभिक्रान्तामुपसम्गृह्य राघवः |

परिष्वक्तश्च बाहुभ्यामुपाग्रातश्च मूर्धनि || २-२०-२१
verses.wordMeaning:
21. raamaH = Rama upa samgR^ihya = offered salutation by touching the
feet abhikraantam = of approaching maataram = mother; parishhvaktaH = was embraced
baahubhyaam = by her arms; upaaghraatashcha = was smelt muurdhani = in his head.

verses.sanskrit:
तम् उवाच दुराधर्षम् राघवम् सुतम् आत्मनः |

कौसल्या पुत्र वात्सल्यात् इदम् प्रिय हितम् वचः || २-२०-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. kausalya = Kausalya vuvaacha = spoke idam = these priyahitham =
pleasing and salutary vachaH = words putravaatsalyaat = due to affection on son aatmanaH = her
sutam = son tam raaghavam = that Rama duraadharshham = who was unassailable

verses.sanskrit:
व्ऱ्द्धानाम् धर्म शीलानाम् राजर्षीणाम् महात्मनाम् |

प्राप्नुहि आयुः च कीर्तिम् च धर्मम् च उपहितम् कुले || २-२०-२३
verses.translation:
"Obtain long life and glory, as obtained by the aged , the virtuous, the great
souled and the royal sages .Protect the righteousness engrained in your race"

verses.sanskrit:
सत्य प्रतिज्ञम् पितरम् राजानम् पश्य राघव |

अद्य एव हि त्वाम् धर्म आत्मा यौवराज्ये अभिषेक्ष्यति || २-२०-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. raaghava = Oh; Rama! pashya = see raajaanam = the king
pitaram = your father; satya pratiG^yam = who is having true promise adyaiva = Today
itself; dharmaatmaa = that virtuous man abhishhekshhyati = will install tvaam = you
youvaraajye = as successor to kingdom.
verses.translation:
"Oh,Rama ! King Dasaratha ,your father is a man of true promise. Today itself, you
will be installed as successor to the kingdom by your virtuous father."

verses.sanskrit:
दत्तमासनमालभ्य भोजनेन निमन्त्रितः |

मातरम् राघवः किंचित् प्रसार्य अन्जलिम् अब्रवीत् || २-२०-२५

स स्वभाव विनीतः च गौरवाच् च तदा आनतः |

प्रस्थितो दण्डकारण्यमाप्रष्टुमुपचक्रमे || २-२०-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. tadaa = Then saH = that Rama ; svabhaava viniitashcha =
being humble in nature; nataHcha = and modest gouravaat = due to respect for mother;
prasthtaH = setting fourth journey daNd^akaaraNy^am = to Dandaka forest; upakrame
= was set about aaprasTum = to seek permission.

verses.sanskrit:
देवि नूनम् न जानीषे महद् भयम् उपस्थितम् |

इदम् तव च दुह्खाय वैदेह्या लक्ष्मणस्य च || २-२०-२७
verses.wordMeaning:
27. devii = oh; mother! na jaaniishhe = you do not know
mahat = -great bhayam = dismay upasthitam = is approaching nuunam = indeed. idam = This duHkhaaya =
brings grief tavaacha = to you ; vaidehyaa = to Sita lakshhmaN^asyacha = and to
Lakshmana.
verses.translation:
"Oh,mother! You do not know that a great dismay is approaching now. It brings grief
to you, to Sita and to Lakshmana"

verses.sanskrit:
गमिष्ये दण्डकारण्यम् किमनेनासनेन मे |

विष्टरासनयोग्यो हि कालोऽयम् मामुपस्थितः || २-२०-२८
verses.translation:
"I am going to Dandaka forest. Why this seat for me? Time has come for me to sit on
a seat made of Kusha grass."

verses.sanskrit:
चतुर्दश हि वर्षाणि वत्स्यामि विजने वने |

मधु मूल फलैः जीवन् हित्वा मुनिवद् आमिषम् || २-२०-२९
verses.wordMeaning:
29. vatsyaami = I shall live vane = in forest; vijane =
bereft of people munivat = like sage chaturdasha = fourteen varshhaaNi = years hitvaa = leaving off
aamishham = meat; jiivan = living madhu muulaphalaiH = with honey; roots and fruits.
verses.translation:
"I shall live in a solitary forest like a sage for fourteen years, leaving off meat
and living with roots, fruits and honey".

verses.sanskrit:
भरताय महा राजो यौवराज्यम् प्रयच्चति |

माम् पुनर् दण्डक अरण्यम् विवासयति तापसम् || २-२०-३०
verses.wordMeaning:
30. mahaarajaH = the great king prayachhati = is giving bharataaya =
to Bharata youvaraajyam = the succession to kingdom. maam punaH = To me; however; taapasam
= as a sage nivaasayati = making me stay daN^dakaaraNye = in forest
verses.translation:
"The great king is giving to Bharata the succession to kingdom and to me, however,
he is making me a sage to stay in the forest of Dandaka."

verses.sanskrit:
स ष्ट्चाअष्टौ च वर्षाणि वत्स्यामि विजने वने |

आसेवमानो वन्यानि फलमूलैश्च चर्तयन् || २-२०-३१
verses.wordMeaning:
31. saH = such of myself; aasevamaanaH = has to satisfy
vanyaani = with things existing in the forest; vartayam = live phala nuulai = with roots
and fruits vatsyaami = reside in vijane vane = forest; of people shaTcha = ashhToucha =
for fourteen varshhaaNi = years.
verses.translation:
"I have to satisfy with the things existing in the forest and subsist with roots and
fruits in a solitary forest for fourteen years."

verses.sanskrit:
सा निकृत्तैव सालस्य यष्टिः परशुना वने |

पपात सहसा देवी देवतेव दिवश्च्युता || २-२०-३२
verses.wordMeaning:
32. saa devii = That queen papaata = fell on floor sahasaa = all at
once yashTiH iva = like the branch saalasya = of the tree nikRitta = cut down parashunaa = by axe
vane = in the forest; devatena = as angel chyutaa = dropping down divaH = from heaven

verses.sanskrit:
ताम् अदुह्ख उचिताम् द्ऱ्ष्ट्वा पतिताम् कदलीम् इव |

रामः तु उत्थापयाम् आस मातरम् गत चेतसम् || २-२०-३३
verses.wordMeaning:
33. raamaH = Rama dR^ishTvaa = saw taam maataram = that mother
aduHkhochitaam = who was fit to be from sorrow; utthaapayaamaasa = lifted up her
gatachetanam = who lost consciousness; patitaam = who fell on the floor kadaLiimiva = like
a plantain tree.

verses.sanskrit:
उपाव्ऱ्त्य उत्थिताम् दीनाम् वडवाम् इव वाहिताम् |

पांशु गुण्ठित सर्व अग्नीम् विममर्श च पाणिना || २-२०-३४
verses.wordMeaning:
34. vi mamarsha = Rama touched paaNinaa = with hand diinaam =
miserable Kausalya; paamsukuNThita sarvaaN^giim = whose limbs were covered with fragrant
powder badamaamiva = like a female horse; vaahitaam = made to be carried of load;
utthitaam = rose upaavR^ittya = after rolling from side to side.

verses.sanskrit:
सा राघवम् उपासीनम् असुख आर्ता सुख उचिता |

उवाच पुरुष व्याघ्रम् उपश्ऱ्ण्वति लक्ष्मणे || २-२०-३५
verses.wordMeaning:
35. saa = That kausalya = sukhochita = who was fit for happiness
asukhaartaa = but afflicted with sorrow; uvaacha = spoke raaghavam = to Rama
purushhavyaaghram = the best of men upaasiinam = who was sitting nearby; lakshhmaN^e =
while Lakshmana upashR^iNvati = was hearing.

verses.sanskrit:
यदि पुत्र न जायेथा मम शोकाय राघव |

न स्म दुह्खम् अतः भूयः पश्येयम् अहम् अप्रजा || २-२०-३६
verses.wordMeaning:
36. putra = oh;son! raaghavaa = Rama! jayethaaH yadi = If you were
not born shokaaya = to cause grief mama = to me aham = I nasma pashyeyam = would not have seen
bhuuyaH = greater duHkham = grief ataH = than this aprajaaH = without progeny.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! I would not have felt this much grief if I were childless .You are born
only to produce sorrow to me."

verses.sanskrit:
एकएव हि वन्ध्यायाः शोको भवति मानवः |

अप्रजा अस्मि इति सम्तापो न हि अन्यः पुत्र विद्यते || २-२०-३७
verses.wordMeaning:
37. putra = Oh;son! na vidyate = There is no anyaH = other santaapaH
= grief vandhyaayaaH = to a barren women bhavati hi = There is indeed ekaH = one shokaH eva = only
grief maanasaH = relating to mind iti = that asmi aprajaaH "I have no children"
verses.translation:
"Oh, son! There is no other grief to a barren woman except the only worry that "I
have no sons".

verses.sanskrit:
न द्ऱ्ष्ट पूर्वम् कल्याणम् सुखम् वा पति पौरुषे |

अपि पुत्रे विपश्येयम् इति राम आस्थितम् मया || २-२०-३८
verses.wordMeaning:
38. raamaH = oh; Rama na dR^ishTa puurvam = earlier was not
seen sukham vaa = happiness or kalyaaNam = prospering pate pourushhe = during my husband's
manliness. asthitam = It was believed mayaa = by me iti = that api pashyeyam = can I see putre_api
= at least in my son
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! I have not seen any happiness or prosperity earlier, while my husband is
in power. I believed that I could see them at last when my son comes to power."

verses.sanskrit:
सा बहूनि अमनोज्ञानि वाक्यानि ह्ऱ्दयच्चिदाम् |

अहम् श्रोष्ये सपत्नीनाम् अवराणाम् वरा सती || २-२०-३९
verses.wordMeaning:
39. saa aham = such a person as myself; varaa satii = who is
a better and virtuous wife; bahuuni = so many amaroG^Yaani = disagreeable vaakyaani =
words sapatniinaam = of fellow wives; avaraaNaam = who are inferior to me;
hR^idayachhidaam = who pierce the heart.
verses.translation:
"I have to hear so many disagreeable words from my fellow-wives who are inferior to
me and who pierce my heart with their words though I am a better and virtuous wife among them."

verses.sanskrit:
अतः दुह्खतरम् किम् नु प्रमदानाम् भविष्यति |

त्वयि सम्निहिते अपि एवम् अहम् आसम् निराक्ऱ्ता || २-२०-४०
verses.wordMeaning:
40. kim nu = which one bhavishhyati = will be duHkhataram = more
sorrowful pramadaanaam = to women ataH = than yaadR^ishaH = which -anantakaH = unending
vilaapashcha = and dissolving shokaH = grief mama = to me
verses.translation:
"Which one will be more sorrowful to women than this unending and consuming grief
that occurred to me now?"

verses.sanskrit:
त्वयि सन्निहितेऽप्येवमहमासं निराकृता |

किम् पुनः प्रोषिते तात ध्रुवम् मरणम् एव मे || २-२०-४१
verses.wordMeaning:
41. taata = Oh; father(a term of affection addressed to a junior)
tvayi sannihite api = Even when you are nearby; aham = I niraakR^itaa aasam = was
rejected evam = thus kim punaH = what to tell proshhite = when you go to exile. maraNam = death
dhR^ivam is certain me = to me
verses.translation:
"Even when you are near by, I am being rejected. What to tell after you leave for
exile? Death indeed is certain to me."

verses.sanskrit:
अत्यन्तनिगृहीतास्मि भर्तुर्नित्य्मतन्त्रिता |

परिवारेण कैकेय्या समा वाप्यथवाऽवरा || २-२०-४२
verses.wordMeaning:
42. asmi = Iam; nityam ;always atyanta nigR^ihiitaa = held
down heaving bhartuH = by husband; atantritaa = without independence samaava = equal to
athavaa = or avaraa = lower than parivaareNa = servants kaikeyyaaH = of Kaikeyi
verses.translation:
"My husband always held me down, without giving me any independence and treat me
equal to or even lower than the servants of Kaikeyi"

verses.sanskrit:
यो हि माम् सेवते कश्चित् अथ वा अपि अनुवर्तते |

कैकेय्याः पुत्रम् अन्वीक्ष्य स जनो न अभिभाषते || २-२०-४३
verses.wordMeaning:
43. yaH = kashchit = Anyone whatsoever sevate = serves maam = me
athava = or anuvartate = follows saHjanaH = that person na abhibhaashhate hi = will not indeed
speak anviikshhya = after seeing kaikeyyaaH = Kaikeyi's putram = son

verses.sanskrit:
नित्यक्रोधतया तस्याः कथं नु खरवादि तत् |

कैकेय्या वदनम् द्रष्टुम् पुत्र शक्ष्यामि दुर्गता || २-२०-४४
verses.wordMeaning:
44. putra = oh; son! durgataa = Myself; the miserable
one! katham nu = how shakshhyaami = can I be able drashhTum = to see adanam = face tasyaaH
kaikeyyaaH = of that Kaikeyi khara vaadi = who talks hurtingly nitya krodhatayaa ;because of the
continuous anger

verses.sanskrit:
दश सप्त च वर्षाणि तव जातस्य राघव |

असितानि प्रकान्क्षन्त्या मया दुह्ख परिक्षयम् || २-२०-४५
verses.wordMeaning:
45. raaghava = Oh; Rama! aasitaani = sat down mayaa = by me
dasha saptacha = for seventeen varshhaa Ni = years tava jaatasya = after your second birth of your
thread ceremony praakaaNkshhantyaa = with hope of duHkha parikshhantyaam = disappearance of
troubles
verses.translation:
"Oh,Rama! I have been waiting for seventeen years after your second birth of thread
ceremony, with the hope that my troubles will disappear at one time or the other."

verses.sanskrit:
तदक्षयम् महाद्दुःखम् नोत्सहे सहितुम् चिरम् |

विप्रकारम् सपत्नीनामेवम् जीर्णापि राघव || २-२०-४६
verses.wordMeaning:
46. raaghava = oh; Rama! tat = for that reason;
evam = in the way jiir Naapi = even if old aged; notsahe = I do not wish sahitum
= to bear viprakaaram = insult sapatniinaam = of co-wives; akshhayam = un-ending mahat =
great du Hkham = sorrow chiram = for long time

verses.sanskrit:
अपश्यन्ती तव मुखम् परिपूर्णशशिप्रभम् |

कृपणा वर्तयिष्यामि कथम् कृपणजीविकाम् || २-२०-४७
verses.wordMeaning:
47. katham = How kR^ipaNaa = the pitiable me;
vartayishhyaami = can roll on kR^ipaNa jiivikaam = this miserable life apashhyantii =
without seeing tava = your mukham = face; paripuurNa sashiprabham = radiating like full
moon
verses.translation:
"How can I the pitiable one, spend this miserable life without seeing your face
radiating like full moon."

verses.sanskrit:
उपवासैः च योगैः च बहुभिः च परिश्रमैः |

दुह्खम् सम्वर्धितः मोघम् त्वम् हि दुर्गतया मया || २-२०-४८
verses.wordMeaning:
48. tvam = you samvardhitaH = were nourished mogham = in vain mayaa =
by me durgatayaa = the unfortunate one; upavaasaishcha = and painful voes.
verses.translation:
"You were nourished in vain by me ,the important one by fasts, by meditations, by
various difficult and painful vows ."

verses.sanskrit:
स्थिरम् तु हृदयम् मन्ये मम इदम् यन् न दीर्यते |

प्रावृषि इव महा नद्याः स्पृष्टम् कूलम् नव अम्भसा || २-२०-४९
verses.wordMeaning:
49. idam = This; mama = my hR^idayam = heart yat = for which
reason na diiryate = is not breached kuulam iva = like bank mahaanadyaa = of great river
spR^ishhTam = struck navaam bhasaa = by new water praavR^ishhi = in rainy season; manye =
I think it sthhiram = as strong.
verses.translation:
"My heart is not breached, as bank of a great river, struck by new water in monsoon,
by hearing this news.That is why, I feel it is strong."

verses.sanskrit:
मम एव नूनम् मरणम् न विद्यते |

न च अवकाशो अस्ति यम क्षये मम |

यद् अन्तको अद्य एव न माम् जिहीर्षति |

प्रसह्य सिम्हो रुदतीम् म्ऱ्गीम् इव || २-२०-५०
verses.wordMeaning:
50. na vidyate = There is no maraNam = death naasti = There is no
nuunam = indeed. avakaashaH = place; mama = to me yamakshhaye = in the abode of yama. yat
= for which reason; antakaH = yama; the god of death najihiirshhati = is not
wishing to carry off maam = me adyaiva = now itself prasahya = forcibly.
verses.translation:
"There is no death to me, indeed .I have no place in the abode of god yama who
presides over the spirits of the dead .That is why, Yama is not forcingly carrying me off like a
lion carrying off a wailing antelope."

verses.sanskrit:
स्थिरम् हि नूनम् हृदयम् मम आयसम् |

न भिद्यते यद् भुवि न अवदीर्यते |

अनेन दुःखेन च देहम् अर्पितम् |

ध्रुवम् हि अकाले मरणम् न विद्यते || २-२०-५१
verses.wordMeaning:
51. anena duHkhena = By the grief; deham = body arpitam = is
pierced. nuunam = Indeed mama = my hR^idayam = heart sthhiram = which is hard aayasam = is made of
iron. yat = for which reason na bhidyate = it is not broken naavadiiryate = it has not fallen into
pieces; bhuvi = on the floor na vidyate = There is no akaale = untimely = maraNam = death
dhR^ivam = It is certain.
verses.translation:
"This grief is inserted into my body. Even then, my heart is stable. It has not
broken into pieces and fallen on the floor .It is indeed made of iron. There is no untimely death,
it is certain"

verses.sanskrit:
इदम् तु दुःखम् यद् अनर्थकानि मे|

व्रतानि दानानि च सम्यमाः च हि |

तपः च तप्तम् यद् अपत्य कारणात् |

सुनिष्फलम् बीजम् इव उप्तम् ऊषरे || २-२०-५२
verses.wordMeaning:
52. idam = This is duHkham = sad anarthakaaniiti = to speak about
waste of me = my vrataani = religious voes daanaamicha = charities samyamaashcha = and restraints.
yat = which tapaH = austerity taptam = is performed apatya kaaraNaat = for the sake of offspring;
sunishhphalam = is completely in vain biijam iva = as seed uptam = sown uushhare = in
saline soil.
verses.translation:
"I feel bad since all my religious vows, charities, restraints have all gone
waste.The austerity I performed for the sake of of -spring has gone in vain, as a seed sown in a
saline soil."

verses.sanskrit:
यदि हि अकाले मरणम् स्वया इच्चया |

लभेत कश्चित् गुरु दुःख कर्शितः |

गता अहम् अद्य एव परेत संसदम् |

विना त्वया धेनुर् इव आत्मजेन वै || २-२०-५३
verses.wordMeaning:
53. kaschit = If a person guruduHkhakarshitaH = in great distress
labheta yadi = can get akaale = premature maraNam = death svayaa = ichchhayaa = out of his own
will; aham = I gataa = would have got pareta sampadam = glory of the dead adyaiva = now
itself tvayaa vinaa = without you dhenuriva = like cow aatmajenavina = without calf
verses.translation:
"If a man in great distress can get premature death out of his own will, I being
separated from you ,would have attain the glory of the dead now itself like cow without the
calf."

verses.sanskrit:
अथापि किम् जीवित मद्य मे वृथा |

त्वया विना च्न्द्रनिभाननप्रभ |

अनुव्रजिष्यामि वनम् त्वयैव गौः |

सुदुर्बला वत्समिवानुकाङ्क्षया || २-२०-५४
verses.wordMeaning:
54. athaapi = Moreover; chandranibhaanana prabha = oh;
Rama! With your brilliant face shining like moon! kim = what me = my jiivitam = life
tvayaavinaa = without you! vR^ithaa = waste indeed. adya = Now anuvrajishhyaami = Ican accompany
tvayaiva = you only vanam = to the forest; vatsam iva = like with calf sudurbalaa = a
completely weak cow gouH = cow anukaaNkshhayaa = with compassion
verses.translation:
"Moreover, what is the use of life? Oh, Rama! With your brilliant face shining like
moon! My life is useless without you. I shall accompany you to the forest like a weak cow going
behind it's calf"

verses.sanskrit:
भृशम् असुखम् अमर्षिता तदा |

बहु विललाप समीक्ष्य राघवम् |

व्यसनम् उपनिशाम्य सा महत् |

सुतम् इव बद्धम् अवेक्ष्य किम्नरी || २-२०-५५
verses.wordMeaning:
55. tadaa = Then; saa = that Kausalya samiikshhya = looked
at raaghavam = Rama; upanishaamya = beholden with mahat = great vyasanam = calamity;
avekshhya = saw sutam = son badhham = tied up; vilaapa = lamented bhR^isham =
very much bahu = in many ways asukham = uneasily amarshhitaa = with anger kinnariiva = like kinnara
woman.

sargas.title: Sarga 21
sargas.introduction: This chapter describes Lakshmana's anger and Rama consoling Lakshmana.

verses.sanskrit:
तथा तु विलपन्तीम् ताम् कौसल्याम् राम मातरम् |

उवाच लक्ष्मणो दीनः तत् काल सदृशम् वचः || २-२१-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. lakshhmaNaH = Lakshmana; diinaH = became dejected; uvaacha 
= spoke; vachaH = the words; tatkaala sadR^isham = suitable for that time; taam 
kausalyyaam = to that kausalya; rama maataram = Rama's mother; tathaa = thus;
vilapantiim = weaping.

verses.sanskrit:
न रोचते मम अपि एतत् आर्ये यद् राघवो वनम् |

त्यक्त्वा राज्य श्रियम् गच्चेत् स्त्रिया वाक्य वशम् गतः || २-२१-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. "aarye = Oh; the venerable lady! atat = this; na rochate 
= is not liking; mamaapi = to me also; itiyat = as in the manner this; raaghavaH 
= Rama; vaakyaarasham gataH = being influenced by the words; striyaaH = of a woman;
gachchhet = go; vanam = to forest; tyaktvaa = leaving; raajya sriiyam 
= prosperous kingdom."
verses.translation:
"Oh, the honoured lady ! I also do not like Rama to be influenced by the words of a woman 
and go to the forest, leaving the prosperous kingdom."

verses.sanskrit:
विपरीतः च वृद्धः च विषयैः च प्रधर्षितः |

नृपः किम् इव न ब्रूयाच् चोद्यमानः समन्मथः || २-२१-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. "nR^ipaH = king; vipariitasheha = with perverted mind; 
vR^idhdhhashcha = old aged; pradharshhitaH = who is outraged; vishhayaiH = by 
sensual enjoyments; samanimadhaH = who is possessed of passion; na bruuyaat = can 
he not talk; kimiva = of anything; chodyamaanaH = prompted (by Kaikeyi).
verses.translation:
"The king with perverted mind, of old age, one who is outraged by sensual enjoyments 
and possessed of passion can talk any thing, prompted by Kaikeyi."

verses.sanskrit:
न अस्य अपराधम् पश्यामि न अपि दोषम् तथा विधम् |

येन निर्वास्यते राष्ट्रात् वन वासाय राघवः || २-२१-४
verses.translation:
"I can not see any offence or cause for blame in Rama that can expel him from the state 
to the forest."

verses.sanskrit:
न तम् पश्याम्य् अहम् लोके परोक्षम् अपि यो नरः |

स्वमित्रोऽपि निरस्तोऽपि योऽस्यदोषमुदाहरेत् || २-२१-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. aham = I; na pashyaami = have not seen; loke = in 
this world; tam = such a person; udaaharet = who talks; dosham = ill; 
asya = of Rama; parokshhamapi = even indirectly; yaH naraH = that person who 
is; svamitro api = even an enemy; nirasto api = or an expellee."

verses.sanskrit:
देव कल्पम् ऋजुम् दान्तम् रिपूणाम् अपि वत्सलम् |

अवेक्षमाणः को धर्मम् त्यजेत् पुत्रम् अकारणात् || २-२१-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. "kaH = who; avekshhamaaNaH = observing; dharmam = 
ethicalness; akaaraNaat = causelessly; tyajet = get rid of; putram = son;
devakalpam = who is equal to god; R^ijum = who is honest; daantam = who is 
self-restrained; vatsalam = who is affectionate; R^ipuuNaamapi = even towards enemies?"
verses.translation:
"Can anybody observing ethicalness, causelessly get rid of a son who is equal to god 
who is honest, who is self-restrained and who is affectionate even towards adversaries?"

verses.sanskrit:
तत् इदम् वचनम् राज्ञः पुनर् बाल्यम् उपेयुषः |

पुत्रः को हृदये कुर्यात् राज व्ऱ्त्तम् अनुस्मरन् || २-२१-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. "kaH = which son; anusmaran = who remembers; raja vR^ittam 
= royal usage; kuryaat = act; hR^idaye = with heart; tat idam vachanam = 
such of these words; raajJNaH = of king; upeyushhaH = who got; baalyam = 
childhood punaH = again?"
verses.translation:
"Which son, knowing royal usage, can agree to the words of this king who is behaving 
as though he got childhood again?"

verses.sanskrit:
यावद् एव न जानाति कश्चित् अर्थम् इमम् नरः |

तावद् एव मया साधम् आत्मस्थम् कुरु शासनम् ||२-२१-८
verses.translation:
"Even before others get to know about this matter, make this dominion your own, with 
my help."

verses.sanskrit:
मया पार्श्वे सधनुषा तव गुप्तस्य राघव |

कः समर्थो अधिकम् कर्तुम् कृत अन्तस्य इव तिष्ठतः || २-२१-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. raaghava = Oh; Rama! guptasya = protected; mayaa 
= by me ; parshve = on your side; sadhamshhaa = along with a bow; tishhThataH 
= you standing; kR^itaantasyeva = like Yama; the god of death; kaH = who; samarthaH 
= the competent one; kartum = to do; adhikam = too much?"
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! While I am standing by your side along with a bow, protecting you who are 
standing as God of Death, who is capable of doing too much ?"

verses.sanskrit:
निर्मनुष्याम् इमाम् सर्वाम् अयोध्याम् मनुज ऋषभ |

करिष्यामि शरैअः तीक्ष्णैः यदि स्थास्यति विप्रिये || २-२१-१०
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama, the best of men! If the city of Ayodhya turns against you, I shall make it 
desolate of men with sharp arrows"

verses.sanskrit:
भरतस्य अथ पक्ष्यो वा यो वा अस्य हितम् इच्चति |

सर्वान् एतान् वधिष्यामि मृदुर् हि परिभूयते || २-२१-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. "atha = and vadhishhyaami = I shall kill; etaam sarvaan = 
all those; pakshhyovaa = who are siding; bharatasya = Bharata; yovaa = and 
who; ichchhati = desire; asya = his; hitam = benefit; mR^iduH = 
soft person; paribhuuyate hi = indeed gets disgraced."
verses.translation:
"I shall kill all those who are siding Bharata ,and are favourable to him. Soft person 
indeed gets disgraced!"

verses.sanskrit:
प्रोत्साहितोऽयम् कैकेय्या स दुष्टो यदिः पिता |

अमित्रभूतो निस्सङ्गम् वध्यताम् बध्यतामपि || २-२१-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. "protsaahitaH = Instigated; kaikeyyaa = by Kaikeyi saH = 
such of; naH putraa = our father; dushhTaH = as bad person; amitra bhuutaH 
= becomes enemy; ayam badhyataam = let him be imprisoned; mi ssaNgam = without personal 
attachment; vaadhyataamapi = and be killed!"
verses.translation:
"If our father with an evil mind behaves like our enemy with instigation by Kaikeyi. 
I shall keep him imprisoned with out personal attachment or if necessary, kill him."

verses.sanskrit:
गुरोरप्यवलिप्तस्य कार्याकार्यमजानतः |

उत्फथम् प्रतिपन्नस्य कार्यम् भवति शासन्म् || २-२१-१३
verses.translation:
"Even a venerable person is to be punished, if he becomes arrogant, if he does not know 
good and evil actions and if he takes to a wrong route."

verses.sanskrit:
बलमेष किमाश्रित्य हेतुम् वा पुरुषर्षभ |

दातुमिच्छति कैकेय्य राज्यम् स्थितमिदम् तव || २-२१-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. purushharshhabha = Oh the best of men! kim = what; balam 
= strength; hetum vaa = or reason; eshhaH = he; aastritya = has taken shelter;
ichchhati = desiring ; daatum = to give; idam = this ; raajyam = 
kingdom; tava sthitam = belonging to you; kaikeyyai = for Kaikeyi.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama, the best of men! On what strength or season has he taken shelter to give away 
this kingdom belonging to you to Kaikeyi?"

verses.sanskrit:
त्वया चैव मया चैव कृत्वा वैरम् अनुत्तमम् |

कस्य शक्तिः श्रियम् दातुम् भरताय अरि शासन || २-२१-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. arishaasana = Oh; the chastiser of enemies ! kaa shaktiH 
= what ability is there; asya = to him; daatum = to give; shriyam = high 
rank; bharataaya = to Bharata; kR^itvaa = by making; anuttamam = great;
vairam = enmity; tvayaa chaiva = with you; mayaa chaiva = and with me."
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama the chestiser of enemies! Where is the ability for him to give kingdom to Bharata, 
by making great enmity against you and me "

verses.sanskrit:
अनुरक्तः अस्मि भावेन भ्रातरम् देवि तत्त्वतः |

सत्येन धनुषा चैव दत्तेन इष्टेन ते शपे || २-२१-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. devi = Oh; queen! tattvataH = really; anuraktaH asmi 
= I am attached; bhaavena = devotedly; bhraataram = my brother Rama; shape 
= I am swearing an oath; te = to you; satyena = by truth; dhanushhaachaiva 
= by bow; daltena = by the act of giving; ishhTena = by the act of sacrifice."
verses.translation:
"Oh, queen! Really, I am devotedly attached to my brother Rama. I am swearing an oath 
to you by truth by bow, by the act of giving and by the act of sacrifice."

verses.sanskrit:
दीप्तम् अग्निम् अरण्यम् वा यदि रामः प्रवेक्ष्यते |

प्रविष्टम् तत्र माम् देवि त्वम् पूर्वम् अवधारय || २-२१-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. devi = oh;queen! ramaH pravekshhyati yadi = If Rama can enter; 
diiptam = blazing; agnim = fire; araNyam vaa = or forest; tvam = you 
avadhaaraya = make certain; puurvam = even before; maam = mine; pravishhTam 
= who can enter; tatra = there.
verses.translation:
"Oh, queen! If Rama can enter blazing fire or forest, you make certain that even before, 
I can enter there"

verses.sanskrit:
हरामि वीर्यात् दुह्खम् ते तमः सूर्यैव उदितः |

देवी पश्यतु मे वीर्यम् राघवः चैव पश्यतु || २-२१-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. haraami = I shall alleviate; te duHkham = your grief; 
viiryaat = by my valour; tama iva = as darkness; uditaH = by rising; suuryaH 
= sun; pashyatu devii = Let the queen see; me viiryam = my valour; pashyatu raaghavashchaiva 
= Let Rama also see."
verses.translation:
"I shall alleviate your grief, by showing my valour like the rising sun alleviating darkness 
.Let Rama and yourself see my valour"

verses.sanskrit:
एतत् तु वचनम् श्रुत्वा लक्ष्मणस्य महात्मनः |

उवाच रामम् कौसल्या रुदन्ती शोक लालसा || २-२१-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. shrutva = Hearing; etat vachanam = this word; lakshmaNasya 
= of Lakshmana; mahaat manaH = the magnanimous; kausalya = Kausalya; shokalaalasaa 
= entirely; given up to anguish; rudaati = weaping; uvaacha = spoke; raamam 
= to Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
भ्रातुस् ते वदतः पुत्र लक्ष्मणस्य श्रुतम् त्वया |

यद् अत्र अनन्तरम् तत् त्वम् कुरुष्व यदि रोचते || २-२१-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. putra = Oh;son shrutam = It is heard tvayaa = by you; te 
= your; bhraatuaH = brother lakshhmanasya = Lakshmana; vadataH = speaking; rochate 
yadi = If you agree; kurushhva = you do; yat = what; kaaryam = action 
to be done; anantaram = immediately after."
verses.translation:
"Oh, son !You have heard the words of Lakshmana .If you agree, you do what is to be done, 
immediately."

verses.sanskrit:
न च अधर्म्यम् वचः श्रुत्वा सपत्न्या मम भाषितम् |

विहाय शोक सम्तप्ताम् गन्तुम् अर्हसि माम् इतः || २-२१-२१
verses.translation:
"It is not proper for you to go away from here, leaving me afflicted with grief, after 
hearing the un-just words spoken by my co-wife."

verses.sanskrit:
धर्मज्ञ यदि धर्मिष्ठो धर्मम् चरितुम् इच्चसि |

शुश्रूष माम् इहस्थः त्वम् चर धर्मम् अनुत्तमम् || २-२१-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. dharmajJNa = Oh; the knower of goodness! tvam ichchhasi yadi 
= If you desire; charitum = to practice dharmam = virtue; dharnushhTaH = be a virtuous 
man; ihasthaH = stay here; shushruushha = serve maam = me chara = practice anuttamam 
= best dharmam = goodness"
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! You are righteous man. If you desire to practice virtue, be a virtuous man 
and stay here serving me thereby practicing the best morality in the form of serving your mother.

verses.sanskrit:
शुश्रूषुर् जननीम् पुत्र स्व गृहे नियतः वसन् |

परेण तपसा युक्तः काश्यपः त्रिदिवम् गतः || २-२१-२३
verses.translation:
"Oh, son! Formerly Kasyapa was staying in his own house itself with self restraint, performed 
great austerity by serving his mother and went to heaven"

verses.sanskrit:
यथा एव राजा पूज्यः ते गौरवेण तथा हि अहम् |

त्वाम् न अहम् अनुजानामि न गन्तव्यम् इतः वनम् || २-२१-२४
verses.translation:
"Just as the king, by respectable feeling to you is worthy of worship, so also being 
your mother, I am worthy of worship to you. Hence, do not go to the forest"

verses.sanskrit:
त्वद् वियोगान् न मे कार्यम् जीवितेन सुखेन वा |

त्वया सह मम श्रेयः तृणानाम् अपि भक्षणम् || २-२१-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. tvadviyogaat = Because of your separation; na kaaryam = there 
is no purpose; jiivitena = for life; su khena vaa = or comforts; mama = for 
me; shreyaH = it is good; bhakshhaNam api = even to eat; tR^iNaanaam = grass;
tvayaa saha = while staying with you"
verses.translation:
"After your separation, I shall have no use of this life or comforts for me, it is good 
even to eat grass while staying with you"

verses.sanskrit:
यदि त्वम् यास्यसि वनम् त्यक्त्वा माम् शोक लालसाम् |

अहम् प्रायम् इह आसिष्ये न हि शक्ष्यामि जीवितुम् || २-२१-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. tvaam yaasyasiyadi = If you go; vanam = to forest; tyaktvaa 
= leaving; maam = me; shokalaalasaam = who is afflicted with grief; aham 
= I praayam asishhye = shall seek death through starvation; iha = here; na shakshhyaami 
hi = Indeed; I shall not be able; jiivutum = to live
verses.translation:
"I shall not live if you leave for the forest, leaving me who is afflicted with grief. 
I shall seek death through starvation"

verses.sanskrit:
ततः स्त्वम् प्राप्स्यसे पुत्र निरयम् लोक विश्रुतम् |

ब्रह्म हत्याम् इव अधर्मात् समुद्रः सरिताम् पतिः || २-२१-२७
verses.wordMeaning:
27. putra = Oh;son! tataH = Thereafter; tvam = you; praapsyase 
= will attain; lokavishrutam = the universally famous; nirayam = hell; brahmahatyaamiva 
= like obtaining the sin of killing a Brahmana by; samudraH = the god of ocean; saritaampatiH 
= lord of rivers adharmaat = due to un justice behaviour"
verses.translation:
"Oh, son! If I fast myself to death for your sake, you will attain the universally famous 
hell, like the god of the ocean obtaining trouble because of his un justice behaviour, towards the sage 
called Pippalada, producing the latter's wrath."

verses.sanskrit:
विलपन्तीम् तथा दीनाम् कौसल्याम् जननीम् ततः |

उवाच रामः धर्म अत्मा वचनम् धर्म सम्हितम् || २-२१-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. tataH = thereafter; dharmaatma = righteous words ; raamaH 
= Rama; uvaacha = spoke; vachanam = the words; dharmasamhitam = attended 
with virtuous; kausalyaam = to Kausalya; jananiim = the mother; tathaa = 
thus; vilapantiim = weaping; diinaam = miserably.

verses.sanskrit:
न अस्ति शक्तिः पितुर् वाक्यम् समतिक्रमितुम् मम |

प्रसादये त्वाम् शिरसा गन्तुम् इच्चाम्य् अहम् वनम् || २-२१-२९
verses.wordMeaning:
29. naasti = there is no; shaktiH = ability; mama = 
for me ; samatikramitum = to violet; pituH = father's; vaakyam = words;
aham = I; prasaadaye = am asking for favour; tvaam = to you; shirasaa 
= with head; ichchaami = I wish gantum = to go; vanam = to forest."
verses.translation:
"I am not able to violate my father's words. I am bowing my head and asking your favour. 
I shall have to go to the forest"

verses.sanskrit:
ऋषिणा च पितुर् वाक्यम् कुर्वता व्रत चारिणा |

गौर् हता जानता धर्मम् कण्डुना अपि विपश्चिता || २-२१-३०
verses.wordMeaning:
30. gouH api = Even a cow; hataa = was killed; kurvataa 
= acting as per; pituH = father's; vaakyam = words; kandunaa = by Kandu;
R^ishhiNaa = the sage; vratachaariNaa = who performed religious vows; jaanataa 
= who knew; dharmam = righteousness; vipashchita = who was learned."
verses.translation:
"A sage called Kandu, who knew righteousness, who performed religious vows and who was 
a learned man, killed even a cow, acting as per his father's words"

verses.sanskrit:
अस्माकम् च कुले पूर्वम् सगरस्य आज्ञया पितुः |

खनद्भिः सागरैः भूतिम् अवाप्तः सुमहान् वधः || २-२१-३१
verses.translation:
"Previously, the sons of Sagara belonging to our race, were got bitterly digging up the 
earth, as commanded by their father."

verses.sanskrit:
जामदग्न्येन रामेण रेणुका जननी स्वयम् |

कृत्ता परशुना अरण्ये पितुर् वचन कारिणा || २-२१-३२
verses.translation:
"Parasu Rama, the son of Jamadagni personally slayed his mother Renuka in the forest 
with an axe as per his father's words."

verses.sanskrit:
एतैरन्यैश्च बहुभिर्देवि देवसमैः कृतम् |

पितुर्वचनमक्लीबम् करिष्यामि पितुर्शितम् || २-२१-३३
verses.wordMeaning:
33. devi = Oh;queen! etaiH = by these; bahubhiH anyaisheha = 
by many others; devasamaanaiH = equivelant to gods -pituH = father's; vachanam = words 
kR^itam = was made akliibam = no waste; karishhyaami = I shall do; pituH = father's 
hitam = assignment"
verses.translation:
"Oh, mother! These and many others who were equivalent to gods, did not make their father' 
s words to go waste. I shall also do what is desired by my father"

verses.sanskrit:
न खल्व् एतन् मया एकेन क्रियते पितृ शासनम् |

एतैरपि कृतम् देवि ये मया तव कीर्तिताः || २-२१-३४
verses.translation:
"Oh, mother! I am not the only person to act according to father's command. Those I have 
mentioned to you hitherto also complied with their father's command."

verses.sanskrit:
नाहम् धर्ममपूर्वम् ते प्रतिकूलम् प्रवर्तये |

पूर्वैः अयम् अभिप्रेतः गतः मार्गो अनुगम्यते || २-२१-३५
verses.wordMeaning:
35. "aham = I; na pravartaye = am not setting out; apuurvam 
= new; pratikuulam = contradicting; dharmam = customary conduct; te = for 
you; anugamyate = I am following; ayam = this; maargam = way; abhipretaH 
= agreed; gataH = followed by; puurvaiH = the ancient."
verses.translation:
"I am not setting any new contradicting customary conduct for your sake. I am adhering 
to the way agreed and followed by the ancient."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् एतत् तु मया कार्यम् क्रियते भुवि न अन्यथा |

पितुर् हि वचनम् कुर्वन् न कश्चिन् नाम हीयते || २-२१-३६
verses.wordMeaning:
36. na kriyate = It cannot be done; anyathaa = otherwise; 
mayaa = by me; tat etat = than such of this; kaaryam = practice; bhuvi 
= on earth; na hiiyate naama = No deprivation indeed; kashchit = for anyone kurvam 
= doing; pituH = father's; vachanam = words."
verses.translation:
"I cannot do otherwise than acting in accordance with father's words, the prevalent practice 
on earth. There is no deprivation indeed for anyone who complies with father's commands."

verses.sanskrit:
ताम् एवम् उक्त्वा जननीम् लक्ष्मणम् पुनर् अब्रवीत् |

तव लक्ष्मण जानामि मयि स्नेहम् अनुत्तमम् || २-२१-३७
verses.wordMeaning:
37. shreshhThaH = the best; vaakyavidaam = of those knowing the 
speech; shreshhTaH = the best; sarva dhanushhTataam = of all those wearing the bow;
evam = thus; uktaava = spoke; taam jananiim = to that mother; punaH 
= again; abraviit = spoke; lakshmanam = to Lakshmana.

verses.sanskrit:
तव लक्ष्मण जानामि मयि स्नेहमनुत्तमम् |

विक्रमम् चैव सत्यम् च तेजश्च सुदुरासदम् || २-२१-३८
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lakshmana ! I know your highest affection towards me, your heroic valour ,your strength 
and your unassailable splendour ."

verses.sanskrit:
मम मातुर्महद्दुःखमतुलम् शुभलक्षम्ण |

अभिप्रायम् अविज्ञाय सत्यस्य च शमस्य च || २-२१-३९
verses.wordMeaning:
39. subhakshhaNa = Oh; Lakshmana; with good attributes! atulam mahat 
duHkham = there is an unqualled and great grief; mama maatuH = to my mother; aviN^yaaya 
= by not knowing; abhipraayam = the meaning; satyasya cha = of truth and shamasyacha 
= tranquility.
verses.translation:
" Oh, Lakshmana, with good attributes! My mother is feeling a great and unequalled sorrow, 
by not knowing the secret of truth and tranquillity."

verses.sanskrit:
धर्मः हि परमः लोके धर्मे सत्यम् प्रतिष्ठितम् |

धर्म संश्रितम् एतच् च पितुर् वचनम् उत्तमम् || २-२१-४०
verses.translation:
"Righteousness is the best of all qualities in the world. Truth is established in righteousness. 
Even these best words of father are enjoined with righteousness."

verses.sanskrit:
संश्रुत्य च पितुर् वाक्यम् मातुर् वा ब्राह्मणस्य वा |

न कर्तव्यम् वृथा वीर धर्मम् आश्रित्य तिष्ठता || २-२१-४१
verses.wordMeaning:
41. viiraH = oh; Lakshmana the valiant! tishhThataa = one who 
is aashritya = following; dharmam = righteousness; nakartavyam = is not obliged;
vR^ithaa = to waste; vaakyam = the word; samshrutya = promised; pituH 
= to father or; maaturvaa = mother or; brahmanasya vaa = Brahmana.
verses.translation:
"One who follows righteousness, does not waste his promise given to one's father or mother 
or Brahmana."

verses.sanskrit:
सो अहम् न शक्ष्यामि पितुर् नियोगम् अतिवर्तितुम् |

पितुर् हि वचनात् वीर कैकेय्या अहम् प्रचोदितः || २-२१-४२
verses.wordMeaning:
42. viira = oh; the valiant! saH aham = I; being such person;
nashakshhyaami ativartitum = cannot trasgrass; niyogam = the command; pituH 
= of father; vachanaat = for the word; pituH = of father; aham = I; prachoditohi 
= was indeed instigated; kaikeyyaa = by Kaikeyi.
verses.translation:
" I cannot transgress my father's command. On the word given by my father only, Kaikeyi 
instigated me to go to the forest."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् एनाम् विसृज अनार्याम् क्षत्र धर्म आश्रिताम् मतिम् |

धर्मम् आश्रय मा तैक्ष्ण्यम् मद् बुद्धिर् अनुगम्यताम् || २-२१-४३
verses.translation:
"Hence, leave this mean mentality of military heroism. Observe righteousness and not 
rudeness. follow my perception."

verses.sanskrit:
तम् एवम् उक्त्वा सौहार्दात् भ्रातरम् लक्ष्मण अग्रजः |

उवाच भूयः कौसल्याम् प्रान्जलिः शिरसा आनतः || २-२१-४४

अनुमन्यस्व माम् देवि गमिष्यन्तम् इतः वनम् |

शापिता असि मम प्राणैः कुरु स्वस्त्ययनानि मे || २-२१-४५
verses.wordMeaning:
45. devii = oh; mother! anumanyasva = permit; maam = 
me; gamishhyantam = who is about to go; vanam = to forest; shaapitaa asi 
= on my life; kuru = invode; svastyanaani = blessings; me = on me.
verses.translation:
"OH, mother! I have decided to go to forest. Please give me permission. I am swearing 
to you on my life. Please invoke blessings on me."

verses.sanskrit:
तीर्ण प्रतिज्ञः च वनात् पुनर् एष्याम्य् अहम् पुरीम् |

ययातिरिव राजर्षिः पुरा हित्वा पुनर्धिवम् || २-२१-४६
verses.wordMeaning:
46. puraa = in olden times; iva = as; raajarshhiH = 
the sage king; yayaatiH = named yayaati; hitvaa = left; divam = heaven;divam 
= reached heaven; punaH = again; aham eshhyaami = I can come; punaH = back;
puriim = to city; vanaat = from forest; tirNa pratijJNyaH = after fulfuilling 
the promise.
verses.translation:
"As sage king named Yayati in olden times left the heaven and reached back heaven again, 
I shall be back to the city from the forest, after fulfilling my promise."

verses.sanskrit:
शोकस्संधार्यताम् मातर्हृदये साधु मा शुचः |

वनवासादिहैष्यामि पुनः कृत्वा पितुर्वचः || २-२१-४७
verses.wordMeaning:
47. maataH = oh; mother! maashuchaH = do not grieve; samdhaaryaam 
= bear; saadhu = well; hR^idaye = in heart; eshhyaami = I shall come; 
iha = here; punaH = again; vanavaasaat = from forest; kR^itvaa = after 
fulfilling; pituH = father's; vachaH = command.
verses.translation:
"Oh, mother! Please do not grieve, Bear it well in your heart. I shall come back here 
again from the forest after fulfilling the father's command."

verses.sanskrit:
त्वया मया च वैदेह्या लक्ष्मणेन सुमित्रया |

पितुर्नियोगे स्थातव्यमेष धर्मः सनाअनः || २-२१-४८
verses.translation:
"You and I, Sita, Lakshmana, Sumintra and all of us should follow father's directions. 
This is an eternal custom."

verses.sanskrit:
अम्ब सम्हृत्य सम्भारान् दुःखम् हृदि निगृह्य च |

वनवासकृता बुद्धिर्मम धर्म्यानुवर्त्यताम् || २-२१-४९
verses.wordMeaning:
49. amba = oh; mother! mama buddhiH = my intention; vanavaasa 
kR^itaa = of proceeding to forest; dharmyaa = which is righteous; anuvartyataam 
= be approved; samhR^itya = duly with drawing; sambhaaraan = preparations for coronation;
nigR^ihyacha = keeping in check; duHkham = sorrow; hR^idi = in heart.
verses.translation:
"Oh mother! Please approve my righteous intention of going to the forest, duly withdrawing 
preparations for my coronation and keeping in check the sorrow in your heart."

verses.sanskrit:
एतद्वच स्तस्य निशम्य माता |

सुधर्म्यमव्यग्रमविक्लबम् च |

मृतेव सम्ज्ञाम् प्रतिलभ्य देवी |

समीक्ष्य रामम् पुनरित्युवाच || २-२१-५०
verses.wordMeaning:
50. maataa = the mother; devii = kausalya; nishamya 
= heard; tasya = that Rama's; vachaH = words; etat = here; sudharmyam 
= which were the most virtuous; avyagram = which wer cool; aviklabam cha = and which 
were not unsteady; pratilabhya = regained; saNjJNyaam = consciousness; mR^iteva 
= looked at; raamam = Rama; uvaacha = spoke; punaH = again; iti 
= thus .

verses.sanskrit:
यथैव ते पुत्र पिता तथाहम् |

गुरुः स्वधर्मेण सुहृत्तया च |

न त्वानुजानामि न मांविहाय |

सुदुःखितामर्हसि गन्तुमेवम् || २-२१-५१
verses.wordMeaning:
51. putra = oh; son! aham = I am also; guruH = respectable 
person; te = to you; pitaa yathaiva = as indeed your father; svadharmeNa 
= in my own right; suhR^ittayaacha = and due to great affection; naanujaanaami = I 
do not permit; tvaa = you; naarhasi gantum = to go; evam = thus;
vihaaya = leaving; maam = me ; suduHkhitaam = in great sorrow.
verses.translation:
"Oh, son! I am also as respectable a person to you as your father, in my own right and 
due to great affection. I do not give permission to you. You cannot thus go away, leaving me in great 
sorrow."

verses.sanskrit:
किम् जीवितेनेह विना त्वया मे |

लोकेन वा किम् स्वधयाऽमृतेन |

श्रेयो मुहूर्तम् तव सन्निधानम् |

ममेह कृत्स्नादपि जीवलोकात् || २-२१-५२
verses.translation:
"What is the use of my living in this world without you? What is the use of the other 
world or the oblation of food offered to the deceased ancestors or the nectar of immortality? Your proximity 
even for a moment is better than that of the entire world of living beings."

verses.sanskrit:
नरैरिवोल्काभिरपोह्यमानो |

महागजोऽध्वानमनुप्रविष्टः |

भूयः प्रजज्वाल विलापमेवम् |

निशम्य रामः करुणम् जनन्या || २-२१-५३
verses.wordMeaning:
53. nishamya = hearing; karuNam = pathetic; vilaapam 
= lamentation; jananyaa = of mother; prajajvaala = he was highly agonised; bhuuyaH 
= again; mahaagaja iva = as a great elephant; ampravishhTaH = having entered; 
adhvaanam = way; apohyamaanaH = being taken away; naraiH = ny men; ulkaabhiH 
= with flames of fire.

verses.sanskrit:
स मातरम् चैव विसम्ज्ञकल्पा |

मार्तम् च सौमित्रि मभिप्रतप्तम् |

धर्मे स्थितो धर्म्यमुवाच वाक्यम् |

यथा स एवार्हति तत्र वक्तुम् || २-२१-५४
verses.wordMeaning:
54. saH = that Rama; sthitaH = who was established; dharme 
= in righteousness; uvaacha = spoke; vaakyam = the words; dharmyam = endued 
with virtue; yathaa = in which manner; tatra = at that time; sa eva = he 
only; arhati = was fit; vaktum = to speak; maataramchaiva = to mother;
visaMjJNakalpaam = appearing unconscious; saumitrim = to Lakshmana; aartam 
= who was disturbed; abhiprataptam = and exhausted with distress.

verses.sanskrit:
अहम् हि ते लक्ष्मण नित्यमेव |

जानामि भक्तिम् च पराक्रमम् च |

मम त्वभिप्राय मसन्निरीक्ष्य |

मात्रा सहाभ्यर्दसि मा सुदुःखम् || २-२१-५५
verses.wordMeaning:
55. lakshmana = Oh; Lakshmana! aham = I; nityameva = 
always; jaanaami = know; te = your; bhakitmacha = devotion and; parakramamcha 
= strength; tu = but; maatraa saha = along with mother; abhyardamasi = you 
are afflicting; suduHkham = much pain; mama = to me; asanniririikshhya = 
without looking at; mama = my; abhipraayam = opionon.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lakshmana! I always know your devotion towards me as well as your strength. But 
now, you are joining with mother in afflicting much trouble to me without looking at my opinion."

verses.sanskrit:
धर्मार्थकामाः खलु तात लोके |

समीक्षिता धर्मफलोदयेषु |

ते तत्र सर्वे स्युरसंशयम् मे |

भार्येव वश्याभिमता सुपुत्रा || २-२१-५६
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lakshmana! In obtaining the fruit of good works in the world, righteousness utility 
and free will are being considered. As a wife who is obedient, who is beloved and who is having good 
sons, good work yields all these three things."

verses.sanskrit:
यस्मिंस्तु सर्वे स्युरसन्निविष्टा |

धर्मो यतः स्यात् तदुपक्रमेत |

द्वेष्यो भवत्यर्थपरो हि लोके |

कामात्मता खल्वपि न प्रशस्ता || २-२१-५७
verses.translation:
"Only such an action, which is righteous, is to be initiated, leaving that in which wealth, 
desire and righteousness do not come together. One who is intested in wealth alone becomes indeed fit 
to be hated in the world. So also the one whose very essence is desire, cannot indeed be considered 
good.

verses.sanskrit:
गुरुश्च राजा च पिता च वृद्धः |

क्रोधात्प्रहर्ष द्यदि वापि कामात् |

यद्व्यादिशेत् कार्यमवेक्ष्य धर्मम् |

कस्तन्न कुर्यादनृदनृशंसवृत्तिः || २-२१-५८
verses.wordMeaning:
58. pitaa = father; gurshcha = who is venerable; rajaacha 
= a king; vR^iddhaH cha = and old aged; vyaadishet = commends; yat = for 
which reason; kaaryam = an action; krodhaat = either by anger; prahrshhaat 
= or by extreme joy; yadivaa kamaat api = or by even desire; kaH = who; apekshhya 
= with reference to dharmam = righteousness; anR^ishamsa vR^ittiiH = and not choosing cruelty;
nakuryaat = will not do?
verses.translation:
"When father, who is venerable, a king and old-aged, commands an action either by anger 
or by extreme joy or by even desire, which right person will not perform it? Only a person who chooses 
to be cruel will not do it."

verses.sanskrit:
सवै न शक्नोमि पितुः प्रतिज्ञा |

मिमामकर्तुम् सकलम् यथावत् |

स ह्यवयोस्तत गुरुर्नियोगे |

देवाश्च भर्ता स गतिस्स धर्मः || २-२१-५९
verses.wordMeaning:
59. saH = such of myself; na shaknomivgi = cannot; akartum 
= avoid; pituH = father's; imaam pratijJNyaam = this propostion; sakalaam 
= in entirety; taata = oh; father! aavoyaH = for both of us; saH = he;
furuH hi = is indeed important person; niyoge = to command; devyaashcha = 
for mother also; saH = he; bhartaa = the husband; saH = he; gatiH 
= the resource person; dharmaH = the law; personified.
verses.translation:
"I cannot avoid fulfilling the father's command completely. For both of us, he is an 
important person indeed to command us. To mother Kausalya also, he is the husband, the resource person 
and the personified law."

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मिन् पुनर्जीवति धर्मराजे |

विशेषतः स्वे पथि वर्तमाने |

देवी मया सार्थमितोऽपगच्छेत् |

कथम् स्विदन्या विधवेव नारी || २-२१-६०
verses.wordMeaning:
60. dharmaraaje = the righteous king; tasmin = himself; jiivati 
= alive; visheshhatatH = espacially; vartamaane = being; sve pathhi = in 
his own path; katham svit = how; devii = mother kausalya; avagachhet = can 
go out; mayaa saardham = with me; itaH = from here; anyaa naariiva = like 
other woman; vidhavaa = without husband?
verses.translation:
"while the righteous king Dasaratha is alive and especially when he is following his 
own righteous path, how the mother Kausalya can go out with me from here leaving the city like other 
widowed woman?"

verses.sanskrit:
सा मानुमन्यस्व वनम् व्रजन्तम् |

कुरुष्व नः स्वस्त्ययनानि देवि |

यथा समाप्ते पुनराव्रजेयम् |

यथा हि स्त्येन पुनर्ययातिः || २-२१-६१
verses.translation:
"Oh, mother the queen! Permit me to go to forest. As Yayati returned to heaven by the 
power of truth, give me your power of blessings so that I can return here after completion of exile."

verses.sanskrit:
यशो ह्यहम् केवलराज्यकारणात् |

न पृष्ठतः कर्तुमलम् महोदयम् |

अदीर्घकाले न तु देवि जीविते |

वृणेऽवरामद्य महीमधर्मतः || २-२१-६२
verses.wordMeaning:
62. aham = I; na alam = cannot; vR^ishhTataH kartum 
= keep back; yashaH = reputation; mahodayam = and glory ; kevalaraajyakaaraNaat 
= for the sake of kingdom alone; devii = oh; queen! jiivite = In this life; adiirghakaale 
= which is not long; navR^iNe = I cannot long for; adya = now; avaraam = 
inferior; mahiim = earth; adharmataH = unrighteously.
verses.translation:
"I cannot keep back reputation and glory for the sake of kingdom alone. This cannot unrighteously 
long for this inferior kingdom."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रसादयन् नर वृषभः स मातरम् |

पराक्रमाज्जिगमिषुरेव दोम्डकान् |

अथ अनुजम् भ्ऱ्शम् अनुशास्य दर्शनम् |

चकार ताम् ह्ऱ्दि जननीम् प्रदक्षिणम् || २-२१-६३
verses.wordMeaning:
63. saH = that Rama; navR^ishhabhaH = the best of men; jigimishhureva 
= desired to go; daNdakaan = to Dandaka forest ; paraakramaat = with prowess; 
prasaadayan = consoling; maataram = mother; atha = and afterwards; anushaasya 
= advised; amjam = brother; bhR^isham = intensely; darshanam = with good 
thoughts; pradakshhiNam chakaara = made circumbulatory salutation; taamjanamiim = 
to his mother; hR^idi = heartily.

sargas.title: Sarga 22
sargas.introduction:
In this chapter Rama further tries to pacify Lakshmana and tells him that his stay in 
forest is the will of gods. He also wants all the celebration for his coronation to be stopped.

verses.sanskrit:
अथ तम् व्यथया दीनम् सविशेषम् अमर्षितम् |

श्वसन्तम् इव नाग इन्द्रम् रोष विस्फारित ईक्षणम् || २-२२-१

आसद्य रामः सौमित्रिम् सुह्ऱ्दम् भ्रातरम् प्रियम् |

उवाच इदम् स धैर्येण धारयन् सत्त्वम् आत्मवान् || २-२२-२

निगृह्य रोषं शोकं च खैर्यमाश्रित्य केवलम् |

अवमानम् निरस्येमम् गृहीत्वा हर्षमुत्तमम् || २-२२-३

उपक्लुप्तम् हि यत्किंचिदभिषेकार्थमद्य मे |

स्र्वम् विसर्जय क्षिप्रम् कुरु कार्यम् निरत्ययम् || २-२२-४
verses.translation:
" Hold back grief and anger. Forget this insult, by taking refuge in courage alone. Obtain 
great joy! Abandon all these arrangements made today for my coronation and immediately take up action 
that is faultless ."

verses.sanskrit:
सौमित्रे यो अभिषेक अर्थे मम सम्भार सम्भ्रमः |

अभिषेक निवृत्ति अर्थे सो अस्तु सम्भार सम्भ्रमः || २-२२-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. soumitre = Oh;Lakshmana; yaH = which; sambhaara sambhramaH 
= zeal for preparations; mama abhishhekaarthe = for my coronation; saH = that; 
sambhaara sambhramaH = zeal for preparations; astu = be ; abhishheka nivR^ittyarthe 
= for terminating coronation."
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lakshmana! Show the same zeal now in terminating my coronation as the zeal shown 
earlier in preparations for my coronation."

verses.sanskrit:
यस्या मद् अभिषेक अर्थम् मानसम् परितप्यते |

माता नः सा यथा न स्यात् सविशन्का तथा कुरु || २-२२-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. yasyaaH = which mother's; maanasam = mind ; paritapyate 
= is angvished; madabhishhekaarthe = about my coronation; saa = such; me mataa 
= my mother (Kaikeyi); savishaNkaa = having apprehension; yathaa = in which manner;
nasyaat = it should not occur; tathaa = in that matter ; kuru = do it ."
verses.translation:
"My mother Kaikeyi is still agonized about my coronation. Behave in such a way that no 
apprehension comes to her mind on this matter."

verses.sanskrit:
तस्याः शन्कामयम् दुह्खम् मुहूर्तम् अपि न उत्सहे |

मनसि प्रतिसम्जातम् सौमित्रे अहम् उपेक्षितुम् || २-२२-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. "soumitre = Oh; Lakshmana! aham = I ; notsahe = do 
not wish; upekshhitum = to disregard ; duHkham = pain; shaNkaamayam = in 
the form of suspision; pratisanjaatamapi = created; manasi = in mind ; tasyaaH 
= of her; muhuurtamapi = even for a moment."
verses.translation: "Oh, Lakshmana! I do not wish to disregard the painful doubt in her mind even for a moment".

verses.sanskrit:
न बुद्धि पूर्वम् न अबुद्धम् स्मरामि इह कदाचन |

मातृणाम् वा पितुर् वाहम् कृतम् अल्पम् च विप्रियम् || २-२२-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. "aham = I; na smaraami = do not remember; kR^itam 
= to have done; alpam = small; vipriyam cha = offensive thing indeed; iha 
= here ; kadaachana = at any time; buddhipuurvam = intentionally; maatR^iiNaamvaa 
= either to mothers; piturvaa = or to fathers; na = nor do I remember; abuddham 
= even un intentional action.
verses.translation:
"I do not remember to have done even a small disagreeable thing here at any time to my 
mothers or to my father, either intentionally or unintentionally."

verses.sanskrit:
सत्यः सत्य अभिसंधः च नित्यम् सत्य पराक्रमः |

पर लोक भयात् भीतः निर्भयो अस्तु पिता मम || २-२२-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. astu = Let it be ; mama pitaa = my father; satyaH 
= who is truthfull; satya paraakramaH = who is truly mighty; bhiitaH = who is afraid 
of; paralokabhayaat = the fear of the other world; nityam = always; satyaabhisandhaH 
= true to his promise; nirbhayaH = fearless".
verses.translation:
"Let the promise made by my father ,who is truthful, who is truly mighty and who is afraid 
of the fear of the other world, become true .Let him be fearless."

verses.sanskrit:
तस्य अपि हि भवेद् अस्मिन् कर्मणि अप्रतिसम्ह्ऱ्ते |

सत्यम् न इति मनः तापः तस्य तापः तपेच् च माम् || २-२२-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. tasyaapi = To him also; manastaapaH = anguish; bhavet 
= will arise; iti = that; satyam na = his word has not come true; asmin karmaNi 
apasamhR^ite = if this coronation is not with drawn; tasya = his; tapaaH = anguish;
tapechcha = will pain; maam = me."
verses.translation:
"Our father will feel anguished that his word has not come true ,if this coronation is 
not withdrawn. His distress will cause pain to me."

verses.sanskrit:
अभिषेक विधानम् तु तस्मात् सम्हृत्य लक्ष्मण |

अन्वग् एव अहम् इच्चामि वनम् गन्तुम् इतः पुनः || २-२२-११
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lakshmana for that reason, I wish to go to forest immediately from her, by withdrawing, 
coronation arrangement."

verses.sanskrit:
मम प्रव्राजनात् अद्य कृत कृत्या नृपात्मजा |

सुतम् भरतम् अव्यग्रम् अभिषेचयिता ततः || २-२२-१२
verses.translation:
"Soon after my departure to the forest now, Kaikeyi having accomplished her purpose, 
will coolly get her son coronated."

verses.sanskrit:
मयि चीर अजिन धरे जटा मण्डल धारिणि |

गते अरण्यम् च कैकेय्या भविष्यति मनः सुखम् || २-२२-१३
verses.translation:
"If go to forest , wearing deer -skin rags and with my hair twisted together Kaikeyi 
will have mental peace."

verses.sanskrit:
बुद्धिः प्रणीता येन इयम् मनः च सुसमाहितम् |

तत् तु न अर्हामि सम्क्लेष्टुम् प्रव्रजिष्यामि माचिरम् || २-२२-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. na arhaami = to cause trouble tam = to such father yena = by whom 
iyam = this buddhiH = decision praNiitam = was taken susamaahitam = with well composed manascha = mind 
maa = without chiram = delay."
verses.translation:
"I do not want to create trouble to my father, who has taken this hard decision in his 
mind . Hence, I will go to the forest at once

verses.sanskrit:
कृत अन्तः तु एव सौमित्रे द्रष्टव्यो मत् प्रवासने |

राज्यस्य च वितीर्णस्य पुनर् एव निवर्तने || २-२२-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. soumitre = Oh;Lakshmana! drashhTavyaH = It is perceivable kR^itaantastyaiva 
= as destiny mivartanecha = in taking back punaraiva = again raajyasya = of kingdom vitiirNasya = given. 
Matpravaasane = and in sending me to exile".
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lakshmana! Look at it as destiny which has taken back kingdom given to me and which 
is sending me to exile"

verses.sanskrit:
कैकेय्याः प्रतिपत्तिर् हि कथम् स्यान् मम पीडने |

यदि भावो न दैवो अयम् कृत अन्त विहितः भवेत् || २-२२-१६

जानासि हि यथा सौम्य न मातृषु मम अन्तरम् |

भूत पूर्वम् विशेषो वा तस्या मयि सुते अपि वा || २-२२-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. soumya = Oh; gentleman jaanaasi = you know ; yathaa 
= how na bhuutapuurvam = that in the past; there was no antaram = discrimination mama = to 
me maatR^ishu = among mothers. tasyaaH = to her; visheshhaH = particularly mayi = either in 
me sute api vaa = or in her son Bharata."
verses.translation:
"You know that I had no discrimination among mothers and Kaikeyi also in the past had 
no discrimination between me and her son Bharata"

verses.sanskrit:
सो अभिषेक निवृत्ति अर्थैः प्रवास अर्थैः च दुर्वचैः |

उग्रैः वाक्यैः अहम् तस्या न अन्यद् दैवात् समर्थये || २-२२-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. aham = I na samarthaye = do not perceive anyat = any other thing daivaat 
= than an accident tasyaaH vaakyaiH = for her words; abhishheka nivR^ittyarthaiH = intended 
for abolishion of my coronation ; pravaasaarthaishena = desirous of sending me to exile;
ugraiH = which are ferocious; durvachaiH = and are not to be talked about."
verses.translation:
"I do not perceive any other reason than an accident that made Kaikeyi to abolish my 
coronation , to send me to exile ,and to speak ferocious words ,not to be talked about."

verses.sanskrit:
