Clothes of bark are to be worn and mass of matted hair has to be worn on the head."

verses.sanskrit:
देवतानाम् पितृइणाम् चकर्तव्यम् विधिपूर्वकम् |

प्राप्तानामतिथीनाम् च नित्यशः प्रतिपूजनम् || २-२८-१४
verses.translation:
"The celestials, the ancestors, the guests who arrive are to be worshipped always
according to the preseribed rites."

verses.sanskrit:
कार्यस्त्रीरभिषेकश्च काले काले च नित्यशः |

चरता नियमेनैव तस्माद्दुःखतरम् वनम् || २-२८-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. abhishhekaH = bath; kaaryaH = is to be performed;
triH = three times; nityashaH = every day; kaale kaale = at appropriate
time; charataa = by the one moving in the forest; niyamenaiva = with restraint in
the mind; tasmaat = hence; vanam = forest; duHkhataram = is very much a
misery.
verses.translation:
"Bath is to be performed three times a day at appropriate time by the one moving in
the forest, with one's mind kept in restraint. Hence, living in a forest is very much a
misery."

verses.sanskrit:
उपहारश्च कर्तव्यः कुसुमैः स्वयमाहृतैः |

आर्षेण विधिना वेद्याम् बाले दुःखमतो वनम् || २-२८-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. baale = Oh; Sita the innocent! upahaarashcha = offering;
kartavyaH = is to be done; dusumaiH = with flowers; aahR^itaiH =
brought; svayam = by one's self; vedyaam = at the altar; aarshheNa
vidhinaa = according to the rites prescribed by sages; ataH = hence; vanam =
forest; duHkham = is hardship.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Sita the innocent! Offering is to be done with flowers brought by one's own
self at the altar, according to the rites prescribed by sages. Hence, living in a forest is a
hardship."

verses.sanskrit:
यथालब्धेन कर्तव्यः सन्तोष्स्तेन मैथिलि |

यताहारैर्वनचरैः सीते दुःखमतो वनम् || २-२८-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. maithili = Oh; Sita the princess of Mithila! vanacharaiH
= the dwellers of forest; kartavyaH = are to be; santoshhaH = satisfied;
yathaa labdhena = with whatever is obtained; tena = there; yataahaaraiH
= the rstricted food; ataH = hence; vanam = forest; duHkham = is
misery.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Sita the princess of Mithila! The dwellers of forest are to be satisfied with
whatever is obtained there, the restricted food. Hence, living in forest is a misery.

verses.sanskrit:
अतीव वातः तिमिरम् बुभुक्षा च अत्र नित्यशः |

भयानि च महान्ति अत्र ततः दुह्खतरम् वनम् || २-२८-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. atra = In the forest; vaataaH = air; timiram =
darkness; atiiva = are very much; nityashaH = always; bubukshhaa =
hunger; mahaanti = great; bhayaani cha = fears also; ataH = hence;
vanam = forest; duHkhataram = is very much a misery.
verses.translation:
"In the forest, air and darkness are too much. There are always hunger and great
fears too. Hence, dwelling in a forest is very much a misery."

verses.sanskrit:
सरी सृपाः च बहवो बहु रूपाः च भामिनि |

चरन्ति पृथिवीम् दर्पात् अतः दुखतरम् वनम् || २-२८-१९
verses.translation:
"Oh, Sita! Various creeping animals of different forms roam about on earth with
pride. Hence dwelling in a forst is a great misery."

verses.sanskrit:
नदी निलयनाः सर्पा नदी कुटिल गामिनः |

तिष्ठन्ति आवृत्य पन्थानम् अतः दुह्खतरम् वनम् || २-२८-२०
verses.translation:
"Snakes dwelling in rivers, moving crookedly like rivers, stay obstucting the
pathways. Hence, living in forest is a great misery."

verses.sanskrit:
पतम्गा वृश्चिकाः कीटा दंशाः च मशकैः सह |

बाधन्ते नित्यम् अबले सर्वम् दुह्खम् अतः वनम् || २-२८-२१
verses.translation:
"Oh, frail princess! Flying insects, scropious, insects including mosquitoes and
files always annoy every one. Hence, forest is full of hardship."

verses.sanskrit:
द्रुमाः कण्टकिनः चैव कुश काशाः च भामिनि |

वने व्याकुल शाखा अग्राः तेन दुह्खतरम् वनम् || २-२८-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. bhaamini = Oh; Sita! drumaaH = trees; kushakaashaH
cha = 'Kusa' grass(poa cynosuroides; a grass with long pointed stalks) and 'kasa'
grass(saccharum spontaneum used for mats; roofts etc); kaNTakinashchaiva = bambooes;
vyaakula shaakhaagraaH = with ends of branches spread on all sides; vane = are in
the forest; tena = henc; vanam = forest; duHkhataram = is very much a
misery.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Sita! Forest is full of trees, kusa grass and bambooes with ends of their
branches spread on all sides. Hence, living in a forest is a great misery."

verses.sanskrit:
कायक्लेशाश्च बहवो भयानि विविधानि च |

अरण्यवासे वसतो क्धुःखमेव ततो वनम् || २-२८-२३
verses.translation:
"An inhabitant living in a forest has to face various bodily troubles and panics.
Hence, forest- life is readly a misery."

verses.sanskrit:
क्रोधलोभे विमोक्तव्यौ कर्तव्या तपसे मतिः |

न भेतव्यम् च भेतव्ये नित्यम् दुःखमतो वनम् || २-२८-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. krodha lobhau = anger; greed; vimoktavyau = are to be
abandoned; matiH = devotion; kartavyaa = is to be done; tapase = on
asceticism; bhetavye = what needs to be feared; bhetavyam = should not be feared;
ataH = hence; vanam = forest; nityam = is always; duHkham =
suffering.
verses.translation:
"Anger and greed are to be abandoned by the dwellers of forest. Devotion is to be
bestowed on asceticism. What needs to be feared, should not be feared. Hence, living in a forest is
a suffereing."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् अलम् ते वनम् गत्वा क्षमम् न हि वनम् तव |

विमृशन्न् इह पश्यामि बहु दोषतरम् वनम् || २-२८-२५
verses.translation:
"Therefore, do away with the idea of your coming to the forest. Forest is not indeed
bearable for you. Reflecting now, I perceive forest as having too many disadvantages."

verses.sanskrit:
वनम् तु नेतुम् न कृता मतिस् तदा |

बभूव रामेण यदा महात्मना |

न तस्य सीता वचनम् चकार तत् |

ततः अब्रवीद् रामम् इदम् सुदुह्खिता || २-२८-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. yadaa = when; raameNa = by Rama; mahaatmanaa =
the great souled; matiH = the mind; na babhuuva = was not; kR^itaa =
set; netum = to take; vanam = to forest; tadaa = then;
suduHkhitaa = the much aggrieved; siitaa = Sita; nachakaara = did not
accept; tasya = his; tat = that; vachanam = word; tataH =
afterwords; abraviit = spoke; idam = these words; raamam = to Rama.

sargas.title: Sarga 29
sargas.introduction:
Seetha implores upon Rama to allow her to come to forests, and says that she heard
through some foretellers that she has to live in forests for some time, when she was with her
father's place. But Rama is not interested to make Seetha's life penurious in forests and once
again tries to pacify her. He asks her to stay back at Ayodhya. For that Seetha laments to become
lonely parted with her husband.

verses.sanskrit:
एतत् तु वचनम् श्रुत्वा सीता रामस्य दुह्खिता |

प्रसक्त अश्रु मुखी मन्दम् इदम् वचनम् अब्रवीत् || २-२९-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. siita = Seetha; shrutvaatu = heard; etat =
these; vachanam = words; raamasya = of Rama; duHkhitaa = distressed;
prasaktaashrumukhii = having the face wet with tears; abraviit = spoke; idam
vachanam = these words; mandam = slowly.

verses.sanskrit:
ये त्वया कीर्तिता दोषा वने वस्तव्यताम् प्रति |

गुणान् इति एव तान् विद्धि तव स्नेह पुरः कृतान् || २-२९-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. viddhi = know; ye = which; doshhaaH =
disadvantages; kiirtitaaH = mentioned; tvayaa = by you; vastavyataam
prati = about dwelling; vane = in the forest; taan = those;
guNaanityeva = to be advantages; tava sneha puraskR^itaan = if your affection is
placed before them.
verses.translation:
"know all those disadvantages you mentioned about dwelling in the forest become
advantages to me, if your affection is placed before them."

verses.sanskrit:
मृगाः सिम्हा गजाश्चैव शार्दूलाः शरभास्तथा |

पक्षिणः सृमराश्चैव ये चान्ये वनचारिणः || २-२९-३

अदृष्टपूर्वरूपत्वात्सर्वे ते तव राघव |

रूपम् दृष्ट्वापसर्पेयुर्भये सर्वे हि बिभ्यति || २-२९-४
verses.translation:
"Oh Rama! Antelopes, lions, elephants, tigers, sarabhas(legendary animal with eight
legs), birds, yaks and all others which roam in the forest, run away after seeing your form, since
they have never seen your figure before. When there is cause for fear, who would not have
fear?"

verses.sanskrit:
त्वया च सह गन्तव्यम् मया गुरु जन आज्ञया |

त्वद् वियोगेन मे राम त्यक्तव्यम् इह जीवितम् || २-२९-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. raama = Oh; Rama! gurujanaaJNjayaa = as per the command
of the elders; mayaa cha = I also; gantavyam = should go; tvayaasaha =
along with you; tvadviyogena = by separation from you; jiivitam = life;
me = to me; tyaktavyam = is to be abandoned; iha = here.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! As per the command of the elders, I also should go along with you. My
life is to be abandoned here, if I were separated from you."

verses.sanskrit:
न च माम् त्वत् समीपस्थम् अपि शक्नोति राघव |

सुराणाम् ईश्वरः शक्रः प्रधर्षयितुम् ओजसा || २-२९-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. raaghava = Oh; Rama! shakraH api = even Devendra;
iishvaraH = who is the lord; suraaNaam = of celestials; na shaknoti =
cannot; ojasaa = by his strength; pradarshhiyitum = over power; maam =
me; tvatsamiipasthaam = who is in your vicinity.
verses.translation:
"Oh Rama! Even Devendra the Lord of celestials, by his strength, cannot over power
me, who is in your vicinity."

verses.sanskrit:
पति हीना तु या नारी न सा शक्ष्यति जीवितुम् |

कामम् एवम् विधम् राम त्वया मम विदर्शितम् || २-२९-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. yaa naarii = which woman; patihiinaa = is without
husband; saa = she; na shakshhyati = should not be able; jiivitum = to
live; raama = Oh; Rama! kaamam = verily; evam vidham = such a thing;
vidarshitam = was taught; mama = to me; tvayaa = by you.
verses.translation:
"Oh Rama! Verily such a thing was taught to me by you, that a woman disunited from
her husband should not be able to survive."

verses.sanskrit:
अथ च अपि महा प्राज्ञ ब्राह्मणानाम् मया श्रुतम् |

पुरा पितृ गृहे सत्यम् वस्तव्यम् किल मे वने || २-२९-८
verses.translation:
"Oh Rama, the very wise! Besides, formerly in my father's house it was heard by me
from the month of brahmanas that I was destined to stay really in a forest."

verses.sanskrit:
लक्षणिभ्यो द्विजातिभ्यः श्रुत्वा अहम् वचनम् गृहे |

वन वास कृत उत्साहा नित्यम् एव महा बल || २-२९-९
verses.translation:
"Oh Rama the man of great strength! Ever since I heard this word from brahmanas who
can interpret marks on the body, I was always eager to stay in a forest."

verses.sanskrit:
आदेशो वन वासस्य प्राप्तव्यः स मया किल |

सा त्वया सह तत्र अहम् यास्यामि प्रिय न अन्यथा || २-२९-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. priya = Oh; Rama the beloved! saH aadeshaH = that
prediction; vanavaasasya = of dwelling in the forst praaptavyaH kila = is to be realised
by me; aham = I saa = as such; yaasyaami = can go; tatra = to forest;
tvayaa saha = along with you; na = not; anyathaa = otherwise.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama the beloved! That prediction of dwelling in the forst is to be realised by
me. I must accordingly accompany you to the forest, it cannot be otherwise."

verses.sanskrit:
कृत आदेशा भविष्यामि गमिष्यामि सह त्वया |

कालः च अयम् समुत्पन्नः सत्य वाग् भवतु द्विजः || २-२९-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. bhavishhyaami = I shall become; kR^itaadeshaa = the one
who has fulfilled brahmana's word; gamishhyaami = I shall come; tvayaa saha =
along with you; ayam = this; kaalashcha = time also; samntpannaH = has
come; bhavatn dvijaH = let brahmana become; bhavatu dvijaH = let brahmana become;
satyavaak = true to his word.
verses.translation:
"I shall be the one who has fulfilled the brahmana's word. I shall accompany you to
the forest. The time has come for brahmana's words to come true."

verses.sanskrit:
वन वासे हि जानामि दुह्खानि बहुधा किल |

प्राप्यन्ते नियतम् वीर पुरुषैः अकृत आत्मभिः || २-२९-१२
verses.translation:
"Oh valiant prince! I know that in the forest, sufferings indeed are many and are
invariably obtained by men having unformed minds."

verses.sanskrit:
कन्यया च पितुर् गेहे वन वासः श्रुतः मया

भिक्षिण्याः साधु वृत्ताया मम मातुर् इह अग्रतः || २-२९-१३
verses.translation:
"While I was an unmarried girl, the prediction about my dwelling the forest was
heard by me in my father's house from a female mendicant having good manners, in the presence of my
mother."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रसादितः च वै पूर्वम् त्वम् वै बहु विधम् प्रभो |

गमनम् वन वासस्य कान्क्षितम् हि सह त्वया || २-२९-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. prabho = Oh; Lord! puurvam = In the past; tvam
= you; prasaadiashcha vai = have been pleased really; bahutitham = for many a
time vanavaasasya gamanam = for the tour of a forest; kaaNkshhitam hi = as desire by me
indeed; tvayaa saha = with you.
verses.translation:
"Oh Lord! In the past, you have been really pleased for many a time to take me along
with you indeed for the tour of a forest, as longed by me."

verses.sanskrit:
कृत क्षणा अहम् भद्रम् ते गमनम् प्रति राघव |

वन वासस्य शूरस्य चर्या हि मम रोचते || २-२९-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. raaghava = Oh; Rama! bhadvam = many all be well;
te = with you! aham = I kR^itakshhaNaa = am waiting; gamanam prati = for a
journey; vanavaasasya = of the forest;charyaa = a work-out; shuurasya = of
adventurist; rochate hi = is really delightful mama = to me.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! May all be well with you! I am waiting for a journey to the forest. A
work-out of adventurist is really delightful to me."

verses.sanskrit:
शुद्ध आत्मन् प्रेम भावाद्द् हि भविष्यामि विकल्मषा |

भर्तारम् अनुगच्चन्ती भर्ता हि मम दैवतम् || २-२९-१६
verses.translation:
"Oh, the pure-minded! Following my husband with loving devotion, I shall become
sin-less; for husband is the supreme deity to me."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रेत्य भावे अपि कल्याणः सम्गमः मे सह त्वया |

श्रुतिर् हि श्रूयते पुण्या ब्राह्मणानाम् यशस्विनाम् || २-२९-१७

इह लोके च पितृभिर् या स्त्री यस्य महा मते |

अद्भिर् दत्ता स्वधर्मेण प्रेत्य भावे अपि तस्य सा || २-२९-१८
verses.translation:
"Even after my death, I shall stay united with you. On this subject, the meritorious
words of brahmanas is being heard thus:- In this world, which woman was given away to whom by
parents with water according to the moral code binding on them, that woman belongs to him alone
even after death.

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् अस्मात् स्वकाम् नारीम् सुवृत्ताम् हि पति व्रताम् |

न अभिरोचयसे नेतुम् त्वम् माम् केन इह हेतुना || २-२९-१९
verses.translation:
"By what reason now are you thus not willing to take me, the woman having good
conduct, devoted to her husband and belonging to you, from this place?"

verses.sanskrit:
भक्ताम् पति व्रताम् दीनाम् माम् समाम् सुख दुह्खयोह् |

नेतुम् अर्हसि काकुत्स्थ समान सुख दुह्खिनीम् || २-२९-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. kaakutthsa = Oh; the seion of Kakustha! arhasi = you
ought to; netum = take; maam = me; bhaktaam = who is a devotee;
pativrataam = devoted to husband; diinaam = who is distressed; samaam =
who is equal; sukhaduHkhayoH = in pleasure and pain; samaana sukhaduHkhaniim =
sharing in your joys and sorrows.
verses.translation:
"Oh Rama, the seion of Kakutsa! You ought to take me, who is a devotee, so devoted
to husband, who is distressed who feels alike in pleasure and pain and shares your joys and
sorrows."

verses.sanskrit:
यदि माम् दुह्खिताम् एवम् वनम् नेतुम् न च इच्चसि |

विषम् अग्निम् जलम् वा अहम् आस्थास्ये मृत्यु कारणात् || २-२९-२१
verses.translation:
"I shall take resort to poison or fire or water for dying, if you are not willing to
take me, afflicted as I am, as above."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् बहु विधम् तम् सा याचते गमनम् प्रति |

न अनुमेने महा बाहुस् ताम् नेतुम् विजनम् वनम् || २-२९-२२

एवम् उक्ता तु सा चिन्ताम् मैथिली समुपागता |

स्नापयन्ती इव गाम् उष्णैः अश्रुभिर् नयन च्युतैः || २-२९-२३

चिन्तयन्तीम् तथा ताम् तु निवर्तयितुम् आत्मवान् |

ताम्रोष्ठीं स तदा सीताम् काकुत्स्थो बह्व् असान्त्वयत् || २-२९-२४

sargas.title: Sarga 30
sargas.introduction:
The persistence of Seetha continues to go along with Rama to forest. But Rama tried
to pacify her telling that rendering service to elders staying back at home is advisable for ladies
like Seetha, but in vain. Finally Rama had to yield to her prevail and accepts her to come along
and asks her to give away her personal belongings to Brahmins in charity before they proceed.

verses.sanskrit:
सान्त्व्यमाना तु रामेण मैथिली जनक आत्मजा |

वन वास निमित्ताय भर्तारम् इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-३०-१

सा तम् उत्तम सम्विग्ना सीता विपुल वक्षसम् |

प्रणयाच् च अभिमानाच् च परिचिक्षेप राघवम् || २-३०-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. saasiita = that Seetha; tam = distressed;
uttamasamvignaa = highly agitated; praNayaat = from affection abhimaanaachcha =
and pride; parichikshhepa = reproached; raaghavam = Rama; vipula
vakshhasam = having a broad chest.

verses.sanskrit:
किम् त्वा अमन्यत वैदेहः पिता मे मिथिला अधिपः |

राम जामातरम् प्राप्य स्त्रियम् पुरुष विग्रहम् || २-३०-३
verses.translation:
"What my father, the king of Mithila belonging to the country of Videha, think of
himself having got as so-in-law you, a woman having the form of a man?"

verses.sanskrit:
अनृतम् बल लोको अयम् अज्ञानात् यद्द् हि वक्ष्यति |

तेजो न अस्ति परम् रामे तपति इव दिवा करे || २-३०-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. bata = what a pity! ayam lokaH = these people; vakshhyati
yadi = if they tell; ajJNaanaat = by ignorance; anR^itam = the falsehood;
param = excellent; tejaH = valour; naasti = is lacking; raame =
in Rama; divaakare iva = as in a sun; tapati = which is blazing.
verses.translation:
"It is a pity if these people of Ayodhya through ignorance tell the falsehood that
excellent valour is lacking in Rama as in a blazing sun."

verses.sanskrit:
किम् हि कृत्वा विषण्णः त्वम् कुतः वा भयम् अस्ति ते |

यत् परित्यक्तु कामः त्वम् माम् अनन्य परायणाम् || २-३०-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. kihikR^itvaa = for what reason; tvam = you;
vishhaNNaH = are cast down? kutaHvaa = whence; bhayam = fear; asti = is
there; te = to you; parityaktukaamaH itityat = for which you are willing to
desert; maam = me; ananya paraayaNaam = for whom there is no other recourse.
verses.translation:
"For what reason are you cast down or whence fear in you, for which you are willing
to desert me, for whom there is no other recourse."

verses.sanskrit:
द्युमत्सेन सुतम् वीर सत्यवन्तम् अनुव्रताम् |

सावित्रीम् इव माम् विद्धि त्वम् आत्म वश वर्तिनीम् || २-३०-६
verses.translation:
"Oh, Brave man! Know me to be as dependent on your will as Savitri who was devoted
to Satyavan the son of Dyumatsena."

verses.sanskrit:
न तु अहम् मनसा अपि अन्यम् द्रष्टा अस्मि त्वद् ऋते अनघ |

त्वया राघव गच्चेयम् यथा अन्या कुल पांसनी || २-३०-७
verses.translation:
"Rama, the faultless man! I will not see, even in thought, anyone except you as any
other woman bringing disrepute to her family would! I shall accompany you to the forest."

verses.sanskrit:
स्वयम् तु भार्याम् कौमारीम् चिरम् अध्युषिताम् सतीम् |

शैलूषैव माम् राम परेभ्यो दातुम् इच्चसि || २-३०-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. raama = Oh; Rama! kaumaariim = I am a young girl;
adhyushhitaam = lived; chiram = for a long time; satiim = a chaste
woman; bhaaryaam = and a wife; ichchhasi = you intend; daatum = to
deliver; parebhyaH = to others; maam = me; shailuushhaH iva = like an
actor; svayam = by your own will.
verses.translation:
" Oh, Rama! I am a young girl, lived for a long time with you, a chaste woman and
your wife. How would you like an actor intend to deliver me to oehrs by your own will?"

verses.sanskrit:
यस्य पथ्यम् च रामात्थ यस्य चार्थेऽवरुध्यसे |

त्वम् तस्य भव वश्यश्च विधेयश्छ सदानघ|| २-३०-९
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama the sinless one! Be you always dutiful and obedient to him, whom you speak
of welfare, for whose sake I am being kept back.(But it is improper to ask me to stay obedient to
him)."

verses.sanskrit:
स माम् अनादाय वनम् न त्वम् प्रस्थातुम् अर्हसि |

तपो वा यदि वा अरण्यम् स्वर्गो वा स्यात् सह त्वया || २-३०-१०
verses.translation:
"It is not proper for you as such to set out to the forest without taking me. Any
term of austerities or forest or even heaven, let it be to me with you only."

verses.sanskrit:
न च मे भविता तत्र कश्चित् पथि परिश्रमः |

पृष्ठतः तव गच्चन्त्या विहार शयनेष्व् अपि || २-३०-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. me = to me; gachchhantyaaH = who is going;
pR^ishhThataH = behind; tava = you; tatra = there; na bhavitaa
= will be no; parishramaH = fatigue; kashchit = whatsoever; pathi = in
the path; vitaarashayaneshhviva = as in a place of recreation or sleep.
verses.translation:
"To me, who follow you behind, there will be no tiresomeness. I shall remain in the
path without any fatigue, as remaining in a place of recreation or as in a sleep.

verses.sanskrit:
कुश काश शर इषीका ये च कण्टकिनो द्रुमाः |

तूल अजिन सम स्पर्शा मार्गे मम सह त्वया || २-३०-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. maarge = while in the path; tvayaasaha = with you;
kushakaasha shareshhiikaaH = blades of kusha grass; shrubs by the name of Kasa; reeds and
rushes; yedrumaaH cha = which plants; kaNtakina = with prickles; tulaajina
sama sparshaaH = will touch like heap of cotton or soft deerskin.
verses.translation:
"While walking with you, blades of kusha grass, shrubs by the name of kaasa, reeds
and rushes and plants with prickles which fall in the path will touch my soles like a heap of
cotton or soft deerskin."

verses.sanskrit:
महा वात समुद्धूतम् यन् माम् अवकरिष्यति |

रजो रमण तन् मन्ये पर अर्ध्यम् इव चन्दनम् || २-३०-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. ramaNa = Oh; beloved! yat = which; rajaH =
dust; mahaavaata samuddhuutam = hurled by a strong wind; avakarishhyati = will
cover; maam = me; manye = I reckon; tat = it; chandanam iva =
as sandal dust; paraarthyam = of highest blessing.
verses.translation:
"Oh, beloved! I shall reckon the dust raised by the strongest wind that will cover
my body as sandal dust of highest advantage."

verses.sanskrit:
शाद्वलेषु यद् आसिष्ये वन अन्ते वन गोरचा |

कुथा आस्तरण तल्पेषु किम् स्यात् सुखतरम् ततः || २-३०-१४
verses.translation:
"While dwelling in the forest, in its midst, I shall lie down on green grass. Will
lying in beds with carpets be more comfortable than that?"

verses.sanskrit:
पत्रम् मूलम् फलम् यत् त्वम् अल्पम् वा यदि वा बहु |

दास्यसि स्वयम् आहृत्य तन् मे अमृत रस उपमम् || २-३०-१५
verses.translation:
"Leaves, tubers and fruits either a little or abundant in quantity brought and given
by you yourself will be like nectar to me."

verses.sanskrit:
न मातुर् न पितुस् तत्र स्मरिष्यामि न वेश्मनः |

आर्तवानि उपभुन्जाना पुष्पाणि च फलानि च || २-३०-१६
verses.translation:
"Enjoying the flowers and fruits belonging to various seasons there, I shall neither
recollect my mother nor father nor my home."

verses.sanskrit:
न च तत्र गतः किंचित् द्रष्टुम् अर्हसि विप्रियम् |

मत् कृते न च ते शोको न भविष्यामि दुर्भरा || २-३०-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. tataH = hence; naarhasi = you are not justified
drashhTum = to see; kimchit = anything; vipriyam = unpleasant; tatra =
there; natkR^ite = because of me; na = no; shokaH = annoyance;
te = to you; na bhavishhyaami = I will not be; durbharaa = difficult to
be maintained.
verses.translation:
"Hence, you are not justified to see anything unpleasant there. There will be no
annoyance to you because of me. I will not be difficult to be maintained."

verses.sanskrit:
यः त्वया सह स स्वर्गो निरयो यः त्वया विना |

इति जानन् पराम् प्रीतिम् गच्च राम मया सह || २-३०-१८
verses.translation:
"Your companionship will be a heaven to me. Without you, it will be a hell. Oh,
Rama! By knowing thus my great love, obtain supreme joy with me."

verses.sanskrit:
अथ माम् एवम् अव्यग्राम् वनम् न एव नयिष्यसि |

विषम् अद्य एव पास्यामि मा विशम् द्विषताम् वशम् || २-३०-१९
verses.translation:
"On the contrary, if you do not take me, who is not alarmed of the forest as such, I
shall drink poison now itself. But on no account would I bow to the enemies."

verses.sanskrit:
पश्चात् अपि हि दुह्खेन मम न एव अस्ति जीवितम् |

उज्झितायाः त्वया नाथ तदा एव मरणम् वरम् || २-३०-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. naatha = Oh; Lord! mam = to me; ujjhitaayaaH =
abandored; tvayaa = by you; duHkhana = because of grief; jiivitam =
life; naivaasti = will not be there; pashchaadapi = even afterwards;
maraNam = death; varam = is better; tadaiva = at the time of your
relinquishment itself.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lord! As a result of grief I will not going to live even afterwards when
abandoned by you. Death is therefore better at the time of your relinquishment itself."

verses.sanskrit:
इदम् हि सहितुम् शोकम् मुहूर्तम् अपि न उत्सहे |

किम् पुनर् दश वर्षाणि त्रीणि च एकम् च दुह्खिता || २-३०-२१
verses.translation:
"I cannot bear this grief even for a moment, why again fourteen years in
misery."

verses.sanskrit:
इति सा शोक सम्तप्ता विलप्य करुणम् बहु |

चुक्रोश पतिम् आयस्ता भृशम् आलिन्ग्य सस्वरम् || २-३०-२२

सा विद्धा बहुभिर् वाक्यैः दिग्धैः इव गज अन्गना |

चिर सम्नियतम् बाष्पम् मुमोच अग्निम् इव अरणिः || २-३०-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. viddhaa = pierced; bahubhiH = by many; vaakyaiH
= words; saa = she; gajaaNganaa iva = like a female elephant; dighaiH =
by poisoned arrows; bhaashhpam = tears; chirasanniyatam = which were held back
for long; mumocha = were shed; agrimiva = like fire; araNiH = by piece
of wood(through attrition with another)

verses.sanskrit:
तस्याः स्फटिक सम्काशम् वारि सम्ताप सम्भवम् |

नेत्राभ्याम् परिसुस्राव पन्कजाभ्याम् इव उदकम् || २-३०-२४

तच्चैवामलचन्ध्रभम् मुखमायतलोचनम् |

पर्यशुष्यत बाष्पेण जलोद्धृतमिवामुबुजम् || २-३०-२५

ताम् परिष्वज्य बाहुभ्याम् विसम्ज्ञाम् इव दुह्खिताम् |

उवाच वचनम् रामः परिविश्वासयंस् तदा || २-३०-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. tadaa = then; raamaH = Rama; parishhvajya =
embraced; taam = her; baahubhyaam = with arms; duHkhitaam = who was
depressed; visaMjJNaami = and had fainted; uvaacha = spoke; vachanam =
the words; parivishvaasayan = fully reassuring.

verses.sanskrit:
न देवि तव दुह्खेन स्वर्गम् अपि अभिरोचये |

न हि मे अस्ति भयम् किंचित् स्वयम्भोर् इव सर्वतः || २-३०-२७
verses.translation:
"Oh, queen! I do not relish even heaven while you are in grief. There is indeed no
fear whatsoever to me, like the god of Brahma.'

verses.sanskrit:
तव सर्वम् अभिप्रायम् अविज्ञाय शुभ आनने |

वासम् न रोचये अरण्ये शक्तिमान् अपि रक्षणे || २-३०-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. shubhaanane = Oh; Seetha with the auspicious face!
shaktimaanapi = eventhough I am able; rakshhaNe = to protect; avijJNaaya =
without knowing; tava = your; abhipraayam = opinion; sarvam = in
entirety; na rochaye = I do not like; vaasam = your dwelling; araNye =
in the forest.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Seetha with the auspicious face! Eventhough I am able to protect you, without
knowing your entire opinion, I do not like to take you to the forest."

verses.sanskrit:
यत् सृष्टा असि मया सार्धम् वन वासाय मैथिलि |

न विहातुम् मया शक्या कीर्तिर् आत्मवता यथा || २-३०-२९
verses.translation:
"Oh, Seetha! it appears that you are created indeed for dwelling in the forest with
me. Hence, you cannot be left behind by me, like the honour by a man of self-regard."

verses.sanskrit:
धर्मः तु गज नास ऊरु सद्भिर् आचरितः पुरा |

तम् च अहम् अनुवर्ते अद्य यथा सूर्यम् सुवर्चला || २-३०-३०
verses.translation:
"Oh Seetha, with thighs like the posteriors of an elephant! Earlier, righeousness
(like fulfilling the father's command) was practised by good men. Now, I shall follow that
righteousness, like Suvarchala(wife of the sun) following the sun."

verses.sanskrit:
न खल्वहम् न गच्छेयम् वनम् जनकनन्दिनि |

वचनम् त्न्नयति माम् पितुः सत्योपबृंहितम् || २-३०-३१
verses.translation:
"Oh, Seetha! I cannot desist from not going to forest. That word, of my father, made
strong by his truthfulnes is taking me to the forest."

verses.sanskrit:
एष धर्मः तु सुश्रोणि पितुर् मातुः च वश्यता |

अतः च आज्ञाम् व्यतिक्रम्य न अहम् जीवितुम् उत्सहे || २-३०-३२
verses.translation:
"Oh, comely lady! Being obedient to father and mother is one's sacred duty. That is
why, I do not wish to survive, in violation of that duty."

verses.sanskrit:
अस्वाधीनम् कथम् दैवम् प्रकारैरभिराध्यते |

स्वाधीनम् समतिक्रम्य मातरम् पितरम् गुरुम् || २-३०-३३
verses.wordMeaning:
33. samatikramya = by neglecting; maataram = mother;
pitaram = father; gurum = teacher; svaadhiinam = who are at one's
disposal; katham = how; daivam = god; asvaadhiinam = who is not one's
disposal; abhiraadhyate = can be worshipped; prakaaraiH = by various modes?
verses.translation:
"Mother, father and teacher are at our own disposal. By negelecting them as such,
how can we worship god, who is not at our disposal, by various modes?"

verses.sanskrit:
यत्त्रयम् तत्त्रयो लोकाः पवित्रम् तत्समम् भुवि |

नान्यदस्ति शुभापाङ्गे तेनेदमभिराध्यते || २-३०-३४
verses.wordMeaning:
34. shubhaapaaNge = Oh; lady with enchanting glances! yat =
which trayam = tinity (of mother; father and teacher) is there; tat = that is ; trayaH
lokaaH = the world-triad; naasti = there is none; anyat = other;
loke = in the world; tatsamam = equal to it; tena = hence; idam
= this; abhiraadhyate = is being worshipped.
verses.translation:
"Oh, lady with enchanting glances! The triad of mother, father and teacher is the
world-triad. There is none other in the world equal to it. Hence, this triad is worthy of
worship."

verses.sanskrit:
न सत्यम् दानमानौ वा न यज्ञाश्चाप्तदक्षिणाः |

तथा बलकराः सीते यथा सेवा पितुर्मता || २-३०-३५
verses.translation:
"Oh, Seetha! In which manner service to father is regarded as strengthening, in the
same manner truth or sacrificial rites where presents are received by officiating priest are not
strengthening."

verses.sanskrit:
स्वर्गो धनम् वा धान्यम् वा विद्याः पुत्राः सुखानि च |

गुरुवृत्त्यनुरोधेन न किंचित्\dapi दुर्लभम् || २-३०-३६
verses.wordMeaning:
36. guruvR^ittyanurodhena = by compliance with the wishes of one's
elders; svargaH = heavenly bliss; dhanamvaa = or riches; dhaanyamvaa =
foodgrains; vidyaaH = or learning; putraaH- sons; sukhaanicha = and amenities of
life; na = nothing; kimchidapi = whatsoever; durlabham = is hard to
obtain.
verses.translation:
"By compliance with the wishes of one's elders, heavenly bliss or riches, foodgrains
or leaving, sons or amenities of life - nothing whatsoever is hard to obtain."

verses.sanskrit:
देवगन्धर्वगोलोकान् ब्रह्मलोकम् तथापरान् |

प्राप्नुवन्ति महात्मानो मातापितृपरायणाः || २-३०-३७
verses.wordMeaning:
37. mahaatmanaH = high souled men; maataapitR^iparaayaNaaH =
wholly devoted to their mother and father; praapnuvanti = obtain; deva gandharva
golokaan = the regions of gods and the gandharvas; and goloka (highest heaven abiding in cows
and presided over by Sri Radha and Sri Krishna the first couple) brahma loka; = the seventh heaven
presided by Brahma ( creator) tathaa = and; aparan = other regions.
verses.translation:
"High souled men, wholly devoted to their parents obtain the regions of gods and the
gandharvas, the seventh heaven presided the gandharvas, the seventh heaven presided by Brahma (the
creator) and Goloka (the highest heaven abiding in cows and presided over by Sri Radha and Sri
Krishna the first couple).

verses.sanskrit:
स माम् पिता यथा शास्ति सत्य धर्म पथे स्थितः |

तथा वर्तितुम् इच्चामि स हि धर्मः सनातनः || २-३०-३८
verses.translation:
"I desire to obey in the same manner as to how my father, abiding in truthfulness
and virtue, commands me. That itself is indeed the law of morality, which is eternal."

verses.sanskrit:
मम सन्ना मतिः सीते त्वाम् नेतुम् दण्डकावनम् |

वसिष्यामीति सात्वम् मामनुयातुम् सुनिश्चिता || २-३०-३९
verses.translation:
"Oh, Seetha! My mind was depressed to take you to the forest of Dandaka. But you are
saying that you will reside in the forst, duly determined to follow me."

verses.sanskrit:
सा हि दिष्टाऽनवद्याङ्गी वनाय वदिरेक्षणे |

अनुगच्चस्व माम् भीरु सह धर्म चरी भव || २-३०-४०
verses.translation:
"Oh, mistress with fascinating eyes and flawless limbs! Since you were allowed to
come to forest, Oh Seetha the timid lady, follow me and become my help-mate."

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वथा सदृशम् सीते मम स्वस्य कुलस्य च |

व्यवसायमनुक्रान्ता कान्ते त्वमतिशोभनम् || २-३०-४१
verses.translation:
"Oh, Seetha the beautiful! You adopted very auspicious resolve, suitable in all ways
to me and to your race."

verses.sanskrit:
आरभस्व शुभश्रोणि वनवासक्षमाः क्रियाः |

नेदानीम् त्वदृते सीते स्वर्गोऽपि मम रोचते || २-३०-४२
verses.translation:
"Oh, lady with charming hip and loins! Now itself, begin with duties appropriate for
living in exile. Oh, Seetha! Even a heaven is not a liking to me without your presence."

verses.sanskrit:
ब्राह्मणेभ्यः च रत्नानि भिक्षुकेभ्यः च भोजनम् |

देहि च आशंसमानेभ्यः सम्त्वरस्व च माचिरम् || २-३०-४३
verses.wordMeaning:
43. dehi = give; ratnaanicha = valnable gifts;
braahmaNebhyaH = to Brahmanas; bhojanamcha = and good; bhikshhukebhyaH =
to mendicants; santvarasvacha = be very quick; maa = do not; chiram =
delay.
verses.translation:
"Give valuable gifts to Brahmanas and food to mendicants. Be very quick. Do not
delay."

verses.sanskrit:
भूषणानि महार्हाणि वरवस्त्राणि यानि च |

रमणीयाश्च ये केचित्क्रीडार्थाश्चापुयुपस्कराः || २-३०-४४

शयनीयानि यानानि मम चान्यानि यानि च |

देहि स्वभृत्यवर्गस्य ब्राह्मणानामनन्तरम् || २-३०-४५
verses.translation:
"Give to the various classes of your dependents whatever costly ornaments, beautiful
and fine articles of wearing apparel, gadgets used for sports, my couches, conveyances and other
articles left after satisfying brahmanas."

verses.sanskrit:
अनुकूलम् तु सा भर्तुर् ज्ञात्वा गमनम् आत्मनः |

क्षिप्रम् प्रमुदिता देवी दातुम् एव उपचक्रमे || २-३०-४६

ततः प्रहृष्टा परिपूर्ण मानसा |

यशस्विनी भर्तुर् अवेक्ष्य भाषितम् |

धनानि रत्नानि च दातुम् अन्गना |

प्रचक्रमे धर्मभृताम् मनस्विनी || २-३०-४७

sargas.title: Sarga 31
sargas.introduction:
Overhearing the conversation of Rama and Seetha, Lakshmana who came there earlier
enters into dialogue with his brother, insisting that he too shall accompany Rama and Seetha to
forests. Rama tries to persuade him to stay back, in vain. Then Rama had to agree to Lakshmana's
insistence and asks him to fetch the divine bows, arrows and swords given by gods in the
Vedic-ritual of Seetha's father, namely King Janaka.

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् श्रुत्वा तु सम्वादम् लक्ष्मणः पूर्वमागतः |

बाष्पपर्याकुलमुखः शोकम् सोढुमशक्नुवन् || २-३१-१

स भ्रातुश्चरणौ गाढम् निपीड्य रघुनन्दनः |

सीतामुवाचातियशाम् राघवम् च महाव्रतम् || २-३१-२

यदि गन्तुम् कृता बुद्धिर्वनम् मृगगजायुतम् |

अहम् त्वानुगमिष्यामि वनमद्रे धनुर्धरः || २-३१-३
verses.translation:
"If you decided to go to the forest filled with antelopes and elephants, I, in front
wearingthe bow, shall accompany you to the forest."

verses.sanskrit:
मया समेतोऽरण्यानि बहूनि विचरिष्यसि |

पक्षिभिर्मृगयूथैश्च सम्घुष्टानि समन्ततः || २-३१-४
verses.translation:
"Together with me, you will travel in the various forests, resonant with sounds of
birds and herds of wild animals."

verses.sanskrit:
न देवलोकाक्रमणम् नामरत्वमहम् वृणे |

ऐश्वर्यम् वापि लोकानाम् कामये न त्वया विना || २-३१-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. tvayaa vinaa = without you; aham = I; navR^iNe =
do not want; devalokakramaNam = ascendency to heaven; na = nor desiring;
amaratvam = immortality; na kaamaye = now wishing; aishvaryam vaapi =
even sovereignity; lokaanaam = over the globes.
verses.translation:
"Without you, I do not want ascendency to heaven now do I desire immortality now do
I wish for sovereignity over the globes."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् ब्रुवाणः सौमित्रिर्विनवासाय निश्चितः |

रामेण बहुभिः सान्वैर्निषिद्धः पुनरब्रवीत् || २-३१-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. saumitriH = Lakshmana; bruvaaNaH = who was speaking;
evam = thus; nishchitaH = determined; vanavaasaaya = to dwell in the
forest; nishhiddhaH = was forbidden; raameNa = by Rama; bahubhiH
saantvaiH = through many pacifying words; punaH = again; abraviit =
spoke.

verses.sanskrit:
अनुज्ञातश्च भवता पूर्वमेव यदस्म्यहम् |

किमिदानीम् पुनरिदम् क्रियते मे निवारणम् || २-३१-७
verses.translation:
"I was indeed permitted by you earlier. How is it that you are prohibiting me
now?"

verses.sanskrit:
यदर्थम् प्रतिषेधो मे क्रियते गन्तुमिच्छतः |

एतदिच्छामि विज्ञातुम् संशयो हि ममानघ || २-३१-८
verses.translation:
"Oh, the faultless man! I desire to know the reason for prohibiting me, who is
indeed willing to come to the forest, because there is doubt in my mind."

verses.sanskrit:
ततः अब्रवीन् महा तेजा रामः लक्ष्मणम् अग्रतः |

स्थितम् प्राग् गामिनम् वीरम् याचमानम् कृत अन्जलिम् || २-३१-९

स्निग्धो धर्मरतो वीरस्सततम् सत्पथे स्थितः |

प्रियः प्राणसमो वशो भ्राता चापि सखा च मे || २-३१-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. snigdhaH = friendly; dharmarataH = interested in
righeousness; viiraH = valiant; sthitaH = always; satpathe = in good
path; priyaH = the beloved one; praaNa samaH = as dear as life; vashyaH
= obedient; bhraatraachaapi = the brother; sakhaacha = and a companion.
verses.translation:
"You are so friendly, interested in righteousness, valiant, abiding always in a good
path, dear to me as life, obedient, my brother and a companion."

verses.sanskrit:
मया अद्य सह सौमित्रे त्वयि गच्चति तत् वनम् |

को भरिष्यति कौसल्याम् सुमित्राम् वा यशस्विनीम् || २-३१-११
verses.translation:
"Oh Lakshmana! If you proceed to the forest along with me now, who will support
Kausalya or the illustrious Sumitra?"

verses.sanskrit:
अभिवर्षति कामैः यः पर्जन्यः पृथिवीम् इव |

स काम पाश पर्यस्तः महा तेजा मही पतिः || २-३१-१२
verses.translation:
"That emperor of great splendour, who used to shower blessings on the people as a
cloud sends down rain on the earth, stands circumscribed by cord of love."

verses.sanskrit:
सा हि राज्यम् इदम् प्राप्य नृपस्य अश्व पतेः सुता |

दुह्खितानाम् सपत्नीनाम् न करिष्यति शोभनम् || २-३१-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. saa = that Kaikeyi; sutaa = daughter; nR^ipasya
= of king; ashvapateH = Aswapathi; praapya = after getting; idam raajyam
= this kingdom; na karishhyati hi = will certainly not accord; shobhanam = good
treatment; sapatniinaam = to step-wives; duHkhitaanaam = who are at grief.
verses.translation:
"That Kaikeyi daughter of king Aswapathi, after obtaining this kingdom, will
certainly not accord good treatment to her step-wives, who are at grief."

verses.sanskrit:
न स्मरिष्यति कौसल्याम् सुमित्राम् च सुदुःखिताम् |

भरतो राज्यमासाद्य कैकेय्याम् पर्यवस्थितः || २-३१-१४
verses.translation:
"Bharata on acquiring the kingdom will be devoted to Kaikeyi and will not think of
sorrowful Kausalya or Sumitra."

verses.sanskrit:
तामार्याम् स्वयमेवेह राजाऽनुग्रहणेन वा |

सौमित्रे भर कौसल्या मुक्तमर्थमिमम् चर || २-३१-१५
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lakshmana! You stay here and support the venerable Kausalya through your own
self or by obtaining favour through the king. Do this thing, as said."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् मम च ते भक्तिर्भविष्यति सुदर्शिता |

धर्मज्ञ गुरुपूजायाम् धर्मश्चाप्यतुलो महान् || २-३१-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. dharmajJNaa = Oh; Lakshmana; knower of righteousness!
evam = thus; te = your; te = your; bhaktiH = devotion;
mama = towards me; bhavishhyati = will be; sudarshitaa = well
demonstrated; gurupuujaayaam = by honouring the elders; mahaan; a great; atulaH =
unequalled; dharmashcha = religious merit(will accrue).
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lakshmana the knower of righteousness! Thus, your devotion towards me will have
been fully demonstrated by you. By honouring the elders, a great unqualled religious merit will
accrue to you."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् कुरुष्व सौमित्रे मत्क्ऱ्^ते रघुनन्दन |

अस्माभिर्विप्रहीनाया मातुर्नो न भवेत्सुखम् || २-३१-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. saumitre = OH; Lakshmana; raghunandana = who exhilarates
Raghu dynasty; kurushhva = do; evam = thus; matkR^ite = for my sake;
na bhavet = there will be no; sukham = happiness; naH = maatuH = to our
mother; viprahiinaayaaH = who is left behind; naH = bye us.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lakshmana, the exhilarator of Raghu dynasty! Do this for my sake. There will be
no happiness to our mother, if she is left behind by us."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्तः तु रामेण लक्ष्मणः श्लक्ष्णया गिरा |

प्रत्युवाच तदा रामम् वाक्यज्ञो वाक्य कोविदम् || २-३१-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. lakshmaNaH = Lakshmana; vaakyaajJNaH = who is
intelligent in speech; uktaH = who was spoken; evam = thus; raameNa = by
Rama; pratyuvaacha = replied; tadaa = then; giraa = with the voice;
shlakshhNayaa = so gentle; raamam = to Rama; vaakyakovidam = who is
skilled in speech.

verses.sanskrit:
तव एव तेजसा वीर भरतः पूजयिष्यति |

कौसल्याम् च सुमित्राम् च प्रयतः न अत्र संशयः || २-३१-१९
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama the valiant! Bharata being inspired by your splendour of morality will
respect Kausalya and Sumitra. There is no doubt in this matter."

verses.sanskrit:
कौसल्या बिभृयात् आर्या सहस्रम् अपि मद् विधान् |

यस्याः सहस्रम् ग्रामाणाम् सम्प्राप्तम् उपजीवनम् || २-३१-२०
verses.translation:
"Kausalya obtained (by grant) thousand villages , which are dependent on her. Hence,
that venerable Kausalya can maintain even thousand people like me."

verses.sanskrit:
तदात्मभरणे चैव मम मातुस्तथैव च |

पर्याप्ता मद्विधानाम् च भरणाय यशस्विनी || २-३१-२१
verses.wordMeaning:
21. tat = that is why; yashasvinii = the illustrious
Kausalya; paryaaptaa = is competent not only; aatmabharaNaichaiva = to maintain
herself; tathaivacha = but also; mama maatuH = my mother; bharaNaayacha
= and the support of; madvidhaanaamcha = people like me.
verses.translation:
"The illustrious Kausalya is competent not only to maintain herself but also my
mother and people like me in addition."

verses.sanskrit:
कुरुष्व मामनुचरम् वैधर्म्यम् नेह विद्यते |

कृतार्थोऽहम् भविष्यामि तव चार्थः प्रकल्पते || २-३१-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. kurushhva = make; maam = me; amcharam = your
attendant; iha = in this; navidyate = there is no; vaidharmyam =
unlawfulness; aham = I; bhavishhyaami = will be; kR^itaarthaH =
accomplishing my object; tava = your; arthattcha = purpose also;
prakalpate = will be fulfilled.
verses.translation:
"Make me your attendant. In this, there is no unrighteousness. Besides, I will be
accomplishing my object. Your purpose also will be fulfilled."

verses.sanskrit:
धनुर् आदाय सशरम् खनित्र पिटका धरः |

अग्रतः ते गमिष्यामि पन्थानम् अनुदर्शयन् || २-३१-२३
verses.translation:
"Taking my bow and arrows and carrying a spade and a basket, I will walk in front of
you showing the path."

verses.sanskrit:
आहरिष्यामि ते नित्यम् मूलानि च फलानि च |

वन्यानि यानि च अन्यानि स्वाहाराणि तपस्विनाम् || २-३१-२४
verses.translation:
"I will procure for you for all time the tubers, fruits and other things which are
good food stuffs available in the forest for sages."

verses.sanskrit:
भवांस् तु सह वैदेह्या गिरि सानुषु रंस्यते |

अहम् सर्वम् करिष्यामि जाग्रतः स्वपतः च ते || २-३१-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. bhavaamstu = be it so; that you; vaidehyaasaha = along
with Seetha; ramsyate = enjoy yourself; girisaamushhu = on mountain = ridges;
aham = I; karishhyaami = I shall do; sarvam = everything; te =
(while) you; jaagrataH = are waking; svapatashcha = or sleeping.
verses.translation:
"You along with Seetha enjoy yourself on mountain-ridges. I shall do everything
while you are waking or sleeping."

verses.sanskrit:
रामः तु अनेन वाक्येन सुप्रीतः प्रत्युवाच तम् |

व्रज आपृच्चस्व सौमित्रे सर्वम् एव सुहृज् जनम् || २-३१-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. raamastu = Rama; supriitaH = very much delighted;
anena vaakyena = by these words; pratyuvaacha = replied; tam = to him(
as follows); saumitre = Oh; Lakshmana! vraja = go; aapR^ichchhasva =
take leave; sarvameva = of all; suhR^ijjanam = your friends.

verses.sanskrit:
ये च राज्ञो ददौ दिव्ये महात्मा वरुणः स्वयम् |

जनकस्य महा यज्ञे धनुषी रौद्र दर्शने || २-३१-२७

अभेद्य कवचे दिव्ये तूणी च अक्षय सायकौ |

आदित्य विमलौ च उभौ खड्गौ हेम परिष्कृतौ || २-३१-२८

सत्कृत्य निहितम् सर्वम् एतत् आचार्य सद्मनि |

स त्वम् आयुधम् आदाय क्षिप्रम् आव्रज लक्ष्मण || २-३१-२९
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lakshma! At a grand sacrifice perfomed by Janaka, the great-souled Varuna(god
of water) personally gave heavently bows which are dreadful to look at, divine impenetrable pices
of armour, quivers containing an inexhanstible stock of arms, two swords decked with gold and with
spotless lustre like that of a sun - all these were kept at the residence of our receptor Vasista,
after paying due reverence. Take all those arms and return soon."

verses.sanskrit:
स सुहृज् जनम् आमन्त्र्य वन वासाय निश्चितः |

इष्क्वाकु गुरुम् आमन्त्र्य जग्राह आयुधम् उत्तमम् || २-३१-३०

तत् दिव्यम् राज शार्दूलः सत्कृतम् माल्य भूषितम् |

रामाय दर्शयाम् आस सौमित्रिः सर्वम् आयुधम् || २-३१-३१
verses.wordMeaning:
31. saumitriH = Lakshmana; raajashaarduulaH = a tiger among
princes; dadarshayaamaasa = showed; raamaaya = to Rama; sarvam = all;
tat aayadham = those arms; divyam = which are divine; satkR^itam = which
were worshipped maalya bhuushhitam = and decorated by garlands.

verses.sanskrit:
तम् उवाच आत्मवान् रामः प्रीत्या लक्ष्मणम् आगतम् |

काले त्वम् आगतः सौम्य कान्क्षिते मम लक्ष्मण || २-३१-३२

अहम् प्रदातुम् इच्चामि यद् इदम् मामकम् धनम् |

ब्राह्मणेभ्यः तपस्विभ्यः त्वया सह परम्तप || २-३१-३३
verses.wordMeaning:
33. paramtapa = Lakshmana; the chastiser of foes! aham = I;
tvayaa saha = along with you; ichchhaami = desire; pradaatum = to give
away; yat = which; idam = this; dhanam = wealth; mamakam =
pertaining to me; braahmaNebhyaH = to brahmanas; tapasvibhyaH = practising
ansterities.
verses.translation:
"Lakshmana, the chastiser of foes! along with you, I want to give all this wealth
pertaining to me to brahmanas, practising ansterities."

verses.sanskrit:
वसन्ति इह दृढम् भक्त्या गुरुषु द्विज सत्तमाः |

तेषाम् अपि च मे भूयः सर्वेषाम् च उपजीविनाम् || २-३१-३४
verses.translation:
"I also desire to give to those excellent brahmanas residing here as strong devotees
to their proceptors and to all my dependents."

verses.sanskrit:
वसिष्ठ पुत्रम् तु सुयज्ञम् आर्यम् |

त्वम् आनय आशु प्रवरम् द्विजानाम् |

अभिप्रयास्यामि वनम् समस्तान् |

अभ्यर्च्य शिष्टान् अपरान् द्विजातीन् || २-३१-३५
verses.translation:
"You bring boon the venerable Suyagna, the son of Vasista, the best. After adoring
all other brahmanas also who are cultured, I will go to the forest."
verses.commentary:
Thus completes 31st chapter in the Ayodhya kanda of the glorious Ramayayana of
Valmiki, the work of a sage and the oldest epic.
Verse Locator for Book II : Ayodhya Kanda - Book
Of Ayodhya : Chapter 31
