sargas.title: Sarga 32
sargas.introduction:
Rama bestows parting gifts to Vedic scholars and their wives. Thereafter he also
accords charities to Brahmins, young scholars, servants, and others. Rama accords an unusual gift,
of cows filling a space of miles and miles, to a poor Brahmin named Trijata. Then Rama is blessed
for a bon voyage by all of the eminent Vedic scholars.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः शासनम् आज्ञाय भ्रातुः शुभतरम् प्रियम् |

गत्वा स प्रविवेश आशु सुयज्ञस्य निवेशनम् || २-३२-१

तम् विप्रम् अग्नि अगारस्थम् वन्दित्वा लक्ष्मणो अब्रवीत् |

सखे अभ्यागच्च पश्य त्वम् वेश्म दुष्कर कारिणः || २-३२-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. lakshmanaH = Lakshmana; vanditvaa = after offering
salutation; tam vipram = to that brahmana; agnya gaarastham = living in a house
of fire; abraviit = spoke (as follows): sakhe = "Oh; friend! abhyaagachchha =
come; tvam = you; pashya = see; veshma = the house; dushhkara
kaariNaH = of Rama; who is doing an ardons task.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः संध्याम् उपास्य आशु गत्वा सौमित्रिणा सह |

जुष्टम् तत् प्राविशल् लक्ष्म्या रम्यम् राम निवेशनम् || २-३२-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. tataH = then; upaasya = having performed worship;
sandhyaam = at the juncture of day and night; saumitriNaa saha = along with
Lakshmana; gatvaa = went ; aashu = at once; praavishat = and entered tat
raamanivesham = that Rama's house; ramyam = which was beautiful; jushhtam = and
inhabited; lakshhmyaa = by Lakshmi; the the goddess of wealth.

verses.sanskrit:
तम् आगतम् वेदविदम् प्रान्जलिः सीतया सह |

सुयज्ञम् अभिचक्राम राघवो अग्निम् इव अर्चितम् || २-३२-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. raaghavaH = Rama; siitayaa saha = along with; Seetha
praaNjaliH = with joined palms; abhichakraama = circumambulated; agnim iva =
as around sacrifical fire; tam suyajJNam = that suyajna; architam = the venerable
man; veda vidam = who had knowledge of the Vedas; aagatam = who arrived.

verses.sanskrit:
जात रूपमयैः मुख्यैः अन्गदैः कुण्डलैः शुभैः |

सहेम सूत्रैः मणिभिः केयूरैः वलयैः अपि || २-३२-५

अन्यैः च रत्नैः बहुभिः काकुत्स्थः प्रत्यपूजयत् |

सुयज्ञम् स तदा उवाच रामः सीता प्रचोदितः || २-३२-६

हारम् च हेम सूत्रम् च भार्यायै सौम्य हारय |

रशनाम् च अधुना सीता दातुम् इच्चति ते सखे || २-३२-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. sakhe = Oh; friend; saumya = the gentle sage! adhunaa =
now; siitaa = Sita; ichchhati = wants; daatum = to give;
bhaaryaayai = for your wife; haaram cha = a pearl necklace; hema
suutramcha = a string of gold; rashanaam cha = and a girdle; haaraya =
carry(them)
verses.translation:
"Oh the gentle sage, my friend! Now, Seetha wants to give for your wife a pearl
necklace, a string of gold and a girdle. please take them."

verses.sanskrit:
अङ्गदानि विचित्राणि केयूराणि शुभानि च |

पर्यन्कम् अग्र्य आस्तरणम् नाना रत्न विभूषितम् || २-३२-८
verses.translation:
"Oh, friend! Seetha who is departing to the forest, is giving you for your wife
armlets adorned with figures cut in them and beautiful keyuras."

verses.sanskrit:
पर्यङ्कमग्र्यास्तरणम् नानारत्नविभूषितम् |

तम् अपि इच्चति वैदेही प्रतिष्ठापयितुम् त्वयि || २-३२-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. vaidehii = Seetha; a princess of videha clan; ichchhati =
wants; pratishhThaapayitum = to offer; tvayi = you; tam paryaNkam api =
that couch also; naanaa ratna vibhuushhitam = inlaid with various jewels;
agryyaastaranam = previded with an excellent coverlet.
verses.translation:
"Seetha wants to offer you that couch also inlaid with various jewels and provided
with an excellent coverlet."

verses.sanskrit:
नागः शत्रुम् जयो नाम मातुलो यम् ददौ मम |

तम् ते गज सहस्रेण ददामि द्विज पुम्गव || २-३२-१०
verses.translation:
"Oh, the best of brahmanas! I am giving you this elephant called Satrunjaya, given
earlier to me by my maternal uncle as well as those thousand elephants."

verses.sanskrit:
इति उक्तः स हि रामेण सुयज्ञः प्रतिगृह्य तत् |

राम लक्ष्मण सीतानाम् प्रयुयोज आशिषः शिवाः || २-३२-११

अथ भ्रातरम् अव्यग्रम् प्रियम् रामः प्रियम् वदः |

सौमित्रिम् तम् उवाच इदम् ब्रह्मा इव त्रिदश ईश्वरम् || २-३२-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. atha = then; raamaH = Rama; priyamvadaH = who
speaks polite words avyagram = coolly; brahmaa = as brahma; tridasheshvaram iva =
to Devendra; uvaacha = spoke; idam = thus; tam saumitrim = to that
Lakshmana; priyam = his beloved; bhraataram = brother.

verses.sanskrit:
अगस्त्यम् कौशिकम् चैव ताव् उभौ ब्राह्मण उत्तमौ |

अर्चय आहूय सौमित्रे रत्नैः सस्यम् इव अम्बुभिः || २-३२-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. saumitre = Oh; Lakshmana! aahuuya = call; tau =
those; ubhau = two; brahmaNottamau = excellent brahmanas; agastyam =
Agastya; kaushikam chaiva = and Kausika; archaya = worship; ratinaiH =
with; ratnaiH = valuable gifts; amububhiH iva = as water; sasyam = to a
crop of corn.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lakshmana! Call those two excellent brahmanas Agastya and Kausika and worship
them with valuable gifts as water is poured to a crop of corn."

verses.sanskrit:
तर्पयस्व महाबाहो गोसहसरैश्च मानद |

सुवर्णै रजतैश्चैव मणिभिश्च महाधनैः || २-३२-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. maanada = Oh Lakshmana; the honour-giver; mahaabaaho =
with great arms! tarpayasva = satiate them; tarpayasva = with thousands of cows;
suvarnaiH = with gold; rajataishchaiva = and silver; maNibhishcha = and
gems; mahaadhanaiH = and gems; mahaadhanaiH = of great value.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lakshmana the honour-giver, with great arms! Satiate them with thousands of
cows, gold, silver and with gems of great value."

verses.sanskrit:
कौसल्याम् च याअशीर्भिर् भक्तः पर्युपतिष्ठति |

आचार्यः तैत्तिरीयाणाम् अभिरूपः च वेदवित् || २-३२-१५

तस्य यानम् च दासीः च सौमित्रे सम्प्रदापय |

कौशेयानि च वस्त्राणि यावत् तुष्यति स द्विजः || २-३२-१६
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lakshmana! Which brahman is studying Taittiriya(a school of yajurveda), a
preceptor, a man of conformity; a knower of Vedas, serving Kausalya with his devotion and blessing,
to him see that he is duly gifted conveyance, servant maids and silken clothing till he gets
satisfied."
verses.commentary:
comment: Taittiriya was first taught by sage Vaisampayana to 27 pupils, among
whom was Yagnavalkya, Subsequently Vaisampayana being offended with Yaganavalkya, made him disgorge
the Veda committed to him which he did in a tangible form; whereupon the older disciples of
Vaisampayana being commanded to pick it up, took in the form of patridges, and swallowed the soiled
texts, hence named black(called Krishna Yajurveda) the other name of Taittiriya referring to the
partridges Yagnavalkya then received from the Sun a new or white version of the yajurveda (called
Shukla Yajurveda)

verses.sanskrit:
सूतः चित्र रथः च आर्यः सचिवः सुचिर उषितः |

तोषय एनम् महा अर्हैः च रत्नैः वस्त्रैः धनैअः तथा || २-३२-१७
verses.translation:
"The charioteer called Chitraratha the companion of our venerable father was of very
long standing. Gratify him with valuable gifts of great worth, with clothes, with money, with all
types of small animals and with thousands of cows."

verses.sanskrit:
पशुकाभिकछ सर्वाभिर्गवाम् दशशतेन च |

ये चेमे कथकालापा बहवो दण्डमाणवाः || २-३२-१८

नित्यस्वाध्यायशीलत्वान्नान्यत्कुर्वन्ति किंचन |

अलसाः स्वादुकामाश्च महताम् चापि सम्मताः || २-३२-१९

शालि वाह सहस्रम् च द्वे शते भद्रकांस् तथा |

व्यन्जन अर्थम् च सौमित्रे गो सहस्रम् उपाकुरु || २-३२-२०

मेखलीनाम् महासघः कौसल्याम् समुपस्थितः |

तेषाम् सहस्रम् सौमित्रे प्रत्येकम् सम्प्रदापय || २-३२-२१
verses.translation:
"Oh, Lakshmana! Many celebates wearing fillets stand near Kausalya, cause them to be
given a thousand cows each."

verses.sanskrit:
अम्बा यथा च सा नन्देत्कौसल्या मम दक्षिणाम् |

तथा द्विजातीम् स्तान्सर्वान् लक्ष्मणार्च || २-३२-२२
verses.translation:
"Honour all those brahmanas in every way, so that my mother Kausalya will feel happy
to see my gifts."

verses.sanskrit:
ततः स पुरुष व्याघ्रः तत् धनम् लक्ष्मणः स्वयम् |

यथा उक्तम् ब्राह्मण इन्द्राणाम् अददात् धनदो यथा || २-३२-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. tataH = then; saH lakshhmaNaH = that Lakshman;
purushha vyaaghraH = the tiger among men; svayam = himself; adadaat =
gave; dhanado yathaa = like Kubera; braahmaNendraaNaam = to the best of
brahmanas; tat dhanam = that wealth; yathoktam = as instructed.

verses.sanskrit:
अथ अब्रवीद् बाष्प कलांस् तिष्ठतः च उपजीविनः |

सम्प्रदाय बहु द्रव्यम् एकैकस्य उपजीविनः || २-३२-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. atha = then; sampradaaya = after giving; bahu =
abundant; dravyam = wealth; ekaikasya = to each one of; upajiivinaH =
the dependents; tishhTataH = standing; bhaashhpagaLan = with tears in their
throat; abraviit = spoke(as follows)

verses.sanskrit:
लक्ष्मणस्य च यद् वेश्म गृहम् च यद् इदम् मम |

अशून्यम् कार्यम् एकैकम् यावद् आगमनम् मम || २-३२-२५
verses.translation:
"Till my return, the house belonging to Lakshmana and also this house which is
occupied by me, should be guarded by each one of you, by turn."

verses.sanskrit:
इति उक्त्वा दुह्खितम् सर्वम् जनम् तम् उपजीविनम् |

उवाच इदम् धन ध्यक्षम् धनम् आनीयताम् इति || २-३२-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. iti uktvaa = having thus spoke; tam janam = to those
people; sarvam = all; upajiivanam = dependent on him; duHkhitam =
distressed as they were; uvaacha = he spoke; dhanaakshhyam = to treasurer;
idam = these words; iti = thus; aamiiyataam dhanam = "Let my wealth be
brought."

verses.sanskrit:
ततः अस्य धनम् आजह्रुः सर्वम् एव उपजीविनः |

स राशिः सुमहांस्तत्र दर्शनीयो ह्यदृश्यत || २-३२-२७

ततः स पुरुष व्याघ्रः तत् धनम् सह लक्ष्मणः |

द्विजेभ्यो बाल वृद्धेभ्यः कृपणेभ्यो अभ्यदापयत् || २-३२-२८

तत्र आसीत् पिन्गलो गार्ग्यः त्रिजटः नाम वै द्विजः |

क्षतवृत्तिर्वने नित्यम् फालकुद्दाललाङ्गली || २-३२-२९
verses.wordMeaning:
29. tatra = there; aasiit = remained; dvijaH = a
brahmana; trijaTonaama = by name Trijata; gaargyaH = who was born in garga
community; piNgaLaH = reddish-born in color; kshata vR^iHiH = with an occupation
of digging the soil; vane = in the forest; nityam = always; phaala
kuNdaalalaaNgalii = having an axe; a spade and a plough.
verses.translation:
"There lived a brahmana by name Trijata who was born in garga community and was
reddish born in colour. He used to make his living in the forest by digging the soil, always
carrying an axe, a spade and a plough."

verses.sanskrit:
तम् वृद्धम् तरुणी भार्या बालानादाय दारकान् |

अब्रवीद्बाह्मणम् वाक्यम् दारिद्र्येणाभिपीडिता || २-३२-३०

अपास्य फालम् कुद्दालम् कुरुष्व वचनम् ममम् |

रामम् दर्शय धर्मज्ज़्नम् यदि किंचिदवाप्स्यसि || २-३२-३१
verses.translation:
"Throw away the axe and the spade. Carry out my word. Seek for the presence of Rama
who knows his duty and see if you get at least something."

verses.sanskrit:
स भार्यावचनम् श्रुत्वा शाटीमाच्छाद्य दुश्छदाम् |

स प्रतिष्ठत पन्थानम् यत्र रामनिवेशनम् || २-३२-३२
verses.wordMeaning:
32. saH = He; shrutvaa = listed to; bharyaa
vachanam = his wife's words; aachchhaadya = wrapped; shatiim = a strip of
cloth; duHshchhadaam = that could hardly cover his body; praatishhThata = set
out; panthaanam = towards the track; yatra = wherein(lies) raama niveshanam =
Rama's palace.

verses.sanskrit:
भृग्वङ्गिरसमम् दीप्त्या त्रिजटम् जनसंसदि |

आ पन्चमायाः कक्ष्याया न एनम् कश्चित् अवारयत् || २-३२-३३
verses.wordMeaning:
33. evam = this; trijaTam = Trijata;
bhR^igvaNgirasamam = who was equal to sages of Bhrign and Angrira; diiptyaa = by
splendour; jana sanasadi = in a society of men; na avaarayat = could not be
obstructd; kashchit = by any one; aa paNchamaayaaH kakashhyaayaaH = upto the
fifth gate.

verses.sanskrit:
स राज पुत्रम् आसाद्य त्रिजटः वाक्यम् अब्रवीत् |

निर्धनो बहु पुत्रः अस्मि राज पुत्र महा यशः |

क्षतवृत्तिर्वने नित्यम् प्रत्यवेक्षस्व मामिति || २-३२-३४
verses.wordMeaning:
34. saH trijaTaH = that Tijata; aasaadya = having
approached; raajaputram = the prince Rama; abraviit = spoke; vaakyam =
(these) words; mahaayashaH = "Oh; the most famous raajaputra = prince;
nirdhanaH = I am poor; bahuputraH = having many children; asmi = I am;
nityam = always; vane = in the forest; kshhata vR^ittiH = with digging
the soil as an occupation; pratyavekshhasva = look; maam = to me; iti =
as you know.

verses.sanskrit:
तमुवाच ततो रामः परिहाससमन्वितम् |

गवाम् सहस्रमप्येकम् न च विश्राणितम् मया |

परिक्षिपसि दण्डेन यावत्तावदवाप्य्ससि || २-३२-३५
verses.wordMeaning:
35. tataH = thereupon; raamaH = Rama; uvaacha =
replied; parihaasa samanvitam = jestingly; tam = to him ( as follows:) mayaa = by
me; ekam gavaam sahasramapi = even one thousand of cows; na cha vishraaNitam =
were not given away; avaapsyasi = you will get; yaavat = as many(cows) taavat =
to such an extent; parikshhipasi = as you will throw; daNDena = by the staff.

verses.sanskrit:
स शाटीम् त्वरितः कट्याम् सम्ब्रान्तः परिवेष्ट्य ताम् |

आविद्ध्य दण्डम् चिक्षेप सर्वप्राणेन वेगितः || २-३२-३६

स तीर्त्वा सरयूपारम् दण्डस्तस्य कराच्च्युतः |

गोव्रजे बहुसाहास्रे पपातोक्षणसन्निधौ || २-३२-३७

तम् परिष्वज्य धर्मात्मा आतस्मात्सरयूतटात् |

आनयामास ता गोपैस्त्रिजटायाश्रमम् प्रति || २-३२-३८
verses.wordMeaning:
38. dharmaatmaa = Rama; of virtuous mind; parishhvajya =
embraced; tam = him; aanayaamaasa = having caused to be delivered;
gopaiH = by cowherds; taaH = those cows; aasarayuu taTaat = extending
upto Sarayu; aashramampati = to his hermitage; trijaTaaya = for Trijata.

verses.sanskrit:
उवाच च ततो रामस्तम् गार्ग्यमभिसान्त्वयन् |

मन्युर्न खलु कर्तव्यः परिहासो ह्ययम् मम || २-३२-३९
verses.wordMeaning:
39. tataH = thereupon; raamaH = Rama;
abhisaantvayan = pacifyingly; uvacha = spoke; tam gaargyam = to that son
of Garga(as follows:) nakaravyaH khalu = not fit for making; manyuH = expression of
contempt; ayam = this; mama = my; parihaasaH = jest.

verses.sanskrit:
इदम् हि तेजस्तव यद्धुरत्ययम् |

तदेव जिज्ञासितु मिच्छता मया |

इमम् भवानर्थमभिप्रचोदितो |

वृणीष्व किंचेदपरम् व्यवस्यति || २-३२-४०
verses.wordMeaning:
40. yat = which; idam tejaH = this strength; tava =
of you; duratyaayam = which is unfathomable; tat = that; ichchhataa iva
= is only desired; mayaa by me; jijJNaasitum = to know; imam artham = for this
sake; bhavaan = you; abhiprachoditaH = were instigated; vyavasyati chat
= If you desire; kim = any; aparam = other thing; vR^iNiishhva = opt for
(it).
verses.translation:
"I only desired to know your strength, which is unfathomable and hence this trial
for you. If you desire any other thing, opt for it."

verses.sanskrit:
ब्रवीमि सत्येन न तेऽस्ति यन्त्रणा |

धनम् हि यद्यन्मम विप्रकारणात् |

भवत्सु सम्यक्र्पतिपादनेन त |

न्मयार्जितम् प्रीतियश्स्करम् भवेत् || २-३२-४१
verses.translation:
" I am telling the truth here. There is no limitation for you. Whatever riches
belonging to me, are indeed for brahmanas. If the wealth earned by me is given abundantly to you,
it will bring in joy and fame."

verses.sanskrit:
तत स्सभार्य स्त्रिजटो महामुनि |

र्गवामनीकम् प्रतिगृह्य मोदितः |

यशोबलप्रीतिसुखोपबृम्हणी |

स्तदाशिषः प्रत्यवदन्महात्मनः || २-३२-४२

स चापि रामः प्रतिपूर्णमानसो |

महद्धनम् धर्मबलैरुपार्जितम् |

नियोजयामास सुहृज्जनेऽचिरा |

द्यथार्हसम्मानवचःप्रचोदितः || २-३२-४३
verses.wordMeaning:
43. saH = that; raamaHchaapi = Rama also; pratipuurNa
maanasaH = having his heart satisfies; yathaarha sammaana vachaH prachoditaH = being
inspired by suitable words of honour; achiraat = immediately; niyogayaamaasa =
bestwed on; suhR^ijjane = the friendly people; mahat dhanam = a great wealth;
upaarjitam = earned; dharmabalaiH = by righteous might.

verses.sanskrit:
द्विजः सुहृद्भृत्यजनोऽथवा तदा |

दरिद्रभिक्षाचरणश्च योऽभवत् |

न तत्र कश्चिन्न बभूव तर्पितो |

यथार्ह सम्मानन दान सम्ब्रमैः || २-३२-४४
verses.wordMeaning:
44. tadaa = at that time; yaH = whoever of; dvijaH
= the brahmana; suhR^it = the relative; athavaa = or; bhR^ityujanaH =
the dependent; daridrabhikshhaacharaNashcha = or the panper or the mendicant;
tatra = there; nababhuuva = not; kashchit = anyone of them; na
tarpitaH = was not satiated; yathaarha sammaananadaana sambhramaiH = with befittingly
desrving honour; by gifts; by respect.

sargas.title: Sarga 33
sargas.introduction:
Rama while going to his father at Queen Kaikeyi's palace listens the people's voice
of sorrow for his exile. They sadly express their heartfelt feelings. On reaching the palace, Rama
bids Sumantra to inform Dasharatha about his arrival for paying respects at the time of
departure.

verses.sanskrit:
दत्त्वा तु सह वैदेह्या ब्राह्मणेभ्यो धनम् बहु |

जग्मतुः पितरम् द्रष्टुम् सीतया सह राघवौ || २-३३-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. raaghava = Rama and Lakshmana; vaidehyaa sha = along with
Seetha; datvaa = gave away; bahu = abundant; dhanam = wealth;
braahmanebhyaH = to brahmans; jagmatuH = went; siitayaa saha = along
with Seetha; drashhTum = to see; pitaram = their father.

verses.sanskrit:
ततो गृहीते दुष्प्रेक्ष्येअशोभेताम् तदा आयुधे |

माला दामभिर् आसक्ते सीतया समलम्कृते || २-३३-२

ततः प्रासाद हर्म्याणि विमान शिखराणि च |

अधिरुह्य जनः श्रीमान् उदासीनो व्यलोकयत् || २-३३-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. tataH = then; shriimaan = wealthy; janaH =
people; adhiruhya = ascended; praasaada harmyaaNi = lofty palatial mansions and
large buildings; vimaana sikharaaNicha = and top of palaces of seven stories high;
vyalokayat = and saw them; udaasiinaH = spiritlessly.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि रथ्याः स्म शक्यन्ते गन्तुम् बहु जन आकुलाः |

आरुह्य तस्मात् प्रासादान् दीनाः पश्यन्ति राघवम् || २-३३-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. rathyaaH = the streets; na shakyante hi = were indeed
difficult; gantum = to walk; bahujanaakulaaH = congested as they were; with many
men; tasmaat = for that reason; diiraaH = being distressed; pashyanti =
they were seeing; raaghavam = Rama; aaruhya = by ascending; praasaadaan
= lofty palatial buildings.

verses.sanskrit:
पदातिम् वर्जितच् चत्रम् रामम् दृष्ट्वा तदा जनाः |

ऊचुर् बहु विधा वाचः शोक उपहत चेतसः || २-३३-५

यम् यान्तम् अनुयाति स्म चतुर् अन्ग बलम् महत् |

तम् एकम् सीतया सार्धम् अनुयाति स्म लक्ष्मणः || २-३३-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. yaantam = marching; yam = along whom; mahat = a
strong; chaturaNga balam = four wings of army(comprising elephants; chariots; cavalry and
infantry); anuyaati sma = sued to follow; tam = to such of him; ekam =
who is lonely; anuyaati sma = is following; lakshhmaNaH = Lakshmana; siitayaa
saartham = along with Seetha.

verses.sanskrit:
ऐश्वर्यस्य रसज्ञः सन् कामिनाम् चैव कामदः |

न इच्चति एव अनृतम् कर्तुम् पितरम् धर्म गौरवात् || २-३३-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. rasajJNaH san = though knowing the taste; aishvaryasya =
of sovernignity; kaamadaHchaiva = though granting desires; kaaminaam = to the
desirous; nechchhatyeva = Rama is not willing; kartum = to do; vachanam- his
father's words; anR^itam = untrue; dharmagauravaat = because of his respect for
virtue.

verses.sanskrit:
या न शक्या पुरा द्रष्टुम् भूतैः आकाशगैः अपि |

ताम् अद्य सीताम् पश्यन्ति राज मार्ग गता जनाः || २-३३-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. puraa = earlier; yaa = which Seetha; bhuutairapi
= even beings; aakashagaiH = going through the air; na shakyaa = could not;
drashhTum = see; adya = today; taam siitaam = such Seetha janaaH =
people; maarga gataaH = walking on road; pashyanti = are seeing.

verses.sanskrit:
अङ्ग राग उचिताम् सीताम् रक्त चन्दन सेविनीम् |

वर्षम् उष्णम् च शीतम् च नेष्यति आशु विवर्णताम् || २-३३-९

अद्य नूनम् दशरथः सत्त्वम् आविश्य भाषते |

न हि राजा प्रियम् पुत्रम् विवासयितुम् अर्हति || २-३३-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. adya = today; dasharathaH = Dasaratha; nuunam =
certainy; bhaashhate = is talking; aavishya = by taking possession of sattvam =
some evil spirit; raajaa = the king; na arhati hi vivaasayitum = to send
to exile; priyam = his affectionate; putram = son.

verses.sanskrit:
निर्गुणस्य अपि पुत्रस्या काथम् स्यात् विप्रवासनम् |

किम् पुनर् यस्य लोको अयम् जितः वृत्तेन केवलम् || २-३३-११

आनृशंस्यम् अनुक्रोशः श्रुतम् शीलम् दमः शमः |

राघवम् शोभयन्ति एते षड् गुणाः पुरुष उत्तमम् || २-३३-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. ete = these; shhaT = six; guNaaH = virtues;
anR^ishamsyam = harmlessness; anukroshaH = compassion; shrutam =
learning; shiilam = good nature; damaH = self-control; shamaH =
tranquility; shobhayanti = adorn; raaghavam = Rama; purushhottamam = the
best of men.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मात् तस्य उपघातेन प्रजाः परम पीडिताः |

औदकानि इव सत्त्वानि ग्रीष्मे सलिल सम्क्षयात् || २-३३-१३

पीडया पीडितम् सर्वम् जगद् अस्य जगत् पतेः |

मूलस्य इव उपघातेन वृक्षः पुष्प फल उपगः || २-३३-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. sarvam = the entire; jagat = world; piiditam =
is afflicted; piidayaa = by the hardship; asya = of Rama; jagatpate =
the protector of the world; vR^ikshhaH iva = as a tree; pushhpa phalopagaH =
which acquired flowers and fruits; upaghaatena = is damaged; muulasya = in
root.

verses.sanskrit:
मूलं ह्येष मनुष्याणाम् धर्मसारो महाद्युतिः |

पुष्पम् फलम् च पत्रम् च शाखाश्चा स्येतरे जनाः || २-३३-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. eshhaH = this Rama; dharma saaraH = the essence of
virtue; mahaadyutiH = having great lustre; muulam = is indeed the origin;
manushhyaaNaam = of men; itare = other; janaaH = men; asya =
are his; pushhpam = flower; phalamcha = fruit; shaakhaashcha = and
branches.

verses.sanskrit:
ते लक्ष्मणैव क्षिप्रम् सपत्न्यः सह बान्धवाः |

गच्चन्तम् अनुगच्चामः येन गच्चति राघवः || २-३३-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. te = we; as such; sapatnyaH = along with our wives;
sahabaandhavaaH = and relatives; kshhipram = at once; anugachchhaama =
will follow; yena = in which path; raaghava = Rama; gachchhati = is
going; lakshhmaNa iva = as Lakshman; gachchhantam = the departing Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
उद्यानानि परित्यज्य क्षेत्राणि च गृहाणि च |

एक दुह्ख सुखा रामम् अनुगच्चाम धार्मिकम् || २-३३-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. parityajya = abandoning; udyaanaani = gardens;
kshhetraani = fields; gR^ihaaNicha = and houses; anugachchhaama = we
shall follow; dhaarmikam = the righteous; raamam Rama; eka duHkhasukhaaH = owning
the same sorrows and joys.

verses.sanskrit:
समुद्धृत निधानानि परिध्वस्त अजिराणि च |

उपात्त धन धान्यानि हृत साराणि सर्वशः || २-३३-१८

रजसा अभ्यवकीर्णानि परित्यक्तानि दैवतैः |

मूषकैः परिधावद्भिरुद्बिलैरावृतानि च || २-३३-१९

अपेतोदकधूमानि हीनसम्मार्जनानि च |

प्रनष्टबलिकर्मेज्यमन्त्रहोमजपानि च || २-३३-२०

दुष्कालेनेव भग्नानि भिभाजनवन्ति च |

अस्मत् त्यक्तानि वेश्मानि कैकेयी प्रतिपद्यताम् || २-३३-२१

वनम् नगरम् एव अस्तु येन गच्चति राघवः |

अस्माभिः च परित्यक्तम् पुरम् सम्पद्यताम् वनम् || २-३३-२२

बिलानि दम्ष्ट्रिणः सर्वे सानूनि मृग पक्षिणः |

अस्मत् त्यक्तम् प्रपद्यन्ताम् सेव्यमानम् त्यजन्तु च || २-३३-२३

इति एवम् विविधा वाचो नाना जन समीरिताः |

तृणमांसफलादानाम् देशम् व्यालमृगद्विजम् || २-३३-२४

प्रपद्यताम् हि कैकेयी सपुत्रा सहबान्धवैः |

राघावेण वने सर्वे सह वत्स्याम निर्वृताः || २-३३-२५

इत्येवम् विविधा वाचो नानाजनसमीरिताः|

शुश्राव रामः श्रुत्वा च न विचक्रे अस्य मानसम् || २-३३-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. raamaH = Rama; shushraava = heard; vividhaaH =
various kinds; vaachaH = of words; ityevam = thus; naanaajane
samiiritaaH = spoken by many people; shrutvaacha = hearing them also; asya =
his; maanasam = mind; na vichakre = was not distruebed.

verses.sanskrit:
स तु वेश्म पितुर्दूरा त्कैलासशिखरप्रभम् |

अभिचक्राम धर्मात्मा मत्तमातङ्गविक्रमः || २-३३-२७
verses.wordMeaning:
27. saH = that Rama; dharmaatmaa = the virtuous man;
maatta maataNga vikramaH = with strides of an elephant in rut; abhichakraama =
approached; pituH = his father's; veshma = palace; kailaasa shikhara
prabham = which glowed like a peak of the Kailasa mountain; kuuraat = from a
distance.

verses.sanskrit:
विनीतवीरपुरुषं प्रविश्य तु नृपालयम् |

ददर्शवस्थितम् दीनम् सुमन्त्रमविदूरतः || २-३३-२८

प्रतीक्षमाणो अभिजनम् तदा आर्तम् |

अनार्त रूपः प्रहसन्न् इव अथ|

जगाम रामः पितरम् दिदृक्षुः |

पितुर् निदेशम् विधिवच् चिकीर्षुः || २-३३-२९

तत् पूर्वम् ऐक्ष्वाक सुतः महात्मा |

रामः गमिष्यन् वनम् आर्त रूपम् |

व्यतिष्ठत प्रेक्ष्य तदा सुमन्त्रम् |

पितुर् महात्मा प्रतिहारण अर्थम् || २-३३-३०

पितुर्निदेशेन तु धर्मवत्सलो |

वन प्रवेशे कृत बुद्धि निश्चयः |

स राघवः प्रेक्ष्य सुमन्त्रम् अब्रवीन् |

निवेदयस्व आगमनम् नृपाय मे || २-३३-३१

sargas.title: Sarga 34
sargas.introduction:
Rama comes to see his father Dasharatha and Dasharatha summons Sumantra to fetch all
his wives to hither, and asks Rama to stay with him at least for a night. But Rama resolved in his
vow, persuades his father to be peaceful. Dasharatha's anguish intensifies and he swoons at the
departure of Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
ततःकमलपत्राक्षः श्यामो निरुपमो महान् |

उवाच रामस्तम् सूतं पितुराख्याहि मामिति || २-३४-१

स राम प्रेषितः क्षिप्रम् सम्ताप कलुष इन्द्रियः |

प्रविश्य नृपतिम् सूतः निह्श्वसन्तम् ददर्श ह || २-३४-२

उपरक्तमिवादित्यं भस्मच्छन्नमिवानलम्|

तटाकमिव निस्तोयमपश्यज्जगतीपतिम्|| २-३४-३

आलोक्य तु महा प्राज्ञः परम आकुल चेतसम् |

रामम् एव अनुशोचन्तम् सूतः प्रान्जलिर् आसदत् || २-३४-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. suutaH = the charioteer; mahaapraaJNaH = a great
intellectual; aalokyatu = saw; anushochantam = the repenting; varamaakula
chetasam = mind greatly agitated; aasadat = approached; praaN^jalaiH = with
joined pams.

verses.sanskrit:
तम् वर्धयित्वा राजानम् सूतः पूर्वम् जयाशिषा|

भयविक्लबया वाचा मन्दया श्लक्ष्णमब्रवीत् || २-३४-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. suutaH = the charioteer; puurvam = first;
vardhayitvaa = inspiring; tam raajaanam = the king; jayaashishhaa = with
blessings of triumph; abraviit = spoke; mandayaa = in slow; shlakshhNam
= and sweet; vachaa = words; bhaya viklabayaa = overcome with fear.

verses.sanskrit:
अयम् स पुरुष व्याघ्र द्वारि तिष्ठति ते सुतः |

ब्राह्मणेभ्यो धनम् दत्त्वा सर्वम् चैव उपजीविनाम् || २-३४-६
verses.translation:
"Your son, the tiger among men, having given away all his wealth to brahmanas and
dependents, is waiting at the gate."

verses.sanskrit:
स त्वा पश्यतु भद्रम् ते रामः सत्य पराक्रमः |

सर्वान् सुहृदाअपृच्च्य त्वाम् इदानीम् दिदृक्षते || २-३४-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. bhadram = auspiciousness; te = to you! saH raamaH = (let)
that Rama; satya paraakramaH = who is truly brave; pashyatu = see; tvaa
= you; aapR^ichchhya = after bidding farewell; sarvam suhR^idaH = to all friends;
didR^ikshhate = (he) wants to see; tvaam = you; idaaniim = now.
verses.translation:
"May auspiciousness betide you! Let that Rama, who is unfailingly brave, see you.
After bidding farewell to all friends, he wants to see you now."

verses.sanskrit:
गमिष्यति महा अरण्यम् तम् पश्य जगती पते |

वृतम् राज गुणैः सर्वैः आदित्यम् इव रश्मिभिः || २-३४-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. jagatiipate = Oh; king! gamishhyati = (He) is seeting
out; mahaaraNyam = to a great forest; pashya = see; tam = that Rama;
aadityamiva = who is like sun; rashmibhiH = with rays of light; vR^itam
= displaying; raajaguNaiH = royal qualities.
verses.translation:
"Oh, king! Rama is setting out to a great forest. See him who is resembling a sun
with rays of light, displaying royal qualities."

verses.sanskrit:
स सत्य वादी धर्म आत्मा गाम्भीर्यात् सागर उपमः |

आकाशैव निष्पन्को नर इन्द्रः प्रत्युवाच तम् || २-३४-९

सुमन्त्र आनय मे दारान् ये केचित् इह मामकाः |

दारैः परिवृतः सर्वैः द्रष्टुम् इच्चामि राघवम् || २-३४-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. sumantra = Oh; Sumantra! ye kachit = whoever;
maamakaaH = my wives; iha = are here; anaya = bring; me daaraan
= such of my wives; parivR^itaH = surrounded by; sarvaiH = all; daaraiH
= wives; ichchhaami = I want; drashhTum = to see; dhaarmikam = the
virtuous Rama.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Sumantra! being all my wives, who are here. Surrounded by all of them, I want
to see the virtuous Rama."

verses.sanskrit:
सो अन्तः पुरम् अतीत्य एव स्त्रियः ता वाक्यम् अब्रवीत् |

आर्यो ह्वयति वो राजा गम्यताम् तत्र माचिरम् || २-३४-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. saH = He; atiityaiva = after entering; antaH
puram = the gynaecium; abraviit = spoke; vaakyam = (these) words; taaH
striyaH = to those women; aaryaaH = "Oh; the venerable ladies! raajaa = the
king; hvayati = is calling; vaH = you; gamyataam = go; tatra =
there; maa chiram = without delay.

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्ताः स्त्रियः सर्वाः सुमन्त्रेण नृप आज्ञया |

प्रचक्रमुस् तत् भवनम् भर्तुर् आज्ञाय शासनम् || २-३४-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. sarvaaH = all; stiyaH = women; uktaaH = spoken;
evam = thus; sumantreNa = by Sumantra; nR^ipaaJNayaa = as per king's
orders; prachakramaH = moved; tadbhavanam = to his house; aaJNaaya =
after knowing; shaasanam = the instruction; bhartuH = of their husband.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्ध सप्त शताः ताः तु प्रमदाः ताम्र लोचनाः |

कौसल्याम् परिवार्य अथ शनैः जग्मुर् धृत व्रताः || २-३४-१३

आगतेषु च दारेषु समवेक्ष्य मही पतिः |

उवाच राजा तम् सूतम् सुमन्त्र आनय मे सुतम् || २-३४-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. aagateshhu = after arrival;daareshhu = of wives; raajaa
= Dasaratha; mahiipatih = the lord of the earth; samavekshhya = looked; tam
suutam = towards that chariorteer; uvaacha = and spoke(thus); sumantra = Oh;
Sumantra! aanaya = bring; me sutam = my son.

verses.sanskrit:
स सूतः रामम् आदाय लक्ष्मणम् मैथिलीम् तदा |

जगाम अभिमुखः तूर्णम् सकाशम् जगती पतेः || २-३४-१५

स राजा पुत्रम् आयान्तम् दृष्ट्वा दूरात् कृत अन्जलिम् |

उत्पपात आसनात् तूर्णम् आर्तः स्त्री जन सम्वृतः || २-३४-१६

सो अभिदुद्राव वेगेन रामम् दृष्ट्वा विशाम् पतिः |

तम् असम्प्राप्य दुह्ख आर्तः पपात भुवि मूर्चितः || २-३४-१७

तम् रामः अभ्यपातत् क्षिप्रम् लक्ष्मणः च महा रथः |

विसम्ज्ञम् इव दुह्खेन सशोकम् नृपतिम् तदा || २-३४-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. tadaa = tehn; raamaH = Rama; lakshhmaNashche =
and Lakshmana; mahaarathaH = an eminent car-warrior; kshhipram = quickly;
abhyapatat = approached; tam nR^ipatim = that king; visamJNam iva = who
seemed senseless; duHkhena = with grief; sashokam = through agony.

verses.sanskrit:
स्त्री सहस्र निनादः च सम्जज्ञे राज वेश्मनि |

हाहा राम इति सहसा भूषण ध्वनि मूर्चितः || २-३४-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. samjaJNe = generated; sahasaa = all at once;
raajaveshmani = from the royal palace; striisahasraninaadashcha = sound of
thousands of women crying; haa haa raama iti = "Alas! Alas! Oh Rama !" bhuushhaNa
dhvanimuurchhitaH = mixed with the sound of tinkling of ornaments.

verses.sanskrit:
तम् परिष्वज्य बाहुभ्याम् ताव् उभौ राम लक्ष्मणौ |

पर्यन्के सीतया सार्धम् रुदन्तः समवेशयन् || २-३४-२०

अथ रामः मुहूर्तेन लब्ध सम्ज्ञम् मही पतिम् |

उवाच प्रान्जलिर् भूत्वा शोक अर्णव परिप्लुतम् || २-३४-२१
verses.wordMeaning:
21. atha = thereafter; raamaH = Rama; bhuutvaa =
becoming; praNjaliH = one with folded hands; uvaacha = spoke(as follows)
mahiipatim = to king Dasaratha; labdha samJNam = who got consciousness;
muhuurtena = within a short time; shokaarNava pariplutam = imundated in a sea of
sorrow.

verses.sanskrit:
आपृच्चे त्वाम् महा राज सर्वेषाम् ईश्वरः असि नः |

प्रस्थितम् दण्डक अरण्यम् पश्य त्वम् कुशलेन माम् || २-३४-२२
verses.translation:
"Oh, emperor! I take leave of you, who are the lord of all of us. See auspiciously
towards me, who am about to leave for Dandaka forest."

verses.sanskrit:
लक्ष्मणम् च अनुजानीहि सीता च अन्वेति माम् वनम् |

कारणैः बहुभिस् तथ्यैः वार्यमाणौ न च इच्चतः || २-३४-२३
verses.translation:
"Permit Lakshmana also and Seetha too who is accompanying me to the forest. Even if
prevented(by me0 on many true reasons, these two are not agreeing to stay behind"

verses.sanskrit:
अनुजानीहि सर्वान् नः शोकम् उत्सृज्य मानद |

लक्ष्मणम् माम् च सीताम् च प्रजापतिर् इव प्रजाः || २-३४-२४
verses.translation:
"Giving up grief, Oh the bestower of honour, grant leave to all of us, Lakshmana
myself and Seetha as Brahma(the lord of creation) did to his sons(sanaka and his three brothers who
intended to go to forest for practising austerities)."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रतीक्षमाणम् अव्यग्रम् अनुज्ञाम् जगती पतेः |

उवाच रर्जा सम्प्रेक्ष्य वन वासाय राघवम् || २-३४-२५

अहम् राघव कैकेय्या वर दानेन मोहितः |

अयोध्यायाः त्वम् एव अद्य भव राजा निगृह्य माम् || २-३४-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. raaghava = Oh; Rama! aham = I; mohitaH = was
stupefied; kaikeyyaa = by Kaikeyi; varadaanena = through a boon; adya =
now; nigR^ihya = by confirning; maam = me; tvameva = you alone;
bhava = be; raajaa = the king; ayodhyaayaaH = of Ayodhya.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! I was stupefied by Kaikeyi through a boon. Now, by confining me, be you
the king of Ayodhya."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्तः नृपतिना रामः धर्मभृताम् वरः |

प्रत्युवाच अन्जलिम् कृत्वा पितरम् वाक्य कोविदः || २-३४-२७

भवान् वर्ष सहस्राय पृथिव्या नृपते पतिः |

अहम् तु अरण्ये वत्स्यामि न मे कार्यम् त्वया अनृतम् || २-३४-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. nR^ipate = Oh; King! bhavaan = you be; patiH =
the lord; pR^ithivyaaH = of the earth; varshhasahasraaya = for thousand years;
aham tu = but I; vatsyaami = shall stay arNye = in the forest; me = for
me; na kaaryam = do not make; anR^itam = untruth; tvayaa = of you.
verses.translation:
"Oh, king! You be the ruler of the earth for thousand years. But, I for my part,
will stay in the forest. For my sake, do not generate untruth about you."

verses.sanskrit:
नव पञ्च च वर्षाणि वनवासे विहृत्य ते |

पुनः पादौ ग्रहीष्यामि प्रतिज्ञान्ते नराधिपः || २-३४-२९
verses.translation:
"Oh, ruler of men! Having strolled in the forest for fourteen years, I shall clasp
your feet once more after fulfilling my promise."

verses.sanskrit:
रुदन्नाह प्रियम् पुत्रं सत्यपाशेन संयतः |

कैकेय्या चोद्यमान्स्तु मिथो राजा तमब्रवीत् || २-३४-३०

श्रेयसे वृद्धये तात पुनर् आगमनाय च |

गच्चस्व अरिष्टम् अव्यग्रः पन्थानम् अकुतः भयम् || २-३४-३१
verses.translation:
"Oh, darling! Proceed for welfare, for advancement and for coming again. Let your
path of travel be auspicious, undistracted and fearless from any quarter."

verses.sanskrit:
न हि सत्यात्मनस्तात धर्माभिमनसस्तव |

विनिवर्त यितुं बुद्धि शक्यते रघुनन्दन || २-३४-३२
verses.wordMeaning:
32. taata = Oh; darling! raghunandana = Oh; Rama; the
seion of Raghu! satyaatmanaH = your mind being given to righteousness; tava = your;
buddhiH = decision; na shakya hi = cannot be; vinivartayitum =
reversed.
verses.translation:
"Oh, darling, the seion of Raghu! Truthful as you are by nature and your mind being
given to righteousness, your decision cannot be reversed."

verses.sanskrit:
अद्य तु इदानीम् रजनीम् पुत्र मा गच्च सर्वथा |

मातरम् माम् च सम्पश्यन् वस इमाम् अद्य शर्वरीम् || २-३४-३३
verses.translation:
"Oh, son! Do not leave in any case today, now at night time. Even by seeing you for
one day, I may live happily."

verses.sanskrit:
मातरं माम् च सम्पश्यन् वसेमामद्य शर्वरीम् |

तर्पितः सर्वकामैस्त्वम् स्वः काले साधयिष्यसि || २-३४-३४
verses.translation:
"Looking at your mother and myself, stay at least for this night today. Satiated by
all the desired objects, you may leave tomorrow at dawn."

verses.sanskrit:
दुष्करम् क्रियते पुत्र सर्वथा राघव तया |

मत्प्रियार्थम् प्रियांस्त्यक्त्वा यद्यासि विजनम् वनम् || २-३४-३५
verses.translation:
"Oh Rama, my son! An incorrigible thing is being done by you since for my pleasure
you are going to the forest a solitary place, leaving beloved ones."

verses.sanskrit:
न चैतन्मे प्रियम् पुत्र शपे सत्येन राघव |

छन्नया छलितस्त्वस्नु स्त्रुया छन्नाग्निकल्पया || २-३४-३६
verses.translation:
"Oh Rama my son! Your exile is not agreeable to me. I was cheated by Kaikeyi who had
concealed intentions and resembled fire with ashes."

verses.sanskrit:
पञ्चना या तु लब्धा मे तां त्वम् निस्तर्तुमिच्छसि |

अनया वृत्तसादिन्या कैकेय्याऽभिप्रचोदितः || २-३४-३७

न चैतदाश्चर्यतमम् यत्तज्ज्येष्ठस्सुतो मम |

अपानृतकथम् पुत्र पितरम् कर्तुमिच्छ्सि || २-३४-३८
verses.translation:
"It is not a great surprise, my son, that you my eldest son should seek to make your
father as one who does not have false promises."

verses.sanskrit:
अथ रामः तथा श्रुत्वा पितुर् आर्तस्य भाषितम् |

लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा दीनो वचनम् अब्रवीत् || २-३४-३९

प्राप्स्यामि यान् अद्य गुणान् को मे श्वस्तान् प्रदास्यति |

अपक्रमणम् एव अतः सर्व कामैः अहम् वृणे || २-३४-४०
verses.wordMeaning:
40. yaan = which; guNaam = wordly pleasures praapsyaami = I
can get; adya = today; taan = those; kaH = who; pradaasyati =
will offer; me = to me; shvaH = tomorrow? ataH = therfore; aham = I;
vR^iNe = desire; apakramaNameva = only to get away; sarvaka = in lien of
all enjoyments.

verses.sanskrit:
इयम् सराष्ट्रा सजना धन धान्य समाकुला |

मया विसृष्टा वसुधा भरताय प्रदीयताम् || २-३४-४१
verses.wordMeaning:
41. iyam = vasudhaa = this earth; visR^ishhTaa = being
relinquished; mayaa = by me; sa raashhTraa = which includes various provinces;
sajanaa = filled with people; dhana dhaanya samaakulaa = inbued with riches and
foodgrains; pradiiyataam = may be given; bharataaya = to Bharata.
verses.translation:
"This earth being relinquished by me; which included various provinces, filled with
people, imbued with riches and food grains, may be given to Bharata."

verses.sanskrit:
वनवासकृता बुद्धिर्न च मेऽद्य चलिष्यति |

यस्तुष्टेन वरो दत्तः कैकेय्यै वरद त्वया || २-३४-४२

दीयताम् निखिलेनैव सत्यस्त्वम् भव पार्थिव |
verses.translation:
"I cannot budge now from my decision made about my stay in the forest. Oh king the
bestower of boons! You were pleased to give a boon to Kaikeyi and let it be fulfilled completely.
Be you a truthful man."

verses.sanskrit:
अहम् निदेशम् भवतो यथोक्तमनुपालयन् || २-३४-४३

चतुर्दश समा वत्स्ये वने वनचरैः सह |
verses.translation:
"I , as promised, fulfilling your command, shall live in the forest along with
forest-dwellers for fourteen years."

verses.sanskrit:
मा विमर्शो वसुमती भरताय प्रदीयताम् || २-३४-४४

न हि मे काम्क्षितम् राज्यम् सुखमात्मनि वा प्रियम् |

यथा निदेशम् कर्तुम् वै तवैव रघुनन्धन || २-३४-४५
verses.translation:
"Oh, king! Do not have any hesitation in offering the kingdom to Bharata. Neither
kingdom nor happiness indeed is desired by my nature. It is dear to me only to do your command as
directed."

verses.sanskrit:
अपगच्चतु ते दुह्खम् मा भूर् बाष्प परिप्लुतः |

न हि क्षुभ्यति दुर्धर्षः समुद्रः सरिताम् पतिः || २-३४-४६
verses.wordMeaning:
46. te duHkham apagachchhatu = let your sorrow disappear! maa bhuuH =
do not be; bhaashhpa pariplutaH = overwheled with tears; samudraH = the ocean;
patiH = the lord; saritaam = of rivers; durdharshhaH = which is
difficult to assail; na kshhubhyatihi = is not indeed agitated.
verses.translation:
"Let your sorrow disappear! Do not be overwhelmed with tears. The ocean, the lord of
rivers which is difficult to assail, indeed does not get agitated."

verses.sanskrit:
न एव अहम् राज्यम् इच्चामि न सुखम् न च मैथिलीम् |

त्वाम् अहम् सत्यम् इच्चामि न अनृतम् पुरुष ऋषभ || २-३४-४७
verses.translation:
"I do not crave for kingdom or happiness nor again for Seetha nor even all these
enjoyments nor for heaven nor even for life."

verses.sanskrit:
त्वामहम् सत्यमिच्छामि नानृतम् पुरुषर्षभ |

प्रत्यक्षम् तव सत्येन सुकृतेन च ते शपे || २-३४-४८
verses.translation:
"Oh, Jewel among men! I wish you to be a truthful mean, not a fallacious man. I
swear to you in your presence by truth and by virtue."

verses.sanskrit:
न च शख्यम् मया तात स्थातुम् क्षणमपि प्रभो |

स शोकम् ध्रारयस्वेमम् न हि मेऽस्ति विपर्ययः || २-३४-४९
verses.wordMeaning:
49. taata = Oh; father! prabho = Oh; lord! mayaa =
by me; nashakyam = it is not possible; sthaatum = to stay; khhaNamapi =
even for a moment; saH = you as such; dhaarayasva = should contian; evam
= this; shokam = grief; naastihi = there is indeed no; viparyayaH =
going back; me = for me.
verses.translation:
"It is not possible for me, Oh father, to stay on even for a moment. Therefore,
contain this grief, Oh Lord, for there is no going back upon my word."

verses.sanskrit:
अर्थितो ह्यस्मि कैकेय्या वनम् गच्छेति राघव |

मया चोक्तं प्रजामीति तत्सत्यमनुपालये || २-३४-५०
verses.wordMeaning:
50. raaghava = Oh; Dasaratha the seion of Raghu! asmihi = I
was indeed; arthitaH = asked; kaikeyyaa = by Kaikeyi; iti = as follows:
gachchha = go; vanam = to the forest." uktamcha = It was also replied; mayaa = by
me; prajaami iti = that I would go; anupaalaye = I shall redeem; tat =
that; satyam = pledge.
verses.translation:
"Oh Dasaratha the seion of Raghu! I was indeed asked by Kaikeyi to go to the forest.
It was also replied by me that I would go. I shall redeem that pledge."

verses.sanskrit:
मा चोत्कण्थां कृथा देव वने रंस्यामहे वयम् |

प्रशान्तहरिणाकीर्णे नानाशकुनिनादिते || २-३४-५१
verses.translation:
"Oh, king! Do not feel anxious. We shall sport in the forest, flocked with peaceful
deer and rendered noisy by birds of various types."

verses.sanskrit:
पिता हि दैवतम् तात देवतानामपि स्मृतम् |

तस्माद्दैवतमित्येव करिष्यामि पितुर्वचः || २-३४-५२
verses.wordMeaning:
52. tat = Oh; father! smR^itamhi = it was said indeed;
pitaa = father; daivatam = is the god; devataanaamapi = even for
celestials; tasmaat = therefore; karishhyaami = I shall do; pituH =
father's; vachaH = word; daivatam ityeva = considering it s divine.
verses.translation:
"Oh, father! It was indeed said that even for celestials, father is the god.
Therefore, I shall carry out the father's word, considering it as divine."

verses.sanskrit:
चतुर्धशसु वर्षेषु गतेषु नरसत्तम |

पुनर्द्रक्ष्यसि माम् प्राप्तम् सन्तापोऽयम् विमुच्यताम् || २-३४-५३
verses.wordMeaning:
53. narasattama = Oh; best among men! praaptam = coming;
gateshhu = after passing away; chaturdasha = fourteen; varshheshhu =
years; drakshhyasi = you will see; maam = me; ayam samtaapaH
vimuchyataam = let this grief be given up.
verses.translation:
"Oh father, the best among men! You will see me, when I come back after elapsing
fourteen years. Let this grief be given up."

verses.sanskrit:
येन संस्तम्भनीयोऽयम् सर्वो बाष्पगळो जनः |

स त्वम् पुरुषशार्दूल किमर्थम् विक्रियाम् गतः || २-३४-५४
verses.translation:
"Oh, tiger among men! Why have you got perturbed-you by whom all these people bathed
in tears, ought to be consoled?"

verses.sanskrit:
पुरम् च राष्ट्रम् च मही च केवला |

मया निसृष्टा भरताय दीयताम् |

अहम् निदेशम् भवतः अनुपालयन् |

वनम् गमिष्यामि चिराय सेवितुम् || २-३४-५५
verses.translation:
"Let this city, province and whole of this earth left by me, be given to Bharata. I
for one following your instructions, shall proceed to the forest to dwell in it for long."

verses.sanskrit:
मया निसृष्टाम् भरतः महीम् इमाम् |

सशैल खण्डाम् सपुराम् सकाननाम् |

शिवाम् सुसीमाम् अनुशास्तु केवलम् |

त्वया यद् उक्तम् नृपते यथा अस्तु तत् || २-३४-५६
verses.translation:
"Oh, king! Let this earth with multitude of mountains, comprising of cities with
their gardens relinquished by me, be ruled over barely by Bharata, within the bounds of
righteousness. Let the word of honour given by you(to Kaikeyi) come true."

verses.sanskrit:
न मे तथा पार्थिव धीयते मनो |

महत्सु कामेषु न च आत्मनः प्रिये |

यथा निदेशे तव शिष्ट सम्मते |

व्यपैतु दुह्खम् तव मत् कृते अनघ || २-३४-५७
verses.translation:
"Oh, the faultless king! My mind is not directed on haughty physical enjoyments or
sensuous pleasures which is approved by the wise. Let your agony disappear, for my sake."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् अद्य न एव अनघ राज्यम् अव्ययम् |

न सर्व कामान् न सुखम् न मैथिलीम् |

न जीवितम् त्वाम् अनृतेन योजयन् |

वृणीय सत्यम् व्रतम् अस्तु ते तथा || २-३४-५८
verses.wordMeaning:
58. anagha = Oh the sinless king! tat = therefore; adya =
now; yojayan = connecting; tvaam = you; anR^itena = with untruth;
naiva vR^itena = I just cannot yearn; raajyam = for kingdom; na = nor
desirous; sarva kaamaan = of various enjoyment; na = nor; sukham = for
happiness; na = now; maithiliim = Seetha; na = nor; jiivitam =
life; te = (let) your; vratam = voe; tathaa = thus; astu =
become; satyam = true.

verses.sanskrit:
फलानि मूलानि च भक्षयन् वने |

गिरीमः च पश्यन् सरितः सरांसि च |

वनम् प्रविश्य एव विचित्र पादपम् |

सुखी भविष्यामि तव अस्तु निर्वृतिः || २-३४-५९
verses.translation:
"I shall be happy, entering the forest filled with wonderful trees, eating fruits
and tubers as well as seeing mountains rivers and lakes in the forest. Let there be satisfaction
for you.

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् स राजा व्यसनाभिपन्नः |

शोकेन दुःखेन च ताम्यमानः |

आलिङ्ग्य पुत्रम् सुविनष्टसम्ज्ञो |

मोहम् गतो नैव चिचेश्ट किंचित् || २-३४-६०
verses.wordMeaning:
60. aaliNgya = embracing; putra = his son; saH
raajaa = that king Dasaratha; evam = who had thus; vyasanaabhipannaH =
fallen on evil days; taamyamaanaH = was tormented; shokena = with grief;
duHkhena = and distress; mahomgataH = got fainted; suvinashhTa samJNaH =
fully losing consciousness; naiva chicheshhTa = not moving; kimchit = even a
little.

verses.sanskrit:
देव्यस्ततः सम्रुरुदुः समेता |

स्ताम् वर्जयित्वा नरदेवपत्नीम् |

रुदन् सुमन्त्रोऽपि जगाम मूर्छाम् |

हा हा कृतम् तत्र बभूव सर्वम् || २-३४-६१

sargas.title: Sarga 35
sargas.introduction:
Minister Sumantra requests and reproaches Queen Kaikeyi and asks her to take back
her impositions. Even the elderly minister and a scholar named Siddhartha tries to pacify Kaikeyi
by telling the legend of Sagara, the ancestor of Dasharatha, quoting the exile of his notorious son
Asamanja. But she is adamant to all holy advices, but wants Rama to go to forests.

verses.sanskrit:
ततो निर्धूय सहसा शिरो निःश्वस्व चासकृत् |

पाणौ पाणिम् विनिष्पिष्य दन्तान् कटकटाय्य च || २-३५-१

लोचने कोपसम्रक्ते वर्णम् पूर्वोचितम् जहत् |

कोपाभिभूतः सहसा सम्तापमशुभम् गतः || २-३५-२

मनः समीक्षमाणश्च सूतो दशरथस्य सः |

कम्पयन्निव कैकेय्या हृदयम् वाक्छरैश्शितैः || २-३५-३

वाक्यवज्रैरनुपमैर्निर्भिन्दन्निव चाशुगैः |

कैकेय्या सर्वमर्माणि सुमन्त्रः प्रत्यभाषत || २-३५-४

यश्यास्तव पतिस्त्यक्तोराजा दशरथः स्वयम् |

भर्ता सर्वस्य जगतः स्थावरस्य चरश्य च || २-३५-५

न ह्यकार्यतमम् किंचित् तव देवीह विद्यते |
verses.translation:
"There is nothing on earth, anything which is most disgraceful for you by whom your
husband king Dasaratha the supporter of the entire creation the unmoving and the moving, is
betrayed, Oh, Kaikeyi!"

verses.sanskrit:
पतिघ्नीम् त्वामहम् मन्ये कुलघ्नीमपि चान्ततः || २-३५-६

यन्म हेंद्रमिवाजय्यम् दुष्प्रकम्प्यमिवाचलम् |

महोदधिमिवाक्षोभ्यम् सन्तापयसि कर्मभिः || २-३५-७
verses.translation:
"King Dasaratha is unconquerable as great Indra, unshakable as mountain and
imperturbable as a great ocean. By tormenting him with your actions, you are considered by me as
murderess of your husband and ultimately the exterminator of your race too."

verses.sanskrit:
मावमंस्था दशरथम् भर्तारम् वरदम् पतिम् |

भर्तुरिच्छा हि नारीणाम्पुत्रकोट्या विशिष्यते || २-३५-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. maavamamsthaaH = do not despise; dasharatham = Dasaratha;
bhartaaram = the supporter; varadam = the bestower of boon; patim = and
your husband; naariiNaam = for women; bhartaH = husband's; ichchhaa =
will; vishishhyate = is superior; putrakotyaaH = to ten million sons.
verses.translation:
"Do not despise Dasaratha the supporter, the bestower of boons and your husband. For
women, will of the husband is superior to that of ten million sons."

verses.sanskrit:
यथावयो हि राज्यानि प्राप्नुवन्ति नृपक्षये |

इक्ष्वाकुकुलनाथेऽस्मिंस्तल्लोपयितुमिच्छसि || २-३५-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. nR^ipakshhaye = after the death of a king; raajyaani =
kingdoms; prapnuvantihi = are indeed obtained; yathaavayaH = as per the age(of
princes) ichchhasi = you want; lopayitum = to deprive; tat = it; asmin
ikshhvaakukulanaathe = in this lord Ikshvaku dynasty; Rama.
verses.translation:
"After death of a king, kingdoms are obtained as per the age of the princes. But,
you want to deprive this condition in the case of Rama the lord of Ikshvaku dynasty."

verses.sanskrit:
राजा भवतु ते पुत्रो भरतश्शास्तु मेदिनीम् |

वयम् तत्र गमिष्यामो रामो यत्र गमिष्यति || २-३५-१०
verses.translation:
"Let your son Bharata become the king and rule the earth. wherever Rama goes, we (on
our part) shall go there".

verses.sanskrit:
न हि ते विषये कश्चिद्ब्राह्मणो वस्तुमर्हति |

तादृशम् त्वममर्यादमद्य कर्म चिकीर्षसि || २-३५-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. te vishhaye = In your kingdom; kashchit braahmaNaH =
even any brahmana; na arhati hi = cannot indeed; vastum = dwell; tvam =
you; chikiirshhasi = want to do; taadR^isham = such; karma = act;
adya = now; amaryaadam = transgressing due limits.
verses.translation:
"Now any brahmana cannot indeed dwell in your kigdom as now you want to do such an
act, transgressing the due limits."

verses.sanskrit:
नूनम् सर्वे गमिष्यामो मार्गम् रामनिषेवितम् |

त्यक्ताया बान्धवैः सर्वैर्ब्राह्मणैः साधुभिः सदा || २-३५-१२

का प्रीती राज्यलाभेन तव देवि भविष्यति |

तादृशम् त्वममर्यादम् कर्म कर्तुम् चिकीर्षसि || २-३५-१३
verses.translation:
"Certainly, all of us will proceed in the path followed by Rama. Oh, queen Kaikeyi!
What pleasure will be derived through attainment of soverignity, when your relatives, all brahmanas
and pious souls desert you forever? Now, you want to do such an act of impropriety"

verses.sanskrit:
आश्चर्यमिव पश्यामि यस्यास्ते वृत्तमीदृशम् |

आचरन्त्या न विदृता सद्यो भवति मेदिनी || २-३५-१४
verses.translation:
"I am looking only with surprise, why the earth is not immediately rent asunder,
while you conduct yourself with such an act"

verses.sanskrit:
महाब्रह्मर्षिसृष्टा वा ज्वलन्तो भीमदर्शना |

धिग्वाग्दण्डणा न हिंसन्ति रामप्रव्राजने स्थिताम् || २-३५-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. dhikvaagdanNdaaH vaa = rods of reproaching words;
jvalantaH = which are burning with fire; bhiima darshanaaH = and fearful to look
at; mahaabrahmarshhi sR^ishhTaaH = uttered by great brahmana sages; na himsanti =
are not killing; sthitaam = you; who have decided; raamapravraajane = to send
Rama to exile.
verses.translation:
"It is also surprising me why rods of reproaching words fiery and fearful to look at
uttered by great brahmana sages are not killing you, who are bent on sending Rama to exile."

verses.sanskrit:
आम्रम् चित्वा कुठारेन निम्बम् परिचरेत्तु यः |

यश्चेनम् पयसा सिञ्चेन्नैवास्य मधुरो भवेत् || २-३५-१६
verses.translation:
"Who will nurture a neem tree, cutting down a mango tree with an axe? And a neem
tree will not turn sweet for him who nourishes it with milk."

verses.sanskrit:
अभिजात्यम् हि ते मन्ये यथा मातुस्तथैव च |

न हि निम्बात्स्रवेत्क्षौद्रम् लोके निगदितम् वचः || २-३५-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. manye = I think; abhijaatyam = your nature; by birth;
yathaa tathaivacha = is as exactly as; maatuH = a proverb; nigaditam =
is quoted; loke = in the world; kshhaudram = "honey; na sraveti hi =
does not ooze surely; nimbaat = from a neem tree."
verses.translation:
"I think, by birth your nature is as exactly as that of your mother. a proverb is
quoted in the world saying that honey does not ooze from a neem tree."

verses.sanskrit:
तव मातुरसद्ग्राहम् विद्मः पूर्वम् यथाश्रुतम् |

पितुस्ते वरदः कश्चिद्ददौ वरमनुत्तमम् || २-३५-१८

सर्वभूतरुतम् तस्मात्सम्जज्ञे वसुधाधिपः |

तेन तिर्यग्गतानाम् च भूतानाम् विदितम् वचः || २-३५-१९
verses.translation:
"We know, as heard of in the past, your mother's conduct of evil satisfaction.
Someone capable of giving boons, granted a great boon to your father. By that boon he could
identify the language of all created beings. The talk of those beings belonging to sub human
species could be known by him."

verses.sanskrit:
ततो जृम्भस्य शयने विरुताद्भूरिवर्चसा |

पितुस्ते विदितो भावः स तत्र बहुधाऽहसत् || २-३५-२०
verses.translation:
"From the sound of an ant called Jrimbha near his bed, the meaning of that sound was
deciphered by your exceedingly brilliant father and he laughed over it repeatedly."

verses.sanskrit:
तत्र ते जननी क्रुद्धा मृत्युपाशमभीप्सती |

हासम् ते नृपते सौम्य जिज्ञासामीति भाब्रवीत् || २-३५-२१

नृपश्चोवाच ताम् देवीम् देवि शंसामि ते यदि |

ततो मे मरणम् सद्यो भविष्यति न संशयः || २-३५-२२
verses.translation:
"The king replied to that queen as follows: "Oh, royal lady! If I tell you the
reason of my laughter, my death will immediately follow thereafter. There is no doubt about
it."

verses.sanskrit:
माता ते पितरम् देवि ततह् केकयमब्रवीत् |

शंस मे जीव वा मा वा न मामपहसिष्यसि || २-३५-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. devi = Oh Kaikeyi! tataH = then; te maataa = your
mother; abraviit = spoke; kekayam = Kekaya king; pituram = your
father(thus); jiiva vaa = either live; maavaa = or die; shamsa = tell;
me = me; na apahisishhasi = do not ridicule; maam = me.
verses.translation:
"Your mother then said to your father, the king of Kekayas, Live or die as you will.
tell me the laughter. Do not ridicule me."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रियया च तथोक्तः सन् केकयः पृथीवीपतिः |

तस्मै तम् वरदायार्थम् कथयामास तत्त्वतः || २-३५-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. uktaH san = having been spoken; tathaa = thus;
priyayaa = by his beloved consort; kekayaH prithvii patiH = the ruler of Kekaya
kingdom; kathayaamaasa = reported; tasmai = to that; varadaaya =
bestower of boon; tattvataH = correctly; tam artham = that matter.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः स वरदह् साधु राजानम् प्रत्यभाषत |

म्रियताम् ध्वंसताम् वेयम् मा कृथास्त्वम् महीपते || २-३५-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. tataH = then; saH saadhuH = that sage; varadaH
= who had bestowed the boon; pratyabhaashhataH = replied; rajaanam = to the
king(as follows): mahiipate = "Oh; king! iyam = let her; mriyataam = die;
dhamsataam vaa = or ruin; tvam = you; maakR^ithaaH = do not persue that
course.

verses.sanskrit:
स तच्छ्रुत्वा वचस्तस्य प्रसन्नमनसो नृपः |

मातरम् ते निरस्याशु विजहार कुबेरवत् || २-३५-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. shrutvaa = having heard; tat vachaH = the word;
tasya = of him; prasanna manasaH = who was pure-minded; saH nR^ipaH =
that king; nirasya = repudiated; maataram = your mother; vijahaara =
lived happily; kuberavat = like Kubera; the lord of riches.
verses.translation:
"Having heard the words of that sage who was pure-minded, the king repudiated your
mother and lived happily like Kubera the Lord of riches."

verses.sanskrit:
तथा त्वमपि राजानम् दुर्जनाचरिते पथि |

असद्ग्राहमिमम् मोहात्कुरुषे पापदर्शिनि || २-३५-२७
verses.wordMeaning:
27. paapadarshini = Oh; Kaikeyi; seeing evil(everywhere)
tvamapi = you too; tathaa = thus; pathi = in the path;
durjanaacharite = trodden by wicked men; kurushhe = are making; imam =
this; asadgraaham = bad resolution; mohaat = by igonorance.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Kaikeyi, seeing evil everywhere! You, too, thus following the path trodden by
wicked men, are making this bad resolution by ignorance."

verses.sanskrit:
सत्यश्चाद्य प्रवादोऽयम् लौकिकः प्रतिभाति मा |

पित्ऱ्^ऊन् समनुजायन्ते नरा मातरमङ्गनाः || २-३५-२८
verses.translation:
"The popular saying that males are born confirming to their fathers and females to
their mothers, occurs to me as true."

verses.sanskrit:
नैवम् भव ग़ृहाणेदम् यदाह वसुधाधिपः |

भर्तुरिच्चामुपास्वेह जनस्यास्य गतिर्भव || २-३५-२९

मा त्वम् प्रोत्साहिता पापैर्देवराजसमप्रभम् |

भर्तारम् लोकभर्तारमसद्धर्ममुपादधाः || २-३५-३०
verses.wordMeaning:
30. protsaahitaa = Incited; paapaiH = by the evil minded;
tvam = you; maa upaadadhaaH = do not put before the public; asaddhaarmam
= an improper course of conduct; bhartaaram = of your husband; devaraaja
samprabham = whose splendour is equal to the king Devendra; lokabhartaaram = and who
is a protector of this world.
verses.translation:
"Incitedby the evil minded, do not put before the public an improper course of
conduct of your husband who spendour is equal to that of king Devendra and who is a protector of
this world.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि मिथ्या प्रतिज्ञातम् करिष्यति तवानघः |

श्रीमान्दशरथो राजा देवि राजीवलोचनः || २-३५-३१
verses.translation:
"Oh, Kaikeyi! Dasaratha the faultless man, the majesty, the lotus-eyed man and the
king cannot indeed belie the promise given to you(Hence, you alone has to change your opinion)"

verses.sanskrit:
ज्येष्ठो वदान्यः कर्मण्यः स्वधर्मपरिरक्षिता |

रक्षिता जीवलोकस्य बली रामोऽभिषिच्यताम् || २-३५-३२
verses.wordMeaning:
32. raamaH = Rama; jyeshhTaH = the eldest;
vadaanyaH = the generous; karmaNyaH = the skilled; svadharma
parirakshhitaa = the saviour of one's own righteousness; jiivalokasya = of the world
of living being; balii = the strong; abhishhichyataam = be installed on the
throne(of Ayodhya).
verses.translation:
"Let Rama the eldest(of the sons) who is generous strong skilled, capable of
protecting his own duty as Kshatriya but also of protecting the world of living beings, be
installed on the throne of Ayodhya"

verses.sanskrit:
परिवादो हि ते देवि महान्लोके चरिष्यति |

यदि रामो वनम् याति विहाय पितरम् नृपम् || २-३५-३३
verses.wordMeaning:
33. devi = Oh; Kaikeyi! raamaH = Rama; vihaaya =
leaving; pitaram = his father; nR^ipam = the king; yaati yadi = and in
case goes; vanam = to the forest; mahaan = a great; parivaadaH =
accusation; charishhyati = will surely roam; te = about you;
charishhyati = in the world.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Kaikeyi! A great accusation will surely roam about you in the world, if Rama
leaving his royal father goes to the forest.

verses.sanskrit:
स राज्यम् राघवः पातु भवत्वम् विगतज्वरा |

न हि ते राघवादन्यः क्षमः पुरवरे वसेत् || २-३५-३४
verses.translation:
"Let Rama of that character protect this kingdom! Be you free from distress. There
is none other than Rama living in your excellent city who is competent to rule this Ayodhya"

verses.sanskrit:
रामे हि यौवराज्यस्थे राजा दशरथो वनम् |

प्रवेक्ष्यति महेष्वासः पूर्ववृत्तमनुस्मरन् || २-३५-३५
verses.wordMeaning:
35. raame = Rama; yauvaraajyasthe = being installed in the
office of Price Regent; dasarathaH = Dasaratha; raajaa = the king;
maheshhvaasaH = the wielder of the great bow; pravekshhyati hi = will surely have
recourse; vanam = to the forest; anusmaran = cherishly recollecting; puurva
vR^ittam = the conduct of his forebears.
verses.translation:
"In the event of Rama being installe din the office of the prince Regent, King
Dasaratha wielder of the great bow will surely have recourse to the forest, cherishly recollecting
the conduct of his forebears"

verses.sanskrit:
इति सान्वैश्च तीक्ष्णै कैकेयीम् राजसंसदि |

सुवन्त्रः क्षोभयामास भूय एव कृताञ्जलिः || २-३५-३६

नैवसाक्षुभ्यते देवी न च स्म परिदूयते |

न चास्या मुखवर्णस्य विक्रिया लक्ष्यते तदा || २-३५-३७
verses.wordMeaning:
37. saa devii = that queen Kaikeyi; naiva kshhubhyate = did
not get excted; na pariduuyatesma = was not reapented in her heart; na lakshhyate
= nor was seen; vikrayaacha = even change in; asyaaH = her;
mukhavarNasya = colour of the face; tada = then.

sargas.title: Sarga 36
sargas.introduction:
Dasharatha orders to send army and treasury with Rama to forests. Kaikeyi objects to
such a lavish exile and she retorts the same legend of Sagara who exiled his son, Asamanja, without
riches. Minister Siddhartha still ties to persuade her. Then vexed King Dasharatha also tries to
embark on the exile along with Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः सुमन्त्रम् ऐक्ष्वाकः पीडितः अत्र प्रतिज्ञया |

सबाष्पम् अतिनिह्श्वस्य जगाद इदम् पुनः पुनः || २-३६-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. tataH = then; aikshhvaakaH = Dasaratha; the scion of
Ikshvaku dynasty; piiditaH = squeezed; pratiJNayaa = by his promise;
atra = in this matter; atiniHshvasya = sighed heavly; sabhaashhpam =
with tears; jagaada = spoke; idam = the following words; punaH punaH =
again and again; sumantra = to Sumantra.

verses.sanskrit:
सूत रत्न सुसम्पूर्णा चतुर् विध बला चमूः |

रागवस्य अनुयात्रा अर्थम् क्षिप्रम् प्रतिविधीयताम् || २-३६-२
verses.translation:
"Oh, Sumantra! Let a military body consisting of (all) the four limbs of the arms,
filled with the best of its kind, be arranged immediately"

verses.sanskrit:
रूप आजीवा च शालिन्यो वणिजः च महा धनाः |

शोभयन्तु कुमारस्य वाहिनीम् सुप्रसारिताः || २-३६-३
verses.translation:
"Let courtesans well versed in speaking and merchants with great riches having
exposure for extension of sales bestow elegance to the forces of the Prince"

verses.sanskrit:
ये च एनम् उपजीवन्ति रमते यैः च वीर्यतः |

तेषाम् बहु विधम् दत्त्वा तान् अपि अत्र नियोजय || २-३६-४

आयुधानि च मुख्यानि नागराः शकटानि च |

अनुगच्छन्तु काकुत्थ्सम् व्याधाश्चारण्यगोचराः || २-३६-५
verses.translation:
"Let important weapons, chiefs of police and vehicles, hunters knowing the secrets
of forest accompany Rama(scion of Kakutsa)"

verses.sanskrit:
निघ्नन् मृगान् कुन्जरामः च पिबमः च आरण्यकम् मधु |

नदीः च विविधाः पश्यन् न राज्यम् संस्मरिष्यति || २-३६-६
verses.translation:
"Killing deer and elephants, drinking forest-honey, seeing various rivers, he would
not remember of sovereignty"

verses.sanskrit:
धान्य कोशः च यः कश्चित् धन कोशः च मामकः |

तौ रामम् अनुगच्चेताम् वसन्तम् निर्जने वने || २-३६-७
verses.translation:
"Let the granary and the treasury belonging to me be sent to Rama residing in the
uninhabited forest."

verses.sanskrit:
यजन् पुण्येषु देशेषु विसृजमः च आप्त दक्षिणाः |

ऋषिभिः च समागम्य प्रवत्स्यति सुखम् वने || २-३६-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. pravatsyati = he can live away; sukham = happily;
yajan = by performing sacrificial rite; desheshhu = in places; puNyeshhu
= which are holy; visR^ijamshcha = and disbursing; aaptu dakshhiNaaH =
appropriate gifts; samaagamya = along with; R^ishhibhiH = sages; vane =
in the forest.
verses.translation:
"Rama can spend his exile happily, by performing sacrificial rite in holy places and
disbursing appropriate gifts, along with sages living in the forest "

verses.sanskrit:
भरतः च महा बाहुर् अयोध्याम् पालयिष्यति |

सर्व कामैः पुनः श्रीमान् रामः संसाध्यताम् इति || २-३६-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. mahaabaahuH = the mighty armed; bharatashcha = Bharata;
paalayishhyati = will rule; ayodhyaam = Ayodhya; shriimaan = (let) the
glorious; raamaH = Rama; samsaadhyataam = be sent; sarvakaamaiH saha =
along with all objects of enloyments"; iti = thus spoke.
verses.translation:
"The mighty armed Bharata will rule Ayodhya. Let the glorious Rama be sent alog with
all objects of enjoyment"

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् ब्रुवति काकुत्स्थे कैकेय्या भयम् आगतम् |

मुखम् च अपि अगमात् शेषम् स्वरः च अपि न्यरुध्यत || २-३६-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. kakutthse = while Dasaratha; bruvati = was speaking;
evam = thus; bhayam = fear; aagatam = drew near; kaikeyyaaH =
Kaikeyi; mukhamchaapi = here mouth too; aagamat = got; shoshham =
parched; svarashchaapi = her voice also; nyarndhyata = got choked.

verses.sanskrit:
सा विषण्णा च सम्त्रस्ता कैकेयी वाक्यम् अब्रवीत् |

राजानमेवाभिमुखी कैकेयी वाक्यमब्रवीत् || २-३६-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. vishhaNNaacha = feeling depressed; samtrastaa = and
apprehensive; mukhena = with her mouth; parishushhyataa = fetting parched;
abhimukhii = with her face turned; raamameva = towards the king; saa
kaikeyi = that Kaikeyi; abraviit = spoke; vaakyam = these words.

verses.sanskrit:
राज्यम् गत जनम् साधो पीत मण्डाम् सुराम् इव |

निरास्वाद्यतमम् शून्यम् भरतः न अभिपत्स्यते || २-३६-१२
verses.translation:
"Bharata will not accept the kingdom, which will become uninhabited, hollow and
which will be absolutely unenjoyable like wine, quaffed of its spiriteous part, Oh noble soul!"

verses.sanskrit:
कैकेय्याम् मुक्त लज्जायाम् वदन्त्याम् अतिदारुणम् |

राजा दशरथो वाक्यम् उवाच आयत लोचनाम् || २-३६-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. kaikeyyaam = (when) Kaikeyi; muktalajjaayaam = who has
cast off her shame; vadantyaam = was uttering; atidaaruNam = the most cruel
words; raajaa = king; dasharatha = Dasaratha; uvaacha = spoke;
vaakyam = these words; aayatalochanaam = to that lady who has large eyes.

verses.sanskrit:
वहन्तम् किम् तुदसि माम् नियुज्य धुरि मा आहिते |

अनार्ये कृत्यमार्ब्धम् किम् न पूर्वमुपारुधः || २-३६-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. ahite = Oh; hostile woman! kim = why; tudasi =
do you prick; maam = me; vahantam = who is carrying forward; niyujya =
while charged;maa = me; dhuri = with a burden? kim = why; na upaarudhaH = did you
not check; puurvam = before; kR^ityam = the deed; aarabdham = in the
begining(itself); anaarye = Oh the vulgar woman!

verses.sanskrit:
तस्यैतत्क्रोधसम्युक्तमुक्तम् श्रुत्वा वराङ्गना |

कैकेयी द्वि गुणम् क्रुद्धा राजानम् इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-३६-१५

तव एव वंशे सगरः ज्येष्ठम् पुत्रम् उपारुधत् |

असमन्जैति ख्यातम् तथा अयम् गन्तुम् अर्हति || २-३६-१६

एवम् उक्तः धिग् इति एव राजा दशरथो अब्रवीत् |

व्रीडितः च जनः सर्वः सा च तन् न अवबुध्यत || २-३६-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. uktaH = spoken to ; evam = as above; rajaa =
king; dasharathaH = Dasaratha; abraviit = uttered; thik ityeva = as
only"shame!"; sarvaH = the entire; janaH = people; vriiditaH = were
ashamed; saacha = that Kaikeyi; naavabudhyata = did not perecive; tam =
it.

verses.sanskrit:
तत्र वृद्धो महा मात्रः सिद्ध अर्थो नाम नामतः |

शुचिर् बहु मतः राज्ञः कैकेयीम् इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-३६-१८

असमन्जो गृहीत्वा तु क्रीडितः पथि दारकान् |

सरय्वाः प्रक्षिपन्न् अप्सु रमते तेन दुर्मतिः || २-३६-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. asamNjaHtu = but Asamanja; durmatiH = the evil-minded;
gR^ihiitvaa = catching hold; baalakaan = of children; kriidataH =
playing; pathi = in the street; prakshhipan = throwing; apsu = in
waters; sarayvaaH = of Sarayu river; ramate = took delight; tena = by
it.
verses.translation:
"Asamanja the evil-minded, catching hold of children playing in the street and
throwing them in the waters of saryu river, took delight in it"

verses.sanskrit:
तम् दृष्ट्वा नागरः सर्वे क्रुद्धा राजानम् अब्रुवन् |

असमन्जम् वृषीण्व एकम् अस्मान् वा राष्ट्र वर्धन || २-३६-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. dR^ishhTvaa = by watching; tam = him; sarve =
all the; naagaraaH = citizens; kR^iddhaaH = were angry; abruvan = and
spoke; raajaanam = to the king; raashhTra vardhana = "Oh promoter of the state!
vR^iNiishhva = choose; asamNjam = either Asamanja; ekam = alone;
asmaanvaa = or us.

verses.sanskrit:
तान् उवाच ततः राजा किम् निमित्तम् इदम् भयम् |

ताः च अपि राज्ञा सम्पृष्टा वाक्यम् प्रकृतयो अब्रुवन् || २-३६-२१

क्रीडितः तु एष नः पुत्रान् बालान् उद्भ्रान्त चेतनः |

सरय्वाम् प्रक्षिपन् मौर्ख्यात् अतुलाम् प्रीतिम् अश्नुते || २-३६-२२
verses.translation:
"He obtains incomparable delight by throwing, through stupidity, our playing
children in sarayu river in a whirled state of mind."

verses.sanskrit:
स तासाम् वचनम् श्रुत्वा प्रकृतीनाम् नर अधिप |

तम् तत्याज अहितम् पुत्रम् तासाम् प्रिय चिकीर्षया || २-३६-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. shrutvaa = hearing; vachanam = the words; taasaam
prakR^itiinaam = of those people; saH naraadhipaH = that king; tatyaaja =
abandoned; tam putram = that son; ahitam = repugnant(to his subjects)
priyachikiirshhayaa = in a bid to do good; taasaam = to them.

verses.sanskrit:
तम् यानम् श्रीघ्रमारोप्य सभार्यम् सपरिच्छदम् |

यावज्जीवम् विवास्योऽयमिति स्वानन्वशात् पिता || २-३६-२४

स फालपिटकम् गृह्य गिरिदुर्गान्यलोडयत् |

दिशः सर्वास्त्वनुचरन् स यथा पापकर्मकृत् || २-३६-२५

इति एवम् अत्यजद् राजा सगरः वै सुधार्मिकः |

रामः किम् अकरोत् पापम् येन एवम् उपरुध्यते || २-३६-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. agaraH raajaa = king Sagara; sudhaarmikaH = the highly
righteous man; atyajat = abandoned; enam = him; iti = in this manner;
kim paapam = what sin; raamaH = Rama; akarot = has done; yena =
for which; uparudhyate = he was debarred; evam = in this way?
verses.translation:
"The highly righteous King Sagara abandoned Asamanja for perpetration sinful deeds
as above. But, what sin Rama has done, for which he was debarred in this way?"

verses.sanskrit:
न हि कंचन पश्यामो राघवस्यागुणम् वयम् |

दुर्लभो यस्य निरयः श्शाङ्कस्येव कल्मषम् || २-३६-२७

अथवा देवि दोषम् त्वं कंचित्पश्यसि राघवे |

तमद्य ब्रूहि तत्वैन तदा रोमो विवास्यताम् || २-३६-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. athavaa = Or; pashyasi = if you see; kamchit
doshham = any fault; raaghave = in Rama; devi = Oh; Kaikeyi; tvam =
you; bruuhi = tell; tam = about it; adya = now; tattvena =
correctly; tadaa = then; raamaH = rama; vivaasyataam = will be
exiled.
verses.translation:
"Or if you see any fault in Rama, Oh Kaikeyi you tell about it now correctly. Then,
Rama will be exiled."

verses.sanskrit:
अदुष्टस्य हि सम्त्यागः सत्पथे निरतस्य च |

निर्दहे दपि शक्रस्य द्युतिम् धर्मनिरोधनात् || २-३६-२९

तदलम् देवि रामस्य श्रिया विहतया त्वया |

लोकतोऽप् हि ते रक्ष्यः परिवादः शुभानने || २-३६-३०
verses.translation:
"Oh, Kaikeyi! Hence, there is no need of impeding the royal fortune of Rama. Oh,
lady with a charming face! Even blame from the public should be taken care of by you."

verses.sanskrit:
श्रुत्वा तु सिद्ध अर्थ वचो राजा श्रान्ततर स्वनः |

शोक उपहतया वाचा कैकेयीम् इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-३६-३१
verses.wordMeaning:
31. shrutvaa = hearing; siddhaarthavachaH = the words of
Siddhartha; raaja = king Dasaratha; abraviit = spoke; kaikeyiim = to
Kaikeyi; shraantatara svanaH = in the most tiresome tone; vaachaa = in a voice;
shokapahataa = choked with deep angnish.

verses.sanskrit:
एतद्वचो नेच्छ्सि पापवृत्ते |

हितम् न जानासि ममात्मनो वा |

आस्थाय मार्गम् कृपणम् कुचेष्टा |

चेष्टा हि ते साधुपदादपेता || २-३६-३२
verses.wordMeaning:
32. paapavR^itte = Oh; the sinful being! nechchhasi = do you
not agree; etat = with these; vachaH = words? kucheshhTaa = you; with a bad
behaviour; aasthaaya = by resortin gto; maargam = the path; kR^ipaNam =
of the deplorable; na jaanaasi = are you not alive; hitam = to the interest;
mama = of myself; aatmanovaa = or of your own; te = you;
cheshhTaa = action; apetaa = is deprived of; saadhupathaat = the path of
righteousness.
verses.translation:
"Oh, the sinful being! Do you not agree with these words of Siddhartha? By resorting
to the path of the deplorable, you with a bad behaviour, are not alive to my interests or to your
own. Your action is deprived of a righteous path."

verses.sanskrit:
अनुव्रजिष्याम्य् अहम् अद्य रामम् |

राज्यम् परित्यज्य सुखम् धनम् च |

सह एव राज्ञा भरतेन च त्वम् |

यथा सुखम् भुन्क्ष्व चिराय राज्यम् || २-३६-३३
verses.translation:
"I am accompanying Rama now, by abandoning my kingdom, happiness and wealth. You
enjoy the kingdom for a long time, happily together with Bharata the King."
verses.commentary:
Thus completes 36th chapter in the Ayodhya Kanda of the glorious Ramayana of
Valmiki, the work of a sage and the oldest epic.
Verse Locator for Book II : Ayodhya Kanda - Book
Of Ayodhya : Chapter 36
