sargas.title: Sarga 59
sargas.introduction:
Sumantra narrates the ghastly fate of Ayodhya, both its animate and inanimate
beings. Dasaratha laments and soon falls unconscious.

verses.sanskrit:
मम तु अश्वा निवृत्तस्य न प्रावर्तन्त वर्त्मनि |

उष्णम् अश्रु विमुन्चन्तः रामे सम्प्रस्थिते वनम् || २-५९-१
verses.translation:
"Rama having departed to the forest, horses on the return path however, shedding
warm tears, did not proceed further on the way."

verses.sanskrit:
उभाभ्याम् राज पुत्राभ्याम् अथ कृत्वा अहम् ज्ञलिम् |

प्रस्थितः रथम् आस्थाय तत् दुह्खम् अपि धारयन् || २-५९-२
verses.translation:
"I, on my part, offering salutation with joined palms to both the princes and
keeping back from that grief, ascended the chariot and moved on."

verses.sanskrit:
गुहा इव सार्धम् तत्र एव स्थितः अस्मि दिवसान् बहून् |

आशया यदि माम् रामः पुनः शब्दापयेद् इति || २-५९-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. aashayaa = with the hope; iti = that; shabdaapayed
dyadi = I shall be called; punaH = again; raamaH- (by) Rama; sthitaH asmi =
I stayed; tatraiva = there only; guhena saartham = along with Guha;
bahuun = for many (three); divasaan = days.
verses.translation:
"With the hope that I shall be called again by Rama, I stayed there along with Guha
for three days."

verses.sanskrit:
विषये ते महा राज माम व्यसन कर्शिताः |

अपि वृक्षाः परिम्लानः सपुष्प अन्कुर कोरकाः || २-५९-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. mahaaraaja = Oh; emperor; vR^iSaaH api = even trees;
te viSaye = in your domain; parimlaanaH = have withered; sa puSpa ankura
korakaaH = without flowers; sprouts and buds; raama vyasana karshitaaH = having
emaciated due to Rama's separation.
verses.translation:
"Oh, emperor! Even trees in your domain have withered, without giving flowers
sprouts and buds, having emaciated due to Rama's separation."

verses.sanskrit:
उपतप्तोदका नद्यः पल्वलानि सरांसि च |

परिष्कुपलाशानि वनान्युपवनानि च || २-५९-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. nadyaH = rivers; palvalaani = pools; saraamsi ca
= lakes; upataptodakaaH = were with lot water; vanaani = groves; upavanaani
cha = and gardens; pariSkupalaashaani = were with their leaves; parched up.
verses.translation:
"Rivers, pools and lakes were with hot water. Groves and gardens were with their
leaves, parched up."

verses.sanskrit:
न च सर्पन्ति सत्त्वानि व्याला न प्रसरन्ति च |

राम शोक अभिभूतम् तन् निष्कूजम् अभवद् वनम् || २-५९-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. sattvaani = Living beings; na ca sarpanti = are not
moving; vyaalaaH ca = wild animals too; na prasaranti = are not roaming around;
tat vanam = that forest; abhavad = became; niSkuujam = silent; raama
shoka abhibhuutam = predominated by grief towards Rama
verses.translation:
"Living beings are not moving. Wild animals too are not roaming around. That forest
became silent, predominated by grief towards Rama."

verses.sanskrit:
लीन पुष्कर पत्राः च नर इन्द्र कलुष उदकाः |

सम्तप्त पद्माः पद्मिन्यो लीन मीन विहम्गमाः || २-५९-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. narendram = Oh; emperor; padminyaH = lotus-lakes (were);
liina puSkara patraaH ca = with lotus-leaves hiding under water; having pressed themselves
closely together; kaluSa udakaaH = with muddy waters; samtapta padmaaH = with
parched lotus-flowers; liina miina vihamgamaaH = and in which fish and water-fowls having
disappeared.
verses.translation:
"Oh, emperor! Lotus-lakes were with lotus-leaves hiding under water pressing
themselves closely together, with muddy waters, with parched lotus-flowers and in which fish and
water fowls have completely disappeared."

verses.sanskrit:
जलजानि च पुष्पाणि माल्यानि स्थलजानि च |

न अद्य भान्ति अल्प गन्धीनि फलानि च यथा पुरम् || २-५९-८
verses.translation:
"Water-born flowers and flowers living on dry lands now give very little fragrance
and fruits do not have a luscious look as before."

verses.sanskrit:
अत्रोद्यानानि शून्यानि प्रलीनविहगानि च |

न चाभिरामानारामान् पश्यामि मनुजर्षभ || २-५९-९
verses.translation:
"Oh, best of men! The gardens here are empty of men and the birds have vanished. I
am not seeing the gardens as charming (as before)."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रविशन्तम् अयोध्याम् माम् न कश्चित् अभिनन्दति |

नरा रामम् अपश्यन्तः निह्श्वसन्ति मुहुर् मुहुः || २-५९-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. na kashchit = nobody; abhinandati = greeted me;
pravishantam = entering; ayodhyaayaam = into Ayodhya City; apashyantaH =
having not seen; raamam = Rama; naraaH = the people; nishvasanti = are
breathing; muhur muhuH = again and again.
verses.translation:
"Nobody greeted me, entering into Ayodhya City. Having not seen Rama, the people are
breathing their sigh of lamentation again and again."

verses.sanskrit:
देव राजरथम् दृष्ट्वा विना राममिहागतम् |

दुःखादश्रुमुखः सर्वो राजमार्गगतो जनः || २-५९-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. deva = Oh emperor; dR^iSTvaa = seeing;
raajaratham = the royal chariot; aagatam = coming; iha = here; ninaa
raamam = without Rama; sarvaH = all; janaH = the people; raajamarga
gataH = going along the royal high-way; ashrumukhaH = are having their faces filled
with tears; duHkhaat = by grief.
verses.translation:
"Oh, emperor! Seeing the royal chariot returning here without Rama, all the people
along the royal high-way are shedding tears with grief."

verses.sanskrit:
हर्म्यैः विमानैः प्रासादैः अवेक्ष्य रथम् आगतम् |

हाहा कार कृता नार्यो राम अदर्शन कर्शिताः || २-५९-१२
verses.translation:
"Seeing the returned chariot, women from mansions, seven-storied buildings and royal
palaces are crying �ha ha!', being troubled by the non-appearance of Rama."

verses.sanskrit:
आयतैः विमलैः नेत्रैः अश्रु वेग परिप्लुतैः |

अन्योन्यम् अभिवीक्षन्ते व्यक्तम् आर्ततराः स्त्रियः || २-५९-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. striyaH = women; aartataraaH = being more sorrowful;
abhiviikSante = are looking at; anyonyam = each other; avyaktam =
indistinctly; aayataiH = with their long; vimalaiH = bright; netraiH =
eyes; ashru vega pariplutaiH = overwhelmed with a flood of tears.
verses.translation:
"Women, being more sorrowful, are looking at each other indistinctly with their long
bright eyes overwhelmed with a flood of tears."

verses.sanskrit:
न अमित्राणाम् न मित्राणाम् उदासीन जनस्य च |

अहम् आर्ततया कंचित् विशेषम् न उपलक्षये || २-५९-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. aham = I; na upalakSaye = do not see; kimchit =
any; visheSam = distinction; aartatayaa = in their agony; amitraaNaam =
in non-friends; mitraaNaam = friends; udaasiina janasya = and neutral people.
verses.translation:
"I do not see any distinction in their agony, whether they are non-friends, friends
or neutral people."

verses.sanskrit:
अप्रहृष्ट मनुष्या च दीन नाग तुरम्गमा |

आर्त स्वर परिम्लाना विनिह्श्वसित निह्स्वना || २-५९-१५

निरानन्दा महा राज राम प्रव्राजन आतुला |

कौसल्या पुत्र हीना इवायोध्या प्रतिभाति मा मा || २-५९-१६
verses.translation:
"Oh, emperor! The city of Ayodhya with its joy-less people, with its elephants and
horses looking miserable, with sighing exhaustion due to cries of pain, filled with sounds of
moaning, cheerless and feeling anguish due to Rama's exile, appears to me, like Queen Kausalya
without her son."

verses.sanskrit:
सूतस्य वचनम् श्रुत्वा वाचा परम दीनया |

बाष्प उपहतया राजा तम् सूतम् इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-५९-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. raajaa = the king; shrutvaa = hearing; suutasya
= Sumantra's; vachanam = words; abraviit = spoke; idam = this; tam
suutam = to that charioteer; vaacaa = in a voice; baaSpa upahatayaa = choked
with tears; parama diinayaa = and very much woeful.

verses.sanskrit:
कैकेय्या विनियुक्तेन पाप अभिजन भावया |

मया न मन्त्र कुशलैः वृद्धैः सह समर्थितम् || २-५९-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. viniyuktena = enjoined; kaikeyyaa = by Kaikeyi;
paapa abhijana bhaavayaa = with sinful birth and intention; na samarthitam = it
was not deliberated; mayaa = by me; mantra kushalaiH = with people experienced in
counsel; vR^iddhaiH saha = and with elders.
verses.translation:
"Enjoined by Kaikeyi with her sinful birth and intention, I could not deliberate
with people experienced in counsel or with elders beforehand."

verses.sanskrit:
न सुहृद्भिर् न च अमात्यैः मन्त्रयित्वा न नैगमैः |

मया अयम् अर्थः सम्मोहात् स्त्री हेतोह् सहसा कृतः || २-५९-१९
verses.translation:
"This act has been done by me in haste due to infatuation for the sake of a woman,
without consulting with friends or ministers or with interpreters of sacred texts."

verses.sanskrit:
भवितव्यतया नूनम् इदम् वा व्यसनम् महत् |

कुलस्य अस्य विनाशाय प्राप्तम् सूत यदृच्चया || २-५९-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. suuta = Oh; Sumantra; idam = this; mahat =
great; vyasanam = calamity; nuunam = surely; praaptam = has come;
bhavitavyatayaa = as an inevitable consequence; vaa = r; vinaashaaya =
for the ruin; asya = of this; kulasya = race; yadR^icchayaa = or
accidentally.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Sumantra! This great calamity, surely, has come as an inevitable consequence or
for the ruin of this race or accidentally."

verses.sanskrit:
सूत यद्य् अस्ति ते किंचिन् मया अपि सुकृतम् कृतम् |

त्वम् प्रापय आशु माम् रामम् प्राणाः सम्त्वरयन्ति माम् || २-५९-२१

यद् यद् या अपि मम एव आज्ञा निवर्तयतु राघवम् |

न शक्ष्यामि विना राम मुहूर्तम् अपि जीवितुम् || २-५९-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. yadyat aaGYaa = if there is the same unlimited authority;
mama = of mine; yaa api = even now; raaghavam nivartayatu = let Rama be
brought back; na shakSyaami = I cannot; jiivitum = survive; raamam vinaa
= without Rama; muhuurtam api = even for a moment.
verses.translation:
"If there is the same unlimited authority of mine even now, let Rama be brought back
to Ayodhya. I cannot survive without Rama even for a moment."

verses.sanskrit:
अथवा अपि महा बाहुर् गतः दूरम् भविष्यति |

माम् एव रथम् आरोप्य शीघ्रम् रामाय दर्शय || २-५९-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. athavaa = or perhaps; mahaa baahuH = Rama the mighty
armed; gataH bhaviSyati = might have gone; duuram = along way; aaropya maam
eva = make me to ascend; ratham = the chariot; shiighram = quickly;
darshaya = and show (me); raamaaya = to Rama.
verses.translation:
"Or perhaps Rama the mighty-armed might have gone a long way. Make me to ascend the
chariot and quickly show me to Rama."

verses.sanskrit:
वृत्त दम्ष्ट्रः महा इष्वासः क्व असौ लक्ष्मण पूर्वजः |

यदि जीवामि साध्व् एनम् पश्येयम् सह सीतया || २-५९-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. kva = where is; asau = that; lakSmaNa puurvajaH
= Rama; vR^itta damSTro = having round teeth; mahaa iSvaasaH = and wearing a
large bow? Pashyeyam yadi = If I can see; enam = him; saadhu = well; siitayaa
saha = with Seetha; jiivaami = I can survive.
verses.translation:
"Where is that Rama having pearl-like teeth and wearing a large bow? If only I can
see him well with Seetha, I can survive."

verses.sanskrit:
लोहित अक्षम् महा बाहुम् आमुक्त मणि कुण्डलम् |

रामम् यदि न पश्यामि गमिष्यामि यम क्षयम् || २-५९-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. na pashyeyam yadi = if I cannot see; raamam = Rama;
lohita akSam = having red eyes; mahaa baahum = mighty arms; aamukta maNi
kuNDalam = with ear-rings made of gems; gamiSyaami = I shall proceed; yam
kSayam = to the world of death.
verses.translation:
"If I cannot see Rama having red eyes, mighty arms and with ear-rings made of gems,
I shall proceed to the world of Death."

verses.sanskrit:
अतः नु किम् दुह्खतरम् यो अहम् इक्ष्वाकु नन्दनम् |

इमाम् अवस्थाम् आपन्नो न इह पश्यामि राघवम् || २-५९-२६
verses.translation:
"What is more distressing to me after getting into this condition, in not seeing
here, Rama who is a delight to Ikshvaku dynasty?"

verses.sanskrit:
हा राम राम अनुज हा हा वैदेहि तपस्विनी |

न माम् जानीत दुह्खेन म्रियमाणम् अनाथवत् || २-५९-२७
verses.wordMeaning:
27. haa raama = Oh; Rama; haa raamaanuja = Oh; younger
brother of Rama; tapasvinii = unfortunate; haa vaidehi = Oh; Seetha; na
jaaniita = you do not know; maam = me; mriyamaaNam = as dying;
duHkena = with grief; anaathavat = like one abandoned.
verses.translation:
"Oh, Rama! Oh, Younger Brother of Rama! Oh, unfortunate Seetha! You do not know that
I am dying with grief, like one abandoned."

verses.sanskrit:
स तेन राजा दुःखेन भृशमर्पितचेतनः |

अवगाढः सुदुष्पारम् शोकसागमब्रवीत् || २-५९-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. saH raajaa = that Dasaratha; arpitachetasaH = his mind
despaired; bhR^iSam = very much; duHkena = with grief; avagaaDhaH =
plunged; shoka saagaram = in an ocean of sorrow; suduSpaaram = which is very
difficult to be crossed; abraviit = spoke (as follows)

verses.sanskrit:
रामशोकमहाभोगः सीताविरहपारगः |

श्वसितोर्मिमहावर्तो बाष्पफेनजलाविलः || २-५९-२९

बाहुविक्षेपमीनौघो विक्रन्दितमहास्वनः |

प्रकीर्णकेशशैवालः कैकेयीबडबामुखः || २-५९-३०

ममाश्रुवेगप्रभवः कुब्जावाक्यमहाग्रहः |

वरवेलो नृशंसाया रामप्रव्राजनायतः || २-५९-३१

यस्मिन् बत निमग्नोऽहम् कौसल्ये राघवम् विना |

दुस्तरः जीवता देवि मया अयम् शोक सागरः || २-५९-३२
verses.translation:
"Oh, Queen Kausalya! I am plunged in this ocean of grief. Its area of sorrow is for
Rama. its other shore is Seetha's separation. Its waves and huge whirlpools are sighs of anguish.
It is agitated with water and foam as tears. Throwing away of arms is the swarm of fishes. Its
great sounds are cries of lamentation. The scattered hari is its duck-week. Kaikeyi is its
submarine fire. Which is the cause for the rush in my tears. The words of the hum-backed are its
huge crocodiles. Its shores are the boons asked by the cruel Kaikeyi. Its long stretch is due to
sending of Rama to far away place. I cannot cross this ocean alive, without Rama. What a pity!"

verses.sanskrit:
अशोभनम् यो अहम् इह अद्य राघवम् |

दिदृक्षमाणो न लभे सलक्ष्मणम्

इति इव राजा विलपन् महा यहाशः

पपात तूर्णम् शयने स मूर्चितः || २-५९-३३
verses.translation:
"Though I want to see Rama and Lakshmana now, I am not able to see them here. It is
very bad." - thus lamenting, the king of great renown soon became unconscious and fell down on his
couch.

verses.sanskrit:
इति विलपति पार्थिवे प्रनष्टे |

करुणतरम् द्विगुणम् च राम हेतोः |

वचनम् अनुनिशम्य तस्य देवी |

भयम् अगमत् पुनर् एव राम माता || २-५९-३४

sargas.title: Sarga 60
sargas.introduction:
Sumantra was unable to console Kausalya, who lied on the floor due to her extreme
grief over her separation from Rama, even though he tries to avert her grief by telling her that
Rama can reside in the forest delightfully, by warding off his agony.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः भूत उपसृष्टा इव वेपमाना पुनः पुनः |

धरण्याम् गत सत्त्वा इव कौसल्या सूतम् अब्रवीत् || २-६०-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. tataH = then; kausalyaa = Kausalya; vepamaanaa =
trembling; punaH punaH = again and again; bhuuta upasR^iSTaa iva = as though
possessed by a spirit; dharaNyaam = and lying on the floor; gata sattvaa iva =
without proper disposition of mind; abraviit = spoke; suutam = to Sumantra (as
follows)

verses.sanskrit:
नय माम् यत्र काकुत्स्थः सीता यत्र च लक्ष्मणः |

तान् विना क्षणम् अपि अत्र जीवितुम् न उत्सहे हि अहम् || २-६०-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. yatra = wherever are; kaakutsthaH = Rama; siitaa
ca = and Seetha; yatra = wherever is; lakSmaNaH = Lakshmana; naya =
take; maam = me; tatra = there; taan vinaa = without them; aham
= I; na utsahe = do not cherish; jiivitum = to live; atra = here;
kSaNam api = even for a moment.
verses.translation:
"Wherever Rama, Seetha and Lakshmana are there, take me to them. Without them, I do
not cherish to live here even for a moment."

verses.sanskrit:
निवर्तय रथम् शीघ्रम् दण्डकान् नय माम् अपि |

अथ तान् न अनुगच्चामि गमिष्यामि यम क्षयम् || २-६०-३
verses.translation:
"Turn back the chariot quickly. Take also myself to the forest of Dandaka. Now, if I
do not go after them, I shall enter the Death's abode."

verses.sanskrit:
बाष्प वेगौपहतया स वाचा सज्जमानया |

इदम् आश्वासयन् देवीम् सूतः प्रान्जलिर् अब्रवीत् || २-६०-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. suutaH = Sumantra; vaacaa = with a voice; baaSpa
vegaupahatayaa = choked with tears; sajjamaanayaa = in faint accents;
praanjaliH = with joined palms; aashvaasayan = consoling; deviim =
Kausalya; abraviit = spoke; idam = these words (to her).

verses.sanskrit:
त्यज शोकम् च मोहम् च सम्भ्रमम् दुह्खजम् तथा |

व्यवधूय च सम्तापम् वने वत्स्यति राघवः || २-६०-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. tyaja = abandon; shokam = grief; moham =
delusion; tathaa = and; sambhramam ca = heaste; duhkhajam = born of
affliction; raaghavaH = Rama; vatsyati = can reside; vane = in the
forest; vyavadhuuya = warding off; samtaapam = anguish.
verses.translation:
"Abandon grief, delusion and haste born of affliction. Rama, can reside in the
forest, warding off anguish."

verses.sanskrit:
लक्ष्मणः च अपि रामस्य पादौ परिचरन् वने |

आराधयति धर्मज्ञः पर लोकम् जित इन्द्रियः || २-६०-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. lakSmaNaH ca api = Lakshmana too; dharmaGYaH = knowing
about righteous conduct; jitendriyaaH = having subdued his senses; paricaran =
serving; paadau = the feet; raamasya = of Rama; vane = in the forest;
aaraadhayati = is propitiating; para lokam = the other world.
verses.translation:
"Lakshmana too, knowing about a righteous conduct, having subdued his senses and
serving the feet of Rama in the forest, is propitiating the other world."

verses.sanskrit:
विजने अपि वने सीता वासम् प्राप्य गृहेष्व् इव |

विस्रम्भम् लभते अभीता रामे सम्न्यस्त मानसा || २-६०-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. siitaa = Seetha; praapya = getting; vaasam = a
dwelling place; gR^iheSva iva = resembling a house; vijane vane api = even in a
lonely forest; samnyasta maanasaa = her mind being encamped; raame = in Rama;
abhiitaa = being fearless; labhate = and acquiring; visrambham =
confidence.
verses.translation:
"Seetha, getting a dwelling place resembling a house even in a lonely forest, her
mind encamped in Rama and being fearless, is acquiring confidence.

verses.sanskrit:
न अस्या दैन्यम् कृतम् किंचित् सुसूक्ष्मम् अपि लक्षये |

उचिता इव प्रवासानाम् वैदेही प्रतिभाति मा || २-६०-८
verses.translation:
"Not even very minute depression, even a little, is seen developed in Seetha. It
appears to me as though Seetha is accustomed to so many exiles."

verses.sanskrit:
नगर उपवनम् गत्वा यथा स्म रमते पुरा |

तथैव रमते सीता निर्जनेषु वनेष्व् अपि || २-६०-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. yathaa = how; siitaa = Seetha; ramate sma = was
rejoicing; puraa = earlier; gatvaa = going; nagara upavanam = to gardens
I the city; tathaiva = in the same manner; ramate = (She) is taking delight;
vaneSva api = in forests also; nirjaneSu = which are desolate.
verses.translation:
"Seetha is taking delight in the desolate forests in the same manner as she was
earlier enjoying in visiting gardens in the city."

verses.sanskrit:
बाला इव रमते सीता बाल चन्द्र निभ आनना |

रामा रामे हि अदीन आत्मा विजने अपि वने सती || २-६०-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. siitaa = Seetha; raamaa = a charming woman; bala
candra nibhaananaaH = having her face resembling a full moon; adiina atmaa = her mind
settled; raame = in Rama; satii api = even though staying; vijane = in a
lonely; vane = forest; ramate = is enjoying it; baalaa iva = like a
little girl.
verses.translation:
"Seetha, a charming woman with her face resembling a full moon and with her mind
absorbed in Rama, even though staying in a lonely forest, is enjoying it like a little girl."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् गतम् हृदयम् हि अस्याः तत् अधीनम् च जीवितम् |

अयोध्या अपि भवेत् तस्या राम हीना तथा वनम् || २-६०-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. asyaaH = Her; hR^idayam = heart; tat gatam = is
directed towards Rama; jiivitam ca = her life also; tat adhiinam = is resting on
him; ayodhyaa api = even if Ayodhya; raama hiinaa = is without Rama;
bhavet = it becomes; vanam = a forest; tasyaaH = to her; tadaa
= then.
verses.translation:
"Seetha's heart is directed towards Rama. Her life also is dependent on him. Even if
Ayodhya is without Rama, then it becomes a forest to her."

verses.sanskrit:
परि पृच्चति वैदेही ग्रामामः च नगराणि च |

गतिम् दृष्ट्वा नदीनाम् च पादपान् विविधान् अपि || २-६०-१२

रामम् हि लक्ष्मनम् वापि पृष्ट्वा जानाति जानती |

अयोध्याक्रोशमात्रे तु विहारमिव संश्रिता || २-६०-१३
verses.translation:
"As if only a couple of miles away from Ayodhya and as being in a garden there,
Seetha on seeing villages, towns, movement of rivers, and various types of trees, enquires with
Rama or Lakshmana and Knows well about them."

verses.sanskrit:
इदमेव स्मराम्यस्याः सहसैवोपजल्पितम् |

कैकेयीसंश्रितम् वाक्यम् नेदानीम् प्रतिभाति माम् || २-६०-१४
verses.translation:
"I am remembering only these incidents about Seetha. It does not flash to my mind
now of the words hurriedly spoken of by Seetha about Kaikeyi."

verses.sanskrit:
ध्वंसयित्वा तु तद्वाक्यम् प्रमादात्पर्युपस्थितम् |

ह्लदनम् वचनम् सूतो देव्या मधुरमब्रवीत् || २-६०-१५

अध्वना वात वेगेन सम्भ्रमेण आतपेन च |

न हि गच्चति वैदेह्याः चन्द्र अंशु सदृशी प्रभा || २-६०-१६
verses.translation:
"Seetha's radiance resembling a moon's gleam is not fading away due to her travel in
the forest or due to the velocity of wind or because of her bewilderment or due to heat of the
sun."

verses.sanskrit:
सदृशम् शत पत्रस्य पूर्ण चन्द्र उपम प्रभम् |

वदनम् तत् वदान्याया वैदेह्या न विकम्पते || २-६०-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. tat vadanam = that face; vaidehyaaH = of Seetha;
vadaanyaayaaH = which is altruistic; sadR^isham = is similar; shata
patrasya = to a lotus; puurNa candra upama prabham = whose lustre is similar to that
of a full moon; na vikampate = did not become changed."
verses.translation:
"That face of altruistic Seetha resembling a lotus flower, whose lustre is similar
to that of a full moon, did not become changed."

verses.sanskrit:
अलक्त रस रक्त अभाव् अलक्त रस वर्जितौ |

अद्य अपि चरणौ तस्याः पद्म कोश सम प्रभौ || २-६०-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. tasyaaH = Her; caraNau = feet; alakta rasa
varjitau = which even though no longer painted with vermilion; adya api = still;
alakta rasa rakta abhaava = looks red as Alakta (red juice obtained from resin of certain
trees); padma kosha sama prabhau = with a lustre equal to that of red lotus buds.
verses.translation:
"Her feet, which even though no longer painted with vermilion, still looks red as
Alakta (red juice obtained from resin of certain trees), with lustre equal to that of red lotus
buds."

verses.sanskrit:
नूपुर उद्घुष्ट हेला इव खेलम् गच्चति भामिनी |

इदानीम् अपि वैदेही तत् रागा न्यस्त भूषणा || २-६०-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. bhaaminii = Seetha; nuupura udghuSTa helaa = sporting
her tinkling anklets; khelam = playfully; gacchati = walks; vaidehii =
Seetha; idaaniim api = even now; nyasta bhuuSaNaa = is donned with her ornaments;
tat raagaa = as a mark of passion towards Rama.
verses.translation:
"Seetha, sporting her tinkling anklets, walks playfully. Even now, Seetha dons her
ornaments, as a mark of Her passion towards Rama."

verses.sanskrit:
गजम् वा वीक्ष्य सिम्हम् वा व्याघ्रम् वा वनम् आश्रिता |

न आहारयति सम्त्रासम् बाहू रामस्य संश्रिता || २-६०-२०
verses.translation:
"Seetha who stays in the forest, takes refuge in the arms of Rama and hence does not
give Herself to fear, even by seeing an elephant or a lion or a tiger."

verses.sanskrit:
न शोच्याः ते न च आत्मा ते शोच्यो न अपि जन अधिपः |

इदम् हि चरितम् लोके प्रतिष्ठास्यति शाश्वतम् || २-६०-२१
verses.translation:
"There is no need to pity them nor us nor the king too. This story will thrive in
the world forever."

verses.sanskrit:
विधूय शोकम् परिहृष्ट मानसा |

महर्षि याते पथि सुव्यवस्थिताः |

वने रता वन्य फल अशनाः पितुः |

शुभाम् प्रतिज्ञाम् परिपालयन्ति ते || २-६०-२२
verses.translation:
"Abandoning grief, possessing cheerful minds, settling well in the path followed by
great sages, delighting in the forest-life and eating fruits of the forest, they are keeping up the
promise given to their father."

verses.sanskrit:
तथा अपि सूतेन सुयुक्त वादिना |

निवार्यमाणा सुत शोक कर्शिता |

न चैव देवी विरराम कूजितात् |

प्रिय इति पुत्र इति च राघव इति च || २-६०-२३

sargas.title: Sarga 61
sargas.introduction:
Kausalya, while weeping, rebukes Dasaratha for his evil act of sending Rama to
exile. She explains various difficulties being faced by Rama, Lakshmana and Seetha in their
forest-life. She says that even if Rama returns to Ayodhya in the fifteenth year, he may not accept
the kingdom since enjoyed by Bharata.

verses.sanskrit:
वनम् गते धर्म परे रामे रमयताम् वरे |

कौसल्या रुदती स्वार्ता भर्तारम् इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-६१-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. raame = (When) Rama; dharma vare = excellent in virtue;
vare = excellent; ramayataam = among those causing delight; gate =
having gone; vanam = to the forest; kausalyaa = Kausalya; svaartaa =
herself pained; rudatii = (and) weeping; abraviit = spoke; idam = these
words; bhartaaram = to her husband.

verses.sanskrit:
यद्यपि त्रिषु लोकेषु प्रथितम् ते मयद् यशः |

सानुक्रोशो वदान्यः च प्रिय वादी च राघवः || २-६१-२

कथम् नर वर श्रेष्ठ पुत्रौ तौ सह सीतया |

दुह्खितौ सुख सम्वृद्धौ वने दुह्खम् सहिष्यतः || २-६१-३
verses.translation:
"Despite the fame in the three worlds of your great glory that Dasaratha is
compassionate, bountiful and kind in his words, how your two sons along with Seetha who grew
comfortably can bear suffering in the forest when faced with hardships, O the best among the
foremost of men!"

verses.sanskrit:
सा नूनम् तरुणी श्यामा सुकुमारी सुख उचिता |

कथम् उष्णम् च शीतम् च मैथिली प्रसहिष्यते || २-६१-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. saa maithilii = that Seetha; taruNii = who is a young
lady; shyaamaa = in the prime of Her youth; sukumaarii = and very delicate;
katham = how prasahiSyate = (She) can tolerate; nuunam = really; uSNam
ca = heat; shiitam ca = and cold?
verses.translation:
"That Seetha, who is a young lady in the prime of her youth and very delicate, can
really tolerate heat and cold?"

verses.sanskrit:
भुक्त्वा अशनम् विशाल अक्षी सूप दंश अन्वितम् शुभम् |

वन्यम् नैवारम् आहारम् कथम् सीता उपभोक्ष्यते || २-६१-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. katham = how; siitaa = Seetha; vishaala akSii =
the large-eyed; upabhokSyate = can eat; aahaaram = a good; vanyam
naivaaram = prepared with wild rice; bhuktvaa = having eaten; shubham = a
good; ashanam = food; suupa damsha anvitam = containing soups and snacks?
verses.translation:
"How the large-eyed Seetha can eat a food prepared with wild rice, she having eaten
here a good food containing soups and snacks?"

verses.sanskrit:
गीत वादित्र निर्घोषम् श्रुत्वा शुभम् अनिन्दिता |

कथम् क्रव्य अद सिम्हानाम् शब्दम् श्रोष्यति अशोभनम् || २-६१-६
verses.translation:
"How that irreproachable Seetha can hear the horrid sounds of cruel animals and
lions, after hearing the auspicious sounds of singing and music here?"

verses.sanskrit:
महा इन्द्र ध्वज सम्काशः क्व नु शेते महा भुजः |

भुजम् परिघ सम्काशम् उपधाय महा बलः || २-६१-७

पद्म वर्णम् सुकेश अन्तम् पद्म निह्श्वासम् उत्तमम् |

कदा द्रक्ष्यामि रामस्य वदनम् पुष्कर ईक्षणम् || २-६१-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. kadaa = when; drakSyaami = shall I see; vadanam
= the face; raamasya = of Rama; padma varNam = with the colour of a lotus;
sukesha antam = framed with marvelous locks; padma niHshvaasam = of
lotus-perfume; puSkara iikSaNam = with eyes resembling lotus-petals; uttamam =
and excellent?
verses.translation:
"When can I see the face of Rama with the colour of a lotus, framed with marvelous
locks of lotus-perfume, with eyes resembling lotus-petals and excellent?"

verses.sanskrit:
वज्र सारमयम् नूनम् हृदयम् मे न संशयः |

अपश्यन्त्या न तम् यद् वै फलति इदम् सहस्रधा || २-६१-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. me hR^idayam = my heart; nuunam = is surely; vajra
saaramayam = made fully of the solid-interior of diamond; na = no; samshayaH
= doubt; yat = for which; reason; idam = this heart; na phalati vai = is
not split; sahasradhaa = into thousand pieces; apashyantaaH = even in my not
seeing; tam = that Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
यत्त्वया करुणम् कर्म व्यपोह्य मम बान्धवाः |

निरस्ता परिधावन्ति सुखार्हः कृपणा वने || २-६१-१०
verses.translation:
"It was not a kindly act by you that you banished my kith and kin, who were worthy
of comforts to wander around in the forest in such a miserable condition."

verses.sanskrit:
यदि पञ्चदशे वर्षे राघवः पुनरेष्यति |

जह्याद्राज्यम् च कोशम् च भरतो नोपल्स्ख्यते || २-६१-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. raaghavaH punareSyati yadi = even if Rama comes back;
paNcadashevarshhe = in the fifteenth year; nopalakshhyete = it cannot be
inferred; bharataH = that Bharata; jayaat = will abandon; raajyam ca =
the kingdom and; kosham ca = the treasury."
verses.translation:
"Even if Rama comes back in the fifteenth year, it cannot be inferred that Bharata
will abandon the kingdom and the treasury."

verses.sanskrit:
भोजयन्ति किल श्राद्धे केचित्स्वनेव बान्धवान् |

ततः पश्चात्समीक्षन्ते कृतकार्या द्विजर्षभान् || २-६१-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. kechit = some; shraadhhe = in a ceremony in honour of
dead relative; bhojayanti kila = indeed feed; svaan = their won; baandhavaan
iva = relatives only; kR^itakaaryaaH = having completed the ceremony; tataH
pashchaat = thereafter; samiikSante = they look for; dvijarshhabhaan = the
illustrious Twice-born.
verses.translation:
"There are those who in a ceremony in hour of dead relatives, first feed their
relatives and thereafter, to fulfill their duty, remember to invite the illustrious Twice-born.

verses.sanskrit:
तत्र ये गुणवन्तश्च विद्वांसश्च द्विजातयः |

न पश्चात्तेऽभिमन्यन्ते सुधामपि सुरोपमाः || २-६१-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. tatra = there; ye = which; guNavantashca = the
virtuous; vidvaamsashca = and the learned dvija tayaH = Brahmanas; suropamaaH =
who are like unto gods; te = they; na abhimanyante = do not accept;
pashcaatt = afterwards; sudhaamapi = even ambrosia.
verses.translation:
"The virtuous and the learned Brahmins, who are like unto gods, do not accept
afterwards even food as delicious as ambrosia."

verses.sanskrit:
ब्राह्मणेष्वपि तृप्तेषु पश्चाद्भोक्तुम् द्विजर्षभाः |

नाभ्युपैतुमलम् प्राज्ञाः शृङ्गच्चेदमिवर्ष्भाः || २-६१-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. tR^ipteSu = the contented; braahmaNeSvapi = even if they
are Brahmanas; praaJNaaH = wise; dvijarSabhaaH = and the best of Brahmanas;
naabhyupaitumalam = cannot agree; bhoktum = to eat; pashcaat =
afterwards; shR^iN^gacchedamiva = as splitting of thorns; R^iSabhaaH = of a
bull.
verses.translation:
"The best of Brahmins in their wisdom, will not accept what remains of the food of
which other Brahmins have partaken, regarding it as a bull shorn of its horns."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् कनीयसा भ्रात्रा भुक्तम् राज्यम् विशाम् पते |

भ्राता ज्येष्ठा वरिष्ठाः च किम् अर्थम् न अवमंस्यते || २-६१-१५
verses.translation:
"O, king! Why not the eldest and the best brother refuse to accept the kingdom
enjoyed by the younger brother?"

verses.sanskrit:
न परेण आहृतम् भक्ष्यम् व्याघ्रः खादितुम् इच्चति |

एवम् एव नर व्याघ्रः पर लीढम् न मंस्यते || २-६१-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. vyaaghraH = a tiger; necchati = does not wish;
khaaditum = to eat; bhakSyam = a food; ashitam = eaten; vareNa
= by another animal; evam eva = in the same manner; naravyaaghraH = a tiger among
men; na manyate = does not accept; paraliiDham = that which has been enjoyed by
another.
verses.translation:
"A tiger does not wish to share a part of food eaten by another animal. In the same
manner, Rama the tiger among men may not accept that which has been enjoyed by another."

verses.sanskrit:
हविर् आज्यम् पुरोडाशाः कुशा यूपाः च खादिराः |

न एतानि यात यामानि कुर्वन्ति पुनर् अध्वरे || २-६१-१७
verses.translation:
"Oblations, clarified butter, leavings of an offering, sacred grass and sacrificial
posts made of trunk of Khadira tree, once used, are not put to use again in a sacrifice."

verses.sanskrit:
तथा हि आत्तम् इदम् राज्यम् हृत साराम् सुराम् इव |

न अभिमन्तुम् अलम् रामः नष्ट सोमम् इव अध्वरम् || २-६१-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. tathaa = thus; raamaH = Rama; naalam = cannot;
abhimantum = accept; idam raajyam = this kingdom; attam = taken away by
others; suraamiva = as an ambrosia; hR^itasaaram = whose essence has been taken
away; adhvaramiva = and as in a sacrifice; naSTa somam = when the stalks of Soma
plant (from which a beverage called Soma is prepared) are lost.
verses.translation:
"Thus, Rama cannot accept the kingdom taken away by other, as those not accepting an
ambrosia whose essence has been taken away or as in a sacrifice, stalks of Soma plant (from which a
beverage called Soma is prepared) are lost."

verses.sanskrit:
न एवम् विधम् असत्कारम् राघवो मर्षयिष्यति |

बलवान् इव शार्दूलो बालधेर् अभिमर्शनम् || २-६१-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. raaghavaH = Rama; na marSayiSyati = will not go through;
evam vidham = such a type; astakaaram = of dishonour; avamarshanam iva =
as touching; vaaladhe = of the tail; balavaan = by a strong; shaarduulaH
= tiger.
verses.translation:
"Rama will not go through such a type of dishonour, as a strong tiger does not
endure even touching of its tail."

verses.sanskrit:
नैतस्य सहिता लोका भयम् कुर्युर्महामृधे |

अधर्मम् त्विह धर्मात्मा लोकम् धर्मेण योजयेत् || २-६१-२०
verses.translation:
"Even if all the worlds combine together in a great battle, they cannot stir up fear
in him. The virtuous minded Rama will provide righteousness to the people with
unrighteousness."

verses.sanskrit:
नन्वसौ काञ्चनैर्बाणैर्महावीर्यो महाभुजः |

युगान्त इव भूतानि सागरानपि निर्दहेत् || २-६१-२१
verses.translation:
"Rama with great prowess and with his mighty arms can surely burn up all beings and
even the ocean itself by his golden arrows; like at the time of destruction of the world."

verses.sanskrit:
स तादृशः सिम्ह बलो वृषभ अक्षो नर ऋषभः |

स्वयम् एव हतः पित्रा जलजेन आत्मजो यथा || २-६१-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. saH naraSabhaH = that excellent man; taadR^iS\shaH = of
such; simhabalaH = strength of a lion; vR^iSabhaakSaH = and with eyes like those
of a bull; hataH = was mined; pitraa iva = indeed by father; svayam = of
his own; aatmajoyathaa = like (killing) its child; jalajena = by a fish.
verses.translation:
"That excellent man with such a lion's strength and with eyes like those of a bull
was ruined indeed by his own father, like killing its child-fish by an adult fish."

verses.sanskrit:
द्विजाति चरितः धर्मः शास्त्र दृष्टः सनातनः |

यदि ते धर्म निरते त्वया पुत्रे विवासिते || २-६१-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. putre = a son; dharma nirate = who is devoted to
righteousness; nirvaasite = being sent to exile; tvayaa = by you; dharmaH
yadi = is it a right thing; shaasradR^iSTah = as seen by scriptures; te =
for you; sanaatanaH = or an eternal thing; dvijaaticaritaH = practised by the
Twice born?
verses.translation:
"A son, who is devoted to righteousness, has been sent to exile by you. Is it a
right thing for you, as seen by scriptures or is it an eternal conduct as practiced by the Twice
born?"

verses.sanskrit:
गतिर् एवाक् पतिर् नार्या द्वितीया गतिर् आत्मजः |

तृतीया ज्ञातयो राजमः चतुर्थी न इह विद्यते || २-६१-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. raajan = O; king; patiH = husband; ekaa = is a
chief; gatiH = refuge; naaryaaH = for a womn; dvitiiyaa gatiH = her
second refuge; aatmajaH = is a son; tR^iiyaa = the third refuge;
GYaatayH = is a near relative; na vidyate = there is no; chaturdhii =
fourth one; iha = here.
verses.translation:
"O, king! A husband is a chief refuge for a woman. Her second refuge is a son. The
third refuge is her near-relative. There is no fourth one here."

verses.sanskrit:
तत्र त्वम् चैव मे न अस्ति रामः च वनम् आश्रितः |

न वनम् गन्तुम् इच्चामि सर्वथा हि हता त्वया || २-६१-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. tatra = in those three refuges; tvam = you; naiva
asi = do not indeed exist; raamastu = Rama on his part; aashritaH = took
refuge; vanam = in a forest; na icchaami = I do not wish; gantum = to
go; vanam = to the forest; nihataa = I have been mined; sarvathaa = in
all ways; tvayaa = b you.
verses.translation:
"Among those three refuges, you do not indeed exist. Rama on his part took refuge in
a forest and I do not wish to go there. Thus, I have been ruined in all ways, by you."

verses.sanskrit:
हतम् त्वया राज्यम् इदम् सराष्ट्रम् |

हतः तथा आत्मा सह मन्त्रिभिः च |

हता सपुत्रा अस्मि हताः च पौराः |

सुतः च भार्या च तव प्रहृष्टौ || २-६१-२६
verses.translation:
"This kingdom along with neighboring states has been ruined by you. You yourself
along with ministers have been ruined. I along with my son are ruined. Citizens too are ruined.
Your son Bharata and your wife Kaikeyi only are delighted."

verses.sanskrit:
इमाम् गिरम् दारुण शब्द संश्रिताम् |

निशम्य राजा अपि मुमोह दुह्खितः |

ततः स शोकम् प्रविवेश पार्थिवः |

स्वदुष्कृतम् च अपि पुनः तदा अस्मरत् || २-६१-२७

sargas.title: Sarga 62
sargas.introduction:
After hearing harsh words from Kausalya, king Dasaratha loses consciousness and
restores it after a long time. Afterwards, Kausalya repents for her mistake of speaking such crude
words and consoles the king with her reconciliatory words. Overcome by grief, Dasaratha fell into
the grip, Dasaratha fell into the grip of slumber as the night prevailed.

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् तु क्रुद्धया राजा राम मात्रा सशोकया |

श्रावितः परुषम् वाक्यम् चिन्तयाम् आस दुह्खितः || २-६२-१

चिन्तयित्वा स च नृपो मुमोह व्याकुलेन्द्रियः |

अथ दीर्घेण कालेन सम्ज्ञामाप परतपः || २-६२-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. saH = that; nR^ipaH = king; cintyitvaa = (thus)
thought over; vyaakulendriyaH = perplexed in mind; mumoha = and lost his
consciousness; atha = thereafter; paramtapaH = that king who scorches the
enemies; aapa = regained; samj~aam = his; consciousness; diirgheNa =
after a long; kaalena = time.

verses.sanskrit:
स सम्ज्ञाअमुपलब्यैव दीर्घमुष्णम् च निःससन् |

कौसल्याम् पार्श्वतो दृष्ट्वा ततश्चिन्तामुपागमत् || २-६२-३

तस्य चिन्तयमानस्य प्रत्यभात् कर्म दुष्कृतम् |

यद् अनेन कृतम् पूर्वम् अज्ञानात् शब्द वेधिना || २-६२-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. cintayamaanasya = as he brooded over; duSkR^itam = the
sinful; karma = deed; yat = that; kR^itam = was done; anena =
by him; aJNaanaat = out of ignorance; puurvam = long ago; shabda
vidhinaa = by shooting with an arrow an unseen object; the sound of which is only heard;
pratyabhaat = was recalled in his mind.

verses.sanskrit:
अमनाः तेन शोकेन राम शोकेन च प्रभुः |

द्वाभ्यामपि महाराजः शोकाब्यामभितप्यतो || २-६२-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. prabhuH = the mighty; mahaaraajaH = emperor; tena
shokena = by that agony; amanaaH = and low spirited; raama shokena = by the
agony for Rama; anvatapyata = felt tormented; advabhyaam = by duel;
shokaabhyaam api = grief.

verses.sanskrit:
दह्यमानः तु शोकाभ्याम् कौसल्याम् आह भू पतिः |

वेपमानोऽञ्जलिम् कृत्वा प्रसादर्तमवाङ्मुखः || २-६२-६

प्रसादये त्वाम् कौसल्ये रचितः अयम् मया अन्जलिः |

वत्सला च आनृशंसा च त्वम् हि नित्यम् परेष्व् अपि || २-६२-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. Kausalyaa = O Kausalya; tvaam prasaadaye = I seek your
grace; ayam = this; anjaliH = joining of palms; rachiteH = is set out;
mayaa = by me; tvam = you; nityam = are always; vatshalaa =
affectionate; pareSvapi = and even towards others; anR^ishamsaaca hi = and even
indeed king.
verses.translation:
"O, Kausalya! I seek your grace. This joining of palms is set out by me. You are
always affectionate even towards others and even indeed kind."

verses.sanskrit:
भर्ता तु खलु नारीणाम् गुणवान् निर्गुणो अपि वा |

धर्मम् विमृशमानानाम् प्रत्यक्षम् देवि दैवतम् || २-६२-८
verses.translation:
"O, queen! For women reflecting on righteousness, a husband whether he is virtuous
or worthless, is a visible god indeed."

verses.sanskrit:
सा त्वम् धर्म परा नित्यम् दृष्ट लोक पर अवर |

न अर्हसे विप्रियम् वक्तुम् दुह्खिता अपि सुदुह्खितम् || २-६२-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. saa tvam = you as such; nityam dharmaparaa = who is ever
intent on virtue; dR^iSTa loka paraavaraa = who has understood good and evil fortunes in
the world; duHkhita.api = even if grief-stricken; naarhasi = ought not;
vaktum = to speak; vipriyam = unpleasant words; suduHkhitam = to me; who
is too much in distress.
verses.translation:
"You as such, who is ever intent on virtue, who has understood good and evil
fortunes in the world, even if grief-stricken, ought not to have spoken unpleasant words to me, who
is too much in distress."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् वाक्यम् करुणम् राज्ञः श्रुत्वा दीनस्य भाषितम् |

कौसल्या व्यसृजद् बाष्पम् प्रणाली इव नव उदकम् || २-६२-१०

स मूद्र्ह्नि बद्ध्वा रुदती राज्ञः पद्मम् इव अन्जलिम् |

सम्भ्रमात् अब्रवीत् त्रस्ता त्वरमाण अक्षरम् वचः || २-६२-११

प्रसीद शिरसा याचे भूमौ निततिता अस्मि ते |

याचिता अस्मि हता देव हन्तव्या अहम् न हि त्वया || २-६२-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. deva = O; king; yaa ca = I appeal; te = to you;
shirasaa = with bowed head; nivataa asmi = I lie prostrate; bhuumau = on
the floor; hataa asmi = I am ruined; aham = I; kSantavyaahi = an not
indeed to be forgiven; tvayaa = by you.
verses.translation:
"O, king! I appeal to you with my bowed head. I lie prostrate on the floor. I am
ruined. I am not to be forgiven indeed by you."

verses.sanskrit:
न एषा हि सा स्त्री भवति श्लाघनीयेन धीमता |

उभयोः लोकयोः वीर पत्या या सम्प्रसाद्यते || २-६२-१३
verses.translation:
"O, Valiant man! In both the worlds, it is ill-becoming of a woman, being
propitiated by her husband, who is praise worthy and possessing good disposition."

verses.sanskrit:
जानामि धर्मम् धर्मज्ञ त्वाम् जाने सत्यवादिनम् |

पुत्र शोक आर्तया तत् तु मया किम् अपि भाषितम् || २-६२-१४
verses.translation:
"O, valiant man! In both the worlds, it of a woman, being propitiated by her
husband, who is praise worthy and possessing good disposition."

verses.sanskrit:
शोको नाशयते धैर्यम् शोको नाशयते श्रुतम् |

शोको नाशयते सर्वम् न अस्ति शोक समः रिपुः || २-६२-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. shokaH = grief; naashayate = ruins; dairyam =
courage; shokaH = grief; naashayate = ruins; shrutam = sacred learning;
shokaH = all; naasti = there is not; ripuH = enemy; shokasamaH
= like grief.
verses.translation:
"Grief ruins courage. Grief ruins sacred learning, grief ruins all. There is no
enemy like grief."

verses.sanskrit:
शक्यम् आपतितः सोढुम् प्रहरः रिपु हस्ततः |

सोढुम् आपतितः शोकः सुसूक्ष्मः अपि न शक्यते || २-६२-१६
verses.translation:
"A hitting that descended from the hands of an enemy is possible to be tolerated.
But, the grief suddenly descended, even if so small, is not possible to be tolerated."

verses.sanskrit:
दर्मज्ञाः श्रुतिमन्तोऽपि चिन्नधर्मार्थसंशयाः |

यतयो वीर मुह्यन्ति शोकसम्मूढचेतसः || २-६२-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. viira = O; valiant man; yatayaH.api = even ascetis;
dharmaJNaaH = who know righteousness; shrutimantaH = who have learnt sacred
texts; chinna dharmaartha samshayaaH = who have retn asunder doubts relating to religious
merit and wealth; muhyanti = go astray; shoka sammuuDha cetasaH = having their
minds infatuated with grief.
verses.translation:
"O, valiant man! Even ascetics, who know righteousness, who have learnt sacred texts
and who have rent asunder doubts relating to religious merit and wealth, go astray having their
minds infatuated with grief."

verses.sanskrit:
वन वासाय रामस्य पन्च रात्रः अद्य गण्यते |

यः शोक हत हर्षायाः पन्च वर्ष उपमः मम || २-६२-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. gaNyate = it is counted; adya = today;
paNcaraatraH = as five days; raamasya = since Rama; vanavaasaaya = has
gone to exile; yaH = which; mama = for me; shoka hata harSayaaH = whose
happiness is mined by grief; paNcavarSopamaH = equal to five years.
verses.translation:
"Today it is counted as five days since Rama has gone to exile. It is equal to five
years for me, since grief has ruined my happiness."

verses.sanskrit:
तम् हि चिन्तयमानायाः शोको अयम् हृदि वर्धते |

अदीनाम् इव वेगेन समुद्र सलिलम् महत् || २-६२-१९
verses.translation:
"While I think of Rama, this grief in my heart is increasing, like water in a great
ocean increases with the fast streaming of rivers.

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् हि कथयन्त्याः तु कौसल्यायाः शुभम् वचः |

मन्द रश्मिर् अभूत् सुर्यो रजनी च अभ्यवर्तत || २-६२-२०

तथ प्रह्लादितः वाक्यैः देव्या कौसल्यया नृपः |

शोकेन च समाक्रान्तः निद्राया वशम् एयिवान् || २-६२-२१
verses.wordMeaning:
21. nR^ipaH = the king; tathaa = thus; prasaaditaH
= cheered up; devyaa = by the queen; kausalyayaa = Kausalya; eyivaan =
got; vasham = subjected; nidraayaaH = to sleep; samaakraantaH ca = after
having been overcome; shokena = be grief.

sargas.title: Sarga 63
sargas.introduction:
Dasaratha woke up with anxious thoughts. Recalling his earlier sin, he starts
recounting the story of a young ascetic to Kausalya. He says that while he was Prince Regent of
Ayodhya, he went out for hunting in a forest one day and heard the sound of a hermit-boy filling
his pitcher with water by suberging it into Sarayu River. Mistaking the gurgling sound for
trumpeting of an elephant the prince hit the boy with an arrow, which went deep into the body and
mortally wounded him. On approaching the victim, he discovered the fatal blunder and tendered his
heart felt apologies to the hermit boy, who asked him to extract the arrow from his body and inform
his parents. The boy died soon after the arrow was drawn out from the body.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रतिबुद्धो मुहुर् तेन शोक उपहत चेतनः |

अथ राजा दशरथः स चिन्ताम् अभ्यपद्यत || २-६३-१

राम लक्ष्मणयोः चैव विवासात् वासव उपमम् |

आविवेश उपसर्गः तम् तमः सूर्यम् इव आसुरम् || २-६३-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. vivaasaat = due to the exile; raama lakSmaNayoH = of Rama
and Lakshmana; upasargaH = a great calamity; aavivesha = befell; tam =
on that Dasaratha; vaasavopamam = who is equal to Indra; suuryamiva = like sun
(seized); tamaH = by obscurity; asuram = which is attributed to the demon
Rahu.

verses.sanskrit:
सभार्ये निर्गते रामे कौसल्याम् कोसलेश्वरः |

विवक्षुरसितापाङ्गाम् स्मृवा दुष्कृतमात्मनः || २-६३-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. raame = after Rama; nirgate sati = having departed (to
the forest); sabhaarye = along with his wife; kosaleshvaraH = Dasaratha the king
of Kosala; smR^itvaa = recalling aatmanaH = his; duSkR^itam = sin;
vivakSuH = wanted to speak; kausalyaam = to Kausalya; astaapaaNgaam =
whose eye-corners were dark in colour.

verses.sanskrit:
स राजा रजनीम् षष्ठीम् रामे प्रव्रजिते वनम् |

अर्ध रात्रे दशरथः संस्मरन् दुष्कृतम् कृतम् || २-६३-४

स राजा पुत्रशोकार्तः स्मरन् दुष्कृतमात्मनः |

कौसल्याम् पुत्र शोक आर्ताम् इदम् वचनम् अब्रवीत् || २-६३-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. saH raajaa = that king; putrashokaartaH = afflicted by
grief for his son; smaram = recalling; aatmanaH = his; duSkR^itam = sin;
abraviit = spoke; idam = these; vachanam = words; kausalyaam =
to Kausalya; putrashokaartaam = who was suffering from sorrow for her son.

verses.sanskrit:
यद् आचरति कल्याणि शुभम् वा यदि वा अशुभम् |

तत् एव लभते भद्रे कर्ता कर्मजम् आत्मनः || २-६३-६
verses.translation:
"O, blessed lady! O gracious one! A doer reaps surely the fruit of his own deeds
corresponding to the nature of work either good or bad, of that which he does!"

verses.sanskrit:
गुरु लाघवम् अर्थानाम् आरम्भे कर्मणाम् फलम् |

दोषम् वा यो न जानाति स बालैति ह उच्यते || २-६३-७
verses.translation:
"He who does not consider, while undertaking actions, the relative value of their
fruits as well as the utilities or defects accruing from them, is surely called a fool."

verses.sanskrit:
कश्चित् आम्र वणम् चित्त्वा पलाशामः च निषिन्चति |

पुष्पम् दृष्ट्वा फले गृध्नुः स शोचति फल आगमे || २-६३-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. kashchit = anyone; dR^iSTvaa = seeing; puSpam =
their flowers; gR^idhnuH = and greedily desirous of; phale = their; fruits;
niSiNchati = nourishes; palaashran = Palasa trees; chhittvaa = by
cutting off; aamravanam = mango groves; saH = he; shochati = will come
to grief; phalaagame = after arrival of fruit."
verses.translation:
"Anyone, who after seeing their (charming and big) flowers, greedily desires their
(large and luscious) fruits and nourishes Palasa trees, by cutting off a mongo grove, he will come
to grief after bearing of their fruit."

verses.sanskrit:
अविज्ञाय फलम् यो हि कर्म त्वेवानुधावति |

स शोचेत्फलवेळायाम् यथा किंशुकसेचकः || २-६३-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. yaH = who; avijJNaaya = without knowing; phalam
= the fuit; anudhaavati = follows running; karmatu eva = towards action;
saH = he kimshukaschakaH iva = as a nourisher of Kimsuka trees; phalavelaayaam =
at the time of fruit-bearing; shochet = will come to grief.
verses.translation:
"He who without knowing the result goes running towards action, will come to grief
as a nourisher of Kimsuka trees coming to grief at the time of their fruit-bearing."

verses.sanskrit:
सो अहम् आम्र वणम् चित्त्वा पलाशामः च न्यषेचयम् |

रामम् फल आगमे त्यक्त्वा पश्चात् शोचामि दुर्मतिः || २-६३-१०
verses.translation:
"I nourished Palasa trees, by cutting off mango grove. Being wicked-minded in
abandoning Rama at the time of arrival of fruit, I am lamenting thereafter."

verses.sanskrit:
लब्ध शब्देन कौसल्ये कुमारेण धनुष्मता |

कुमारः शब्द वेधी इति मया पापम् इदम् कृतम् || २-६३-११
verses.translation:
"This sin was done by me while I was young and wielding a bow. At that time, I was
famous as a young man, who can shoot heard (but unseen) object by an arrow, O Kausalya!"

verses.sanskrit:
तत् इदम् मे अनुसम्प्राप्तम् देवि दुह्खम् स्वयम् कृतम् |

सम्मोहात् इह बालेन यथा स्यात् भक्षितम् विषम् || २-६३-१२
verses.translation:
"This grief, obtained by my own accord, has befallen me, as by a boy in ignorance
eating poison in the world; O, Kausalya!"

verses.sanskrit:
यथान्यः पुरुषः कश्चित्पलाशैर्मोओहितो भवेत् |

एवम् मम अपि अविज्ञातम् शब्द वेध्यमयम् फलम् || २-६३-१३
verses.translation:
"How the other man became disillusioned by Palasa trees, I also became disillusioned
in the same manner, without knowing accurately the correct result of hitting a target by means of
sound only."

verses.sanskrit:
देव्य् अनूढा त्वम् अभवो युव राजो भवाम्य् अहम् |

ततः प्रावृड् अनुप्राप्ता मद काम विवर्धिनी || २-६३-१४
verses.translation:
"O, Kausalya! You were unmarried till then and I was the Prince Regent. Then came
the rainy season which enhanced excitement and desire (for hunting)."

verses.sanskrit:
उपास्यहि रसान् भौमांस् तप्त्वा च जगद् अंशुभिः |

परेत आचरिताम् भीमाम् रविर् आविशते दिशम् || २-६३-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. raviH = the Sun; upaasya = partaking; rasaan =
the fluid; bhaumaan = relating to earth; taptvaacha = and warming; jagat
= the world; amshubhiH = with its rays; aavishat = entered; bhiimaam =
the terrible; disham = (southern) quarter; paretaacharitaam = haunted by
spirits.
verses.translation:
"The sun, partaking fluids from earth and warming the world with its rays, entered
the terrible southern quarter haunted by spirits."

verses.sanskrit:
उष्णम् अन्तर् दधे सद्यः स्निग्धा ददृशिरे घनाः |

ततः जहृषिरे सर्वे भेक सारन्ग बर्हिणः || २-६३-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. uSNam = heat; antardadhe = disappeared; sadyaH
= suddenly; snigdhaaH = resplendent; ghanaaH = clouds; daadR^ishire =
were seen; tataH = thereafter; sarve = all; bhekasaaraNga barihiNaH =
frogs; cuckoos and peacocks; kajR^iSire = were full of joy."
verses.translation:
"Heat disappeared suddenly. Resplendent clouds appeared in the sky. All frogs,
cuckoos and peacocks were full of joy."

verses.sanskrit:
क्लिन्नपक्षोत्तराः स्नाताः कृच्च्रादिव वतत्रिणः |

वृष्टिवातावधूताग्रान् पादपानभिपेदिरे || २-६३-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. patatriNaH = birds; klinnapakSottaraaH = with moistened
top of their wings; snaataaH = af if bathed; kR^ichchraat iva = with great
difficulty; abhipadire = reached; paadapaan = the trees;
vR^iSTivaataavadhuutaagraan = the tops of which were being shaken off by rain and
wind."
verses.translation:
"Birds with moistened top of their wings as if they bathed, reached with great
difficulty the trees, the tops of which were shaken off by rain and wind."

verses.sanskrit:
पतितेन अम्भसा चन्नः पतमानेन च असकृत् |

आबभौ मत्त सारन्गः तोय राशिर् इव अचलः || २-६३-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. chhannaH = enveloped; ambhasaa = by water;
patamaanena = falling; asakR^it = again and again; patitena = (on the
water already) fallen; achalaH = the mountain; mattasaarNgaH = with antelopes in
rut; ababhau = glowed; toyaraashiriva = like an ocean.
verses.translation:
"Enveloped by water falling again and again on the water already fallen, the
mountain with its antelopes in rut, glowed like an ocean."

verses.sanskrit:
पाण्डुरारुणवर्णानि स्रोओतांसि विमलान्यपि |

सुस्रुवुर्गिरिधातुभ्यः सभस्मानि भुजङ्गवत् || २-६३-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. srotaamsi = the water-streams; vimalaanyapi = thought
crystal clear; susrusuH = flowed; bhujaN^gavat = like serpents; paaNDuraaruNa
varNaani = with white and red colours; sabhasmaani = along with ashes;
giridhaatubhyaH = or mountain-minerals
verses.translation:
"Although the water-streams were crystal clear, they flowed in serpentine courses,
with white and red colours because of their admixture with ashes and mountain-minerals on the
way."

verses.sanskrit:
आकुलारुणतोयानि स्रोओतांसि विमलान्यपि |

उन्मार्गजलवाहीनि बभूवुर्जलदागमे || २-६३-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. srotaamsi = the water-streams; vimalaanyapi = even
though crystal-clear; babhuuvuH = became; jalagaamini aadulaaruNa toyaami =
churned with reddish water; unmaargajalogaamini = carrying water by a wrong way;
jaladaagame = after the onset of rain.
verses.translation:
"The water-streams, which were crystal-clear till then, became churned with reddish
waters, carrying water by a wrong way, after the onset of rain."

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मिन्न् अतिसुखे काले धनुष्मान् इषुमान् रथी |

व्यायाम कृत सम्कल्पः सरयूम् अन्वगाम् नदीम् || २-६३-२१
verses.wordMeaning:
21. vyaayaama kR^ita samkalpaH = with a wish to do (Hunting as) an
exercise; tasmin kaale = in that season; atisukhe = which was most comfortable;
anvagaam = I went along; sarayuunadiim = Sarayu River; rathii = going in
a chariot; dhanuSmaan; wearing a bow; ishhumaan = and arrows.
verses.translation:
"With a wish to do hunting as an exercise in that most comfortable season, I went
along Sarayu River, in a chariot, wearing bow and arrows."

verses.sanskrit:
निपाने महिषम् रात्रौ गजम् वा अभ्यागतम् नदीम् |

अन्यम् वा श्वा पदम् कंचिज् जिघांसुर् अजित इन्द्रियः || २-६३-२२

तस्मिंस्तत्राहमेकान्ते रात्रौ विवृतकार्मुकः |
verses.wordMeaning:
22. tatra = there; aham = I; ajitendriyaH = without
my senses subdued; jihaamsuH = with an intent to kill; mahiSam = a (wild)
buffalo; gajamvaa = or an elephant; anyam shvaapadam vaa = or any other wild
animal; abhyaagatam = coming; nadiim = the river; raatrau = in the
night; tasmin = at that place; nipaame = for drinking water; vivR^ita
kaarmukaH = I was ready with my bow; ekaante = at a solitary place.
verses.translation:
"Without my senses under control and with an intent to kill there a (wild) buffalo
or an elephant or any other wild animal coming in the night at that place for the purpose of
drinking water, I was ready with my bow at a solitary place."

verses.sanskrit:
तत्राहम् सम्वृतम् वन्यम् हतवांस्तीरमागतम् || २-६३-२३

अन्यम् चापि मृगम् हिंस्रम् शब्दम् श्रुत्वाभु पागतम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
23. shrutvaa = hearing; shabdam = the sound; aham =
I; hatavaan = killed; samvR^itam = turning towards (it); vanyam = a wild
animal; to; tiiram aagata = which came to bank (of Sarayu River); anyam = (and)
another; hrimsram = violent; mR^igam cha = animal too; abhyupaagatam =
which arrived; tatra = there.
verses.translation:
"Hearing the sound, I killed turning towards it, a wild animal, which came to the
bank of Sarayu River and another violent beast too which came there."

verses.sanskrit:
अथ अन्ध कारे तु अश्रौषम् जले कुम्भस्य पर्यतः || २-६३-२४

अचक्षुर् विषये घोषम् वारणस्य इव नर्दतः |
verses.translation:
"Meanwhile, in that darkness and not within the react of the eye, I heard the sound
of a pitcher being filled up, the sound of which appeared like that of an elephant."

verses.sanskrit:
ततः अहम् शरम् उद्धृत्य दीप्तम् आशी विष उपमम् || २-६३-२५

शब्दम् प्रति गजप्रेप्सुरभिलक्ष्य त्वपातयम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
25. tataH = then; gajaprepsuH = with an intent to obtain the
elephant; aham = I; uddhR^itya = drew out; abhilakSya = targeted;
shabdam prati = towards that sound; apaatayam = and released; sharam =
an arrow; diiptam = shining; ashiiviSopamam = and equal to a serpent.
verses.translation:
"With an intent to hit that elephant, I drew out a shining arrow resembling a
serpent, targeted towards the direction sound and discharged it."

verses.sanskrit:
अमुन्चम् निशितम् बाणम् अहम् आशी विष उपमम् || २-६३-२६

तत्र वाग् उषसि व्यक्ता प्रादुर् आसीद् वन ओकसः |

हा हा इति पततः तोये बाणाभिहतमर्मणः || २-६३-२७
verses.translation:
"I released a sharp arrow, resembling a serpent. There, at dawn, arose a distinct
voice uttering 'Ah! Ah!' of a forest-inhabitant, dropping down in water after his vital part was
hit by an arrow."

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मिन्निपतिते बाणे वागभूत्तत्र मानुषी |

कथम् अस्मद् विधे शस्त्रम् निपतेत् तु तपस्विनि || २-६३-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. tasmin = (while) that; baaNe = arrow; nipatite
= was attacking; maanushhu = a human; vaak = voice; abhuut = was
manifest; tatra = there; katham = how; shastram = a weapon;
nipatettu = did rush upon; tapasvini = an ascetic; asmadvidhe = like
me?
verses.translation:
"While that arrow was attacking a human voice there was heard, saying, 'Oh, how a
weapon did rush upon an ascetic like me?' "

verses.sanskrit:
प्रविविक्ताम् नदीम् रात्राव् उदाहारः अहम् आगतः |

इषुणा अभिहतः केन कस्य वा किम् कृतम् मया || २-६३-२९

ऋषेर् हि न्यस्त दण्डस्य वने वन्येन जीवतः |

कथम् नु शस्त्रेण वधो मद् विधस्य विधीयते || २-६३-३०
verses.translation:
"To a seer like me, of having laid down violence and living a fruits and roots of
wild plants in the forest, how a killing by an arrow is enjoined?"

verses.sanskrit:
जटा भार धरस्य एव वल्कल अजिन वाससः |

को वधेन मम अर्थी स्यात् किम् वा अस्य अपकृतम् मया || २-६३-३१
verses.translation:
"Who is desirous of killing me, wearing a mass of locks of hair and clad with bark
of trees and deer-skin? What harm was done to him by me?"

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् निष्फलम् आरब्धम् केवल अनर्थ सम्हितम् |

न कश्चित् साधु मन्येत यथैव गुरु तल्पगम् || २-६३-३२
verses.wordMeaning:
32. kevaalaanarthasamhitam = concerning only with a useless
objective; niSphalam = a vain act; aarabdham = is undertaken; evam = in
this way; na kashchit = no one; manyate = will admire him; as for instance who
violates his teacher's bed; saadhu = as good.
verses.translation:
"A vain act concerning only with a useless objective is undertaken in this manner.
No one will admire him, as for instance like any man seeking the bed of his preceptor's wife, as
good."

verses.sanskrit:
नहम् तथा अनुशोचामि जीवित क्षयम् आत्मनः |

मातरम् पितरम् च उभाव् अनुशोचामि मद् विधे || २-६३-३३
verses.wordMeaning:
33. aham = I; naanushochaami = do not worry; tathaa
= that much; aatmanaH = of my; jiivitakSayam = destroyed of life;
madvadhe = In the case of my death;; anushochaami = I worry; ubhau =
both; maataram = for my mother; pitaaramcha = and father.
verses.translation:
"I do not worry that much of my death. I worry both for my mother and father, in the
case of my death."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् एतान् मिथुनम् वृद्धम् चिर कालभृतम् मया |

मयि पन्चत्वम् आपन्ने काम् वृत्तिम् वर्तयिष्यति || २-६३-३४

वृद्धौ च माता पितराव् अहम् च एक इषुणा हतः |

केन स्म निहताः सर्वे सुबालेन अकृत आत्मना || २-६३-३५
verses.translation:
"I and my aged parents are killed by a single arrow. By a man without self control
and being very much a fool, all of as are virtually killed."

verses.sanskrit:
तम् गिरम् करुणाम् श्रुत्वा मम धर्म अनुकान्क्षिणः |

कराभ्याम् सशरम् चापम् व्यथितस्य अपतत् भुवि || २-६३-३६
verses.wordMeaning:
36. shrutvaa = Hearing; taamgiram = those words;
karaNaam = which were pitiable; karaabhyaam = from the hands; mama = of
me; dharmaanukaaNkSiNaH = who is intent on righteousness; vyathitasya = and
having anguish; anpatat = fell; chaapam = the bow; sasharam = with
arrows."
verses.translation:
"Hearing those pitiable words and since I was always intent on righteousness, I felt
distressed. The bow and arrow fell down from my hands to the ground."

verses.sanskrit:
तस्याहम् करुणम् श्रुत्वा निशि लालपतो बहु |

सम्भ्रानतः शोकवेगेन भृशमास विचेतनः || २-६३-३७
verses.wordMeaning:
37. shrutvaa = hearing; tasya = his; karuNam =
pathetic cry; laalavataH = weeping; bahu = very much; nishi = during the
night; asa = I became; vichetasaH = unconscious; bhR^isham = frequently;
sambhraantaH = utterly bewildered ( as I was); shokavegena = due to flare-up of
grief."
verses.translation:
"Hearing his pathetic cry, weeping profusely during the night, I became unconscious
frequently, utterly bewildered as I was, due to flare-up of my grief."

verses.sanskrit:
तम् देशम् अहम् आगम्य दीन सत्त्वः सुदुर्मनाः |

अपश्यम् इषुणा तीरे सरय्वाः तापसम् हतम् || २-६३-३८

अवकीर्णजटाभारम् प्रविद्धकलशोदकम् |

पासुशोणितदिग्धाङ्गम् शयानम् शल्यपीडितम् || २-६३-३९
verses.translation:
"Distressed in mind and with much sorrowful feeling, I reached that place and saw
the ascetic, hit by the arrow at the bank of Sarayu River, with his tresses of hair scattered, his
pitcher of water thrown asunder, having his limbs anointed with sand and blood lying down as he
was, hurt by the arrow."

verses.sanskrit:
स माम् उद्वीक्ष्य नेत्राभ्याम् त्रस्तम् अस्वस्थ चेतसम् |

इति उवाच वचः क्रूरम् दिधक्षन्न् इव तेजसा || || २-६३-४०

किम् तव अपकृतम् राजन् वने निवसता मया |

जिहीर्षिउर् अम्भो गुर्व् अर्थम् यद् अहम् ताडितः त्वया || २-६३-४१
verses.translation:
"O, king! I, who was carrying water for my parents, was hit by you. What was the
harm done to you by me who is residing in a forest?"

verses.sanskrit:
एकेन खलु बाणेन मर्मणि अभिहते मयि |

द्वाव् अन्धौ निहतौ वृद्धौ माता जनयिता च मे || २-६३-४२

तौ नूनम् दुर्बलाव् अन्धौ मत् प्रतीक्षौ पिपासितौ |

चिरम् आशा कृताम् तृष्णाम् कष्टाम् संधारयिष्यतः || २-६३-४३
verses.wordMeaning:
43. tau = both of them; durbalau = weak; andhau =
blind; pipaasitau = and thirsty; smadhaavayiSyataH = might have regrained;
tR^iSNaam = theri thirst; kaSTaam = with difficulty; aashaakR^itaam =
and attended with expectation; chiram = since long; nuunam = I am sure.
verses.translation:
"Both of them, weak blind and thirsty, might have refrained their thirst with
difficulty and waited with expectation of my arrival since long. I am sure."

verses.sanskrit:
न नूनम् तपसो वा अस्ति फल योगः श्रुतस्य वा |

पिता यन् माम् न जानाति शयानम् पतितम् भुवि || २-६३-४४

जानन्न् अपि च किम् कुर्यात् अशक्तिर् अपरिक्रमः |

चिद्यमानम् इव अशक्तः त्रातुम् अन्यो नगो नगम् || २-६३-४५
verses.translation:
"What my disabled father, unable to walk around can do, even if he knows like a tree
unable to protect another tree which is being cut off?"

verses.sanskrit:
पितुस् त्वम् एव मे गत्वा शीघ्रम् आचक्ष्व राघव |

न त्वाम् अनुदहेत् क्रुद्धो वनम् वह्निर् इव एधितः || २-६३-४६

इयम् एक पदी राजन् यतः मे पितुर् आश्रमः |

तम् प्रसादय गत्वा त्वम् न त्वाम् स कुपितः शपेत् || २-६३-४७

विशल्यम् कुरु माम् राजन् मर्म मे निशितः शरः |

रुणद्धि मृदु स उत्सेधम् तीरम् अम्बु रयो यथा || २-६३-४८

सशल्यः क्लिश्यते प्राणैर्विशल्यो विनशिष्यति |

इति मामविशच्चिन्ता तस्य शल्यापकर्षणे || २-६३-४९
verses.translation:
"While extracting the arrow-head from him, this thought entered my mind. With
arrow-head in his body he will suffer, even though surviving. But after extracting the arrow-head,
he will die."

verses.sanskrit:
दुःखितस्य च दीनस्य मम शोकातुरस्य च |

लक्ष्यामास हृदये चिन्ताम् मुनिसुत स्तदा || २-६३-५०
verses.wordMeaning:
50. tadaa = then; munisutaH = the son of the sage;
lakSayaamaasa = perceived; chintaam = the worry; hR^idaye = in the
heart; mama = of myself; duHkhitasya = who was lamenting; diinasya =
pitiable; shokaaturasya = suffering from anguish.
verses.translation:
"Then, the son of the sage perceived worry in the heart of myself, who was lamenting
pitiably, suffering from anguish."

verses.sanskrit:
ताम्यमानः स माम् दुःखादुवाच परमार्तवत् |

सीदमानो विवृत्ताङ्गो वेष्टमानो गतः क्षयम् || २-६३-५१

संस्तभ्य धैर्येण स्थिरचित्तो भवाम्यहम् |

ब्रह्महत्याकृतम् पापम् हृदयादपनीयताम् || २-६३-५२

न द्विजातिर् अहम् राजन् मा भूत् ते मनसो व्यथा |

शूद्रायाम् अस्मि वैश्येन जातः जन पद अधिप || २-६३-५३
verses.wordMeaning:
53. raajan = O; king; janapadaadhipaa = the ruler of the
country! aham = I; na = am not; dvijaatiH = a Brahmana; maabhuut vyathaa
= let there be no agony; manasaH = in your mind; asmi = I am; jaataH =
born; shuudraayaam = = through a Sudra woman; vaishyena = by Vysya.

verses.sanskrit:
इति इव वदतः कृच्च्रात् बाण अभिहत मर्मणः |

विघूर्णतो विचेष्टस्य वेपमाचस्य भूतले || २-६३-५४

तस्य तु आनम्यमानस्य तम् बाणम् अहम् उद्धरम् |

तस्य त्वानम्यमानस्य तम् बाणामहमुद्धरम् || २-६३-५५
verses.translation:
"While he was speaking in that manner with great difficulty, his vital part having
been hit by an arrow and was rolling on the ground, now exerting himself, now trembling and
sinking, I drew out that arrow from him. That sage looked up towards me in fear and relinquished
his life."

verses.sanskrit:
जल आर्द्र गात्रम् तु विलप्य कृच्चान् |

मर्म व्रणम् सम्ततम् उच्चसन्तम् |

ततः सरय्वाम् तम् अहम् शयानम् |

समीक्ष्य भद्रे सुभृशम् विषण्णः || २-६३-५६
verses.translation:
"O, my dear Kausalya! Seeing him, with his body drenched in water, weeping with
anguish, unceasingly sighing his breath, with injury in his vital part and lying down in River
Sarayu as he was, I became very much grief-stricken."
verses.commentary:
© 2003, K. M. K. Murthy [Revised - September
08]
