sargas.title: Sarga 70
sargas.introduction:
The messengers entered the city of Rajagriha and meet the king Kekeya and prince
Yudhajit. They request Bharata to start for Ayodhya urgently. Bharata seeks permission from king
Kekeya his maternal uncle. King Kekeya gives permission to Bharata to leave for Ayodhya and bestows
him various gifts. Bharata and Satrughna along with men. elephants and horses start his journey for
Ayodhya.

verses.sanskrit:
भरते ब्रुवति स्वप्नम् दूताः ते क्लान्त वाहनाः |

प्रविश्य असह्य परिखम् रम्यम् राज गृहम् पुरम् || २-७०-१

समागम्य तु राज्ञा च राज पुत्रेण च अर्चिताः |

राज्ञः पादौ गृहीत्वा तु तम् ऊचुर् भरतम् वचः || २-७०-२

पुरोहितः त्वा कुशलम् प्राह सर्वे च मन्त्रिणः |

त्वरमाणः च निर्याहि कृत्यम् आत्ययिकम् त्वया || २-७०-३
verses.translation:
"Vasistha, the family-priest and all the ministers were asking about your welfare.
Come forth indeed quickly. There is an urgent work with you."

verses.sanskrit:
इमानि च महार्हाणि वस्त्राण्य़ाभरणानि च |

प्रतिगृह्य विशालाक्ष मातुलस्य च दापय || २-७०-४
verses.translation:
"O, Bharata the wide-eyed man! Take these very valuable clothes and jewels and give
them to your maternal uncle"

verses.sanskrit:
अत्र विंशति कोट्यः तु नृपतेर् मातुलस्य ते |

दश कोट्यः तु सम्पूर्णाः तथैव च नृप आत्मज || २-७०-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. nR^ipaatmaja = O; prince! Atra = in these;
vimshatikoTyaH = a hundred crores; nR^ipataH = are to be given to the king
Kekeya; tathaivacha = and like wise; sampuurNaaH = a full; dashakoTyastu
= ten crores ; te = your; maatulasya = maternal uncle."
verses.translation:
"O, prince! In these jewels and clothes, a worth of hundred crores is to be given to
the king Kekeya and likewise a worth of ten crores in full to your maternal uncle."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रतिगृह्य च तत् सर्वम् स्वनुरक्तः सुहृज् जने |

दूतान् उवाच भरतः कामैः सम्प्रतिपूज्य तान् || २-७०-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. bharataH = Bharata; svanuraktaH = who had a great
affection; suhR^ijjane = towards his kind hearted relatives; pratigR^ihya = took;
tat sarvam = all that; taan duutaan samprati puujya = honored those messengers
greatly; kaamaiH = with gifts of their liking; uvaacha = and spoke (as
follows):

verses.sanskrit:
कच्चित् सुकुशली राजा पिता दशरथो मम |

कच्चिच् च अरागता रामे लक्ष्मणे वा महात्मनि || २-७०-७
verses.translation:
"I hope that king Dasaratha, my father is quite safe. I also hope that Rama and the
high-soled Lakshmana are well."

verses.sanskrit:
आर्या च धर्म निरता धर्मज्ञा धर्म दर्शिनी |

अरोगा च अपि कौसल्या माता रामस्य धीमतः || २-७०-८
verses.translation:
"Is Kausalya, the venerable lady, who is intent on virtue, who knows righteousness,
who visualizes goodness and who is the mother of the wise Rama, also well? "

verses.sanskrit:
कच्चित् सुमित्रा धर्मज्ञा जननी लक्ष्मणस्य या |

शत्रुघ्नस्य च वीरस्य सारोगा च अपि मध्यमा || २-७०-९
verses.translation:
"I hope that Sumitra, who knows righteousness, who is the mother of Lakshmana as
well as the heroic Satrughna and our middle mother, is well."

verses.sanskrit:
आत्म कामा सदा चण्डी क्रोधना प्राज्ञ मानिनी |

अरोगा च अपि कैकेयी माता मे किम् उवाच ह || २-७०-१०
verses.translation:
"As Kaikeyi, who loves herself, a forever fiery lady of wrathful nature, who prides
herself to be highly intelligent and my mother, also well? What did she say?"

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्ताः तु ते दूता भरतेन महात्मना |

ऊचुः सम्प्रश्रितम् वाक्यम् इदम् तम् भरतम् तदा || २-७०-११

कुशलाः ते नर व्याघ्र येषाम् कुशलम् इच्चसि |

श्रीश्च त्वाम् वृणुते पद्मा युज्यताम् चापि ते रकः || २-७०-१२
verses.translation:
"O, tiger among men! Those, of whom you do enquire, are all well. The goddess of
prosperity, holding a lotus in her hand, awaits you. Let your chariot be made ready."

verses.sanskrit:
भरतः च अपि तान् दूतान् एवम् उक्तः अभ्यभाषत |

आपृच्चे अहम् महा राजम् दूताः सम्त्वरयन्ति माम् || २-७०-१३

एवम् उक्त्वा तु तान् दूतान् भरतः पार्थिव आत्मजः |

दूतैः संचोदितः वाक्यम् मातामहम् उवाच ह || २-७०-१४

राजन् पितुर् गमिष्यामि सकाशम् दूत चोदितः |

पुनर् अपि अहम् एष्यामि यदा मे त्वम् स्मरिष्यसि || २-७०-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. raajan = O; king! duutachoditaah = asked by the
messengers to move quickly; gamishhyaami = I am going; pituH = to my father's;
sakaasham = presence. eshhyaami = I shall come; punarapi = again; yadaa
= whenever; tvam = you; smarishhyasi = recall; me = me."
verses.translation:
"O, king! Requested by the messengers to come to Ayodhya urgently, I am going to my
father's presence. I shall again come, whenever you recall me."

verses.sanskrit:
भरतेन एवम् उक्तः तु नृपो मातामहः तदा |

तम् उवाच शुभम् वाक्यम् शिरस्य् आघ्राय राघवम् || २-७०-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. tadaa = then; nR^ipaH = the king Kekeya; maataa
mahaH = the maternal uncle; uktaH = having been spoken; bharatena = by
Bharata; evam = as aforesaid; aaghraaya = smelling; shirasi = the head;
taam raaghavam = of that Bharata; uvaacha = spoke; shubham = these
auspicious ; vaakyaam = words:

verses.sanskrit:
गच्च तात अनुजाने त्वाम् कैकेयी सुप्रजाः त्वया |

मातरम् कुशलम् ब्रूयाः पितरम् च परम् तप || २-७०-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. gachchha = go; taata = my dear child! anujaane = I am
permitting; tvaam = you; tvayaa = by you; kaikeyii = Kaikeyi;
suprajaaH = has a good son; paramtapa = O; the scourge of foes! BruuyaaH
= Ask; kushalam = the welfare; maataram = of your mother; pitaracha =
and father."
verses.translation:
"Go, my dear child! I am permitting you. Kaikeyi is fortunate to have a good son
like you. Bear my greetings to your mother and father."

verses.sanskrit:
पुरोहितम् च कुशलम् ये च अन्ये द्विज सत्तमाः |

तौ च तात महा इष्वासौ भ्रातरु राम लक्ष्मणौ || २-७०-१८
verses.translation:
" My dear child! Pay obeisance to your family priest, the other illustrious Brahmins
and your brothers Rama and Lakshmana the wielders of bows."

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मै हस्ति उत्तमामः चित्रान् कम्बलान् अजिनानि च |

अभिसत्कृत्य कैकेयो भरताय धनम् ददौ || २-७०-१९

रुक्म निष्क सहस्रे द्वे षोडश अश्व शतानि च |

सत्कृत्य कैकेयी पुत्रम् केकयो धनम् आदिशत् || २-७०-२०

तथा अमात्यान् अभिप्रेतान् विश्वास्यामः च गुण अन्वितान् |

ददाव् अश्व पतिः शीघ्रम् भरताय अनुयायिनः || २-७०-२१
verses.wordMeaning:
21. tathaa = likewise; ashvapatiH = aswapati; dadou
= gave; anuyaayinaH = some attendants; abhipretaan = who were dear;
vishvaasyaan = reliable; guNaanvitaan = endowed with virtues; amaatyaan
= and inmates of his palace; bharataaya = to Bharata.

verses.sanskrit:
ऐरावतान् ऐन्द्र शिरान् नागान् वै प्रिय दर्शनान् |

खरान् शीघ्रान् सुसम्युक्तान् मातुलो अस्मै धनम् ददौ || २-७०-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. maatulaH = his maternal uncle; dadou = gave;
dhanam = as presents; naagaan = elephants; airaavataan = of Airavata
race; aindrashiraan = born in Indrasira mountain range; priya darshanaan = and
which were charming to behold; kharaan = and mules; shiighraan = which could walk
quickly; susamyuktaan = and were well trained.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्तः पुरे अतिसम्वृद्धान् व्याघ्र वीर्य बल अन्वितान् |

दम्ष्ट्र आयुधान् महा कायान् शुनः च उपायनम् ददौ || २-७०-२३

स मातामहम् आपृच्च्य मातुलम् च युधा जितम् |

रथम् आरुह्य भरतः शत्रुघ्न सहितः ययौ || २-७०-२४

बभूव ह्यस्य हृदते चिन्ता सुमहती तदा |

त्वरया चापि दूतानाम् स्वप्नस्यापि च दर्शनात् || २-७०-२५

स स्ववेश्माभ्यतिक्रम्य नरनागश्वसम्वृतम् |

प्रपेदे सुमहच्छ्रीमान् राजमार्गमनुत्तमम् || २-७०-२६

अभ्यतीत्य ततोऽपश्यदन्तः पुरमुदारधीः |

ततस्तद्भरतः श्रीमानाविवेशानिवारितः || २-७०-२७

स माता महमापृच्च्य मातुलम् च युधाजितम् |

रथमारुह्य भरतः शत्रुघ्नसहितो ययौ || २-७०-२८

रथान् मण्डल चक्रामः च योजयित्वा परः शतम् |

उष्ट्र गो अश्व खरैः भृत्या भरतम् यान्तम् अन्वयुः || २-७०-२९
verses.wordMeaning:
29. paraH shataan = with more than hundred; rathaan** =
chariots; ratna vichitraamshcha = variegated with jewels; yojayitvaa = yoked to;
ushhTra goshva balaih = camels; bullocks; horses; and mules; bhR^ityaaH =
servants; anvayuH = followed; bharatam = Bharata; yaantam = as he
departed.

verses.sanskrit:
बलेन गुप्तः भरतः महात्मा |

सह आर्यकस्य आत्म समैः अमात्यैः |

आदाय शत्रुघ्नम् अपेत शत्रुर् |

गृहात् ययौ सिद्धैव इन्द्र लोकात् || २-७०-३०
verses.wordMeaning:
30. guptaH = being protected; balena = by the army;
mahaatmaa = the high soled; bharatah = Bharata; apeta shatruH = who was
free from enemies; aadaaya = and taking; shatrughnam = shatrughna; amaatyaiH
saha = along with companies; aatmasamaih = good enough to him; yayon =
started; gR^ihaat = from the palace; aaryakasya = of his venerable maternal
uncle; indralokaadiva = as from the world of celestials; siddhah = a Siddha(a
semi divine being of great purity and perfection and said to possess supernatural faculties).

sargas.title: Sarga 71
sargas.introduction:
Spending seven nights in his journey, crossing various streams and rivers and
passing through many woods and territories, Bharata reaches the city of Ayodhya, from there ,he
finds the city of Ayodhya in a forlorn mood and discusses this situation with the charioteer. With
an apprehensive and gloomy mind, he enters his father's house.

verses.sanskrit:
स प्रान् मुखो राज गृहात् अभिनिर्याय वीर्यवान् |

ततः सुदामाम् द्युतिमान् सम्तीर्वावेक्ष्य ताम् नदीम् || २-७१-१

ह्लादिनीम् दूर पाराम् च प्रत्यक् स्रोतः तरन्गिणीम् |

शतद्रूम् अतरत् श्रीमान् नदीम् इक्ष्वाकु नन्दनः || २-७१-२

एल धाने नदीम् तीर्त्वा प्राप्य च अपर पर्पटान् |

शिलाम् आकुर्वतीम् तीर्त्वाआग्नेयम् शल्य कर्तनम् || २-७१-३

सत्य संधः शुचिः श्रीमान् प्रेक्षमाणः शिला वहाम् |

अत्ययात् स महा शैलान् वनम् चैत्र रथम् प्रति || २-७१-४

सरस्वतीम् च गङ्गाम् च उग्मेन प्रतिपद्य च |

उत्तरम् वीरमत्स्यानाम् भारुण्डम् प्राविशद्वनम् || २-७१-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. w.wM - pratipadya = arriving at; yugmena = the confluence
of; sarasvatiimcha = saraswati river; gaNgaacha = and Ganga river; (Bharata);
pravishat = entered; bhaaruN^Damvanam = the woods of Bharunda; uttaram =
the north; viiramatsyaanaam = of Viramatsya region.

verses.sanskrit:
वेगिनीम् च कुलिन्ग आख्याम् ह्रादिनीम् पर्वत आवृताम् |

यमुनाम् प्राप्य सम्तीर्णो बलम् आश्वासयत् तदा || २-७१-६

शीतीकृत्य तु गात्राणि क्लान्तान् आश्वास्य वाजिनः |

तत्र स्नात्वा च पीत्वा च प्रायात् आदाय च उदकम् || २-७१-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. shiitiikR^iya = making cool; gaatraaNi = the limbs;
klaantaan = of the tired; vaajinaH = horses; by bathing them; aashvaasya
= and cheering them up; tatra = there; snaatvaa = taking bath(himself);piitvaacha
= drinking; udakam = water; aadaaya = and carrying it (future use) praayaata =
(Bharata) proceeded further.

verses.sanskrit:
राज पुत्रः महा अरण्यम् अनभीक्ष्ण उपसेवितम् |

भद्रः भद्रेण यानेन मारुतः खम् इव अत्ययात् || २-७१-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. bhadraH = the blessed; raajaputraH = bharata; the
prince;atyayaat = traversed;mahaaraNyam = the huge forest; anabhiikshhNopasevitam =
which was rarely frequented; bhadreNa = by his excellent; yaanena = chariot;
khamiva = as through the atmosphere; maarutaH = the wind.

verses.sanskrit:
भागीरथीम् दुष्प्रतरामंशुधाने महानदीम् |

उपायाद्राघवस्तूर्णम् प्राग्वटे विश्रुते पुरे || २-७१-९

स गङ्गाम् प्राग्वटे तीर्त्वे समायात्कुटिकोष्ठिकाम् |

सबलस्ताम् स तीर्त्वाथ समायाद्धर्मवर्धनम् || २-७१-१०

तोरणम् दक्षिण अर्धेन जम्बू प्रस्थम् उपागमत् |

वरूथम् च ययौ रम्यम् ग्रामम् दशरथ आत्मजः || २-७१-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. dasharathaatmajaH = Bharata; upaagamaat = reached;
jambuuprastham = Jambuprastha village; dakshhiNaardhena = which is the southern
part; toraNam = of torana region; (and from there)yayou = got; ramyam =
a beautiful; gramamcha = village too; varuutham = called Varutha.

verses.sanskrit:
तत्र रम्ये वने वासम् कृत्वा असौ प्रान् मुखो ययौ |

उद्यानम् उज्जिहानायाः प्रियका यत्र पादपाः || २-७१-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. kRR^itvaa = making; vaasam = a camp; ramie = in
that beautiful; vane = forest; tatra = there; asou = Bharata;
praaNmukhah = turned towards the east; yayou = and reached; udyaanam = a
garden; ujjihaanaayaaH = in the city of Ujjihana;yatra = in which; there are)
priyakaaH paadapaaH = a number of Kadamba trees.

verses.sanskrit:
सालांस् तु प्रियकान् प्राप्य शीघ्रान् आस्थाय वाजिनः |

अनुज्ञाप्य अथ भरतः वाहिनीम् त्वरितः ययौ || २-७१-१३

वासम् कृत्वा सर्व तीर्थे तीर्त्वा च उत्तानकाम् नदीम् |

अन्या नदीः च विविधाः पार्वतीयैअः तुरम् गमैः || २-७१-१४

हस्ति पृष्ठकम् आसाद्य कुटिकाम् अत्यवर्तत |

ततार च नर व्याघ्रः लौहित्ये स कपीवतीम् || २-७१-१५

एक साले स्थाणुमतीम् विनते गोमतीम् नदीम् |

कलिन्ग नगरे च अपि प्राप्य साल वनम् तदा || २-७१-१६

भरतः क्षिप्रम् आगच्चत् सुपरिश्रान्त वाहनः |

वनम् च समतीत्य आशु शर्वर्याम् अरुण उदये || २-७१-१७

अयोध्याम् मनुना राज्ञा निर्मिताम् स ददर्श ह |

ताम् पुरीम् पुरुष व्याघ्रः सप्त रात्र उषिटः पथि || २-७१-१८

अयोध्याम् अग्रतः दृष्ट्वा रथे सारथिम् अब्रवीत् |
verses.wordMeaning:
18. dR^IshhTvaa = seeing ; taam = that; ayodhyaam
puriim = city of Ayodhya; agrataH = in front; saptaraatroshhitaH = after
spending seven nights; pathi = on his way; purushhvyaaghraH = that tiger among
men; abraviit = spoke; vaakyam = these words; saarathim = to the
charioteer.

verses.sanskrit:
एषा न अतिप्रतीता मे पुण्य उद्याना यशस्विनी || २-७१-१९

अयोध्या दृश्यते दूरात् सारथे पाण्डु मृत्तिका |

यज्वभिर् गुण सम्पन्नैः ब्राह्मणैः वेद पारगैः || २-७१-२०

भूयिष्ठम् ऋषैः आकीर्णा राज ऋषि वर पालिता |
verses.translation:
"O, Charioteer! The famous city of Ayodhya comes into view vaguely to me from a
distance, as a heap of white clay. It has beautiful gardens. It is filled with performers of
sacrifices and with people endowed with good qualities as well as well versed in the Vedas and with
Brahmins inmost abundant reaches , It is being ruled by a royal sage."

verses.sanskrit:
अयोध्यायाम् पुरा शब्दः श्रूयते तुमुलो महान् || २-७१-२१

समन्तान् नर नारीणाम् तम् अद्य न शृणोम्य् अहम् |

उद्यानानि हि साय अह्ने क्रीडित्वा उपरतैः नरैः || २-७१-२२

समन्तात् विप्रधावद्भिः प्रकाशन्ते मम अन्यदा |
verses.wordMeaning:
22. udyaanaani = the parks; prakaashantehi = which used to
beam; naraiH = forth; samantaat = on all sides; saayaahne = in the
evening; mama = to me; anyathaa = otherwise.
verses.translation:
"The parks which used to beam with men streaming forth on all sides, having given up
sporting in the morning after entering them in thee evening having spent the whole night in sport,
now appear to me otherwise"

verses.sanskrit:
तानि अद्य अनुरुदन्ति इव परित्यक्तानि कामिभिः || २-७१-२३

अरण्य भूता इव पुरी सारथे प्रतिभाति मे |
verses.translation:
"These parks deserted by the lovers now turn out to be dejected . O, charioteer! To
me, the city appears to have changed into a forest."

verses.sanskrit:
न हि अत्र यानैः दृश्यन्ते न गजैः न च वाजिभिः || २-७१-२४

निर्यान्तः वा अभियान्तः वा नर मुख्या यथा पुरम् |
verses.translation:
"Here, as before, important persons indeed are not seen going into the city or
coming out it in carriages or on horses or on elephants."

verses.sanskrit:
उद्यानानि पुरा भान्ति मत्तप्रमुदितानि च || २-७१-२५

जनानाम् रतिसम्योगेष्वत्यन्तगुणवन्ति च |
verses.wordMeaning:
25. puraa = earlier; ndyaanaani = parks; shaanti =
used to be conspicuously; matta pranuditaanicha = ezaited;with joy and gaiety;
atyantaguNavanticha = and were most excellent ; ratisamyogeshhu = for love
contacts; janaanaam = of men.
verses.translation:
"Earlier parks used to be conspicuously excited with joy and gaiety and were most
congenial for love contacts of men."

verses.sanskrit:
तान्येतान्यद्य वश्यामि निरानन्दानि सर्वशः || २-७१-२६

स्रस्तपर्णैरनुपथम् विक्रोशद्भिरिव द्रुमैः |
verses.translation:
"I am seeing the same parks., today with lack of enjoyments on all sides and with
trees having their leaves fallen along the road, looking like picture of dismay."

verses.sanskrit:
नाद्यापि श्रूयते शब्दो मत्तानाम् मृगपक्षिणाम् || २-७१-२७

सम्रक्ताम् मधुराम् वाणीम् कलम् व्याहरताम् बहु |
verses.translation:
"Even now at dawn, charming sounds with sweet and much melodious tone of animals and
birds intoxicated with happiness are not being heard."

verses.sanskrit:
चन्दनागुरुसम्पृक्तो धूपसम्मूर्चितोऽतुलः || २-७१-२८

प्रवाति पवनः श्रीमान् किम् नु नाद्य यथापुरम् |
verses.wordMeaning:
28. kimnu = why does; yathaapuram = as before;
bhriimaan = pure and auspicious; chandanaagarusamyuktaH = laden with aloes and
sandal wood; pavanaH = breeze; dhumpasammuurchitaH = intensified with the pure of
burnt incense; na pravaachi = not blow; adya = today?
verses.translation:
"Why does, as before, pure and auspicious breeze laden with aloes and sandal wood
intensified with the fume of burnt incense,not blow today.?"

verses.sanskrit:
भेरीमृदङ्गवीणानाम् कोणसम्घट्टितः पुनः || २-७१-२९

किमद्य शब्दो विरतः सदाऽदीनगतिः पुरा |
verses.wordMeaning:
29. kim = why; adya = today; shabdaH = have the
sounds; bheriimR^idaNga viiNaanaam = of kettle drums clay tomtoms and vinas;
koNasamghaTTitaH = played upon with drum sticks or plums or fingers; virath =
stopped; puraa = which formerly; sadaa adiinagatiH = never ceased; punaH
= at any time.
verses.translation:
"Why today have the sounds of kettledrums, clay tomtoms and Vinas played upon with
drum-sticks or palms or fingers stopped which formerly never ceased at any time."

verses.sanskrit:
अनिष्टानि च पापानि पश्यामि विविधानि च || २-७१-३०

निमित्तानि अमनोज्ञानि तेन सीदति ते मनः |
verses.translation:
"I perceive various kinds of evil sinful and silly omens and by this, my mind is
dejected."

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वथा कुशलम् सूत दुर्लभम् मम बन्धुषु || २-७१-३१

तथा ह्यसति सम्मोहे हृदयम् सीदतीव मे |
verses.wordMeaning:
31. suuta = O; charioteer! Durlabham = it is not possible;
sarvattaa = that all; kushalam = should be well; mama bandhushhu = with
my kinsfolk; tathaahi = as indeed; asati = even without; sammohe = any
cause for a grievance; me = my; hR^Idayam = spirit; siidatiiva = is cast
down.
verses.translation:
"O, charioteer! It is not possible that all sounds be well with my kinsfolk, as
indeed, even without anycause for a grievance, my spirit is cast down"

verses.sanskrit:
विषण्णः शान्तहृदयस्त्रस्तः सुलुलितेन्द्रियः || २-७१-३२

भरतः प्रविवेशाशु पुरीमिक्ष्वाकुपालिताम् |

द्वारेण वैजयन्तेन प्राविशत् श्रान्त वाहनः || २-७१-३३

द्वाह्स्थैः उत्थाय विजयम् पृष्टः तैः सहितः ययौ |
verses.wordMeaning:
33. shaanta vaahanaH = Bharata; with tired horses;
praavishat = entered; dvaareNa = through the gate; vaijayentena = called
Vaijayanta; uktaH = greeted; vijayam = with slogans of victory; utthaaya
= by the standing; dvaaHsthaiH = gate keepers; yayon = he went; sahitaH
= along with; taiH = them.

verses.sanskrit:
स तु अनेक अग्र हृदयो द्वाह्स्थम् प्रत्यर्च्य तम् जनम् || २-७१-३४

सूतम् अश्व पतेः क्लान्तम् अब्रवीत् तत्र राघवः |

किमहम् त्वरयानीतः कारणेन विनानघ || २-७१-३५

अशुभाशङ्कि हृदयम् शीलम् च पततीव मे |
verses.wordMeaning:
35. anagha = O;faultless charioteer! Kim = why was; aham =
I; aniitaH = brought; tvarayaa = so quickly; kaaraNena vinaa = without
any reason? HR^idayam = my mind; ashubhaashaNki = is apprehending some evil; me =
My; shiilamcha = energy is also; patatiiva = lost.
verses.translation:
"O, the faultless charioteer! Why was I brought so quickly without any reason? My
mind is apprehending some evil. I lost my all energy."

verses.sanskrit:
श्रुता नो यादृशाः पूर्वम् नृपतीनाम् विनाशने || २-७१-३६

आकाराः तान् अहम् सर्वान् इह पश्यामि सारथे |
verses.translation:
"Whatever things were heard by us formerly in the same case of ruin of kings, I am
seeing here all those signs."

verses.sanskrit:
सम्मार्जनविहीनानि परुषाण्युपलक्षये || २-७१-३७

असम्यतकवाटानि श्रीविहीनानि सर्वशः |

बलिकर्मविहीनानि धूपसम्मेदनेन च || २-७१-३८

अनाशितकुटुम्बानि प्रभाहीनजनानि च |

अलक्स्मीकानि पश्यामि कुटुम्बिभवनान्यहम् || २-७१-३९
verses.translation:
"I am seeing the family horses in which dirt is not swept away, with their doors
wide open, bereft of splendor on all sides without any offerings being made, and with no incense
burned . Families are starving and people look miserably without any charming appearance."

verses.sanskrit:
अपेतमाल्यशोभानि असम्मृष्टाजिराणि च |

देवागाराणि शून्यानि न चाभान्ति यथापुरम् || २-७१-४०
verses.wordMeaning:
40. devaagaaraaNi = the temples of gods; shuunyaani = are
deserted; naabhaanticga = and have lost their radiance; apetamaalya shobhaani =
with their splendor of floral decoration lost; asammR^ishhTaajiraaNicha = nor are any
assembling to perform sacrifices; yathaapuram = as before.
verses.translation:
"The temples of gods are deserted and have lost their radiance, with their splendor
of floral decorations lost, nor are there any assembling to perform sacrifices as before."

verses.sanskrit:
देवतार्चाः प्रविद्धाश्च यज्ञ्गोष्ठ्यस्तथाविधाः |

माल्यापणेषु राजन्ते नाद्य पण्यानि वा तथा || २-७१-४१
verses.translation:
"Worships of the deities were placed aside. Assemblies of prayers are also not
adorned with saleable flowers and garlands today."

verses.sanskrit:
दृश्यन्ते वणिजोऽप्यद्य न यथापूर्वमत्रवै |

ध्यानसम्विग्नहृदयाः नष्टव्यापारयन्त्रिताः || २-७१-४२
verses.wordMeaning:
42. vaNijo api = even traders; nashhTavyaapaara yantritaa =
who lost interest in trading tie ups; dhyaana samvigrahR^idayaaH = and with their minds
agitated with thoughts ; na dR^ishyante = are not being seen; atra = here;
yathaa puurvam = as before; adya = today.
verses.translation:
"Even traders lost interest in trading tie-ups and their minds are tied up in
thoughts. They are not being seen here today, as before."

verses.sanskrit:
देवायतनचैत्येषुदीनाः पक्षिगणास्तथा || २-७१-४३

मलिनम् च अश्रु पूर्ण अक्षम् दीनम् ध्यान परम् कृशम् |

सस्त्री पुंसम् च पश्यामि जनम् उत्कण्ठितम् पुरे || २-७१-४४
verses.translation:
"Clusters of birds in temples and large trees in the city are looking desolate. The
population of men and women in the city look thoughtful anguished weak messy and despondent with
their eyes held in tears."

verses.sanskrit:
इति एवम् उक्त्वा भरतः सूतम् तम् दीन मानसः |

तानि अनिष्टानि अयोध्यायाम् प्रेक्ष्य राज गृहम् ययौ || २-७१-४५

ताम् शून्य शृन्ग अटक वेश्म रथ्याम् |

रजो अरुण द्वार कपाट यन्त्राम् |

दृष्ट्वा पुरीम् इन्द्र पुरी प्रकाशाम् |

दुह्खेन सम्पूर्णतरः बभूव || २-७१-४६
verses.wordMeaning:
46. dR^ishhTvaa = beholding; taam puriim = that city of
Ayodhya; indra pura prakaasam = that formerly possessed; the splendor of of the capital
city of Indra; shuunya shR^iNgaaTakaveshmarathyaam = highways deserted; rajo
aruNadvaara kavaaTayantraam = the doors and hinges covered with rust; bharataH
bhabhuua = because; sampuurNataraH = overcome; duHkhena = with grief.

verses.sanskrit:
बहूनि पश्यन् मनसो अप्रियाणि |

यानि अन्न्यदा न अस्य पुरे बभूवुः |

अवाक् शिरा दीन मना नहृष्टः |

पितुर् महात्मा प्रविवेश वेश्म || २-७१-४७

sargas.title: Sarga 72
sargas.introduction:
Bharata does not find his father in the royal palace and goes to meet his mother. He
asks his mother about the whereabouts his father. Kaikeyi informs Bharata about the death of
Dasaratha. When Bharata enquired about Rama, Kaikeyi also discloses Rama's exile to the forest
along with Sita and Lakshmana. She also narrates about the two boons she asked the king Dasaratha
as well as how the king accepted her boons and sent Rama to exile. She further advises Bharata to
meet the chief of Brahmans like Vasista and others to get himself crowned for the kingdom.

verses.sanskrit:
अपश्यंस् तु ततः तत्र पितरम् पितुर् आलये |

जगाम भरतः द्रष्टुम् मातरम् मातुर् आलये || २-७२-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. tataH = thereafter; bharataH = Bharata; apashyan
= not seeing; pitaram = his father; tatra = there; pituH = in his
father's; aalaye = palace; jagaama = went; maatuH = to his mother's;
aalaye = house; drashhTum = to see; maataram = his mother.

verses.sanskrit:
अनुप्राप्तम् तु तम् दृष्ट्वा कैकेयी प्रोषितम् सुतम् |

उत्पपात तदा हृष्टा त्यक्त्वा सौवर्ण मानसम् || २-७२-२

स प्रविश्य एव धर्म आत्मा स्व गृहम् श्री विवर्जितम् |

भरतः प्रेक्ष्य जग्राह जनन्याः चरणौ शुभौ || २-७२-३

सा तम् मूर्ध्नि समुपाघ्राय परिष्वज्य यशस्विनम् |

अङ्के भरतम् आरोप्य प्रष्टुम् समुपचक्रमे || २-७२-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. saa = She; upagraaya = smelled; muurdhaani = on
the head; tam bharatam = of that Bharata; yashasvinam = the illustrious man;
parishhvajya = embraced him; aaropya = made him to ascend; aNke = on her
lap; upachakram = to ask questions.

verses.sanskrit:
अद्य ते कतिचित् रात्र्यः च्युतस्य आर्यक वेश्मनः |

अपि न अध्व श्रमः शीघ्रम् रथेन आपततः तव || २-७२-५
verses.translation:
"How many days passed since you have left your maternal grand father's house? Is
there no travel fatigue to you, who have come speedily in a chariot?"

verses.sanskrit:
आर्यकः ते सुकुशलो युधा जिन् मातुलः तव |

प्रवासाच् च सुखम् पुत्र सर्वम् मे वक्तुम् अर्हसि || २-७२-६
verses.translation:
"O,son! Is your grand father doing well? Is Yudhajit, your maternal uncle doing
well? Were you happy in being absent from home ? Be good enough to tell me all?"

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् पृष्ठः तु कैकेय्या प्रियम् पार्थिव नन्दनः |

आचष्ट भरतः सर्वम् मात्रे राजीव लोचनः || २-७२-७

अद्य मे सप्तमी रात्रिः च्युतस्य आर्यक वेश्मनः |

अम्बायाः कुशली तातः युधाजिन् मातुलः च मे || २-७२-८
verses.translation:
"It is seventh day to me today since I left the grand father's house. Yudhajit, your
father as well as my maternal uncle are doing well"

verses.sanskrit:
यन् मे धनम् च रत्नम् च ददौ राजा परम् तपः |

परिश्रान्तम् पथि अभवत् ततः अहम् पूर्वम् आगतः || २-७२-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. yat = which; dhanamcha = gifts; ratnamcha = and
jewels; dadou = were given; me = to me; rajaa = by the king;
paramtapaH = who annihilates enemies; abhavat = became; parishraantam =
tired; pathi = on the way; puurvam = before; tataH = then; aham
= I; aagataH = came.
verses.translation:
"Carrying the gifts and jewels, given to me by the king who annihilates enemies my
followers became tired on the way and I arrived ahead of them"

verses.sanskrit:
राज वाल्य हरैः दूतैअः त्वर्यमाणो अहम् आगतः |

यद् अहम् प्रष्टुम् इच्चामि तत् अम्बा वक्तुम् अर्हसि || २-७२-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. aham = I; aagataH = came; tvaryamaaNaH =
hurriedly; duutaiH- because of messengers; raajavaakya haraiH = who carried messeges of
the king; ambaa = let my mother; arhasi = be good enough; vaktum = to
tell; tat = that; yat = which; aham = I; ichchhaami = am
wishing; prashhTum = to ask.
verses.translation:
"I came soon because of the horridness communicated by royal messengers. Let my
mother be good enough to tell that which I desire to ask her."

verses.sanskrit:
शून्यो अयम् शयनीयः ते पर्यन्को हेम भूषितः |

न च अयम् इक्ष्वाकु जनः प्रहृष्टः प्रतिभाति मे || २-७२-११
verses.translation:
"This couch of yours, which is fit for rest and decorated with gold, is empty. These
men and women of Ikshvaku race do not appear to me to be cheerful."

verses.sanskrit:
राजा भवति भूयिष्ठ्गम् इह अम्बाया निवेशने |

तम् अहम् न अद्य पश्यामि द्रष्टुम् इच्चन्न् इह आगतः || २-७२-१२
verses.translation:
"King Dasaratha mostly used to here in your house. Now, I am not seeing him. I came
here, desirous of seeing him."

verses.sanskrit:
पितुर् ग्रहीष्ये चरणौ तम् मम आख्याहि पृच्चतः |

आहोस्विद् अम्ब ज्येष्ठायाः कौसल्याया निवेशने || २-७२-१३
verses.translation:
"O, mother! I will grasp in obeisance my father's feet. Tell about him, as I ask
you. Or is he in the house of Kausalya the senior most of my mothers?"

verses.sanskrit:
तम् प्रत्युवाच कैकेयी प्रियवद् घोरम् अप्रियम् |

अजानन्तम् प्रजानन्ती राज्य लोभेन मोहिता || २-७२-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. mohitaa = deluded; raajyalobhena = by the greed for
kingdom; kaikeyii = Kaikeyi; prajaanantii = who knew everything closely;
pratyuvaacha = told; tam = that Bharata; ajaanantam = who did not know
anything; ghoram = that terribly; apriyam = unpleasant thing; priyavat =
as though it was delightful.

verses.sanskrit:
या गतिः सर्व भूतानाम् ताम् गतिम् ते पिता गतः |

राजा महात्मा तेजस्वी यायजूकः सताम् गतिः || २-७२-१५
verses.translation:
"King Dasaratha, your father who was having a noble nature, who was respect and
performing frequent sacrifices as well as a refuge to good men followed that path which all beings
follow."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् श्रुत्वा भरतः वाक्यम् धर्म अभिजनवान् शुचिः |

पपात सहसा भूमौ पितृ शोक बल अर्दितः || २-७२-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. bharataH = Bharata; dharmaabhijaanavaan = a righteous
man with a noble descent; suchiH = and an honest man; shrutvaa = hearing;
tat = those; vaakyam = words; sahasaa = immediately; papaata =
fell; bhuumou = on the ground; pitR^ishokabalaarditaH = being exceedingly
tormented by the grief for his father.

verses.sanskrit:
हा हातोऽस्मीति कृपणाम् दीनाम् वाचमुदीरयन् |

निपपात महाबाहुर्बाहु विक्षिप्य वीर्यवान् || २-७२-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. mahaabaahuH = the mighty armed; viiryavaan = and the
heroic Bharata; udiirayaan = raising; kR^ipaaNam = his grievous; diinam
= and depressed; vaacham = voice; iti = thus saying; haa hataHasmi =
"Alas; I am undone'; vikshhipya = and lifting up; baahum = his arms;
nipapaata = fell down.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः शोकेन सम्वीतः पितुर् मरण दुह्खितः |

विललाप महा तेजा भ्रान्त आकुलित चेतनः || २-७२-१८

एतत् सुरुचिरम् भाति पितुर् मे शयनम् पुरा |

शशिनेवामलम् रात्रौ गगनम् तोयदात्यये || २-७२-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. etat = this; ruchiram = beautiful; shayanam =
couch; me pituH = of my father; puraa = formerly; bhaatisma = used to
enhancewith his splendor; shashineva = as the moon; irradiates; amalam = the
stainless; gaganam = sky; raatrou = in the night; toyadaatyaye = at the
end of the rainy season.
verses.translation:
"These beautiful couch of my father earlier used to enhance its charm with his
splendor, as the moon irradiates the stainless sky in the night at the end of a rainy season."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् इदम् न विभाति अद्य विहीनम् तेन धीमता |

व्योमेव श्शिना हीनमप्भुष्क इव सागरः || २-७२-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. vihiinam = being unoccupied; dhiimataa tena = by my
virtuous father; tat idam = the same couch; adya = now; navibhaati = is
bereft of glory; vyomeva = like the moon; saagaraaH iva = or like the sea;
apchchhushhkaH = with water dried up.
verses.translation:
"Being unoccupied by my virtuous father, the same couch now is bereft of its glory,
like the sky without the moon or like the sea with its water dried up."

verses.sanskrit:
बाष्पमुत्सृज्य कण्ठे स्वात्मना परिपीडितः |

आच्चाद्य वदनम् श्रीमद्वस्त्रेण जयताम् वरः || २-७२-२१

तम् आर्तम् देव सम्काशम् समीक्ष्य पतितम् भुवि |

निकृत्तमिव सालस्य स्कन्धम् परशुना वने || २-७२-२२

मत्तमातङ्गसम्काशम् चन्द्रार्कसदृशम् भुवः |

उत्थापयित्वा शोक आर्तम् वचनम् च इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-७२-२३

उत्तिष्ठ उत्तिष्ठ किम् शेषे राज पुत्र महा यशः |

त्वद् विधा न हि शोचन्ति सन्तः सदसि सम्मताः || २-७२-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. mahaayashaH = o; the highly illustrious; raajaputra =
prince! UttishhTha uttishhTha = arise; arise; kim = why; sheshhe = are you lying
down? SantaH = gentlemen; tvadvidhaaH = like you; sammataa = honoured;
sadasi = in the assembly of men; na shochantihi = do not indeed lament.
verses.translation:
"O, the highly illustrious prince! Arise,arise! Why are you lying down? Gentlemen,
like you, respected in the assembly of men, do not grieve indeed."

verses.sanskrit:
दानयज्ञाधिकारा हि शीलश्रुतिवचोनुगा |

बुद्धिस्ते बुद्धिसम्पन्न प्रभेवार्कस्य मन्दिरे || २-७२-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. buddhisam pannaH = O; Bharata endowed with understanding !
te = Your; buddhiH = intellect; shiilashruti vachonugaa = which follows good
character and sacred scriptures; daanayaj^Naadhikaaraa = has an authority to donate and to
sacrifice; (ever shining); prabheva = like a splendor; mandire = in the abode ;
arkasya = of the sun.
verses.translation:
"O, Bharata endowed with understanding! Your intellect, which follows good character
as well as sacred scriptures, has an authority to donate and sacrifice, ever shining as such like a
splendor in the abode of the sun.

verses.sanskrit:
स रुदत्या चिरम् कालम् भूमौ विपरिवृत्य च |

जननीम् प्रत्युवाच इदम् शोकैः बहुभिर् आवृतः || २-७२-२६

अभिषेक्ष्यति रामम् तु राजा यज्ञम् नु यक्ष्यति |

इति अहम् कृत सम्कल्पो हृष्टः यात्राम् अयासिषम् || २-७२-२७
verses.wordMeaning:
27. kR^ita samkalpaH = forming a resolution; raajaa = that
the king; abhishhekshhyati nu = was either anointing raamam = Rama; yakshhyatenu
= or performing; yajN^am = a sacrifice; aayaasishham = I got into ;
yaatraam = the journey; hR^ishhTaH = cheerfully.
verses.translation:
"Making up my mind that the king was either going to anoint Rama as a prince regent
or to perform a sacrifice, I cheerfully got into the journey."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् इदम् हि अन्यथा भूतम् व्यवदीर्णम् मनो मम |

पितरम् यो न पश्यामि नित्यम् प्रिय हिते रतम् || २-७२-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. tat idam = all this; bhuutam = turned to be;
anyathaa = otherwise; na pashyaami = by me not seeing; pitaram = my
father; nityam = forever; ratam = interested; priyahite = in my wishes
and welfare; mama = my; manaH = heart; vyavadiirNam = is broken to
pieces.
verses.translation:
"All this turned to be otherwise. By not beholding my father, who was forever
interested in my wishes and welfare, my heart is broken to pieces"

verses.sanskrit:
अम्ब केन अत्यगात् राजा व्याधिना मय्य् अनागते |

धन्या राम आदयः सर्वे यैः पिता संस्कृतः स्वयम् || २-७२-२९
verses.translation:
"O, mother! On what ailment did the king die, when I was not here? Rama and others
who performed purificatory rites by themselves for our father indeed fortunate."

verses.sanskrit:
न नूनम् माम् महा राजः प्राप्तम् जानाति कीर्तिमान् |

उपजिघ्रेद्द् हि माम् मूर्ध्नि तातः सम्नम्य सत्वरम् || २-७२-३०
verses.wordMeaning:
30. nuunam = It is sure; kiirtimaan = that the illustrious;
mataaraajaH = monarch; najanaati = does not know; maam = me;praaptam =
having arrived; taataH = my father; satvaram = quickly; upajighrethi =
would have indeed smelled; samnamya = by bending; muurdhni = my head (in
affection).
verses.translation:
"It is sure that the illustrious monarch does not know the fact of my arrival.
Otherwise, my father quickly would have indeed smelled my head, by bending me in affection."

verses.sanskrit:
क्व स पाणिः सुख स्पर्शः तातस्य अक्लिष्ट कर्मणः |

येन माम् रजसा ध्वस्तम् अभीक्ष्णम् परिमार्जति || २-७२-३१
verses.wordMeaning:
31. kva = where; taatasya = is father's;suksparshaH =
pleasantly caressing; saHpaaNiH = that hand; yena = which; parimaarjati
= to wipe; maam = me; dhvastam = covered; rajasaa = with dust;
abhiikshhNam = again and again; aklishhTa karmaNah = and which was unwearied in
action?
verses.translation:
"Where is the caressing hand of my dear father of unwearied action wipe away the
dust with which I am covered?"

verses.sanskrit:
यो मे भ्राता पिता बन्धुर् यस्य दासो अस्मि धीमतः |

तस्य माम् शीघ्रम् आख्याहि रामस्य अक्लिष्ट कर्मणः || २-७२-३२
verses.wordMeaning:
32. aakhyaahi = announce; shiighram = soon; maam =
about me; raamasya = to Rama; aklishhTakarmaNaH = of immortal exploits;
yaH = he who; bhraatraa = is the brother; me = to me; pitaa =
my father; bandhuH = my friend; yasya = and to whom; dhiimataH = the
wise; daasaH asmi = I am a servant.
verses.translation:
"Announce soon about my arrival to Rama of immortal exploits, he who is a wise
brother to me, my father, my friend and to whom Iam a servant."

verses.sanskrit:
पिता हि भवति ज्येष्ठो धर्मम् आर्यस्य जानतः |

तस्य पादौ ग्रहीष्यामि स हि इदानीम् गतिर् मम || २-७२-३३
verses.wordMeaning:
33. jyeshhThaH = an elder brother; bhavatihi = indeed
becomes; pitaa = a father; aaryasya = for a faithful man; jaanataH = who
is aware; dharmam = of righteousness; grahishhyaami = I will grasp;
tasya = his; paadou = feet; saH = he; gatiH hi = is indeed the
refuge; mama = to me; idaaniim = now.
verses.translation:
"An elder brother indeed becomes a father for a faithful man who knows
righteousness. I will grasp his feet in obeisance. He is indeed the refuge for me now."

verses.sanskrit:
धर्मविद्धर्मनित्यश्च सत्यसन्धो दृढव्रतः |

आर्ये किम् अब्रवीद् राजा पिता मे सत्य विक्रमः || २-७२-३४
verses.translation:
"What did my father the king, who know virtue , who was forever pious, who was true
to his promise, who was strictly truthful, who was truly valiant and who was honorable, say?"

verses.sanskrit:
पश्चिमम् साधु संदेशम् इच्चामि श्रोतुम् आत्मनः |

इति पृष्टा यथा तत्त्वम् कैकेयी वाक्यम् अब्रवीत् || २-७२-३५
verses.wordMeaning:
35. ichchaami = I want; shrotum = to hear; saadhu =
well; pashchimama = the last; sandesham = message; (of my father;
aatmaanaH = to me; iti = Thus; pR^ishhTaa = asked; kaikeyii =
Kaikeyi; abraviit = spoke; vaakyam = these words; yathaatattvam = in
accordance with truth:
verses.translation:
"I want to hear exactly the last message of my father to me." Thus requested by
Bharata, Kaikeyi spoke the following words in accordance with truth.

verses.sanskrit:
राम इति राजा विलपन् हा सीते लक्ष्मण इति च |

स महात्मा परम् लोकम् गतः गतिमताम् वरः || २-७२-३६

इमाम् तु पश्चिमाम् वाचम् व्याजहार पिता तव |

काल धर्म परिक्षिप्तः पाशैः इव महा गजः || २-७२-३७
verses.translation:
"Your father spoke the following last words also, when he was abandoned by the
operation of time , like a huge elephant surrendered into a trap."

verses.sanskrit:
सिद्ध अर्थाः तु नरा रामम् आगतम् सीतया सह |

लक्ष्मणम् च महा बाहुम् द्रक्ष्यन्ति पुनर् आगतम् || २-७२-३८
verses.wordMeaning:
38. te naraaH = those men; drakshhyanti = who are able to
see; raamam = Rama; punaH aagatam = returning; siitaaya saha = with
Sita; lakshhmaNamcha = and Lakshmana; mahaabaahum = the mighty armed;
siddhaarthaaH = are those who have fulfilled the object(of their coming).
verses.translation:
"Those men who are able to see Rama returning Sita and the mighty armed Lakshmana
are those who are fortunate enough"

verses.sanskrit:
तत् श्रुत्वा विषसाद एव द्वितीया प्रिय शंसनात् |

विषण्ण वदनो भूत्वा भूयः पप्रच्च मातरम् || २-७२-३९

क्व च इदानीम् स धर्म आत्मा कौसल्य आनन्द वर्धनः |

लक्ष्मणेन सह भ्रात्रा सीतया च समम् गतः || २-७२-४०
verses.wordMeaning:
40. kva = where; saH = that; kausalya
nandavardhanaH = Rama; who augments the joy of Kausalya; dharmaatmaa = and the
virtuous man went;lakshhmaNena saha = along with Lakshmana; bhraatraa = the brother;
siitayaacha samam = and with Sita; idaaniim = now?
verses.translation:
"Where did the virtuous Rama, who augments Kausalya' s delight go now along with my
brother Lakshmana and with Sita?"

verses.sanskrit:
तथा पृष्टा यथा तत्त्वम् आख्यातुम् उपचक्रमे |

माता अस्य युगपद् वाक्यम् विप्रियम् प्रिय शन्कया || २-७२-४१
verses.wordMeaning:
41. tathaa = thus; pR^ishhTaa = asked as (aforesaid) asya
maataa = Bharata's mother; upachakrame = started; aakyaatum = telling;
sumahat = very great; vipriyam = unpleasant; vaakyam = words; yathaa
tattvam = exactly; priyashaNkayaa = with an apprehension that they are pleasant.

verses.sanskrit:
स हि राज सुतः पुत्र चीर वासा महा वनम् |

दण्डकान् सह वैदेह्या लक्ष्मण अनुचरः गतः || २-७२-४२
verses.wordMeaning:
42. putra = O; son! saH = he that; raajasutaH =
prince Rama; chiiravaasaaH = wearing long narrow pieces of bark; gataH hi =
indeed went; mahaavanam = to the great forest; daNDakaan = of Dandaka;
lakshhmaNaanucharaH = followed by Lakshmana; vaidehyaasaha = and also Sita.
verses.translation:
"O, son! He, that prince Rama, wearing long narrow pieces of bark, went indeed to
the great forest of Dandaka, followed by Lakshmana and also sita."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् श्रुत्वा भरतः त्रस्तः भ्रातुः चारित्र शन्कया |

स्वस्य वंशस्य माहात्म्यात् प्रष्टुम् समुपचक्रमे || २-७२-४३
verses.wordMeaning:
43. shrutvaa = hearing; tat = those words; bharataH
= Bharata; trastaH = was frightened; charitrashaNkyayaa = with a doubt about the
behaviour; bhraatuH = of his brother; maahaatmyaat = and because of the glory;
syasya vamshasya = of his race; sampachakrame = started; prashhTum = to
ask (his mother as follows):

verses.sanskrit:
कच्चिन् न ब्राह्मण वधम् हृतम् रामेण कस्यचित् |

कच्चिन् न आढ्यो दरिद्रः वा तेन अपापो विहिंसितः || २-७२-४४
verses.wordMeaning:
44. kashyachit = some; braahmaNa dhanam = brahmana's wealth;
nahR^itam kachchit = indeed not stolen; raamaNa = by Rama; aaDhyaH = a
rich; daridrovaa = or a poor; apaapaH = virtuous man; na vihimsitaH
kachchit = indeed not harmed; tena = by him?
verses.translation:
"Has not Rama indeed stolen the wealth of some Brahmana? Has not he done any harm
either to a rich or to a poor virtuous man?"

verses.sanskrit:
कच्चिन् न पर दारान् वा राज पुत्रः अभिमन्यते |

कस्मात् स दण्डक अरण्ये भ्रूणहा इव विवासितः || २-७२-४५
verses.wordMeaning:
45. raajaputraH = has the prince; naabhimaanyate vaa
kachchit = indeed not desired any; paradaaraan = wife of another; kasmaat =
why; saH = was he; bhraataaraamo = my brother Rama; vivaasitaH =
expelled; daNDakaaraN^ye = to the forest of Dandaka?
verses.translation:
"Has the prince indeed not longed for the wife of another? Why was my brother Rama
expelled to the forest of Dandaka?"

verses.sanskrit:
अथ अस्य चपला माता तत् स्व कर्म यथा तथम् |

तेन एव स्त्री स्वभावेन व्याहर्तुम् उपचक्रमे || २-७२-४६

एवमुक्ता तु कैकेयी भरतेन महात्मना |

उवाच वचनम् हृष्टा मूढा पण्डितमानिनी || २-७२-४७
verses.wordMeaning:
47. kaikeyii = Kaikeyi; muuDhaa = a fool; paNDita
maanini = thinking herself to be learned; uktaa = after being spoken; evam =
thus; bharatena = by Bharata; mahaatmanaa = the virtuous man; hR^IshhTaa
= delightfully; uvaacha = spoke; vachanam = words:

verses.sanskrit:
न ब्राह्मण धनम् किंचिद्द् हृतम् रामेण कस्यचित् |

कश्चिन् न आढ्यो दरिद्रः वा तेन अपापो विहिंसितः || २-७२-४८

न रामः पर दारामः च चक्षुर्भ्याम् अपि पश्यति |
verses.translation:
"Not even a little of belonging to a Brahman was stolen by Rama,No harm was done by
him either to a rich or to a poor virtuous man. Rama does not even look with his eyes, the wives of
others."

verses.sanskrit:
मया तु पुत्र श्रुत्वा एव रामस्य एव अभिषेचनम् || २-७२-४९

याचितः ते पिता राज्यम् रामस्य च विवासनम् |
verses.translation:
"O, son! Immediately on hearing decidedly about the coronation ceremony of Rama by
me then, I asked your father to bestow kingdom to you and for banishment of Rama to the forest"

verses.sanskrit:
स स्व वृत्तिम् समास्थाय पिता ते तत् तथा अकरोत् || २-७२-५०

रामः च सह सौमित्रिः प्रेषितः सह सीतया |
verses.wordMeaning:
50. samaasthaaya = submitting to ; svavR^ittim = his own
decree; saH = that king Dasaratha; te pitaa = your father; akarot = has
done; tat = that; tathaa = as requested by me; raamashcha = Rama;
sahasoumitriH = along with Sita too; preshhitaH = were sent into exile.
verses.translation:
"Submitting to his own decree, King Dasaratha, your father has done all that was
requested by me. Rama along with Lakshmana and Sita too were sent into exile."

verses.sanskrit:
तम् अपश्यन् प्रियम् पुत्रम् मही पालो महा यशाः || २-७२-५१

पुत्र शोक परिद्यूनः पन्चत्वम् उपपेदिवान् |
verses.translation:
"That beloved son having not been seen, the very celebrated monarch was made
miserable by the grief for his son and obtained death"

verses.sanskrit:
त्वया तु इदानीम् धर्मज्ञ राजत्वम् अवलम्ब्यताम् || २-७२-५२

त्वत् कृते हि मया सर्वम् इदम् एवम् विधम् कृतम् |
verses.translation:
"O, Bharata knowing righteousness! The king- ship may be taken charge by you now.
All this was indeed done for your sake in this manner by me."

verses.sanskrit:
मा शोकम् मा च सम्तापम् धैर्यमाश्रय पुत्रक || २-७२-५३

त्वदधीना हि नगरी राज्यम् चैतदनामयम् |
verses.translation:
"O, son! Do not dwell in grief and anguish. Dwell in courage. This city along with
the salubrious kingdom indeed is subservient to you."

verses.sanskrit:
तत् पुत्र शीघ्रम् विधिना विधिज्ञैः |

वसिष्ठ मुख्यैः सहितः द्विज इन्द्रैः |

सम्काल्य राजानम् अदीन सत्त्वम् |

आत्मानम् उर्व्याम् अभिषेचयस्व || २-७२-५४
verses.translation:
"Hence, meet the chiefs of Brahmanas like Vasishta and others who know the rituals,
perform soon the sacred rites to the king and become crowned as a king yourself to the earth, being
not depressed in spirit.
verses.commentary:
© February 2003, K. M. K. Murthy

sargas.title: Sarga 73
sargas.introduction:
Bharata reproached Kaikeyi in many ways. Pricking with his unpleasant remarks,
Bharata tells her that he will bring back Rama from the forest, install him on the throne of
Ayodhya and become his attendant.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रुत्वा तु पितरम् वृत्तम् भ्रातरु च विवासितौ |

भरतः दुह्ख सम्तप्तैदम् वचनम् अब्रवीत् || २-७३-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. shrutvaatu = hearing; pitaram = his father;
vR^ittam = to be dead; bhraataram = and both his brothers; vivaasitou =
having been exiled; bharataH = Bharata; duHkhasamtaptaH = was tormented; with
grief; abraviit = and spoke; idam = these; vachanam = words.

verses.sanskrit:
किम् नुण्कार्यम् हतस्य इह मम राज्येन शोचतः |

विहीनस्य अथ पित्रा च भ्रात्रा पितृ समेन च || २-७३-२

दुह्खे मे दुह्खम् अकरोर् व्रणे क्षारम् इव आदधाः |

राजानम् प्रेत भावस्थम् कृत्वा रामम् च तापसम् || २-७३-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. kR^itvaa = making; raajaanam preta bhaavastham = the king
die; raamamcha = and Rama; taapasam = to be an ascetic; akaroH = you
created; duHkham = a sorrow; duHkhe = in another sorrow; me = to
me;adadhaaH iva = like placing; kshhaaram = salt; vraNe = on a wound.
verses.translation:
"Making Dasaratha to die and turning Rama to be an ascetic, you brought one calamity
after another like sprinkling salt on a wound."

verses.sanskrit:
कुलस्य त्वम् अभावाय काल रात्रिर् इव आगता |

अन्गारम् उपगूह्य स्म पिता मे न अवबुद्धवान् || २-७३-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. tvam = you; aagataa = came; abhaavaaya = for
destruction; kulasya = of our race; kaalaraatriiva = like the night of
destruction at the end of the world; me pitaa = my father; na avabuddharaan =
could not be aware; upaguhya = of his embracing; aN^gaaram = a live charcoal.
verses.translation:
"You came for destruction of our race, like the night of destruction coming at the
end of the world. My father could not be aware of his embracing a live char-coal to his bosom."

verses.sanskrit:
मृत्युमापादितो राजा त्वया मे पापदर्शिनि |

सुखम् परिहृतम् मोहात्कुलेऽस्मिन् कुलपांसनि || २-७३-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. paapadarshini = O; malevolent woman! mR^ityum = the
death; me pitaa = of my father; aapaaditaH = was caused; tvayaa = by
you; kulapaamsani = O; the one who made our race unchaste! Asmin kule = In this
race; sukham = happiness; parihR^itam = is deserted; mohaat = by your
ignorance.
verses.translation:
"O, the malevolent woman! You caused the death of my father. O, the one who made our
race unchaste! In this race, happiness is deserted through your ignorance."

verses.sanskrit:
त्वाम् प्राप्य हि पिता मे.द्य सत्यसन्धो महायशाः |

तीव्रदुःखाभिसम्तप्तो वृत्तो दशरथो नृपः || २-७३-६
verses.translation:
"My father, king Dasaratha, who was true to his promise and immensely famous, now
died, tormented as he was with bitter grief, because of you."

verses.sanskrit:
विनाशितो महाराजः पिता मे धर्मवत्सलः |

कस्मात्प्रव्राजितो रामः कस्मादेव वनम् गतः || २-७३-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. kasmaat = why; me pitaa = my father; mahaaraajaH
= the monarch; dharmavatsalaH = fond of righteousness; vinaashitaH = was killed?
Kasmaat = why; raamaH = Rama; gataH = went; pravraajitaH = on exile;
vanam = to the forest?
verses.translation:
"Why did you kill my father the monarch, who was intent on righteousness? Why did
you send Rama on exile to the forest?"

verses.sanskrit:
कौसल्या च सुमित्रा च पुत्र शोक अभिपीडिते |

दुष्करम् यदि जीवेताम् प्राप्य त्वाम् जननीम् मम || २-७३-८
verses.translation:
"It is impossible that Kausalya and Sumitra, who are afflicted with grief for their
sons, will live in fellowship with you, my mother."

verses.sanskrit:
ननु तु आर्यो अपि धर्म आत्मा त्वयि वृत्तिम् अनुत्तमाम् |

वर्तते गुरु वृत्तिज्ञो यथा मातरि वर्तते || २-७३-९
verses.translation:
"Even Rama my elder brother, a pious man who knows how to behave with elders, used
to act with the best behavior in your case, exactly as how he was behaving with his own
mother."

verses.sanskrit:
तथा ज्येष्ठा हि मे माता कौसल्या दीर्घ दर्शिनी |

त्वयि धर्मम् समास्थाय भगिन्याम् इव वर्तते || २-७३-१०
verses.translation:
"In the same manner, Kausalya, my elder mother having far sightedness and
established in piety, indeed used to behave with you as your sister."

verses.sanskrit:
तस्याः पुत्रम् कृत आत्मानम् चीर वल्कल वाससम् |

प्रस्थाप्य वन वासाय कथम् पापे न शोचसि || २-७३-११
verses.translation:
"Why are you not lamenting, after sending Rama, the disciplined Kausalya 's son,
clothed in a bark dress, to live in a forest? O, sinful one!"

verses.sanskrit:
अपाप दर्शिनम् शूरम् कृत आत्मानम् यशस्विनम् |

प्रव्राज्य चीर वसनम् किम् नु पश्यसि कारणम् || २-७३-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. pravraajya = having sent into exile; apaapadarshanam =
having a virtuous disposition; shuuram = a valiant man; kR^itaatmaanam = a
self-controlled; yashasvinam = and illustrious man; chiiravasanam = clothed in
bark dress; kimnu = what; kaaraNam = reason; pashyasi = do you
perceive?
verses.translation:
"You indeed sent into exile, clad in a bark dress, Rama having a virtuous
disposition, a valiant man, a self controlled and illustrious man as he was. What reason do you
gather for it?"

verses.sanskrit:
लुब्धाया विदितः मन्ये न ते अहम् राघवम् प्रति |

तथा हि अनर्थो राज्य अर्थम् त्वया नीतः महान् अयम् || २-७३-१३
verses.translation:
"I think it is not known to you, a greedy woman, about my devotion towards Rama. It
is exactly so. You have brought in this great calamity for the sake of a kingdom."

verses.sanskrit:
अहम् हि पुरुष व्याघ्राव् अपश्यन् राम लक्ष्मणौ |

केन शक्ति प्रभावेन राज्यम् रक्षितुम् उत्सहे || २-७३-१४
verses.translation:
"By which source of strength can I be able to protect the kingdom, without those
lions among men, Rama and Lakshmana in proximity to me?"

verses.sanskrit:
तम् हि नित्यम् महा राजो बलवन्तम् महा बलः |

उअपाश्रितः अभूद् धर्म आत्मा मेरुर् मेरु वनम् यथा || २-७३-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. mahaaraajaH = Dasaratha the monarch; mahaabalaH = having
great strength; dharmaatmaa = and a pious mind; nityam = always;
abhuutihi = indeed used to; upaashritaH = take refuge;tam = that Rama;balavantam
= the strong man; meruvanam yathaa = as a forest surrounding the mountain of Meru( taken
refuge); meruH = by Meru mountain."
verses.translation:
"Dasaratha the monarch, having great strength and a pious mind always indeed used to
take refuge in that strong man Rama as the Meru mountain takes refuge in a forest surrounding the
mountain."

verses.sanskrit:
सो अहम् कथम् इमम् भारम् महा धुर्य समुद्यतम् |

दम्यो धुरम् इव आसाद्य सहेयम् केन च ओजसा || २-७३-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. kena = by what; ojasaa = stamina; saH aham = I
as such; vaheyam = can carry; aasaadya = after obtaining; imam bhaaram =
this burden;( of kingship); damyaH iva = like a young bullock that is yet to be tamed; (to
carry); dhuram = the burden; mahaadhurya samuddhR^itam = being lifted up by a
giant bullock.
verses.translation:
"By what stamina, shall I sustain this burden of kingship any more than a young
bullock that is yet to be tamed, would stand the strain on getting a load, lifted up with ease by a
giant bullock."

verses.sanskrit:
अथ वा मे भवेत् शक्तिर् योगैः बुद्धि बलेन वा |

सकामाम् न करिष्यामि त्वाम् अहम् पुत्र गर्धिनीम् || २-७३-१७
verses.translation:
"Or even if a strength can be brought into existence in me by following certain
suitable methods or by strength of intelligence, I will not allow you, who are greedy in fetching
of kingdom for your son, to fulfill your desire."

verses.sanskrit:
न मे विकाङ्खा जायेत त्यक्तुम् त्वाम् पापनिश्चयाम् |

यदि रामस्य नावेक्षा त्वयि स्यान्मातृवत्सदा || २-७३-१८
verses.translation:
"No aversion would be felt by me even to desert you, a woman of evil designs, if
Rama did not treat you like a mother at all times."

verses.sanskrit:
उत्पन्ना तु कथम् बुद्धिस्तवेयम् पापदर्शिनि |

साधुचारित्रविभ्राष्टे पूर्वेषाम् नो विगर्हिता || २-७३-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. paapa darshini = O; malevolent woman! saadhucharitra
vibhrashhTe = with your good conduct disappeared! katham = How; iyam buddhiH = this
intension; vigarhitaa = forbidden by; naH = our; puurveshhaam =
ancestors; utpannaa = has arisen; tava = to you?
verses.translation:
"O, malevolent woman with your good conduct disappeared! How this idea forbidden by
our ancestors, has arisen in your mind at all?"

verses.sanskrit:
अस्मिन् कुले हि सर्वेषाम् ज्येष्ठो राज्येऽभिषिच्यते |

अपरे भ्रातरस्तस्मिन् प्रवर्तन्ते समाहिताः || २-७३-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. jyeshThaH = the eldest; sarveshhaam = of all; asmin
kule; in this race; abhishhichyate hi = should be indeed; inaugurated; raajye =
in the kingdom; apare = the rest of; bhraataraH = the brothers;
pravartante = behave; samaahitaaH = reverently; tasmin = towards the
elder brother.
verses.translation:
"The eldest of all in this race should be indeed anointed as a king. The rest of his
brothers are to behave reverently towards their elder brother."

verses.sanskrit:
न हि मन्ये नृशसे त्वम् राजधर्ममवेक्षसे |

गतिम् वा न विजानासि राजवृत्तस्य शाश्वतीम् || २-७३-२१
verses.translation:
"O, cruel woman! I feel that you are not catching even a glimpse of the rules
relating to kings or not even aware of a permanent procedure prevailing in the administration of
kings."

verses.sanskrit:
सततम् राजवृत्ते हि ज्येष्ठो राज्येऽभिषिच्यते |

राज्ञामेतत्समम् तत्स्यादिक्ष्वाकूणाम् विशेषतः || २-७३-२२
verses.translation:
"According to the administration of kings the eldest son always indeed gets
inaugurated in kingdom. This procedure is similar to all kings; especially so in the case of
Ikshvaku kings."

verses.sanskrit:
तेषाम् धर्मैकरक्षाणाम् कुलचारित्रयोगिनाम् |

अत्र चारित्रशौण्डीर्यम् त्वाम् प्राप्य विनिवर्ततम् || २-७३-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. chaaritra shouN^Diiryam = the pride of reputation;
teshhaam = of those belonging to Ikshvaku race; dharmaika rakshhaaNaam = who
protect righteousness alone; kulachaaritra yoginaam = and possessed of good racial
conduct; vinivartitam = is turned away; praapya = by obtaining; tvaam =
you; atra = in this case.
verses.translation:
"The pride of reputation of those belonging to Ikshvaku race, who protect
righteousness alone and who possessed good family conduct, is turned away by you today."

verses.sanskrit:
तवापि सुमहाभागा जनेन्द्राः कुलपूर्वगाः |

बुद्धेर्मोहः कथमयम् सम्भूतस्त्वयि गर्हितः || २-७३-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. janendraaH api = even the kings; tavakulapuurvagaaH =
belonging to your ancestral race; sumahaa bhaagaaH = were very great people;
katham = how; ayam = this; garhitaH = contemptible; mohaH =
stupidity; buddheH = of minds; sambhuutaH = is born; tvayi = in you?
verses.translation:
"Even kings belonging to your ancestral race were great people. How has this
contemptible stupidity of mind born in you?"

verses.sanskrit:
न तु कामम् करिष्यामि तवाऽह्म् पापनिश्चये |

त्वया व्यसनमारब्धम् जीवितान्तकरम् मम || २-७३-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. paapanishchaye = O; woman with evil designs! aham tu =
I; for one; nakarishhyaani = will not fulfill; tava = your; kaamam =
desire; vyasanam = a criminal act; mama jiivitaantakaram = which will cause an
end to my life; aarabdham = has been undertaken; tvayaa = by you.
verses.translation:
"O, woman with evil designs! I for one will not fulfill your desire. A criminal act,
which will cause even an end to my life, has been undertaken by you."

verses.sanskrit:
एष त्विदानीमेवाहमप्रियार्थम् तवनघम् |

निवर्तयिष्यामि वनात् भ्रातरम् स्वजन प्रियम् || २-७३-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. eshhaaH aham tu = this I ; for one; idaamiim eva = now
itself; tava apriyaartham = at the cause of your dislike; nivartayishhyaami =
will bring back; bhraataram = my brother; anagham = faultless man;
svajanapriyam = a beloved man of his people; vanaat = from the forest.
verses.translation:
"Now itself at the cause of your dislike, I for one will bring back from the forest,
my brother, who is a faultless man and a beloved man of his people."

verses.sanskrit:
निवर्तयित्वा रामम् च तस्याहम् दीप्ततेजनः |

दासभूतो भविष्यामि सुस्थिरेणान्तरात्मना || २-७३-२७
verses.wordMeaning:
27. nivartayitvaa = bringing back; raamam = Rama;
susthireNa = with a very firm; antaraatmanaa = mind; bhavishhyaami = I
will become; daasabhuutaH = a servant; tasya = to him; diipta tejasaH =
who is radiant with glory.
verses.translation:
"Bringing back Rama with a very firm mind, I will become a servant to him, who is
radiant with glory."

verses.sanskrit:
इति एवम् उक्त्वा भरतः महात्मा |

प्रिय इतरैः वाक्य गणैअः तुदंस् ताम् |

शोक आतुरः च अपि ननाद भूयः |

सिम्हो यथा पर्वत गह्वरस्थः || २-७३-२८

sargas.title: Sarga 74
sargas.introduction:
Bharata censures Kaikeyi saying that because of her, Dasaratha is dead and Rama is
sent to exile. He abuses his mother of her greed for the kingdom and the misfortune thus brought to
Kausalya, Sumitra and other mothers because of his father's death and brother's exile to the
forest. He compares the grief of the Kausalya with the grief of Kamadhenu the mythical cow who was
aggrieved of a pair of bullocks (her sons) being harassed by a plough -man. Bharata then promises
that he would bring Rama back to Ayodhya and make him as a king.

verses.sanskrit:
ताम् तथा गर्हयित्वा तु मातरम् भरतः तदा |

रोषेण महता आविष्टः पुनर् एव अब्रवीद् वचः || २-७४-१

राज्यात् भ्रंशस्व कैकेयि नृशंसे दुष्ट चारिणि |

परित्यक्ता च धर्मेण मा मृतम् रुदती भव || २-७४-२
verses.translation:
"O, Kaikeyi! The cruel and evil-mannered woman! Get lost from this kingdom. You
having abandoned righteousness, remain lamenting about me, who will be dead soon."

verses.sanskrit:
किम् नु ते अदूषयद् राजा रामः वा भृश धार्मिकः |

ययोः मृत्युर् विवासः च त्वत् कृते तुल्यम् आगतौ || २-७४-३
verses.translation:
"What harm king Dasaratha or the highly righteous Rama have done to you so harshly
that Dasaratha's death and Rama's exile have occurred at one and the same time because of you?"

verses.sanskrit:
भ्रूणहत्याम् असि प्राप्ता कुलस्य अस्य विनाशनात् |

कैकेयि नरकम् गच्च मा च भर्तुः सलोकताम् || २-७४-४
verses.translation:
"O, Kaikeyi! You got the sin of killing an embryo because of the destruction of this
race. O, Kaikeyi! Go to hell .Do not get the residence in the same heaven as your husband."

verses.sanskrit:
यत्त्वया हीदृशम् पापम् कृतम् घोरेण कर्मणा |

सर्वलोकप्रियम् हित्वा ममाप्यापादितम् भयम् || २-७४-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. ghoreNa karmaNaa = by which terrific act; yat =
wherefore; iidR^isham = such; paapam = a sin; kR^itam = was committed;
tvayaa = by you; hitvaa = by forsaking; sarvalokapriyam = the persons
beloved by all; bhayam = an awe; aapaditan = is created; mamaapi = in me
also.
verses.translation:
"You have done this terrific act and committed such a great sin. By forsaking the
persons beloved by all, an alarm has been created in me also."

verses.sanskrit:
त्वत् कृते मे पिता वृत्तः रामः च अरण्यम् आश्रितः |

अयशो जीव लोके च त्वया अहम् प्रतिपादितः || २-७४-६
verses.translation:
"My father died and Rama is dwelling in a forest, because of you. You got me ill
fame in this world of beings."

verses.sanskrit:
मातृ रूपे मम अमित्रे नृशंसे राज्य कामुके |

न ते अहम् अभिभाष्यो अस्मि दुर्वृत्ते पति घातिनि || २-७४-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. amitre = O; belligerent woman; mama meatR^iruupe = in the
form of my mother! NR^ishamse = O; cruel woman; raajyakaamuke = O; woman greedy of
kingdom; durvR^itte = of evil conduct; patimaatini = and who killed the husband!
Aham = I; naasmi = am never; abhibhaashhyaH = to address; te = you.
verses.translation:
"Though in appearance you are my mother, you are inimical to me. You are a cruel
woman, greedy of kingdom. With evil conduct, you killed your husband. I should never speak to
you."

verses.sanskrit:
कौसल्या च सुमित्रा च याः च अन्या मम मातरः |

दुह्खेन महता आविष्टाः त्वाम् प्राप्य कुल दूषिणीम् || २-७४-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. kausalyaa = Kausalya; sumitraacha = Sumitra;
mama = and my; anyaaH = other; maataraH = mothers; aavishhTaaH
= are engrossed; mahataa = in a great; duHkhena = misfortune; yaaH =
wherefore; praapya = by falling victim; tvaam = to you; kuladuushhiNiim
= who brought disgrace to our family.
verses.translation:
"Kausalya, Sumitra and my other mothers are engrossed in a great misfortune, by
falling victim to you, who brought disgrace to our family."

verses.sanskrit:
न त्वम् अश्व पतेः कन्या धर्म राजस्य धीमतः |

राक्षसी तत्र जाता असि कुल प्रध्वंसिनी पितुः || २-७४-९

यत् त्वया धार्मिको रामः नित्यम् सत्य परायणः |

वनम् प्रस्थापितः दुह्खात् पिता च त्रिदिवम् गतः || २-७४-१०
verses.translation:
"The righteous Rama, who is forever devoted to truth, was sent to the forest. Due to
grief for his son, my father went to heaven. You do not seem to be the daughter of Asvapati, the
pious and sensible king. You were born there as a demo ness, to destroy the house of my
father."

verses.sanskrit:
यत् प्रधाना असि तत् पापम् मयि पित्रा विना कृते |

भ्रातृभ्याम् च परित्यक्ते सर्व लोकस्य च अप्रिये || २-७४-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. yatpradhaanaasi = by which sin you are mainly having;
tat = that; paapam = sin; kR^ita = has made me; pitraavinaa =
father less; parityakte = abandoned; bhraatR^ibhyaam = by my brothers; mayi
apriyecha = and I was disliked; sarvalokasya = by all the people.
verses.translation:
"The aforesaid sin you have committed has made me father less. Besides, I have been
abandoned by my brothers and all the people dislike me now."

verses.sanskrit:
कौसल्याम् धर्म सम्युक्ताम् वियुक्ताम् पाप निश्चये |

कृत्वा कम् प्राप्स्यसे तु अद्य लोकम् निरय गामिनी || २-७४-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. paapanishchaye = O; woman having evil desires!
Nirayagaamini = moving towards hell! Kam lokam = which world; praapsyase = will you
attain; adya = now; kR^ityaa = after making; kausalyaam = Kausalya;
dharma samyuktaam = endowed with righteousness; viyuktaam = deprived of her
son?
verses.translation:
"O, woman having evil desires, moving towards hell! Which world will you attain now,
after making Kausalya endowed with righteousness, deprived of her son?"

verses.sanskrit:
किम् न अवबुध्यसे क्रूरे नियतम् बन्धु संश्रयम् |

ज्येष्ठम् पितृ समम् रामम् कौसल्याय आत्म सम्भवम् || २-७४-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. kruure = O; cruel woman! kim na avabuddhyase = don't you
know ; raamam = that Rama; aatmasambhavam = the son; kausalyaayaaH = of
Kausalya; niyatam = as self subdued; bandhu samshrayam = who is devoted to his
relatives; jyeshhTham = the eldest brother; pitrR^isamam = and equal to the
father?
verses.translation:
"O, cruel woman! Don't you know that Rama the son of Kausalya is a subdued man, who
is devoted to his relatives and as an eldest brother, equal to a father?"

verses.sanskrit:
अन्ग प्रत्यन्गजः पुत्रः हृदयाच् च अपि जायते |

तस्मात् प्रियतरः मातुः प्रियत्वान् न तु बान्धवः || २-७४-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. putraH = a son; aN^ga pratyaN^gajaH = is born from
primary limbs and secondary members of the body ; jaayate = and born;
hR^idayaachchaapi = from the heart too; tamaat = for that reason; (he is)
priyatamaH = the most beloved; maatuH = to a mother; baandhavaaH = the other
relatives; priyaa evatu = are only like friends.
verses.translation:
"A son is the most beloved to his mother as he is born from the primary and
secondary limbs of her body and also from her heart. The other relatives are only like
friends."

verses.sanskrit:
अन्यदा किल धर्मज्ञा सुरभिः सुर सम्मता |

वहमानौ ददर्श उर्व्याम् पुत्रौ विगत चेतसौ || २-७४-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. anyadaa = once upon a time; surabhiH = Kamadhenu; the
mythical cow of Vasista; dharmaj^Na = who knew righteousness; surasammataa = and
worshipped by celestials; dadarshaka = is said to have seen; putrou = her sons;
vahamaanou = dreaming having burden; urvayaam = on earth; vigatachetasou
= and became unconscious.
verses.translation:
"Once upon a time, Kamadhenu the mythical cow of Vasista, who knew righteousness and
was worshipped by celestials, is said to have seen her sons, drawing a heavy burden on earth and
became unconscious."

verses.sanskrit:
ताव् अर्ध दिवसे श्रान्तौ दृष्ट्वा पुत्रौ मही तले |

रुरोद पुत्र शोकेन बाष्प पर्याकुल ईक्षणा || २-७४-१६
verses.translation:
"Seeing her sons (a pair of bullocks) fatigued, after toiling for half a part of
their day on earth, Kamadhenu the mythical cow cried with her eyes full of tears in grief for the
fate of her sons."

verses.sanskrit:
अधस्तात् व्रजतः तस्याः सुर राज्ञो महात्मनः |

बिन्दवः पतिता गात्रे सूक्ष्माः सुरभि गन्धिनः || २-७४-१७
verses.translation:
"Small and sweet smelling tear drops of that mythical cow fell on the limbs of the
high soled Indra the lord of celestials, who was traveling below in a lower region."

verses.sanskrit:
इन्द्रोऽप्यश्रुनिपातम् तम् स्वगात्रे पुण्यगन्धिनम् |

सुरभिम् मन्यते दृष्ट्वा भूयसीम् ताम् सुरेश्वरः || २-७४-१८
verses.translation:
"Seeing those sweet- scented tears falling on his limbs, Indra the Lord of
celestials identified the tears to be those of the great Kamadhenu the mythical cow."

verses.sanskrit:
निरीक्समाणः शक्रस्ताम् ददर्श सुरभिम् स्थिताम् |

आकाशे विष्ठिताम् दीनाम् रुदतीम् भृशदुःखिताम् || २-७४-१९
verses.translation:
"Looking into the sky, Indra saw that Kamadhenu standing there with anguish and
pitiably weeping with great grief."

verses.sanskrit:
ताम् दृष्ट्वा शोक सम्तप्ताम् वज्र पाणिर् यशस्विनीम् |

इन्द्रः प्रान्जलिर् उद्विग्नः सुर राजो अब्रवीद् वचः || २-७४-२०
verses.translation:
"Indra the lord of celestials with a thunder bolt in his hand, seeing that beautiful
Kamadhenu the mythical cow, tormented with grief, spoke eagerly with joined palms to her, as
follows:

verses.sanskrit:
भयम् कच्चिन् न च अस्मासु कुतश्चित् विद्यते महत् |

कुतः निमित्तः शोकः ते ब्रूहि सर्व हित एषिणि || २-७४-२१
verses.wordMeaning:
21. sarvahitaishhiNi = O; cow the well wisher of all ; na vidyate
kachchit = Is there not indeed; kutashchit = from any where; mahat = a
great; bhayam = panic; asmaasu = to us? Kutonimittam = for what occasion te = is
your; shokaH sorrow?
verses.translation:
"O, cow the well wisher of all! I hope there is no great panic from any quarter to
us. For what occasion is your sorrow?"

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्ता तु सुरभिः सुर राजेन धीमता |

पत्युवाच ततः धीरा वाक्यम् वाक्य विशारदा || २-७४-२२
verses.translation:
"Hearing the words of the intelligent Indra the god of celestials, the prudent
Kamadhenu who was skilled in expressing words, replied as follows:

verses.sanskrit:
शान्तम् पातम् न वः किंचित् कुतश्चित् अमर अधिप |

अहम् तु मग्नौ शोचामि स्व पुत्रौ विषमे स्थितौ || २-७४-२३

एतौ दृष्ट्वा कृषौ दीनौ सूर्य रश्मि प्रतापिनौ |

अर्ध्यमानौ बली वर्दौ कर्षकेण सुर अधिप || २-७४-२४
verses.translation:
"Heaven forefend that evil! O, Indra the lord of celestials! There is no danger
whatsoever to you from any quarter. Seeing these pair of bullocks, my sons, who are in hardship,
being scorched by sun's rays, becoming weak, being troubled by the man who ploughs the land and
being immersed in grief, I am pitiably weeping O, Indra!"

verses.sanskrit:
मम कायात् प्रसूतौ हि दुह्खितौ भार पीडितौ |

यौ दृष्ट्वा परितप्ये अहम् न अस्ति पुत्र समः प्रियः || २-७४-२५
verses.translation:
"By seeing them who are afflicted with the burden and aggrieved, I am greatly
anguished. They are indeed born of my body. There is no dearest one equal to a son indeed!"

verses.sanskrit:
यस्याः पुत्र सहस्त्रैस्तु कृत्स्नम् व्याप्तमिदम् जगत् |

ताम् दृष्ट्वा रुदतीम् शक्रो न सुतान्मन्यते परम् || २-७४-२६

सदाऽप्रतिमवृत्ताया लोकधारणकाम्यया |

श्रीमत्या गुणनित्यायाः स्वभावपरिचेष्टया || २-७४-२७

यस्याः पुत्रसहस्राणि सापि शोचै कामधुक् |

किम् पुनर् या विना रामम् कौसल्या वर्तयिष्यति || २-७४-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
27. saa = such; kaamadhuk api = Kamadhenu even; loka
dhaaraNa kaamyayaa = who is desirous of maintaining the world; sadaa = and always;
apratimavR^ttaayaaH = having unequalled conduct; shriimatyaaH = the venerable
one; svabhaavaparicheshhTayaa = by its nature functioning ; guNa nityaayaaH =
forever with good qualities; yasyaaH = to whom; putrasahasraaNi = there are
thousands of sons; shochati = is lamenting; kimpunaH = how much more;
yaa = indeed; kausalyaa = Kausalya; vartayishhyati = will drag her
existence; raamam vinaa = without Rama?
verses.translation:
"Such a Kamadhenu the sacred cow, which is desirous of maintaining the world always
having unequalled behavior, the venerable one, by its very nature functioning forever with good
qualities and to whom there are thousands of sons, is lamenting, how much more indeed Kausalya will
drag her existence, without Rama?"

verses.sanskrit:
एक पुत्रा च साध्वी च विवत्सा इयम् त्वया कृता |

तस्मात् त्वम् सततम् दुह्खम् प्रेत्य च इह च लप्स्यसे || २-७४-२९
verses.translation:
"She, who has only one son and who is a holy woman, has been made without a child by
you. Thereafter, you will obtain grief forever after your death or even here while living."

verses.sanskrit:
अहम् हि अपचितिम् भ्रातुः पितुः च सकलाम् इमाम् |

वर्धनम् यशसः च अपि करिष्यामि न संशयः || २-७४-३०
verses.translation:
"I for my part, shall toil for the complete reinstatement of my brother and complete
the obsequial rites due to my father as well as for their prosperity and glory. There is no
doubt."

verses.sanskrit:
आनाययित्वा तनयम् कौसल्याया महा द्युतिम् |

स्वयम् एव प्रवेक्ष्यामि वनम् मुनि निषेवितम् || २-७४-३१
verses.translation:
"Rama the son of Kausalya, of great prowess will be brought back to Ayodhya and I,
myself will go to the forest inhabited by the sages."

verses.sanskrit:
न ह्यहम् पापसम्कल्पे पापे पापम् त्वया कृतम् |

शक्तो धारयितुम् पौरैरश्रुकण्ठै र्निरीक्षितः || २-७४-३२
verses.wordMeaning:
32. paape = O; wretched one! Paapasamkalpe = O; the evil
minded one! PouraiH = the citizens; niriikshhitaH = being seen;
ashrukaN^ThaiH = with their throats filled with tears; nashaktaH hi = I am not
indeed able; dhaarayitum = to endure; paapam = the inequity; kR^itam =
done; tvayaa = by you.
verses.translation:
"O, wretched and evil minded woman! By seeing the sorrow stricken citizens, I am
indeed not able to bear the inequity done by you."

verses.sanskrit:
सा त्वमग्निम् प्रविश वा स्वयम् वा दण्डकान्विश |

रज्जुम् बधान वा कण्ठे न हि तेऽन्यत्परायणम् || २-७४-३३
verses.translation:
"As for you, enter the fire or you yourself go to forest of Dandaka.or fasten a rope
around your neck. There is no other recourse for you."

verses.sanskrit:
अहमप्यवनिम् प्राप्ते रामे सत्यपराक्रमे |

कृतकृत्यो भविष्यामि विप्रवासितकल्मषः || २-७४-३४
verses.translation:
"After Rama the truly mighty man, obtains his native land, I will even become an
accomplished man, with my sins duly washed away."

verses.sanskrit:
इति नागैव अरण्ये तोमर अन्कुश चोदितः |

पपात भुवि सम्क्रुद्धो निह्श्वसन्न् इव पन्नगः || २-७४-३५

सम्रक्त नेत्रः शिथिल अम्बरः तदा |

विधूत सर्व आभरणः परम्तपः |

बभूव भूमौ पतितः नृप आत्मजः |

शची पतेः केतुर् इव उत्सव क्षये || २-७४-३६

sargas.title: Sarga 75
sargas.introduction:
Bharata asserts that he does not know how the exile of Rama, Sita and Lakshmana has
occurred. Bharata and Satrughna go to Kausalya's place. Kausalya speaks harsh words to Bharata,
saying that Kaikeyi his mother procured for him the kingdom as desired by him the kingdom as
desired by him. Bharata politely explains, on a number of oaths, his own innocence over the matter,
Kausalya comes to know Bharata's heart, fondly takes him to her lap and weeps distressfully.

verses.sanskrit:
दीर्घकालात्समुत्थाय सम्ज्ञाम् लब्ध्वा च वीर्यवान् |

नेत्राभ्यामश्रुपूर्णाभ्याम् दीनामुद्वीक्ष्य मातरम् || २-७५-१

सोऽमात्यमध्येभरतो जननीमभ्यकुत्सयत् |
verses.wordMeaning:
1. saH bharataH = that bharata; viiryavaan = the valiant
man; samutthaaya = rose up; diirghakaalaat = after a long time; labdhvaa
= obtained; samjNaam = consiousness; udvikshhya = saw; maataram = his
mother; netraabhyaam = with her eyes; diinaam ashrupuuNaabhyaam = filled with
tear; abhyakutsayat = reproached; jananiim = his mother; amaatyamadhye =
in the midst of the ministers.

verses.sanskrit:
राज्यम् न कामये जातु मन्त्रये नापि मातरम् || २-७५-२

अभिषेकम् न जानामि यो.भूद्रज्ज़्ना समीक्षितः |

विप्रकृष्टे ह्यहम् देशे शत्रुघ्न सहितोऽवसम् || २-७५-३
verses.translation:
"I never desired for the kingdom nor consulted about this matter with even my
mother. I was not aware of the proposed coronation, which has of the been contemplated by king
Dasaratha; since I was staying indeed in a distant land, along with Satrughna."

verses.sanskrit:
वनवासम् न जानामि रामस्यहम् महात्मनः |

विवासनम् वा सौमित्रेः सीतायाश्च यथाभवत् || २-७५-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. aham = I; najaanaami = was not aware; vanavaasam
= of the banishment to forest; raamasya = of Rama; mahaatmanaH = the high soled;
(nor aware) yathaa = of how; vivaasanam = the banishment; soumitreH = of
Lakshmana; siitaayaashcha = and of Seetha; abhavat = having occured.
verses.translation:
"I was neither aware of the banishment of Rama to the forest nor about how the exile
of Lakshmana and Seetha have occurred."

verses.sanskrit:
तथैव क्रोशतः तस्य भरतस्य महात्मनः |

कौसल्या शब्दम् आज्ञाय सुमित्राम् इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-७५-५

आगतः क्रूर कार्यायाः कैकेय्या भरतः सुतः |

तम् अहम् द्रष्टुम् इच्चामि भरतम् दीर्घ दर्शिनम् || २-७५-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. bharataH = bharata; sutah = the son; kaikeyyaaH
= of Kaikeyi; kruurakaaryaaH = performer of terrible deeds; aagataH = arrived;
aham = I; ichchhaami = want; drashhTum = to see; tam bharatam =
that Bharata; diirghadarshanam = the far sighted.
verses.translation:
"Bharata, the son of Kaikeyi, the doer terrific deeds arrived. I went to see that
far-sighted Bharata."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्त्वा सुमित्राम् सा विवर्णा मलिन अम्बरा |

प्रतस्थे भरतः यत्र वेपमाना विचेतना || २-७५-७

स तु राम अनुजः च अपि शत्रुघ्न सहितः तदा |

प्रतस्थे भरतः यत्र कौसल्याया निवेशनम् || २-७५-८

ततः शत्रुघ्न भरतौ कौसल्याम् प्रेक्ष्य दुह्खितौ |

पर्यष्वजेताम् दुह्ख आर्ताम् पतिताम् नष्ट चेतनाम् || २-७५-९

रुदन्तौ रुदतीम् दुःखात्समेत्यार्याम् मनस्स्विनीम् |

भरतम् प्रत्युवाच इदम् कौसल्या भृश दुह्खिता || २-७५-१०

इदम् ते राज्य कामस्य राज्यम् प्राप्तम् अकण्टकम् |

सम्प्राप्तम् बत कैकेय्या शीघ्रम् क्रूरेण कर्मणा || २-७५-११

प्रस्थाप्य चीर वसनम् पुत्रम् मे वन वासिनम् |

कैकेयी कम् गुणम् तत्र पश्यति क्रूर दर्शिनी || २-७५-१२
verses.translation:
"The ruthless Kaikeyi condemned my son to wear garments of bark and sent him to
dwell in the forest: What merit has she gained from this?"

verses.sanskrit:
क्षिप्रम् माम् अपि कैकेयी प्रस्थापयितुम् अर्हति |

हिरण्य नाभो यत्र आस्ते सुतः मे सुमहा यशाः || २-७५-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. kaikeyii = Kaikeyi; arhati = is opt;
prasthaapayitum = to send; maamapi = me too; kshhipram = without
delay(to the place); yatra = where; me = my; sumahaayashaaH =
illustrious; sutaH = son; hiraNya naabhaH = with a golden navel(the distinguished
mark of Vishnu the god of creation).
verses.translation:
"Kaikeyi ought to send me too without delay to the place where my illustrious son
with a golden novel (the distinguished mark of Vishnu the god of creation) is there."

verses.sanskrit:
अथवा स्वयम् एव अहम् सुमित्र अनुचरा सुखम् |

अग्नि होत्रम् पुरः कृत्य प्रस्थास्ये यत्र राघवः || २-७५-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. athavaa = otherwise; aham = I; svayameva = for
my own satisfaction; sumitraamcharaa = accompanied by Sumitra; puraskR^itya =
placing in front (carried by Brahmana); agnihotram = the sacred fire**;sukham = will
happily; prasthaasye = set out; (to the place); yatra = where; raaghavah
= Rama(is there).
verses.translation:
"Otherwise, I for my own satisfaction accompanied by Sumitra, placing in
front(carried by Brahmana) the sacred fire*** will happily set out to the place Raghava has
gone."

verses.sanskrit:
कामम् वा स्वयम् एव अद्य तत्र माम् नेतुम् अर्हसि |

यत्र असौ पुरुष व्याघ्रः तप्यते मे तपः सुतः || २-७५-१५
verses.translation:
"Otherwise, you yourself ought to conduct me now, if you so desire, to the place
where my son, the tiger among men, is living as an ascetic."

verses.sanskrit:
इदम् हि तव विस्तीर्णम् धन धान्य समाचितम् |

हस्ति अश्व रथ सम्पूर्णम् राज्यम् निर्यातितम् तया || २-७५-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. idam raajyam = this kingdom; hastyashvaratha sampuurNam
= filled with elephants horses and chariots; dhanadhaanya samaachitam = heaped together
with grains and riches; vistiirNamhi = was indeed procured; tava = to you;
tayaa = by her.
verses.translation:
"This vast kingdom, endowed with elephants, horses and chariots and stock piled with
grains and riches, has been made over to you by her."

verses.sanskrit:
इत्यादिबहुभिर्वाक्यैः क्रूरैः सम्भर्स्तितोऽनघः |

विव्यथे भरतस्तीव्रम् व्रणे तुद्येव सूचिना || २-७५-१७

पपात चरणौ तस्यास्तदा सम्भ्रान्तचेतनः |

विलप्य बहुधाऽसम्ज्ञो लब्धसम्ज्ञ्स्ततः स्थितः || २-७५-१८

एवम् विलपमानाम् ताम् भरतः प्रान्जलिस् तदा |

कौसल्याम् प्रत्युवाच इदम् शोकैः बहुभिर् आवृताम् || २-७५-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. tadaa = then; bharataH = Bharata; praN^jaliH =
with folded hands; pratyuvaacha = replied; idam = these words; taam
kausalyaam = to that Kausalya; aavR^itaam = who was surrounded; shokaiH = by
afflictions; vilapanaamaam = and weeping; evam = as aforesaid; bahubhiH
= in many ways.

verses.sanskrit:
आर्ये कस्मात् अजानन्तम् गर्हसे माम् अकिल्बिषम् |

विपुलाम् च मम प्रीतिम् स्थिराम् जानासि राघवे || २-७५-२०
verses.translation:
"O, noble woman! Why do you reproach me, sinless as I am, for those things that I do
not know anything? Nay, you know well of the greatest affection I have for Rama."

verses.sanskrit:
कृता शास्त्र अनुगा बुद्धिर् मा भूत् तस्य कदाचन |

सत्य संधः सताम् श्रेष्ठो यस्य आर्यो अनुमते गतः || २-७५-२१
verses.translation:
"May the mind of the one who in any way whatsoever allowed for the departure of my
elder brother the best of men who keeps up his promise, be closed to all the seriptures."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रैष्यम् पापीयसाम् यातु सूर्यम् च प्रति मेहतु |

हन्तु पादेन गाम् सुप्ताम् यस्य आर्यो अनुमते गतः || २-७५-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. (May he) anumate = who counseled; gataH = the
banishment; aaryaH = of my noble brother; yaatu = become; preshhyam =
the slave; paapiyasaam = of the vilest scoundrels; mehatu = may he) answer the
calls of nature; suuryaamchaprati = in the face of the sun; hantu = and may he
kick; paadena = with his foot; suptaam = a sleeping; gaam = cow.
verses.translation:
"May he who counseled the banishment of my noble brother become the slave of the
vilest scoundrels, may he answer the calls of nature in the face of the sun and may he kick with
his foot, a sleeping cow."

verses.sanskrit:
कारयित्वा महत् कर्म भर्ता भृत्यम् अनर्थकम् |

अधर्मः यो अस्य सो अस्याः तु यस्य आर्यो अनुमते गतः || २-७५-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. yasya = with whose; anumate = counsel; aaryaH =
my elder brother; gataH = went to exile; asya = to him; saH astu = let
that; asya = to him; saH astu = let that sin incur; yaH bharata = as to
a master; kaarayitvaa = who imposes; mahat = the heaviest; karma =
labour; bhR^ityam = on his servant; anarthakam = without remunerating him.
verses.translation:
"With whose counsel my elder brother went to exile, to him let that sin incur, as to
a master who imposes the heaviest labour on his servant without remunerating him."

verses.sanskrit:
परिपालयमानस्य राज्ञो भूतानि पुत्रवत् |

ततः तु द्रुह्यताम् पापम् यस्य आर्यो अनुमते गतः || २-७५-२४
verses.translation:
"with whose counsel my elder brother has gone to exile, to him let that sin incur as
to those who seek to harm a king who protects his people as if they are his own children."

verses.sanskrit:
बलि षड् भागम् उद्धृत्य नृपस्य अरक्षतः प्रजाः |

अधर्मः यो अस्य सो अस्य अस्तु यस्य आर्यो अनुमते गतः || २-७५-२५
verses.translation:
"May the guilt of a king, who taking the sixth part of the revenue of his people
fails to protect them, be fallen on them with whose counsel my elder brother went to exile."

verses.sanskrit:
संश्रुत्य च तपस्विभ्यः सत्रे वै यज्ञ दक्षिणाम् |

ताम् विप्रलपताम् पापम् यस्य आर्यो अनुमते गतः || २-७५-२६
verses.translation:
"May the sin of those having given the promise for certain sacrificial fees to
priests in a sacrifice, breaks that promise, to be his with whose counsel my elder brother went to
exile."

verses.sanskrit:
हस्ति अश्व रथ सम्बाधे युद्धे शस्त्र समाकुले |

मा स्म कार्षीत् सताम् धर्मम् यस्य आर्यो अनुमते गतः || २-७५-२७
verses.translation:
"May the sin of him, who fails to observe the code of warfare followed by the
virtuous in a battle abundantly provided with elephants horses chariots weapons and men, be his
with whose counsel my elder brother went to exile."

verses.sanskrit:
उपदिष्टम् सुसूक्ष्म अर्थम् शास्त्रम् यत्नेन धीमता |

स नाशयतु दुष्ट आत्मा यस्य आर्यो अनुमते गतः || २-७५-२८
verses.translation:
"May that perverse wretch, with whose counsel my elder brother has gone to exile,
forget the subtle meanings of the scriptures taught with care by a wise teacher."

verses.sanskrit:
मा च तम् प्यूढबाह्वंसम् चन्द्रार्कसम्तेजनम् |

द्राक्षीद्राज्यस्थमासीनम् यस्यार्योऽनुमते गतः || २-७५-२९
verses.wordMeaning:
29. (may he) yasya = with whose; anumate = counsel;
aaryaH = my elder brother; gataH = has gone to exile; maacha draakshhiit
= never behold; tam = that Rama; vyuuDha baahvamsam = having large arms and broad
shoulders; chandraarkasamatejasam = radiant as the sun and the moon; aasiinam =
