sargas.title: Sarga 95
sargas.introduction:
Rama describes to Seetha the beauty of Mandakini River with its multi-coloured
beaches, swans, geese, deer and flowery trees on its banks. He shows some sages taking dip in the
river and herds of elephants and monkeys coming for a drink there. Thus Rama and Seetha spend some
of their time in enjoying the charms of Chitrakuta mountain.

verses.sanskrit:
अथ शैलाद् विनिष्क्रम्य मैथिलीम् कोसल ईश्वरः |

अदर्शयत् शुभ जलाम् रम्याम् मन्दाकिनीम् नदीम् || २-९५-१

अब्रवीच् च वर आरोहाम् चारु चन्द्र निभ आननाम् |

विदेह राजस्य सुताम् रामो राजीव लोचनः || २-९५-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. raamaH = Rama; raajiiva lochanaH = having lotus-eyes;
abraviit = spoke; sutaam = to the daughter; videharaajasya = of the king
of Videha (Janaka); varaarohaam = having elegant waist; chaaruchandra
nibhaananaam = whose face resembled the moon.

verses.sanskrit:
विचित्र पुलिनाम् रम्याम् हंस सारस सेविताम् |

कुसुमैर् उपसम्पन्नाम् पश्य मन्दाकिनीम् नदीम् || २-९५-३
verses.translation:
"Behold this charming river of Mandakini, with its multi-coloured sandy beaches,
frequented by swans and geese and laden with flowers."

verses.sanskrit:
नाना विधैः तीर रुहैर् वृताम् पुष्प फल द्रुमैः |

राजन्तीम् राज राजस्य नलिनीम् इव सर्वतः || २-९५-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. (Behold this river); vR^itaam = hedged; sarvataH = on all
sides; naanaavidhaiH = with various kinds; puSpa phaladrumaiH = of flowers and
fruits; tiiraruhaiH = growing on its banks; raajantiim = and enhance its
loveliness; aliniim iva = like the lake (sangandhika); raajaraajasya = of Kubera
(the ruler of Yakshas).
verses.translation:
"Behold this river, hedged on all sides with various kinds of flowers and fruits,
growing on its banks and enhance its loveliness like the lake (Sangadhika) of Kubera (the ruler of
Yakshas)"

verses.sanskrit:
मृग यूथ निपीतानि कलुष अम्भांसि साम्प्रतम् |

तीर्थानि रमणीयानि रतिम् सम्जनयन्ति मे || २-९५-५
verses.translation:
"At this moment, its pleasant fords, where herds of deer drink and make the waters
turbid, create ecstasy in me."

verses.sanskrit:
जटा अजिन धराः काले वल्कल उत्तर वाससः |

ऋषयः तु अवगाहन्ते नदीम् मन्दाकिनीम् प्रिये || २-९५-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. priye = O; beloved Seetha!; R^iSayaH = sages;
jaTaajinadharaaH = with matted locks and wearing antelope-skings; valkalottara
vaasasaH = and having bark of trees for their upper garment; avagaahante = take a
dip; mandaakiniim nadiim = in the River Mandakini; kaale = at the appointed
hour.
verses.translation:
"O, beloved Seetha! Sages with matted locks and wearing antelope-skins and having
bark of trees for their upper garment, take a dip in the River Mandakini, at the appointed
hour."

verses.sanskrit:
आदित्यम् उपतिष्ठन्ते नियमाद् ऊर्ध्व बाहवः |

एते अपरे विशाल अक्षि मुनयः संशित व्रताः || २-९५-७
verses.translation:
"O, Lady of wide eyes! Here are some other sages of austere vows, worshipping the
sun with uplifted arms; as per the scriptural injunctions."

verses.sanskrit:
मारुत उद्धूत शिखरैः प्रनृत्त इव पर्वतः |

पादपैः पत्र पुष्पाणि सृजद्भिर् अभितो नदीम् || २-९५-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. paadapaiH = the trees; maarutodduuta shikharaiH = their
crowns agitated by the breeze; sR^ijadbhiH = that shower down; patra puSpaaNi =
leaves and flowers; nadiim abhitaH = on both sides of the river; parvataH =
(cause) the mountain; pranR^itta iva = to appear as though it were dancing.
verses.translation:
"The trees, their crowns agitated by the breeze, that shower down leaves and flowers
on both sides of the river cause the mountain to appear as though it were dancing!"

verses.sanskrit:
कच्चिन् मणि निकाश उदाम् कच्चित् पुलिन शालिनीम् |

कच्चित् सिद्ध जन आकीर्णाम् पश्य मन्दाकिनीम् नदीम् || २-९५-९
verses.translation:
"See this River Mandakini with its crystal-like clear water at some places, with its
shining sand-dunes at some places and crowded with Siddhas who are bathing at some other
places."
verses.commentary: Siddha: A class of semi-divine beings endowed with mystic powers.

verses.sanskrit:
निर्धूतान् वायुना पश्य विततान् पुष्प संचयान् |

पोप्लूयमानान् अपरान् पश्य त्वम् जल मध्यगान् || २-९५-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. pashya = behold; tvam = you; puSpa saN^chayaan
= heaps of flowers; nirdhuutaan = shaken down; vaayunaa = by the wind;
vitataan = and scattered; pashya = (and) see; aparaan = some other heaps
of flowers; popluuyamaanaan = floating; tanu madhyame = O; lady iwth slender
waist.
verses.translation:
"Behold you, heaps of flowers shaken down by the wind and shattered. See some other
heaps of flowers floating, O lady with slender waist!"

verses.sanskrit:
तामः च अतिवल्गु वचसो रथ अन्ग आह्वयना द्विजाः |

अधिरोहन्ति कल्याणि निष्कूजन्तः शुभाः गिरः || २-९५-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. kalyaaNi = O; Kalyani!; (Behold); valguvachanaH = the
sweet-voiced; rathaaN^gahvayanaaH dvijaaH = the ruddy geese; adhirohanti =
ascending; taan = the banks; vikuujantaH = utteing; shubhaaH =
harmonious; giraH = tones.
verses.translation:
"O, Kalyani! Behold the sweet-voiced ruddy geese, ascending the banks uttering
harmonious tones."

verses.sanskrit:
दर्शनम् चित्र कूटस्य मन्दाकिन्याः च शोभने |

अधिकम् पुर वासाच् च मन्ये च तव दर्शनात् || २-९५-१२
verses.translation:
"I consider this sight of Mandakini River and of Chitrakuta, particularly at your
sight, to be more delightful than dwelling in the City of Ayodhya, O, lovely woman!"

verses.sanskrit:
विधूत कलुषैः सिद्धैः तपो दम शम अन्वितैः |

नित्य विक्षोभित जलाम् विहाहस्व मया सह || २-९५-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. (Here); siddhaiH = Siddhas; vidhuuta kaluSaiH = free
from taints; tapodama shamaanvitaiH = rich in asceticism and control of senses and the
mind; nitya vikSobhita jalaam = perpetually stir the waters; vigaahasva = you too
should enter; mayaa saha = with me;

verses.sanskrit:
सखीवच् च विगाहस्व सीते मन्दकिनीम् इमाम् |

कमलान्य् अवमज्जन्ती पुष्कराणि च भामिनि || २-९५-१४
verses.translation:
"Enter the Mandakini River, as on the breast of a friend, submerging the red and
white lotuses in it- O, Lovely Seetha!"

verses.sanskrit:
त्वम् पौर जनवद् व्यालान् अयोध्याम् इव पर्वतम् |

मन्यस्व वनिते नित्यम् सरयूवद् इमाम् नदीम् || २-९५-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. vanite = My darling!; tvam = you; nityam =
always; manyasva = think; vyaalaan = of the wild beasts; paura janavat =
as inhabitants of the city; parvatam = the mountain of Chitrakuta; ayodhyaamiva =
as the city of Ayodhya; imaam = and this; nadiim = river; sarayuuvat =
as the Sarayu.
verses.translation:
"My darling! Think of the wild beasts as inhabitants of the city of Ayodhya, this
mountain of Chitrakuta as the city of Ayodhya and this river as the Sarayu from now on!"

verses.sanskrit:
लक्ष्मणः चैव धर्म आत्मा मन् निदेशे व्यवस्थितः |

त्वम् च अनुकूला वैदेहि प्रीतिम् जनयथो मम || २-९५-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. vaidehi = O; Seetha!; dharmaatmaa = the virtuous;
lakSmaNashchaapi = Lakshmana; vyavasthitaH = is obeying; mannideshe = my
commands; tvam cha = you too; anukuulaa = are well-disposed; janayathaH
= causing; priitim = delight; mama = to me.
verses.translation:
"O, Seetha!; The virtuous Lakshmana is obeying my commands and you too are
well-disposed, causing delight to me."

verses.sanskrit:
उपस्पृशमः त्रि षवणम् मधु मूल फल अशनः |

न अयोध्यायै न राज्याय स्पृहये अद्य त्वया सह || २-९५-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. upaspR^ishan = bathing; triSa vaNam = three times a day
(in the river); madhumuula phalaashanaH = living on honey; roots and fruits; tvayaa
saha = along with you; na = I do not desire; raajyaaya = the kingdom; na
spR^ihaye = nor do I long; ayodhyaayai = for Ayodhya; adya = now.
verses.translation:
"Bathing three times a day in the river and living on honey, roots and fruits in
your company, I do not desire for the kingdom nor do I long for Ayodhya now."

verses.sanskrit:
इमाम् हि रम्याम् गज यूथ लोलिताम् |

निपीत तोयाम् गज सिम्ह वानरैः |

सुपुष्पितैः पुष्प धरैर् अलम्कृताम् |

न सो अस्ति यः स्यान् न गत क्रमः सुखी || २-९५-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. naasti saH = there is non in the world; yaH na syaat =
who would not be; gata klamaH = relieved of his fatigue; sukhii = and feel
refreshed; imaam = on the banks of this river; ramyaam = enchanting; gaja
yuutha lo Ditaam = frequented by herds of elephants; gaja simha vaanaraiH = who with
lions and monkeys; nipiita toyaam = come here to drink; alamkR^itaam = and which
is adorned; puSpabharaiH = with innumerable flowers; supuSpitaam = in full
bloom.
verses.translation:
"There is none in the world, who would not be relieved of his fatigue and feel
refreshed on the banks of this enchanting river, frequented by herds of elephants, who with lions
and monkeys, come here to drink and which is adorned with innumerable flowers in full bloom."

verses.sanskrit:
इति इव रामो बहु सम्गतम् वचः |

प्रिया सहायः सरितम् प्रति ब्रुवन् |

चचार रम्यम् नयन अन्जन प्रभम् |

स चित्र कूटम् रघु वंश वर्धनः || २-९५-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. bruvan = speaking; bahu = many; samgatam =
suitable; vachaH = words; itiiva = thus; saritamprati = about Mandakini
River; saH raamaH = that Rama; raghuvamsha vardhanaH = who bestows delight to
Raghu dynasty; chachara = strolled; ramyam = in the beautiful;
chitrakuuTam = mountian of Chitrakuta; nayanaanjana prabham = with the radiance
of a piece of collieries.

sargas.title: Sarga 96
sargas.introduction:
Rama hears a gigantic clamour of an army nearby and asks Lakshmana to find out what
it is. Lakshmana ascends a tree and informs Rama that Bharata's army is approaching towards their
hermitage. Lakshmana expresses his apprehension to Rama, saying that Bharata's army may be coming
there in order to kill both of them. An enraged Lakshmana says that he will kill both Bharata and
Kaikeyi together with the army.

verses.sanskrit:
तां तथा दर्शयित्वा तु मैथिलीं गिरिनिम्नगाम् |

निषसाद गिरिप्रस्थे सीतां मांसेन चन्दयन् || २-९६-१

इदं मेध्यमिदं स्वादु निष्टप्तमिदमग्निना |

एवमास्ते स धर्मात्मा सीतया सह राघवः || २-९६-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. saH raaghavaH = that Rama; dharmaatmaa = of
righteousness; aaste = stayed; siitayaa sha = with Seetha; evam = thus
speaking; idam = this meat; madhyam = is fresh; idam = this;
niSTaptam = was roasted; agninaa = in the fire.

verses.sanskrit:
तथा तत्र आसतः तस्य भरतस्य उपयायिनः |

सैन्य रेणुः च शब्दः च प्रादुर् आस्ताम् नभः स्पृशौ || २-९६-३

एतस्मिन्न् अन्तरे त्रस्ताः शब्देन महता ततः |

अर्दिता यूथपा मत्ताः सयूथा दुद्रुवुर् दिशः || २-९६-४

स तम् सैन्य समुद्भूतम् शब्दम् शुश्रव राघवः |

तामः च विप्रद्रुतान् सर्वान् यूथपान् अन्ववैक्षत || २-९६-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. saH raaghavaH = Rama; shushruuva = heard; tam
shabdam = that sound; sainya samuddhuutam = generated from the army;
anvavaikSatea = and saw; sarvaan = all; taan = those; yuuthapaan
cha = leaders of the animal flock; vipradrutaan = running away.

verses.sanskrit:
तामः च विद्रवतो दृष्ट्वा तम् च श्रुत्वा स निह्स्वनम् |

उवाच रामः सौमित्रिम् लक्ष्मणम् दीप्त तेजसम् || २-९६-६

हन्त लक्ष्मण पश्य इह सुमित्रा सुप्रजाः त्वया |

भीम स्तनित गम्भ्हिरः तुमुलः श्रूयते स्वनः || २-९६-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. hanta = Alas!; lakSmaNa = O; Lakshmana; sumitraa
= sumitra; suprajaaH = is blessed with a noble son; tvayaa = in you; iha
= here; shruuyate = is heard; tumulaH = a tumultuous; svanaH = sound;
bhiimastanitagambhiiraH = as terrific as that of a roaring cloud; pashya = see
(what it is). 8. gajayuuthaani = The lordly elephants; araNye = in the
forest; mahiSaaHvaa = the buffaloes; mahaavane = in the great woods;
mR^igaaH = and the wild beasts; vitraasitaaH = frightened; simhaiH = by
lions; sahasaa = are suddenly; pradrutaaH = running away; dishaH = in
various directions.
verses.translation:
"Alas! O, Lakshmana! Sumitra is blessed with a noble son in you. Here is heard a
tumultuous sound as terrific as that of a roaring cloud. See what it is." "The lordly elephants in the forest, the buffaloes in the great woods and the wild
beasts frightened by lions are suddenly running away in various directions."

verses.sanskrit:
राजा वा राज मात्रो वा मृगयाम् अटते वने |

अन्यद् वा श्वा पदम् किंचित् सौमित्रे ज्नातुम् अर्हसि || २-९६-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. saumitre = O; Lakshmana!; arhasi = you ought to;
j^Naatum = know; (whether); raajaa vaa = a king; raaja putrovaa = or a
prince; mR^igayaam aTate = in roaming for a hunt; vaa = or; anyat = any
other; kiNchit = somewhat; shvaapadam = savage beast.
verses.translation:
"O, Lakshmana! You ought to find out whether it is a king or a prince roaming for a
hunt or some other savage beast."

verses.sanskrit:
सुदुश्चरो गिरिश्चायं पक्षिणामपि लक्ष्मण |

सर्वम् एतद् यथा तत्त्वम् अचिराज् ज्नातुम् अर्हसि || २-९६-१०
verses.translation:
"O, Lakshmana! This mountain is difficult of access even to birds. You ought to find
out soon, all this in an accurate manner."

verses.sanskrit:
स लक्ष्मणः सम्त्वरितः सालम् आरुह्य पुष्पितम् |

प्रेक्षमाणो दिशः सर्वाः पूर्वाम् दिशम् अवैक्षत || २-९६-११

उदन् मुखः प्रेक्षमाणो ददर्श महतीम् चमूम् |

रथ अश्व गज सम्बाधाम् यत्तैर् युक्ताम् पदातिभिः || २-९६-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. prekSamaaNaH = looking; udaN^gumukhaH = towards norhern
direction; dadarsha = (Lakshmana) saw; mahatiim = a huge; chamuum =
army; rathaashva gaja sambaadhaam = full of chariots; steeds and elephants;
yuktaam = accompanied by; yataiH = smart; padaatibhiH =
foot-soldiers.

verses.sanskrit:
ताम् अश्व गज सम्पूर्णाम् रथ ध्वज विभूषिताम् |

शशंस सेनाम् रामाय वचनम् च इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-९६-१३

अग्निम् संशमयतु आर्यः सीता च भजताम् गुहाम् |

सज्यम् कुरुष्व चापम् च शरामः च कवचम् तथा || २-९६-१४
verses.translation:
"O, honourable brother! Extinguish the fire. Let Seetha occupy a hiding place. Make
your bow ready with a bow-string and arrows. Wear an armour."

verses.sanskrit:
तम् रामः पुरुष व्याघ्रो लक्ष्मणम् प्रत्युवाच ह |

अन्ग अवेक्षस्व सौमित्रे कस्य एताम् मन्यसे चमूम् || २-९६-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. raamaH = Rama; puruSavyaaghraH = the tiger among men;
pratyuvaacha La = replied; tam lakSmaNam = to that Lakshmana; anga
saumitre = O; Lakshmana!; avekSasya = see; kasya = whose; chamuum =
army; manyase = your think; imaam = is this?.

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्क्तः तु रामेण लक्ष्माणो वाक्यम् अब्रवीत् |

दिधक्षन्न् इव ताम् सेनाम् रुषितः पावको यथा || २-९६-१६

सम्पन्नम् राज्यम् इग्च्छमः तु व्यक्तम् प्राप्य अभिषेचनम् |

आवाम् हन्तुम् समभ्येति कैकेय्या भरतः सुतः || २-९६-१७
verses.translation:
"Bharata, Kaikeyi's son, having obtained coronation and desirous of making the
kingdom free of enemies, is coming here to kill both of us. It is clear."

verses.sanskrit:
एष वै सुमहान् श्रीमान् विटपी सम्प्रकाशते |

विराजत्य् उद्गत स्कन्धः कोविदार ध्वजो रथे || २-९६-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. eSaH = this; sumahaani = very big; shriimaan =
and glorious; ujvala skandhaH = with superb branches; samprakaashate = is
shining; kovidaara dhvajaH = the ensign bearing the device of a Kovidara (pomegranate)
tree; viraajati = is shining; rathe = on that chariot.
verses.translation:
"The gigantic and glorious tree with superb branches is shining over there
prominently. The ensign bearing the device of a kovidara (pomegranate) tree is shining on that
chariot."

verses.sanskrit:
भजन्त्य् एते यथा कामम् अश्वान् आरुह्य शीघ्रगान् |

एते भ्राजन्ति सम्हृष्टा जगान् आरुह्य सादिनः || २-९६-१९
verses.translation:
"O, warrior! Let us take up our bows and ascend the mountain or let us stay here
itself, fully prepared with our weapons in our hands."

verses.sanskrit:
अपि नौ वशम् आगग्च्छेत् कोविदार ध्वजो रणे |

अपि द्रक्ष्यामि भरतम् यत् कृते व्यसनम् महत् || २-९६-२१

त्वया राघव सम्प्राप्तम् सीतया च मया तथा |
verses.wordMeaning:
21. raaghavaH = O; Rama!; kovidaaradhvajaH = will this flag
with the insignia of a Kovidara tree; apyaagachchhet = come into our; vasham =
subjugation?; apidrakSyaami = Can we see; bharatam = Bharata; yatkR^ite
= because of whom; mahat vyasanam = a great misfortune; sampraaptam = is
obtained; tvayaa = by you; siitayaa = by Seetha; tathaa = and; mayaa
cha = by me.
verses.translation:
"O, Rama! Will that flag with the insignia of a Kovidara tree come into our
subjugation? Can we see Bharata, because of whom a great misfortune has occurred to you, to Seetha
and to me?"

verses.sanskrit:
यन् निमित्तम् भवान् राज्याच् च्युतो राघव शाश्वतीम् |

सम्प्राप्तो अयम् अरिर् वीर भरतो वध्य एव मे || २-९६-२२
verses.translation:
"O, Rama the warrior! By the reason of whom you were deprived of an eternal kingdom,
such Bharata as a foe who is coming here, deserves to be slain by me."

verses.sanskrit:
भरतस्य वधे दोषम् न अहम् पश्यामि राघव |

पूर्व अपकारिणम् हत्वा न ह्यधर्मेण युज्यते || २-९६-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. raaghava = O; Rama!; aham = I; napashyaami = do
not see; doSam = a mistake; vadhe = in the slaying; bharatasya = of
Bharata; hatvaa = by killing; puurvaapikaariNam = the one who has hurt another
before; na yujyate = non is charged adharmaNa = with unlawfulness.
verses.translation:
"O, Rama! I see no sin in slaying Bharata. None is charged with unlawfulness, if one
strikes down an aggressor."

verses.sanskrit:
पूर्वापकारी भरतस्य्तक्तधर्मश्च राघव |

एतस्मिन्न् निहते कृत्स्नाम् अनुशाधि वसुंधराम् || २-९६-२४
verses.translation:
"Bharata is the assailant and his death is legitimate. He being dead, you will rule
the entire earth."

verses.sanskrit:
अद्य पुत्रम् हतम् सम्ख्ये कैकेयी राज्य कामुका |

मया पश्येत् सुदुह्ख आर्ता हस्ति भग्नम् इव द्रुमम् || २-९६-२५
verses.translation:
"Let Kaikeyi, who is craving for the kingdom see, with great sorrow, her son slain
by me today in battle, as a tree is crushed down by an elephant."

verses.sanskrit:
कैकेयीम् च वधिष्यामि सानुबन्धाम् सबान्धवाम् |

कलुषेण अद्य महता मेदिनी परिमुच्यताम् || २-९६-२६
verses.translation:
"I shall kill Kaikeyi too along with her friend the hunch-back and other
accomplices. Let the earth today be relieved of a great sinner."

verses.sanskrit:
अद्य इमम् सम्यतम् क्रोधम् असत्कारम् च मानद |

मोक्ष्यामि शत्रु सैन्येषु कक्षेषु इव हुत अशनम् || २-९६-२७
verses.translation:
"O, warrior bestowing an honour! Today in the concentrated fury that injustice has
kindled I shall scatter the enemy - battalions as a fire consumes the dry wood."

verses.sanskrit:
अद्य एतच् चित्र कूटस्य काननम् निशितैः शरैः |

चिन्दन् शत्रु शरीराणि करिष्ये शोणित उक्षितम् || २-९६-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. adya = today; nishitaiH = with my sharpeed;
sharaiH = arrows; chhindam = I shall slay; shatru shariiraaNi = the
bodies of my foes; kariSye = and make; etat kaananam = this forest; chitra
kuuTasya = of Chitrakuta; shoNitokSitam = overflow with blood.
verses.translation:
"Today, with my sharpened arrows, I shall slay the bodies of my foes and make this
forest of Chitrakuta overflow with blood."

verses.sanskrit:
शरैर् निर्भिन्न हृदयान् कुन्जरामः तुरगामः तथा |

श्वापदाः परिकर्षन्तु नराः च निहतान् मया || २-९६-२९
verses.wordMeaning:
29. kuNjaraan = the elephants; tathaa = and turagaan = the
horses; nirbhinna hR^idayaan = whose breasts were laid open; sharaiH = with my
darts; naraamshcha = and the men; nihataan = struck down; mayaa = by me;
parikarSantu = be dragged about; shvaapadaaH = by savage beasts.
verses.translation:
"The elephants and horses whose breasts were laid open with my darts and the men too
struck down by me, be dragged about by savage beasts."

verses.sanskrit:
शराणाम् धनुषः च अहम् अनृणो अस्मि महा वने |

ससैन्यम् भरतम् हत्वा भविष्यामि न संशयः || २-९६-३०
verses.wordMeaning:
30. hatvaa = by killing; bharatam = Bharata; sa
sainyam = together with his army; asmin = in this; mahaavane = great forest;
bhaviSyaami = I shall; anR^iNaH = discharge my debt; sharaaNaam = to my
arows; dhanuSashcha = and my bow.
verses.translation:
"By killing Bharata together with his army in this great forest, I shall discharge
my debt to my bow and arrows."

sargas.title: Sarga 97
sargas.introduction:
Rama pacifies Lakshmana, saying that Bharata, due to his affection towards his
brothers and due to the distress that troubles his mind after hearing of the news of their exile,
he came to the forest and not with any other motive. Ashamed of himself to hear the opinion of Rama
about Bharata, and descending the tree, Lakshmana hides his abashment by changing the topic.
Commanded by Bharata that no disturbance should be caused to the hermitage occupied by Rama, the
army encamps around the edge of the mountain, chitrakuta.

verses.sanskrit:
सुसम्रब्धम् तु सौमित्रिम् लक्ष्मणम् क्रोध मूर्चितम् |

रामः तु परिसान्त्व्य अथ वचनम् च इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-९७-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. atha = then; raamstu = Rama on the other hand;
parisaantvya = pacifying; lakSmaNam = Lakshmana; saumitrim = son of
sumitra; susuamrabdham = who was very much agitated; krodhamuurchhitam = and
infatuated with anger; abraviit = and spoke; idam = these; vachanam cha
= words.

verses.sanskrit:
किम् अत्र धनुषा कार्यम् असिना वा सचर्मणा |

महा इष्वासे महा प्राज्ने भरते स्वयम् आगते || २-९७-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. mahesvaase = wielding a great bow; mahaapraaj^Ne = and
highly wise man; svayam = himself; aagate = is coming; atra = here;
kim kaaryam = what is the use; dhanuSara = or a sword; sacharmaNaa =
with a shield?
verses.translation:
"While Bharata, wieldin a bow and highly wise-man himself is coming here, what is
the use of a bow or sword with a shield?"

verses.sanskrit:
पितुस्सत्यं प्रतिश्रुत्य हत्वा भरतमागतम् |

किम् करिष्यामि राज्येन सापवादेन लक्ष्मण || २-९७-३
verses.translation:
"O, Lakshmana! Having promised to make our father's wish a reality. What shall I do
with the kingdom by acquiring an ill-fame in killing Bharata who came here to see me?" "I should not enjoy the spoils obtained by slaying a relative or friend, like the
partaking of a poisoned food."

verses.sanskrit:
धर्ममर्थं च कामं च पृथिवीं चापि लक्षण |

इच्छामि भवतामर्थे एतत् प्रतिशृणोमि ते || २-९७-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. lakSmaNa = O; Lakshmana!; bhavataam = arthe = It is for
your sake; ichchhaami = I desire; dharmam = virue; artham cha =
(legitimately acquired) wealth; kaamam cha = and pleasure; pR^ithiviimchaapi = or
even earth itself; pratishR^iNomi = I promise; etat = this; te = to
you.
verses.translation:
"O, Lakshmana! It is for your sake I desire virtue, legitimately acquired wealth and
pleasure or even the earth itself. I promise this to you."

verses.sanskrit:
भ्रातृइणां संग्रहार्थं च सुखार्थं चापि लक्ष्मण |

राज्यमप्यहमिच्छामि सत्येनायुधमालभे || २-९७-६
verses.translation:
"O, Lakshmana! I desire the throne only for the protection and happiness of my
brothers. I touch my weapon and swear this fact."

verses.sanskrit:
नेयं मम मही सौम्य दुर्लभा सागराम्बरा |

न हीच्छेयमधर्मेण शक्रत्वमपि लक्ष्मण || २-९७-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. iyam = this earth; saagaraambaraa = bounded by the sea;
na = is not; mama = to me; durlabhaa = hard to obtain; lakSmaNa
= O; Lakshmana; saumya = the excellent man!; na ichchheyam hi = I do not indeed
desire; shakratvamapi = even the position of Indra; adharmeNa = by
unrighteousness.
verses.translation:
"This earth, bounded by the sea is not hard to obtain for me, O the excellent man! I
do not indeed wish even the position of Indra by unrighteousness."

verses.sanskrit:
यद्विना भरतं त्वां च शत्रुघ्नं चापि मानद |

भवेन्मम सुखं किंचिद्भस्म तत्कुरुतां शिखी || २-९७-८
verses.translation:
"If there be happiness that I could enjoy without Bharata or you or without
Shatrughna, may it be without Shatrughna, may it be burnt to ashes by fire, O the
honour-giver!"

verses.sanskrit:
मन्येऽहमागतोऽयेध्यां भरतो भ्रातृवत्सलः |

मम प्राणात्र्पियतरः कुलधर्ममनुस्मरन् || २-९७-९

श्रुत्वा प्रव्राजितं मां हि जटावल्कलधारिणम् |

जानक्यासहितं वीर त्वया च पुरुषर्षभ || २-९७-१०

स्नेहेनाक्रान्तहृदयः शिकेनाकुलितेन्द्रियः |

द्रष्टुमभ्यागतो ह्येष भरतो नान्यथऽऽगतः || २-९७-११
verses.translation:
"I think Bharata was back to Ayodhya, with full of affection for his brothers. He is
dearer to me than my life- he who is mindful of the duties of his race. Hearing of my exile and
that I was wearing matted locks and the antelope skin, accompanied by Seetha and you, O most
valiant of warriors, in his devotion towards me and due to the distress that troubles his mind,
Bharata has come to see me. He has not come with any other motive."

verses.sanskrit:
अम्बाअं च कैकयीं रुष्य परुषं चाप्रियं वदन् |

प्रसाद्य पितरं स्रीमान् राज्यं मे दातुमागतः || २-९७-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. shriimaan = the glorious Bharata; ruSya = enraged;
ambaam = with his mother; kaikeyaam = Kaikeyi; vadan = speaking paruSam
= harshly; apriyamcha = and unkindly; prasaadya = got cleared; pitaram =
by our father; aagataH = and came; daatum = to bestow; raajyam =
kingdom; me = to me.
verses.translation:
"Getting angry with Kaikeyi his mother and speaking unkind and harsh words to her,
the glorious Bharata after getting permission from our further, came here to bestow kingdom to
me."

verses.sanskrit:
प्राप्त कालम् यद् एषो अस्मान् भरतो द्रष्टुम् इग्च्छति |

अस्मासु मनसा अप्य् एष न अहितम् किंचिद् आचरेत् || २-९७-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. praapta kaalam = this is the proper time; eSaH bharataH
= for this Bharata; yat draSTum = ichchhati = to wish to see; asmaan = us;
naacharet = he does not behave; manasena api = or even think; kimchit =
even a little; apriyam = unkindly; asmaasu = towards us.
verses.translation:
"This is the proper time for Bharata to come here and see us. He does not behave or
even think unkindly towards us."

verses.sanskrit:
विप्रियम् कृत पूर्वम् ते भरतेन कदा न किम् |

ईदृशम् वा भयम् ते अद्य भरतम् यो अत्र शन्कसे || २-९७-१४
verses.translation:
"Has Bharata at any time done any harm to you previously? What so the reason for you
to be so apprehensive of Bharata now?"

verses.sanskrit:
न हि ते निष्ठुरम् वाच्यो भरतो न अप्रियम् वचः |

अहम् ह्य् अप्रियम् उक्तः स्याम् भरतस्य अप्रिये कृते || २-९७-१५
verses.translation:
"Indeed, Bharata must not hear harsh or disagreeable word from your lips. If an
affront is offered to him, it is offered to me!"

verses.sanskrit:
कथम् नु पुत्राः पितरम् हन्युः कस्यांचिद् आपदि |

भ्राता वा भ्रातरम् हन्यात् सौमित्रे प्राणम् आत्मनः || २-९७-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. saumitra = O; Lakshma!; katham nu = How should;
putraaH = sons; hamyuH = strike; pitaram = their father; kasyaam
chit aapadi = (even) in anydistress; bhraataaraa = or a brother; hanyaat =
strike; bhraataram = a brother; aatmanaH praaNam = who is as dear to him as his
life?
verses.translation:
"O, Lakshmana! How should a son strike his father, even in a distress or a brother
strike a brother who is as dear to him as his life?"

verses.sanskrit:
यदि राज्यस्य हेतोः त्वम् इमाम् वाचम् प्रभाषसे |

वक्ष्यामि भरतम् दृष्ट्वा राज्यम् अस्मै प्रदीयताम् || २-९७-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. tvam prabhaase yadi = If you are speaking; imaam vaacham
= these words; raajyasya heto = for the sake of the kingdom; dR^iSTvaa = after
seeing; bharatam = Bharata; vakSyaami = I shall tell; asai = him;
pradiiyataam = to give; raajyam = the kingdom.
verses.translation:
"If you are uttering these words for the sake of the kingdom, I shall tell Bharata
to give the kingdom to you, when I see him."

verses.sanskrit:
उच्यमानो हि भरतो मया लक्ष्मण तत्त्वतः |

राज्यम् अस्मै प्रयग्च्छ इति बाढम् इत्य् एव वक्ष्यति || २-९७-१८
verses.wordMeaning:
18. lakSmaNa = O; Lakshmana!; uchyamaanaH = after being
told; iti = that; prayachchha = to give; raajyam = the kingdom;
asmai = to him; tattvataH = really; vakSyati = he will say; baaDham
ityeva = "So be it".
verses.translation:
"O, Lakshmana! When Bharata receives this command from me, "Cede the empire to him",
he will answer, So, be it."

verses.sanskrit:
तथा उक्तो धर्म शीलेन भ्रात्रा तस्य हिते रतः |

लक्ष्मणः प्रविवेश इव स्वानि गात्राणि लज्जया || २-९७-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. tathaa = thus; uktaH = spoken; dharma shiilena
= by his honourable; bhraatraa = brother; tsya = to whom; hite rataaH =
he was devoted; lakSmaNa = ; lajjayaa = abashed; pravivesheva = as
though sinking; svaam = into his; gaatraaNi = limbs.

verses.sanskrit:
तद्वाक्यं लक्ष्मणः श्रुत्वा व्रीशितः प्रत्युवाच ह |

त्व मन्ये द्रष्टुमायातः पिता दशरथः स्वयम् || २-९७-२०

व्रीडितम् लक्ष्मणम् दृष्ट्वा राघवः प्रत्युवाच ह |

एष मन्ये महा बाहुर् इह अस्मान् द्रष्टुम् आगतः || २-९७-२१

अथवा नौ ध्रुवं मन्ये मन्यमानः सुखोचितौ |

वन वासम् अनुध्याय गृहाय प्रतिनेष्यति || २-९७-२२
verses.wordMeaning:
22. athavaa = or rather; manye dhruam = i think surely;
manyamaanaH = as he knows; nau = that we; sukhochitau = have been
accustomed to comfort; anudhyaaya = reflecting; vanavaasam = that we are dwelling
in the forest; pratineSyati = he wishes to bring us; gR^ihaaya = home.
verses.translation:
"Or rather, to my mind, as he knows that we have been accustomed to comfort,
reflecting that we are dwelling in the forest, he wishes to take us home."

verses.sanskrit:
इमाम् वा अप्य् एश वैदेहीम् अत्यन्त सुख सेविनीम् |

एतौ तौ सम्प्रकाशेते गोत्रवन्तौ मनो रमौ || २-९७-२३
verses.translation:
"The glorious Dasaratha, my father possibly will take back Seetha from the forest,
she who has ever lived in the heart of prosperity."

verses.sanskrit:
एतौ तौ संप्रकाशेते गोत्रवन्तौ मनोरमौ |

वायु वेग समौ वीर जवनौ तुरग उत्तमौ || २-९७-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. viira = O; warrior!; tau = those two; etau =
this; turagothamau = excellent horses; gotravantau = of noble breed;
manoramau = attractively; vaayuvega samsu = vying with the wind; jananau
= in swiftness.
verses.translation:
"O, warrior! See those two excellent horses of noble breed, shining attractively and
vying with the wind in swiftness."

verses.sanskrit:
स एष सुमहा कायः कम्पते वाहिनी मुखे |

नागः शत्रुम्जयो नाम वृद्धः तातस्य धीमतः || २-९७-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. eSaH = here is; saH = that; mahaakaayaH =
colossal; naagaH = elephant; kampate = who is moving; vaahiniimukhe = at
the head of the army; shatruNjayo naama = called Shatrumjaya; vR^iddhaH = the
aged; (companion); dhiimataH = of our sagacious; taatasya = father. 26. mahaa baaho = O; the mighty armed !; na pashyaami = I do
not see; tat chhatramtu = paaNDaram = that white canopy; divyam = the heavenly
one; loka satkR^itam = well-known in the world; me = to me; samshayaH =
an apprehension; iha = on this point; bhavati = is created.
verses.translation:
"Here is that colossal elephant who is moving at the head of the army called
Shatrunjaya, the aged companion of our sagacious father." "O, the mighty armed! But I do not see that white heavenly canopy of our father,
well known insignia in the world. An apprehension on this point is created in my mind."

verses.sanskrit:
वृक्षाग्रादवरोह त्वं कुरु लक्ष्म्मण मद्वचः |

इतीव रामो धर्मात्मा सौमित्रिं तमुवाच ह || २-९७-२७
verses.translation:
"O, Lakshmana! You get down from the top of the tree. Act on my word." Thus spoke
the virtuous Rama to that Lakshmana.

verses.sanskrit:
अवतीर्य तु साल अग्रात् तस्मात् स समितिम् जयः |

लक्ष्मणः प्रान्जलिर् भूत्वा तस्थौ रामस्य पार्श्वतः || २-९७-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. avatiirya desending; tasmaat = fromt hat; saalaagraat =
top of Sala tree; saH lakSmanaH = that ; samitinjayaH = the victorious in battle;
bhuutvaa = becoming; praaNjaliH = one with joined palms; tasthau =
stood; paarshvataH = by the side; raamasya = of Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
भरतेन अथ संदिष्टा सम्मर्दो न भवेद् इति |

समन्तात् तस्य शैलस्य सेना वासम् अकल्पयत् || २-९७-२९

अध्यर्धम् इष्क्वाकु चमूर् योजनम् पर्वतस्य सा |

पार्श्वे न्यविशद् आवृत्य गज वाजि रथ आकुला || २-९७-३०

सा चित्र कूटे भरतेन सेना |

धर्मम् पुरः कृत्य विधूय दर्पम् |

प्रसादन अर्थम् रघु नन्दनस्य |

विरोचते नीतिमता प्रणीता || २-९७-३१

sargas.title: Sarga 98
sargas.introduction:
Bharata instructs Shatrughna and Guha along with some troops each to search for Rama
and Lakshmana in the forest. Bharata, personally, along with his ministers citizens and priests,
proceed on foot to some other side of the forest for the search. He goes through the forest for
some time. Climbing up a sala tree, Bharata observes a plume of smoke at a distance and conjectures
that place as Rama's hermitage. Bharata quickly proceeds with quick steps, along with Guha, to see
Rama.

verses.sanskrit:
निवेश्य सेनाम् तु विभुः पद्भ्याम् पादवताम् वरः |

अभिगन्तुम् स काकुत्स्थम् इयेष गुरु वर्तकम् || २-९८-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. saH = that Bharata; vibhuH = the mighty; varaH =
and the excellent; paadavataam = among men (those having feet); niveshya =
(after) encamping; senaam = the army; iyeshha = sought; abhigantum = to
approach; kaakutthsam = Rama; guruvartakam = who was abiding his;
padbhyaam = by foot.

verses.sanskrit:
निविष्ट मात्रे सैन्ये तु यथा उद्देशम् विनीतवत् |

भरतो भ्रातरम् वाक्यम् शत्रुघ्नम् इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-९८-२

क्षिप्रम् वनम् इदम् सौम्य नर सम्घैः समन्ततः |

लुब्धैः च सहितैर् एभिः त्वम् अन्वेषितुम् अर्हसि || २-९८-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. saumyaH = O; gentle brother!; tvam = you;
narasaN^ghaiH = along with a troop of men; lubdhaishcha sahitaiH = and along with
trackers; arhasi = ought; anveSitum = to search; samantataH = on all
sides; idam anam = of this forest.
verses.translation:
"O, gentle brother! You, along with a troop of men and trackers, ought to search on
all sides of this forest."

verses.sanskrit:
गुहो ज्ञातिसहस्रेण शरचापासिधारिणा |

समन्वेषतु काकुत्थ्समस्मिन् परिवृतः स्वयम् || २-९८-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. guhaH = (Let) Guha; svayam = himself;
samanveSutu = search; kaakutthsau = for Rama and Lakshmana; asmin = in
this forest; parivR^itaH = accompanied; j^Naati sahasreNa = by his multitude of
kinsmen; shara chaapaasi dhaariNaa = wielding arrows; bows and swords.
verses.translation:
"Let Guha search for Rama and Lakshmana in this forest, himself accompanied by a
multitude of his kinsmen, duly wielding their bows, arrows and swords."

verses.sanskrit:
अमात्यैः सह पौरैश्च गुरुभिश्च द्विजातिभिः |

वनं सर्वं चरिष्यामि पद्भ्यां परिवृतः स्वयम् || २-९८-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. svayam = I; too; parivR^itaH = encircled; amaatyaiH
saha = along with ministers; parveshcha = citizens; gurubhishcha = priests;
dvijaatibhiH = and Brahmanas; chariSyaami = shall move; padbhyaam = by
foot; sarvam = in the entire; vanam = forest.
verses.translation:
"I too, surrounded by the ministers, citizens, priests and Brahmanas; shall
personally go round the entire forest on foot.

verses.sanskrit:
यावन् न रामम् द्रक्ष्यामि लक्ष्मणम् वा महा बलम् |

वैदेहीम् वा महा भागाम् न मे शान्तिर् भविष्यति || २-९८-६
verses.translation:
"Peace will not be with me until I do not see Rama or the very mighty Lakshmana or
the illustrious Seetha."

verses.sanskrit:
यावन् न चन्द्र सम्काशम् द्रक्ष्यामि शुभम् आननम् |

भ्रातुः पद्म पलाश अक्षम् न मे शान्तिर् भविष्यति || २-९८-७
verses.translation:
"Peace will not be with me until I do not see the auspicious face of my elder
brother, resembling the moon and having eyes bearing resemblance to lotus-leaves.

verses.sanskrit:
यावन् न चरणौ भ्रातुः पार्थिव व्यन्जन अन्वितौ |

शिरसा धारयिष्यामि न मे शान्तिर् भविष्यति || २-९८-८
verses.translation:
"Peace will nt be with me until I do not hold firmly on my head, the soles of my
elder brother, bearing royal insignia.

verses.sanskrit:
यावन् राज्ये राज्य अर्हः पितृ पैतामहे स्थितः |

अभिषेक जल क्लिन्नो न मे शान्तिर् भविष्यति || २-९८-९
verses.translation:
"Peace will not come to me until my elder brother, who is eligible for the crown, is
not installed in the kingdom of Ayodhya, which was derived from our father and grand father and
consecrated by sprinkling water on his head (as a symbol of crowing the kingdom)."

verses.sanskrit:
सिद्धार्थः खलु सौमित्रिर्यश्चन्द्रविमलोवमम् |

मुखं पश्यति रामस्य राजीवाक्षं महाद्युति || २-९८-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. saumitriH = Lakshmana; yaH = who; pashyati = is
seeing; mukham = the face; raamasya = of Rama; mahaa dyuti = having a
great luminance; chandra vimalopamam = resembling the bright moon; raajiivakSam =
and having lotus = eyes; siddhaarthaH khalu = is indeed an accomplished man.
verses.translation:
"Lakshmana, who is seeing regularly the face of Rama having a great luminance
resembling the bright moon and having lotus eyes, is indeed an accomplished man."

verses.sanskrit:
कृत कृत्या महा भागा वैदेही जनक आत्मजा |

भर्तारम् सागर अन्तायाः पृथिव्या या अनुगग्च्छति || २-९८-११
verses.translation:
"Seetha, the illustrious daughter of Janaka, who is following the steps of Rama the
Lord of the earth extending to the sea, has fulfilled her object."

verses.sanskrit:
सुभगः चित्र कूटो असौ गिरि राज उपमो गिरिः |

यस्मिन् वसति काकुत्स्थः कुबेर इव नन्दने || २-९८-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. asau = that; subhagaH = enchanting; chitrakuuTaH
giriH = mountain of Chitrakuta; giriraajopamaH = resembles the King of mountains;
yasmin = on which; kaakutthsaH = Rama; vasati = is residing; kuberaH
iva = as Kubera the Lord of riches; nanadane = is living in the garden of
nandana."
verses.translation:
"That enchanting mountain of Chitrakuta resembles the King of Mountains on which
Rama is residing, as Kubera the Lord of riches is living in the garden of Nandana."

verses.sanskrit:
कृत कार्यम् इदम् दुर्गम् वनम् व्याल निषेवितम् |

यद् अध्यास्ते महा तेजा रामः शस्त्रभृताम् वरः || २-९८-१३
verses.translation:
"Blessed is this dense forest, inhabited by wild animals, where Rama, the great
warrior and the excellent man among the wielders of weapons, dwells."
verses.commentary:
