    GoFTP FREE Version

sargas.title: Sarga 109
sargas.introduction:
Rama refutes the atheistic arguments of Jabali and enunciates the importance of
truthfulness. He states that truth is God and all virtues follow truthfulness. Rama further adds
that there is nothing higher than truth. He announces that he wants to fulfil the command of is
father, who was a devotee of truth and stay back in the forest. Jabali replies that just for the
sake of convincing Rama to return to Ayodhya, he has spoken the words of a non-believer.

verses.sanskrit:
जाबालेः तु वचः श्रुत्वा रामः सत्य आत्मनाम् वरः |

उवाच परया युक्त्या स्व बुद्ध्या च अविपन्नया || २-१०९-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. shrutvaa = hearing; jaabaaleH = Jabali's; vachaH
= words; raamaH = Rama; varaH = the best; satyaatmanaam = of virtuous
and upright men; uvaacha = spoke; varayaa = with great; bhaktyaa =
devotion; avipannayaa = and with an undisturbed; svabuddhyaa = mind of his
own.

verses.sanskrit:
भवान् मे प्रिय काम अर्थम् वचनम् यद् इह उक्तवान् |

अकार्यम् कार्य सम्काशम् अपथ्यम् पथ्य सम्मितम् || २-१०९-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. vachanam = the words; yat = which; bhavaan =
you; uktavaan = spoke; iha = now; priyakaamaartham = wishing to please
me; akaaryam = are impossible; kaarya saN^kaasham = which appears to be possible;
apathyam = It is like a forbidden food; pathya sammatam = appearing to be an
agreeable one.
verses.translation:
"That which, in your desire to please me, you have counselled, is impossible though
it appears to be possible. It is like a forbidden food, appearing to be an agreeable one."

verses.sanskrit:
निर्मर्यादः तु पुरुषः पाप आचार समन्वितः |

मानम् न लभते सत्सु भिन्न चारित्र दर्शनः || २-१०९-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. puruSastu = A man; nirmaryaadaH = who is unruly;
paapachaara samnvitaH = fully endowed with ill-conduct; bhinna chaaritra
darshinaH = having a bad reputation and seeing differentiation in everything; na
labhate = does not gain; maanam = respect; satsu = from honest men.
verses.translation:
"He who is unruly, fully invested with ill-conduct, having a bad reputation and
seeing differences in everything, does not gain respect from honest men."

verses.sanskrit:
कुलीनम् अकुलीनम् वा वीरम् पुरुष मानिनम् |

चारित्रम् एव व्याख्याति शुचिम् वा यदि वा अशुचिम् || २-१०९-४
verses.translation:
"One's conduct itself explains whether one belongs to a good family or a bad family,
valiant or arrogant and chaste or unchaste."

verses.sanskrit:
अनारयः तु आर्य सम्काशः शौचाद्द् हीनः तथा शुचिः |

लक्षण्यवद् अलक्षण्यो दुह्शीलः शीलवान् इव || २-१०९-५

अधर्मम् धर्म वेषेण यदि इमम् लोक सम्करम् |

अभिपत्स्ये शुभम् हित्वा क्रिया विधि विवर्जितम् || २-१०९-६

कः चेतयानः पुरुषः कार्य अकार्य विचक्षणः |

बहु मंस्यति माम् लोके दुर्वृत्तम् लोक दूषणम् || २-१०९-७
verses.translation:
"What sensible man, able to discern what is just and what is unjust, in this world,
would respect me, if I am ignoble resembling as noble, bereft of honesty, impure, having no good
qualities but appearing like the one having good qualities, ill-behaved but appearing as
well-behaved abandoning righteousness and getting hold of unrighteousness in the guise of piety,
creating confusion in the world and disregarding rules of conduct."

verses.sanskrit:
कस्य यास्याम्य् अहम् वृत्तम् केन वा स्वर्गम् आप्नुयाम् |

अनया वर्तमानो अहम् वृत्त्या हीन प्रतिज्नया || २-१०९-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. aham = (If) I; vartamaanaH = behave; anayaa = in
this; vR^ittyaa = manner; hiina pratijJNayaa = faithlessly; kasya = to
whom; daasyaami = can I give; vR^ittam = good conduct? kena = by which;
aapruyaam = can I attain; svargam = heaven?
verses.translation:
"If I behave in this manner faithlessly, to whom can I advise a prescribed conduct?
How would I attain heaven?"

verses.sanskrit:
काम वृत्तः तु अयम् लोकः कृत्स्नः समुपवर्तते |

यद् वृत्ताः सन्ति राजानः तद् वृत्ताः सन्ति हि प्रजाः || २-१०९-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. ayam = this; kR^itsnaH = entire; lokaH = world;
samupartate = would follow; kaama vR^ittaH = its own whims; yadvR^ittaaH
= for whatever the conduct; raajaanaH = of the kings; santi hi = may be santi
tadvR^ittaaH = such will be the conduct; prajaaH = of their subjects.
verses.translation:
"This entire world would follow its own whims, for, whatever the conduct of the
kings may be, such will be the coduct of their subjects."

verses.sanskrit:
सत्यम् एव आनृशंस्यम् च राज वृत्तम् सनातनम् |

तस्मात् सत्य आत्मकम् राज्यम् सत्ये लोकः प्रतिष्ठितः || २-१०९-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. sanaatanam = the eternal; raaja vR^ittam = royal
governance; satyameva = is indeed an assemble of truth; anR^ishamsamcha = and not
cruel; tasmaat = hence; raajyam- the kingship; satyaatmakam = has the truth as
its essence; lokaH = the world; pratiSThitaH = is established; satye =
in truth.
verses.translation:
"The eternal royal governance is indeed an assemble of truth and not cruel. Hence,
the kingship has the Truth as its essence. The world is established in Truth."

verses.sanskrit:
ऋषयः चैव देवाः च सत्यम् एव हि मेनिरे |

सत्य वादी हि लोके अस्मिन् परमम् गग्च्छति क्षयम् || २-१०९-११
verses.translation:
"Even sages and divine being have respected truthfulness alone. The one who speaks
truth obtains the highest position in this world."

verses.sanskrit:
उद्विजन्ते यथा सर्पान् नराद् अनृत वादिनः |

धर्मः सत्यम् परो लोके मूलम् स्वर्गस्य च उच्यते || २-१०९-१२
verses.translation:
"People fear of a person, who speaks untruth, as one fears a snake. Truth is the
highest virtue and is stated to be the origin of heaven."

verses.sanskrit:
सत्यम् एव ईश्वरो लोके सत्यम् पद्मा समाश्रिता |

सत्य मूलानि सर्वाणि सत्यान् न अस्ति परम् पदम् || २-१०९-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. satyameva = truth alone; iishvaraH = i god loke = in
this world; padmaa = the goddess of fortune; sadaa = always; aashritaa =
attaches herself to; truth; sarvaani = All; satyamuulani = are rooted in truth;
naasti = there is no; padam = position; param = higher; satyaat
= then truth.
verses.translation:
"Truth is god and all virtues follow truth. All are rooted in truth there is nothing
higher than truth."

verses.sanskrit:
दत्तम् इष्टम् हुतम् चैव तप्तानि च तपांसि च |

वेदाः सत्य प्रतिष्ठानाः तस्मात् सत्य परो भवेत् || २-१०९-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. dattam = gift; iSTamcha = sacrifice; hutam
chaia = oblation; tapaamsiaha = austerities; taptaani = performed;
vedaaH = and the scriptural texts; satya pratiSThaanaaH = have the foundation in
Truth; tasmaat = hence; bhavet = one should; satya paraH = thoroughly
surrender to truth.
verses.translation:
"Gift sacrifice, oblation, austerities performed and the scriptural texts have the
foundation in Truth. Hence, one should thoroughly surrender to truth.

verses.sanskrit:
एकः पालयते लोकम् एकः पालयते कुलम् |

मज्जत्य् एको हि निरयः एकः स्वर्गे महीयते || २-१०९-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. ekaH = one; paalayite = rules; lokam = over the
world; ekaH = one paalayate = rules; kuma = a race; ekaH = one;
majjati = sinks into; niraye = hell; ekaH = one; mahiiyate =
rises high; svarge = to heaven; (according to the degree of truthfulness practiced).
verses.translation:
"One rules over the world. One develops a race. One sinks into hell . One rises high
to heaven (according to one's degree of truthfulness practiced)."

verses.sanskrit:
सो अहम् पितुर् निदेशम् तु किम् अर्थम् न अनुपालये |

सत्य प्रतिश्रवः सत्यम् सत्येन समयी कृतः || २-१०९-१६
verses.wordMeaning:
16. saH aham = I as such; satya pratishravaH = am true to my
promise; kimartham = why; naanupalaye = should I not fulfill; satyam =
the true; niyogam = command; pituH = of my father; samayiikR^itam =
which was a devotee satyam = of truth.
verses.translation:
"I am true to my promise. Why should I not fulfilll the command of my father, who
was a devotee of truth?"

verses.sanskrit:
न एव लोभान् न मोहाद् वा न च अज्नानात् तमो अन्वितः |

सेतुम् सत्यस्य भेत्स्यामि गुरोः सत्य प्रतिश्रवः || २-१०९-१७
verses.translation:
"Neither covetousness nor forgetfulness nor pride would cause me to destroy the bond
of morality. I shall honour the vow made to my father."

verses.sanskrit:
असत्य संधस्य सतः चलस्य अस्थिर चेतसः |

न एव देवा न पितरः प्रतीग्च्छन्ति इति नः श्रुतम् || २-१०९-१८
verses.translation:
"Neither gods nor the manes will accept the offerings of those who are wanting in
truth, unsteady and unstable in their minds. This is what is taught to us."

verses.sanskrit:
प्रत्यग् आत्मम् इमम् धर्मम् सत्यम् पश्याम्य् अहम् स्वयम् |

भारः सत् पुरुष आचीर्णः तद् अर्थम् अभिनन्द्यते || २-१०९-१९
verses.translation:
"I perceive this virtue i the form of truthfulness as a universal permeation of
spirit. That is why, this burden, observed as a vow, has been honoured by good men."

verses.sanskrit:
क्षात्रम् धर्मम् अहम् त्यक्ष्ये ह्य् अधर्मम् धर्म सम्हितम् |

क्षुद्रौर् नृशंसैर् लुब्धैः च सेवितम् पाप कर्मभिः || २-१०९-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. aham = I; tyakSye = renounce; kSaatram dharmam
= the so-called duty of a warrior; adharmam = it is injustice; dharma samhitam =
under the name of justice; sevitam = it is practised; kSudraiH = by petty;
nR^ishamsaiH = cruel; lubdhaiH = covetous; paapakar mabhiH = men of evid
deeds.
verses.translation:
"I renounce the so-called duty of a warrior, it is injustice under the name of
justice, it is practised by petty cruel and covetous men of evil deeds."

verses.sanskrit:
कायेन कुरुते पापम् मनसा सम्प्रधार्य च |

अनृतम् जिह्वया च आह त्रिविधम् कर्म पातकम् || २-१०९-२१
verses.translation:
"Sin is committed by the body after it has been conceived by the mind and falsehood
is spoken with the tongue. Thus, the degrading act is of three types (with body, mind and
tongue)"

verses.sanskrit:
भूमिः कीर्तिर् यशो लक्ष्मीः पुरुषम् प्रार्थयन्ति हि |

स्वर्गस्थम् च अनुबध्नन्ति सत्यम् एव भजेत तत् || २-१०९-२२
verses.translation:
"The earth, fame, prosperity and fortune indeed woo a man of truth. They constantly
oblige the truth: truth should therefore be strictly observed!"

verses.sanskrit:
श्रेष्ठम् ह्य् अनार्यम् एव स्याद् यद् भवान् अवधार्य माम् |

आह युक्ति करैर् वाक्यैर् इदम् भद्रम् कुरुष्व ह || २-१०९-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. bhavaan = you; avadhaarya = have made it out;
shreSTham = to be good; 'kuruSva = do; idam = this; bhadvam =
good thing; yat = as; aha = uttered; maan = to me; syaat = must
be; anaaryameva = are really unworthy
verses.translation:
"The logical words, you have made it out to be good, saying 'Do this good thing' as
uttered to me, are really unworthy."

verses.sanskrit:
कथम् ह्य् अहम् प्रतिज्नाय वन वासम् इमम् गुरोः |

भरतस्य करिष्यामि वचो हित्वा गुरोर् वचः || २-१०९-२४
verses.translation:
"Having promised before my father about my exile to the forest, how can I fulfil
Bharata's words now, abandoning the father's words?"

verses.sanskrit:
स्थिरा मया प्रतिज्नाता प्रतिज्ना गुरु सम्निधौ |

प्रहृष्ट मानसा देवी कैकेयी च अभवत् तदा || २-१०९-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. sthiraa = a firm; pratijJNaa = promise;
pratijJNaata = has been pledged; mayaa = by me; gurusannidhau = in the
presence of my father; tadaa = then; saa = that; devii kaikeyii cha =
queen Kaikeyi also; abhavata = became; prahR^iSyamaaNaaH = rejoiced.
verses.translation:
"A firm promise has been made by me in the presence of my father, when Queen Kaikeyi
too became rejoiced."

verses.sanskrit:
वन वासम् वसन्न् एवम् शुचिर् नियत भोजनः |

मूलैः पुष्पैः फलैः पुण्यैः पितृऋन् देवामः च तर्पयन् || २-१०९-२६

सम्तुष्ट पन्च वर्गो अहम् लोक यात्राम् प्रवर्तये |

अकुहः श्रद्दधानः सन् कार्य अकार्य विचक्षणः || २-१०९-२७
verses.translation:
"I will proceed with life-journey in this manner, by accepting this dwelling in the
forest, by remaining pure in body and mind, having controlled my diet, by feasting the Gods and
Ancestors with pure roots, flowers and fruits, with all my five senses fully sated, without any
deceit, fully devout and discriminative of what ought to be done and what ought not to be
done."

verses.sanskrit:
कर्म भूमिम् इमाम् प्राप्य कर्तव्यम् कर्म यत् शुभम् |

अग्निर् वायुः च सोमः च कर्मणाम् फल भागिनः || २-१०९-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. praapya = having reached; imaam = this; karma
bhuumim = terrestrial globe (the realm of action); karma = an action; yat =
which; shubham = is virtuous; kartavyam = is to be done; agniH = the god
of fire; vaayushcha = the wind- god; somashcha = and the moon-god;
phalabhaaginaH = partake the fruits; karmaNaam = of their acts.
verses.translation:
"Having reached this terrestrial globe (the realm of action), only a virtuous act is
to be undertaken. The god of fire, the wind-god and the moon-god reap the fruits of their
acts."

verses.sanskrit:
शतम् क्रतूनाम् आहृत्य देव राट् त्रिदिवम् गतः |

तपांस्य् उग्राणि च आस्थाय दिवम् याता महर्षयः || २-१०९-२९
verses.wordMeaning:
29. aahR^itya = having performed; shatam = a hundred;
kratuunaam = sacrifices; devaraat = Indra the Lord of celestials; gataH
= went; tridivam = to heaven; aasthaaya = having practiced; ugraaNi =
severe; tapaamsi = austerities; maharSayaH = the great sages; yaataaH =
went; divam = to heaven.
verses.translation:
"Having performed ahundred sacrifices, Indra the Lord of celestials went to heaven.
Having practised severe austerities, the great sages went to heaven."

verses.sanskrit:
अमृष्यमाणः पुनरुग्रतेजा |

निशम्य तं नास्तिकवाक्यहेतुम् |

अथाब्रवीत्तं नृपतेस्तनूजो |

विगर्हमाणो वचानानि तस्य || २-१०९-३०
verses.wordMeaning:
30. atha = thereafter; nishamya = having heard; tam
= that; naastika vaakya hetum = argument of atheism; (inuntiated by Jabali); nR^ipateH
tanunjaH = that prince; ugra tejaaH = with a terrible energy; amR^iSyamaaNaH
= without tolerating; tasya = his; vachanaani = words; punaH = again;
abraviit = spoke; vigarhamaaNaH = with a reproach; tam = to him.

verses.sanskrit:
सत्यं च धर्मं च पराक्रमं च |

भूतानुकम्पां प्रियवादिताम् च |

द्विजातिदेवातिथिपूजनं च |

पन्थानमाहुस्त्रिदिवस्य सन्तः || २-१०९-३१
verses.translation:
"The virtuous say that truth, piety, valour, compassion for all beings, polite
speech and worship of Brahmanas, gods and unexpected guests are the paths to heaven."

verses.sanskrit:
तेनैवमाज्ञाय यथावदर्थ |

मेकोदयं सम्प्रतिपद्य विप्राः |

धर्मं चरन्तः सकलं यथाव |

त्काज़्^ख़्षन्ति लोकागममप्रमत्ताः || २-१०९-३२

निन्दाम्यहं कर्म पितुः कृतं त |

द्यस्त्वामगृह्णाद्विषमस्थबुद्धिम् |

बुद्ध्यानयैवंविधया चरन्तं |

सुनास्तिकं धर्मपथादपेतम् || २-१०९-३३
verses.translation:
"I accuse the act done by my father in taking you into his service, you with your
misleading intelligence, a firm atheist fallen from the true path." "It is an exact state of the case that a mere *intellection deserves to be punished
as it were a thief and know an atheist to be on par with a mere intellectual. Therefore he is the
most suspectable and should be punished in the interest of the poeple. In no case should a wise man
consort with an atheist." "Men that lived before you have performed many auspicious acts, abandoning all hopes
of reward in this world as well as the next. Therefore, Brahmanas offer oblations in the sacred
fire and practise noble deeds."
verses.commentary:
* It is the word that is responding to the chanllenge, which we call
intellection. Truth/God is very subtle. A mind that is caught in the net of words/arguments cannot
understand truth/God.

verses.sanskrit:
धर्मे रताः सत् पुरुषैः समेताः |

तेजस्विनो दान गुण प्रधानाः |

अहिंसका वीत मलाः च लोके |

भवन्ति पूज्या मुनयः प्रधानाः || २-१०९-३६
verses.translation:
"The sages who are devoted to righteousness, associating with men of virtue, endowed
with spiritual splendour, practising abundant charity, harm-less and washed free of all taint, are
honoured into the world."

verses.sanskrit:
इति ब्रुवन्तं वचनं सरोषं |

रामं महात्मानमदीनसत्त्वम् |

उवाच पथ्यं पुनरास्तिकं च |

सत्यं वचः सानुनयं च विप्रः || २-१०९-३७
verses.wordMeaning:
37. mahaatmaanam = to the high-souled Rama; adiina sattvam =
who never had self-souled Rama; adiina sattvam = who never had self-pity;
bruvantam = and speaking; saroSam = indignantly; iti = as aforesaid;
vipraH = Jabali brahmana; punaH uvaacha = replied; saanunayam = in a
fitting manner; vachaH = in words; pathyam = that were beneficial; satya
= and truthful; aastikam = which showed his belief in authority of Vedas; the other world
and so on.
verses.translation:
"To the high-souled Rama, who never had self-pity and speaking indignantly as
aforesaid, Jabali (that brahmana) replied in a fitting manner in words that were beneficial and
truthful, which showed his belief in authority of Vedas, the other world and so on."

verses.sanskrit:
न नास्तिकानां वचनम् ब्रवीम्यहं |

न नास्तिकोऽहं न च नास्ति किंचन |

समीक्ष्य कालं पुनरास्तिकोऽभवं |

भवेय काले पुनरेव नास्तिकः || २-१०९-३८
verses.translation:
"I am not speaking the words of non- believers. I am not an atheist, nor is it a
fact that nothing exists whatsoever. Perceiving the time, I have become a believer. When the time
comes, I will become again just a non-believer."

verses.sanskrit:
स चापि कालोऽय मुपागतः शनैः |

यथा मया नास्तिकवागुदीरिता |

निवर्तनार्थं तव राम कारणात् |

प्रसादनार्थं च मयैतदीरितम् || || २-१०९-३९
verses.translation:
"O, Rama! That and this time too came gradually. The words of a non-believer were
spoken by me for your sake, to pacify you and to persuade you to return to Ayodhya."
verses.commentary:

sargas.title: Sarga 110
sargas.introduction:
Vasishta gives details of the creation of the world to Sri Rama. He says that
Brahma, in the form of a boar caused the earth to rise from water and from Brahma is created the
entire world. He recites the Genealogy of Rama's line. He informs that Aja and Suvrata were the two
sons of Nabhaga and it was Aja who begot the virtuous King Dasaratha; Rama's father. Vasishta also
informs Rama that i the whole of Ikshvaku race, only the eldest son becomes the king. He requests
Sri Rama not to violate this tradition and take over the reins of Ayodhya.

verses.sanskrit:
क्रुद्धम् आज्ञाय राम तु वसिष्ठः प्रत्युवाच ह।

जाबालिः अपि जानीते लोकस्य अस्य गत आगतिम्॥ २-११०-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. aajJNaaya = recognising; raamam = Rama's;
kruddham = anger; vasiSThaH = Vasishta; pratyuvaacha ha = spoke as
follows: jabaalirapi = even Jabali; jaaniite = is aware; gataagatam = of the
going and coming; asya lokasya = of this world.

verses.sanskrit:
निवर्तयितु कामः तु त्वाम् एतद् वाक्यम् अब्रवीत् |

इमाम् लोक समुत्पत्तिम् लोक नाथ निबोध मे || २-११०-२
verses.translation:
"He apoke in this manner, on account of his desire that you should return. O, Lord
of the poeple! Learn from me of the creation of the world!"

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वम् सलिलम् एव आसीत् पृथिवी यत्र निर्मिता |

ततः समभवद् ब्रह्मा स्वयम्भूर् दैवतैः सह || २-११०-३
verses.translation:
"All was water only in the beginning" from which element the earth was formed. After
that, the self-existent Brahma with all the gods came into existence."

verses.sanskrit:
स वराहः ततो भूत्वा प्रोज्जहार वसुंधराम् |

असृजच् च जगत् सर्वम् सह पुत्रैः कृत आत्मभिः || २-११०-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. tataH = thereafter; saH = that Brahma; bhuutvaa
= assuming; varaahaH = the form of a boar; projjahaara vasundharaam = caused the
earth to rise (from water); putraiH saha = and with his sons; kR^itaatmabhiH = of
pure soul; aasR^ijachcha = created; sarvam = the entire; jagat =
world.
verses.translation:
"Thereafter, that Brahma, assuming the form of boar, caused the earth to rise from
water and with his sons of pure soul, created the entire world."

verses.sanskrit:
आकाश प्रभवो ब्रह्मा शाश्वतो नित्य अव्ययः |

तस्मान् मरीचिः सम्जज्ने मरीचेः कश्यपः सुतः || २-११०-५
verses.translation:
"The eternal, changeless and imperishable Brahma was begotten from ether and from
his was born Marichi. Marichi's son was kashyapa."

verses.sanskrit:
विवस्वान् कश्यपाज् जज्ने मनुर् वैवस्तवः स्मृतः |

स तु प्रजापतिः पूर्वम् इक्ष्वाकुः तु मनोः सुतः || २-११०-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. kashyapaat = from Kashyapa; vivasvaan = Vivasvan;
jajJNe = was born; manuH = Manu; vaivasvataH sutaH = was the son of
Vivasvan; satu = Manu for his part; puurvam = formerly; prajaapatiH =
was the lord of creation; ikSvaakuH = Ikshvaku; manoH = was Manu's sutaH =
son.
verses.translation:
"From Kashyapa, Vivasvan(sun-god) was born. manu was the son of Vivasvan. Manu for
his part, was formerly the lord of creation. Ikshvaku was Manu' s son."

verses.sanskrit:
यस्य इयम् प्रथमम् दत्ता समृद्धा मनुना मही |

तम् इक्ष्वाकुम् अयोध्यायाम् राजानम् विद्धि पूर्वकम् || २-११०-७
verses.translation:
"The entire fertile earth was given by Manu to Ikshvaku and know that Ikshvaku was
thus the first king of Ayodhya!"

verses.sanskrit:
इक्ष्वाकोः तु सुतः श्रीमान् कुक्षिर् एव इति विश्रुतः |

कुक्षेर् अथ आत्मजो वीरो विकुक्षिर् उदपद्यत || २-११०-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. ikSvaakoH = Ikshvaku's; sutaH = son; vishrutaH =
was known as; shriimaan = the illustrious; kukSireveti = Kukshi; atha =
thereafter; kukSeH = kukshi's; aatmajaH = son; viiraH = was the valiant;
vikukSiH = Vikukshi; udapadyata = born.
verses.translation:
"Ikshvaku's son was known as Kukshi, the illustrious king. Then, Kukshi's son was
the valiant Vikukshi."

verses.sanskrit:
विकुक्षेः तु महा तेजा बाणः पुत्रः प्रतापवान् |

बाणस्य तु महा बाहुर् अनरण्यो महा यशाः || २-११०-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. vikukSeH = to Vikukshi (was born); baaNaH = Bana;
mahaatejaaH = the most splendid; prataapavaan = and powerful; putraH =
son; baaNasya = to Bana; (was born); anaraNyaH = Anaranya; mahaabaahuH =
the mighty armed; mayaayashaaH = and the most illustrious one.
verses.translation:
"To Vikukshi was born the most splendid and powerful son, Bana. To Bana was born
Anaranya the mighty armed and the most illustrious son."

verses.sanskrit:
नाना वृष्टिर् बभूव अस्मिन् न दुर्भिक्षम् सताम् वरे |

अनरण्ये महा राजे तस्करो वा अपि कश्चन || २-११०-१०
verses.translation:
"While this King Anaranya, the most excellent among beings was reigning, there was
neither dearth of rain nor a drought. No one was a thief."

verses.sanskrit:
अनरण्यान् महा बाहुः पृथू राजा बभूव ह |

तस्मात् पृथोर् महा राजः त्रिशन्कुर् उदपद्यत || २-११०-११

स सत्य वचनाद् वीरः सशरीरो दिवम् गतः |
verses.translation:
"From Anaranya was born the mighty armed king Prithu. From Prithu was born the
Emperor Trishanku. That valiant man ascneded to heaven along with his mortal body, because of his
true eloquence."

verses.sanskrit:
त्रिशन्कोर् अभवत् सूनुर् धुन्धुमारो महा यशाः || २-११०-१२

धुन्धुमारान् महा तेजा युवन अश्वो व्यजायत |
verses.translation:
"To Trishanku was born a son, the highly illustrious Dundhumara. From Dundhumara was
born the hero, Yuvanasva."

verses.sanskrit:
युवन अश्व सुतः श्रीमान् मान्धाता समपद्यत || २-११०-१३

मान्धातुः तु महा तेजाः सुसंधिर् उदपद्यत |

सुसंधेर् अपि पुत्रौ द्वौ ध्रुव संधिः प्रसेनजित् || २-११०-१४

यशस्वी ध्रुव संधेः तु भरतो रिपु सूदनः |
verses.translation:
"The illustrious Mandhata was born as a son to Yuvanasva. To Mandhata was born the
hero, Susandhi. There were two sons Dhruvasandhi and prasenajit to Susandhi. From Dhruvasandhi was
born the illustrious Bharata, the annihilator of enemies."

verses.sanskrit:
भरतात् तु महा बाहोर् असितो नाम जायत || २-११०-१५

यस्य एते प्रतिराजान उदपद्यन्त शत्रवः |

हैहयाः ताल जन्घाः च शूराः च शश बिन्दवः || २-११०-१६
verses.translation:
"From the mighty armed Bharata was born a son named Asita, for whom his royal
adversaries, Haihayas, Talajanghas and the valiant Shashibindavas became the enemies."

verses.sanskrit:
तामः तु सर्वान् प्रतिव्यूह्य युद्धे राजा प्रवासितः |

स च शैल वरे रम्ये बभूव अभिरतो मुनिः || २-११०-१७
verses.translation:
"Having drawn out his battle-array against all those kings in a combat, the king
Asita was driven away. Asita then became a devoted sage taking asylum in an excellent and charming
mountain."

verses.sanskrit:
द्वे च अस्य भार्ये गर्भिण्यौ बभूवतुर् इति श्रुतिः |

एका गर्भविनाशाय सपत्न्यै गरळं ददौ || २-११०-१८
verses.translation:
"Asita's two wives became pregnant. It is a hearsay that one of his wives gave
poison to the other co-wife in order to destroy her foetus."

verses.sanskrit:
भार्गवः च्यवनो नाम हिमवन्तम् उपाश्रितः |

तम् ऋषिम् समुपागम्य कालिन्दी तु अभ्यवादयत् || २-११०-१९

स ताम् अभ्यवदद् विप्रो वर ईप्सुम् पुत्र जन्मनि |
verses.wordMeaning:
19. chayaveno naama = A sage called Chyavana; bhaargavaH =
belonging to Bhrign race; upaashritaH = was taking a shelter; himavantam = in a
Himalayan mountain; kaalindii = Kalindi; samupaagamya = approached; tam
R^iSim = that sage; abhyavaadayat = and offered her salutaion; saH vipraH =
that brahmana; abhyavadat = spoke; taam = to her; varepsum = who wanted
to get a boon; putrajanmani = for the birth of a son.
verses.translation:
"A sage called Chyavana, belonging to Bhrign race was staying in a Himalayan
mountain. Kalindi (Asita's wife) approached that sage and offered her salutation. That brahmna
spoke the following words to her, who wanted to obtain a boon for the birth of a son."

verses.sanskrit:
पुत्रस्ते भविता देवि महात्मा लोकविश्रुतः || २-११०-२०

धार्मिकश्च सुशीलश्च वंशकर्तारिसूदनः |
verses.translation:
"O, Queen! A high-souled son, who will be world-famous, rightous, of a good conduct,
a perpetuator of the race and an annihilator of enemies will be born to you."

verses.sanskrit:
कृत्वाप्रदक्षिणं हृष्टा मुनिं तमनुमान्य च || २-११०-२१

पद्मपत्रसमानाक्षं पद्मगर्भसमप्रभम् |

ततः सा गृहम् आगम्य देवी पुत्रम् व्यजायत || २-११०-२२
verses.translation:
"The delighted Queen Kalindi circumambulated that sage, took permission from him to
leave and thereafter on reaching home, had delivered a son, having eyes resembling lotus-leaves and
having a radiance like that of Brahama the Lord of creation."

verses.sanskrit:
सपत्न्या तु गरः तस्यै दत्तो गर्भ जिघांसया |

गरेण सह तेन एव जातः स सगरो अभवत् || २-११०-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. garaH = poison; dattaH = was given; tasyaiH =
to her; sapatnyaa = by her co-wife; garbha jighaamsayaa = with an intention to
kill her foetus; jaataH = born; tena gareNa sahaiva = with that poison itself;
saH = he; abhavat = became; sagaraH = Sagara ( a man with poison).
verses.translation:
"Poison was given earlier by her co-wife with an intention to kill her foetus. Born
with that poison itself, he became Sagara (a man with poison)."

verses.sanskrit:
स राजा सगरो नाम यः समुद्रम् अखानयत् |

इष्ट्वा पर्वणि वेगेन त्रासयन्तम् इमाः प्रजाः || २-११०-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. saH = He; yaH = which; sagaro naama raajaa =
king was named as Sagara; iSTvaa = performed sacrifices; parvaNi = on the day of
the full moon; akhaanayat = got excavated; samndram = the ocean; traasa
yantam = frightening; imaam prajaaH = these people; vegena = by his seed of
digging.
verses.translation:
"It was king SAgara who excavated the ocen and who, by his sacrifice, on the day of
the full moon, by his energy, frightened the people here by the speed of his digging."

verses.sanskrit:
असमन्जः तु पुत्रो अभूत् सगरस्य इति नः श्रुतम् |

जीवन्न् एव स पित्रा तु निरस्तः पाप कर्म कृत् || २-११०-२५
verses.wordMeaning:
25. asamaNJNaH = Asmanja; abhuut = was; sagarasya =
Sagara's; putraH = son; shrutam = there was a hearsay; naH = to us;
iti = that; saH = Asamanja; paapakarama kR^it = on account of his wicked
deeds; nirastah = was banished; pitraa = by his fatehr; jiivanneva =
even while living.
verses.translation:
"Asamanja was Sagara's son. There was a hearsay that on account of his wicked deeds,
Asamanja was banished by his father even during his life time."

verses.sanskrit:
अंशुमान् इति पुत्रो अभूद् असमन्जस्य वीर्यवान् |

दिलीपो अंशुमतः पुत्रो दिलीपस्य भगीरथः || २-११०-२६
verses.translation:
"A valiant son called amshuman was born to Asamanja. Dilipa was Amshuman's son.
Bhagiratha was Dilipa's son.

verses.sanskrit:
भगीरथात् ककुत्स्थः तु काकुत्स्था येन तु स्मृताः |

ककुत्स्थस्य तु पुत्रो अभूद् रघुर् येन तु राघवः || २-११०-२७
verses.wordMeaning:
27. bhagiirathaat = of Bhagiratha; kakutthsaH = was born;
kaakutthaaH = the Kakutsthas; vishrutaaH = take their name; kakutthsasya
cha = to Kakutstha; abhuut = was born; putra = a son; raghuH =
called Raghu; yena = from whence (spring); raaghavaaH = Ragahavas.
verses.translation:
"Of Bhagiratha was born kakutstha, from whom the Kakutsthas take their name. To
Kakutsthas was born a son called Raghu, from whence spring Raghavas."

verses.sanskrit:
रघोः तु पुत्रः तेजस्वी प्रवृद्धः पुरुष अदकः |

कल्माष पादः सौदास इत्य् एवम् प्रथितो भुवि || २-११०-२८
verses.translation:
"From Raghu was born a renowned son named Pravriddha, known in the world under the
names Purushadaka, Kalmashapada and Soudasa."

verses.sanskrit:
कल्माष पाद पुत्रो अभूत् शन्खणः तु इति विश्रुतः |

यः तु तद् वीर्यम् आसाद्य सह सेनो व्यनीनशत् || २-११०-२९
verses.translation:
"Kalmashapada's son was renowned as Shankhana, who, even on attaining his father's
valour, perished (in a battle) along with his army."

verses.sanskrit:
शन्खणस्य तु पुत्रो अभूत् शूरः श्रीमान् सुदर्शनः |

सुदर्शनस्य अग्नि वर्ण अग्नि वर्षस्य शीघ्रगः || २-११०-३०

शीघ्रगस्य मरुः पुत्रो मरोः पुत्रः प्रशुश्रुकः |

प्रशुश्रुकस्य पुत्रो अभूद् अम्बरीषो महा द्युतिः || २-११०-३१

अम्बरीषस्य पुत्रो अभून् नहुषः सत्य विक्रमः |

नहुषस्य च नाभागः पुत्रः परम धार्मिकः || २-११०-३२

अजः च सुव्रतः चैव नाभागस्य सुताउ उभौ |

अजस्य चैव धर्म आत्मा राजा दशरथः सुतः || २-११०-३३
verses.translation:
"You are the eldest son of that Dasaratha, very well-known as Rama, the heir who can
claim over the inheritance. O, King! Hence, take over your kingdom and look after your people
there."

verses.sanskrit:
इक्ष्वाकूणाम् हि सर्वेषाम् राजा भवति पूर्वजः |

पूर्वजेन अवरः पुत्रो ज्येष्ठो राज्ये अभिषिच्यते || २-११०-३५
verses.translation:
"The eldest son only becomes the king in the entire Ikshvaku race. When the eldest
son exists, the younger son will not become a king. The eldest son only is anointed to the
crown.

verses.sanskrit:
स राघवाणाम् कुल धर्मम् आत्मनः |

सनातनम् न अद्य विहातुम् अर्हसि |

प्रभूत रत्नाम् अनुशाधि मेदिनीम् |

प्रभूत राष्ट्राम् पितृवन् महा यशाः || २-११०-३६
verses.wordMeaning:
36. mahaayashaH = O; the celebrated one!; sanaatanam = this
is the eternal; kuladharmam = tradition of your race; raghuvaaNaam = those born
in Raghu dynasty; aatmanaH = yours; saH = as such; naarhasi = and ought
not; vihantum = to be violated by you; adya = now; anushaadhi = rule
over; mediniim = the earth; prabhuuta ratnaam = abundant with precious metals;
prabhuuta raaSTraam = and a vast kingdom; pitR^ivat = as did your father.
verses.translation:
"O, the celebrated one! This is the eternal tradition of your race, those born in
Raghu dynasty and ought not to be violated by you. Rule over the earth, this vast kingdom abudant
with precious metals, as did your father."
verses.commentary:

sargas.title: Sarga 111
sargas.introduction:
Vashishta urges Rama to grant the prayer of Bharata. Rama replies that the command
given by his father cannot be disregarded. Bharata then decides to undertake a fest unto death as a
last resort to exert pressure on Rama. On Rama's dubbing such a step as repugnant to the code of
conduct prescribed for a kshatriya, Bharata requests Rama to allow him to remain in the forest as
the latter's proxy. Rama, however, rules out this proposition also, saying that it is a matter of
reproach. He reaffirms his resolve to enter Ayodhya only after implementing the pledge given to his
father.

verses.sanskrit:
वसिष्ठः तु तदा रामम् उक्त्वा राज पुरोहितः |

अब्रवीद् धर्म सम्युक्तम् पुनर् एव अपरम् वचः || २-१११-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. uktvaa = having spoken (thus); raamam = to Rama;
vasiSThastu = Vasistha; raaja purohitaH = the royal priest; tadaa =
then; punareva = again; abraviit = spoke; aparam vachaH = (the
following) further words; dharmasamyuktam = which were righteous.

verses.sanskrit:
पुरुषस्य इह जातस्य भवन्ति गुरवः त्रयः |

आचार्यः चैव काकुत्स्थ पिता माता च राघव || २-१११-२
verses.translation:
"O, Kakutstha, O offspring the Raghu dynasty! From birth, the three spiritual
instructors of a man are his teacher, his father and his mother."

verses.sanskrit:
पिता ह्य् एनम् जनयति पुरुषम् पुरुष ऋषभ |

प्रज्नाम् ददाति च आचार्यः तस्मात् स गुरुर् उच्यते || २-१११-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. puruSarSabha = O; the excellent among men!; pitaa = the
father; janayatihi enam puruSam = gives man his life; aachaaryaH = the teacher;
dadaati = instructs (him); prajJNaam = in wisdom; tasmaat = and
therefore; saH = the teacher; uchyate = is said to be; guruH = the
superior.
verses.translation:
"O, excellent among men! The father of man his life. The teacher instructs him in
wisdom and therefore instructs the teacher is said to be the superior!"

verses.sanskrit:
से ते अहम् पितुर् आचार्यः तव चैव परम् तप |

मम त्वम् वचनम् कुर्वन् न अतिवर्तेः सताम् गतिम् || २-१११-४
verses.wordMeaning:
4. paramtapa = O; Rama; the tormentator of enemies!; saH
aham = that I; aachaaryaH = am the spiritual preceptor; te pituH = to your
father; tavachaiva = and to you too; kurvam = In obeying; mama = my
vachanam = words; tvam = you; naativartate = will not transgress; niitim
= the path; sataam = of the virtuous.
verses.translation:
"O, Rama the tormentator of the enemies! I am the spiritual Preceptor to your father
and to you too. In obeying my words, you will not transgress the path of the virtuous."

verses.sanskrit:
इमा हि ते परिषदः श्रेणयः च समागताः |

एषु तात चरन् धर्मम् न अतिवर्तेः सताम् गतिम् || २-१११-५
verses.translation:
"O, dear prince! These subjects, the traders, the other categories of people and
Brahmana are your people only. In fulfilling your duty to them, you will not be transgressing your
righteous path."

verses.sanskrit:
वृद्धाया धर्म शीलाया मातुर् न अर्हस्य् अवर्तितुम् |

अस्याः तु वचनम् कुर्वन् न अतिवर्तेः सताम् गतिम् || २-१११-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. naarhasi = you ought not; avartitum = to be lacking in
reverence; maatuH = to your mother; vR^iddhayaa = who is aged; dharma
shiilayaaH = and of noble conduct; kurvan = by carrying out; asyaaH = her;
vachanam = words; naati vartate = you will not deviate; gatim = from the
path; sataam = of the virtuous.
verses.translation:
"You ought not to be lacking in reverence to your mother who is aged and possessing
a noble conduct. By carrying out her words, you will not deviate from the path of the
virtuous."

verses.sanskrit:
भरतस्य वचः कुर्वन् याचमानस्य राघव |

आत्मानम् न अतिवर्तेः त्वम् सत्य धर्म पराक्रम || २-१११-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. raaghava = O; Rama; satyadharmaparaakramaH = excelling in
truth and virtue!; kurvan = by fulfilling; vachaH = the words;
bharatasya = of Bharata; yaachamaanasya = who is beseeching (you); tvam
= you; naativartate = will not be false; aatmaanam = to yourself.
verses.translation:
"O, Rama excelling in truth and virtue. By fulfilling the words of the beseeching
Bharata, you will not be false to yourself."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् मधुरम् उक्तः तु गुरुणा राघवः स्वयम् |

प्रत्युवाच समासीनम् वसिष्ठम् पुरुष ऋषभः || २-१११-८

यन् मातापितरौ वृत्तम् तनये कुरुतः सदा |

न सुप्रतिकरम् तत् तु मात्रा पित्रा च यत् कृतम् || २-१११-९

यथा शक्ति प्रदानेन स्नापनाच् चादनेन च |

नित्यम् च प्रिय वादेन तथा सम्वर्धनेन च || २-१११-१०
verses.translation:
"The good, which a father and mother do, by giving whatever they can, to their son
constantly, by putting him to sleep, rubbing his body with oil etc, nay, by speaking kindly to him
every moment, and even by nourishing him, can never be wholly requited."

verses.sanskrit:
स हि राजा जनयिता पिता दशरथो मम |

आज्नातम् यन् मया तस्य न तन् मिथ्या भविष्यति || २-१११-११
verses.translation:
"The command laid upon me by king Dasaratha, my father, who begot me, cannot
therefore be disregarded."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्तः तु रामेण भरतः प्रत्यनन्तरम् |

उवाच परम उदारः सूतम् परम दुर्मनाः || २-१११-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. evam = thus; uktaH = spoken; raameNa = by Rama;
bharataH = Bharata; paramodaaraH = the highly generous man; parama
durmanaaH = was very much anguished; uvaacha = and spoke; suutam = to
Sumantra the charioteer; pratyanantaram = who was standing nearby.

verses.sanskrit:
इह मे स्थण्डिले शीघ्रम् कुशान् आस्तर सारथे |

आर्यम् प्रत्युपवेक्ष्यामि यावन् मे न प्रसीदति || २-१११-१३
verses.translation:
"O, charioteer! Spread speedily the Kusha grass on the ground here for me. I shall
remain facing my illustrious brother till he gets propitiated (and grant my request)."

verses.sanskrit:
अनाहारो निरालोको धन हीनो यथा द्विजः |

शेष्ये पुरस्तात् शालाया यावन् न प्रतियास्यति || २-१११-१४
verses.translation:
"I shall remain lying down in front of Rama's hut without food or drink and
depriving myself of light until he returns to Ayodhya, like a brahman who is a destitute (lies down
at the door of his crditor)."

verses.sanskrit:
स तु रामम् अवेक्षन्तम् सुमन्त्रम् प्रेक्ष्य दुर्मनाः |

कुश उत्तरम् उपस्थाप्य भूमाउ एव आस्तरत् स्वयम् || २-१११-१५

तम् उवाच महा तेजा रामो राज ऋषि सत्तमाः |

किम् माम् भरत कुर्वाणम् तात प्रत्युपवेक्ष्यसि || २-१११-१६

ब्राह्मणो ह्य् एक पार्श्वेन नरान् रोद्धुम् इह अर्हति |

न तु मूर्धा अवसिक्तानाम् विधिः प्रत्युपवेशने || २-१११-१७
verses.translation:
"A brahmana may lie down on one side in order to obstruct a willful debtor in this
world. But, it is not befitting for a warrior-class.

verses.sanskrit:
उत्तिष्ठ नर शार्दूल हित्वा एतद् दारुणम् व्रतम् |

पुर वर्याम् इतः क्षिप्रम् अयोध्याम् याहि राघव || २-१११-१८
verses.translation:
"O Bharata, Tiger among men! Giving up this formidable resolve, rise up proceed
speedily to Ayodhya the best of cities, from here."

verses.sanskrit:
आसीनः तु एव भरतः पौर जानपदम् जनम् |

उवाच सर्वतः प्रेक्ष्य किम् आर्यम् न अनुशासथ || २-१११-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. aasiinastveva = still sitting like that; bharataH =
Bharata; prekSya = looking at; janam = the people; paura jaanapadam =
the citizens and the rural folk; sarvataH = on all sides; uvaacha = and asked;
kim = why; naanushaasatha = they are not entreating; aaryam = his
venerable brother (to return)

verses.sanskrit:
ते तम् ऊचुर् महात्मानम् पौर जानपदा जनाः |

काकुत्स्थम् अभिजानीमः सम्यग् वदति राघवः || २-१११-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. te jaanaaaH = tose people; paurajaanapadaaH = the
inhabitants of town and country; uuchuH = replied; mahaatmaanam = to high souled;
tam = Bharata (as follows); abhijaaniiya = we know well; kaakutthasam =
Rama; raaghavaH = Rama; vadati = is speaking; samyak = rightly.

verses.sanskrit:
एषो अपि हि महा भागः पितुर् वचसि तिष्ठति |

अत एव न शक्ताः स्मो व्यावर्तयितुम् अन्जसा || २-१११-२१
verses.translation:
"This Rama of exalted merit is indeed standing on his father's words. Hence, we are
not able to convince him to return to Ayodhya."

verses.sanskrit:
तेषाम् आज्नाय वचनम् रामो वचनम् अब्रवीत् |

एवम् निबोध वचनम् सुहृदाम् धर्म चक्षुषाम् || २-१११-२२

एतच् च एव उभयम् श्रुत्वा सम्यक् सम्पश्य राघव |

उत्तिष्ठ त्वम् महा बाहो माम् च स्पृश तथा उदकम् || २-१११-२३
verses.translation:
"O the mighty armed Bharata! Having listened to their testimony and mine, reflect on
the matter carefull. Rise and touch me and drink water."

verses.sanskrit:
अथ उत्थाय जलम् स्पृष्ट्वा भरतो वाक्यम् अब्रवीत् |

शृण्वन्तु मे परिषदो मन्त्रिणः श्रेणयः तथा || २-१११-२४

न याचे पितरम् राज्यम् न अनुशासामि मातरम् |

आर्यम् परम धर्मज्नम् अभिजानामि राघवम् || २-१११-२५
verses.translation:
"I never asked my father for the kingdom, nor did I counsel my mother to enthrone
me. I never gave my approval for the exile of Rama, the venerable hero, who knows his duty very
well."

verses.sanskrit:
यदि तु अवश्यम् वस्तव्यम् कर्तव्यम् च पितुर् वचः |

अहम् एव निवत्स्यामि चतुर्दश वने समाः || २-१११-२६
verses.wordMeaning:
26. avashyam yadi = If it is absolutely essential; vastavyam
= (that one of us_ should remain here; vachaH = and the command (of our father);
kartavyamcha = be executed; ahameva = (then) it is I; nivatsyaami = who
will live; vane = in the forest; chaturdasha samaaH = for fourteen years.
verses.translation:
"If it is absolutely essential that one of us should remain here and the command of
our father be executed, then it is I who will reside in the forest for fourteen years."

verses.sanskrit:
धर्म आत्मा तस्य तथ्येन भ्रातुर् वाक्येन विस्मितः |

उवाच रामः सम्प्रेक्ष्य पौर जानपदम् जनम् || २-१११-२७

विक्रीतम् आहितम् क्रीतम् यत् पित्रा जीवता मम |

न तल् लोपयितुम् शक्यम् मया वा भरतेन वा || २-१११-२८
verses.wordMeaning:
28. vikriitam = the disposal; aahitam = the pledge;
kriitam = and the come by; yat = which; (were given); pitraa = by my
father; jiivataa = while he was alive; tat = that; na shakyam = cannot
be; lopayitum = broken; mayaa vaa = either by me; bharatena vaa = or by
Bharata.
verses.translation:
"The disposal, the pledge and the come-by which he was alive, cannot be broken
either by me or by Bharata."

verses.sanskrit:
उपधिर् न मया कार्यो वन वासे जुगुप्सितः |

युक्तम् उक्तम् च कैकेय्या पित्रा मे सुकृतम् कृतम् || २-१११-२९
verses.translation:
"I cannot accede to the substitute in respect of the sojourn in the forest and it is
distasteful thing. What was advised by Kaikeyi was proper and what was done by my father was a
righteous deed."

verses.sanskrit:
जानामि भरतम् क्षान्तम् गुरु सत्कार कारिणम् |

सर्वम् एव अत्र कल्याणम् सत्य संधे महात्मनि || २-१११-३०
verses.translation:
"I am aware that Bharata has necessary patience and due devotion for the elders. All
will be propitious in the case of this magnanimous Bharata, who is true to his promise."

verses.sanskrit:
अनेन धर्म शीलेन वनात् प्रत्यागतः पुनः |

भ्रात्रा सह भविष्यामि पृथिव्याः पतिर् उत्तमः || २-१११-३१
verses.wordMeaning:
31. pratyaagataH = after returning; punaH = again;
vaanaat = from the forest; bhaviSyaami = I shall become; uttamaH = an
excellent; patiH = king; pR^ithivyaah = of the earth; anena bhraatrena
saha = along with my brother; dharmashiilena = who is of virtuous nature.
verses.translation:
"After returning from the forest, I shall rule the earth, assisted by my virtuous
brother."

verses.sanskrit:
वृतो राजा हि कैकेय्या मया तद् वचनम् कृतम् |

अनृतान् मोचय अनेन पितरम् तम् मही पतिम् || २-१११-३२
verses.translation:
"The king Dasaratha was indeed solicited for a boon by Kaikeyi. That word of the
king has been fulfilled by me. Hence, relieve our father, the emperor from the blame."
verses.commentary:

sargas.title: Sarga 112
sargas.introduction:
On hearing the debate between Rama and Bharata, all the sages there were astonished.
The sages request Bharata to accept Rama's words. Bharata explains to Rama that he is unable to
rule the vast kingdom alone to the satisfaction of its subjects. Bharata further adds that Rama has
the necessary competence to save the world and makes a fervent appeal to Rama to return to Ayodhya.
However, Rama replies that Bharata is quite capable of ruling the kingdom and advises Bharata to
take counsel from his wise ministers before administering the kingdom. Rama reaffirms Bharata that
he will never be fulse to his father's vow. Finally, Bharata agrees to Rama;s proposition, but
requests Rama to offer his sandals to be carried by him to ayodhya so that the peope there would
get gain and security. Rama agrees to it and offers his sandals to Bharata, who takes a vow before
Rama to enter fire in case he did not return immediately after the expiry of his term of exile.
After embracing Bharata and Shatrughna, Rama bids farewell to all with due respect in accord with
the rank of each and enters his hermitage.

verses.sanskrit:
तम् अप्रतिम तेजोभ्याम् भ्रातृभ्याम् रोम हर्षणम् |

विस्मिताः सम्गमम् प्रेक्ष्य समवेता महर्षयः || २-११२-१

अन्तर् हिताः तु ऋषि गणाः सिद्धाः च परम ऋषयः |

तौ भ्रातरौ महात्मानौ काकुत्स्थौ प्रशशंसिरे || २-११२-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. munigaNaaH = the assemblies of ascetics; siddhaashcha =
great saints; paramarSayaH = and distinguished sages; antarhitaaH = who stood
invisible in space; prashashamsire = applauded; tau kaakutthsau = both Rama and
Bharata; mahaatmaanau = the high-souled; bhraatarau = brothers (as follows)

verses.sanskrit:
स धन्यो यस्य पुत्रौ द्वौ धर्मज्नौ धर्म विक्रमौ |

श्रुत्वा वयम् हि सम्भाषाम् उभयोः स्पृहयामहे || २-११२-३
verses.translation:
"That Dasaratha, who begot these two sons, who know righteousness and whose strength
is justice, was fortunate. By hearing the debate between these two princes, we are indeed
delighted."

verses.sanskrit:
ततः तु ऋषि गणाः क्षिप्रम् दशग्रीव वध एषिणः |

भरतम् राज शार्दूलम् इत्य् ऊचुः सम्गता वचः || २-११२-४

कुले जात महा प्राज्न महा वृत्त महा यशः |

ग्राह्यम् रामस्य वाक्यम् ते पितरम् यद्य् अवेक्षसे || २-११२-५
verses.translation:
"O Bharata born in a noble race, highly intelligent, having a superior conduct and a
great repute! If you have regard for your father, Rama's words are to be accepted.

verses.sanskrit:
सदा अनृणम् इमम् रामम् वयम् इग्च्छामहे पितुः |

अनृणत्वाच् च कैकेय्याः स्वर्गम् दशरथो गतः || २-११२-६
verses.wordMeaning:
6. vayam = we; ichchhaamahe = wish; imam raamam =
this Rama; anR^iNam = to be free of debt; sadaa = forever; pituH = in
the case of his father; dasharathaH = Dasaratha; anR^iNatvaachcha = becoming free
of debt; kaikeyyaaH = in the case of Kaikeyi; gataH = went; svargam = to
heaven.
verses.translation:
"We wish to see Rama absolved of all obligations forever to his father. Dasaratha,
having honoured his pledge to Kaikeyi, indeed went to heaven."

verses.sanskrit:
एतावद् उक्त्वा वचनम् गन्धर्वाः समहर्षयः |

राज ऋषयः चैव तथा सर्वे स्वाम् स्वाम् गतिम् गताः || २-११२-७

ह्लादितः तेन वाक्येन शुभेन शुभ दर्शनः |

रामः सम्हृष्ट वदनः तान् ऋषीन् अभ्यपूजयत् || २-११२-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. tena vaakyena = by those words; shubhena = which were
auspicious; shubhadarshanaH = the good-looking; raamaH = Rama; LaaditaH
= looked radiant; abhyapuujayat = and worshipped; taan R^iSiin = those sages;
samhR^iSTa vadanaH = with a joyful countenance.

verses.sanskrit:
स्रस्त गात्रः तु भरतः स वाचा सज्जमानया |

कृत अन्जलिर् इदम् वाक्यम् राघवम् पुनर् अब्रवीत् || २-११२-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. bharatastu = Bharata; on his part; trastagaatraH = with
trembled limbs; kR^itaaJNjaliH = joined his palms in salutation; punaH abraviit =
again spoke; idam vaakyam = these words; raaghavam = to Rama;
sajjamaaneyaa = in a strangled; vaachaa = voice.

verses.sanskrit:
राज धर्मम् अनुप्रेक्ष्य कुल धर्म अनुसम्ततिम् |

कर्तुम् अर्हसि काकुत्स्थ मम मातुः च याचनाम् || २-११२-१०
verses.translation:
"O, Rama! Keep in view the continuance of our royal statue and the statute of the
race. You ought to yield to the entreaties of mine and those of your mother."

verses.sanskrit:
रक्षितुम् सुमहद् राज्यम् अहम् एकः तु न उत्सहे |

पौर जानपदामः च अपि रक्तान् रन्जयितुम् तथा || २-११२-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. aham = I; ekastu = alone; notsahe = am not
able; rakSitum = to protect; sumahat = the very large; raajyam =
kingdom; tathaa = and; raJNjayitum = to the satisfaction; raktaan = of
the beloved; paurajaanapadaan = inhabitants of town and country.
verses.translation:
"I am not able to rule this vast empire alone to the satisfaction of the beloved
inhabitants of town and country!"

verses.sanskrit:
ज्नातयः च हि योधाः च मित्राणि सुहृदः च नः |

त्वाम् एव प्रतिकान्क्षन्ते पर्जन्यम् इव कर्षकाः || २-११२-१२
verses.wordMeaning:
12. naH = our; jJNaatayashcha = kith and kin;
yodhaashcha = soldiers; mitraaNi = companions; suhR^idashcha = and
allies; prativiikSante = are waiting; tvameva = for you indeed; karSakaaH
iva = as farmers (wait); parjanyamiva = for the rainy clouds.
verses.translation:
"Our kith and king, soldiers, companions and allies are waiting for you indeed, as
farmers wait for the rainy clouds."

verses.sanskrit:
इदम् राज्यम् महा प्राज्न स्थापय प्रतिपद्य हि |

शक्तिमान् असि काकुत्स्थ लोकस्य परिपालने || २-११२-१३
verses.translation:
"O, the sagacious Rama! Return to the kingdom and keep it in order. You have the
competence to save the world!"

verses.sanskrit:
इत्य् उक्त्वा न्यपतद् भ्रातुः पादयोर् भरतः तदा |

भृशम् सम्प्रार्थयाम् आस रामम् एवम् प्रियम् वदः || २-११२-१४
verses.wordMeaning:
14. bharataH = Bharata; tadaa = then; uktvaa =
speaking; iti = thus; nyapatat = fell; paadayoH = at the feet;
bhraatuH = of his brother; priyamvadaH = Bharata the kind-spoken; sampraartha
yaamaasa = earnestly requested; raamam eva = Rama; bhR^isham = a great
deal.

verses.sanskrit:
तम् अन्के भ्रातरम् कृत्वा रामो वचनम् अब्रवीत् |

श्यामम् नलिन पत्र अक्षम् मत्त हंस स्वरः स्वयम् || २-११२-१५
verses.wordMeaning:
15. svayam aNkekRTitvaa = placing on his lap; tam bharatam =
that Bharata; shyaamam = of dark hue; nalina patraakSam = wtih eyes resembling
lotus-petals; matta hamsa svaram = having a voice of a swan in rut; raamaH =
Rama; abraviit = spoke; vachanam = (the following) words.

verses.sanskrit:
आगता त्वाम् इयम् बुद्धिः स्वजा वैनयिकी च या |

भृशम् उत्सहसे तात रक्षितुम् पृथिवीम् अपि || २-११२-१६
verses.translation:
"O, my dear brother! You are very much competent to rule the earth, by the intellect
you obtained both inherently and by training."

verses.sanskrit:
अमात्यैः च सुहृद्भिः च बुद्धिमद्भिः च मन्त्रिभिः |

सर्व कार्याणि सम्मन्त्र्य सुमहान्त्य् अपि कारय || २-११२-१७
verses.wordMeaning:
17. sammantrya = take counsel with; amaatyaishcha =
ministers; suhR^idbhishcha = companions; buddhi madbhiH = and intelligent;
mantribhishcha = counsellors; kaaraya = and get done; sarva kaaryaaNi =
all activities; sumhaantyapi = even if they are gigantic.
verses.translation:
"Take counsel with your ministers, companions and intelligent counsellors and get
all activities done, even if they are gigantic."

verses.sanskrit:
लक्ष्मीः चन्द्राद् अपेयाद् वा हिमवान् वा हिमम् त्यजेत् |

अतीयात् सागरो वेलाम् न प्रतिज्नाम् अहम् पितुः || २-११२-१८
verses.translation:
"Light may go off from the moon; Himalayan mountain may shake off its snows; the sea
may transgress its shores, but I will never be false to my father's vow!"

verses.sanskrit:
कामाद् वा तात लोभाद् वा मात्रा तुभ्यम् इदम् कृतम् |

न तन् मनसि कर्तव्यम् वर्तितव्यम् च मातृवत् || २-११२-१९
verses.wordMeaning:
19. taata = my dear brother!; idam = this; kR^itam
= was done; maatraa = by your mother; tubhyam- for your sake; kaamaadvaa = from
her affection; lobhaadvaa = or covetousness; tat = that; nakartavyam =
should not be carried out; manasi = in your mind; vartitavyam = you ought to
obey; maatR^ivat = towards your mother.
verses.translation:
"My dear brother! Your mother acted thus, for your sake, out of her affection or
ambition. It should not be carried out in your mind. You ought to obey her as your mother."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् ब्रुवाणम् भरतः कौसल्या सुतम् अब्रवीत् |

तेजसा आदित्य सम्काशम् प्रतिपच् चन्द्र दर्शनम् || २-११२-२०

अधिरोह आर्य पादाभ्याम् पादुके हेम भूषिते |

एते हि सर्व लोकस्य योग क्षेमम् विधास्यतः || २-११२-२१
verses.wordMeaning:
21. aarya = O; noble brother!; paadabhyaam = by your feet;
adhiroha = mount on; paaduke = (these) wooden sandals; hemabhuuSite =
whjich are adorned with gold; ete = these sandals; vidhaasyataH hi = indeed will
bestow; yogakSemam = gain and security; sarva lokasya = to the entire people.
verses.translation:
"O, noble brother! Pray mount with your feet on the wooden sandals adorned with
gold! Surely, these will bestow, gain and security to all the people!"

verses.sanskrit:
सो अधिरुह्य नर व्याघ्रः पादुके ह्य् अवरुह्य च |

प्रायग्च्छत् सुमहा तेजा भरताय महात्मने || २-११२-२२

स पादुके सम्प्रणम्य रामं वचनम्ब्रवीत् |

चतुर्दश हि वर्षाणि जटाचीरधरो ह्यहम् || २-११२-२३

फलमूलाशनो वीर भवेयम् रघुनंदन |

तवागमनमाकाङ्क्षन् वसन्वै नगराद्बहिः || २-११२-२४

तव पादुकयोर्न्यस्तराज्यतन्त्रः परंतप |

चतुर्दशे तु सम्पूर्णे वर्षेऽहानि रघूत्तम || २-११२-२५

न द्रक्ष्यामि यदि त्वां तु प्रवेक्ष्यामि हुताशनम् |
verses.translation:
"O, Rama! If I do not see you the following day when the fourteenth year has fully
ended, I shall enter the fire once for all."

verses.sanskrit:
तथेति च प्रतिज्ञाय तं परिष्वज्य सादरम् || २-११२-२६

शत्रुघ्नम् च परिष्वज्य भरतं चेदमब्रवीत् |
verses.translation:
"So be it" promised Rama, embraced that Bharata with affection, took Shatrughna also
in his arms and spoke to Bharata as follows:

verses.sanskrit:
मातरम् रक्ष कैकेयीम् मा रोषम् कुरु तां प्रति || २-११२-२७

मया च सीतया चैव शप्तो.सि रघुसत्तम |

इत्युक्त्वाश्रुपरीताक्षो भ्रातरं विससर्ज ह || २-११२-२८
verses.translation:
"O Bharata! Protect your mother, Kaikeyi. Do not get angry with her. You are taken
vow thus by me and by Seetha." speaking thus, Rama with his eyes filled iwth tears, bade farewell
to his brother.

verses.sanskrit:
स पादुके ते भरतः प्रतापवान् |

स्वलम्कृते सम्परिगृह्य धर्मवित् |

प्रदक्षिणम् चैव चकार राघवम् |

चकार चैव उत्तम नाग मूर्धनि || २-११२-२९
verses.wordMeaning:
29. saH bharataH = that Bharata; prataapavaan = the valiant
man; dharmavit = who knew righteousness; samparipuujya = worshipped; te
paaduke = those sandals; svalaNK^Rite = decorated well; chakaara = made;
pradakSiNam = circumambulation; raaghavam = to Rama; chakaara = and
kept; te = those sandals; uttama naaga muurdhani = on the head of an excellent
elephant.

verses.sanskrit:
अथ आनुपूर्व्यात् प्रतिपूज्य तम् जनम् |

गुरूमः च मन्त्रि प्रकृतीः तथा अनुजौ |

व्यसर्जयद् राघव वंश वर्धनः |

स्थितः स्व धर्मे हिमवान् इव अचलः || २-११२-३०
verses.wordMeaning:
30. atha = then; raaghava vamshavardhanaH = rama; the
augmentator of Raghu dynasty; sthiraH = firm; himavaan achalaH iva = as a
Himalayan rock; svadharme = (abiding in) his own duty; pratinandya = greeted;
tam = those men; aanupuurvyaa = in accord with their rank; guruumshcha =
the host of his preceptors; mantriprakR^itiiH = ministers his subjects; tatha =
and; anujau = and his brothers; vyasarjayat = and bade farewell.

verses.sanskrit:
तम् मातरो बाष्प गृहीत कण्ठो |

दुह्खेन न आमन्त्रयितुम् हि शेकुः |

स तु एव मातृऋर् अभिवाद्य सर्वा |

रुदन् कुटीम् स्वाम् प्रविवेश रामः || २-११२-३१

sargas.title: Sarga 113
sargas.introduction:
Keeping Rama's sandals on his head, Bharata ascends his chariot along with
Shatrughna. Bharata advances along with his troops along side the mountain of chitrakuta. On the
way, he beholds Bharadvaja's hermitage and meets the sage. Bharata informs the sage about the
insistance of Rama to stay back in the forest for fourteen years so as to honour the promise of his
father scruprulously and also Vaishta's advice to Rama to offer his sandals to Bharata, to ensure
peace and harmony in Ayodhya.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः शिरसि कृत्वा तु पादुके भरतः तदा |

आरुरोह रथम् हृष्टः शत्रुघ्नेन समन्वितः || २-११३-१
verses.wordMeaning:
1. tataH = thereafter; bharataH = Bharata; shatrughnena
samnvitaH = along with Shatrughna; tadaa = then; kR^ivaa = keeping;
paaduke = the sandals; shirasi = on his head; hR^iSTaH = and having been
pleased; aaruroha = ascended; ratham = his chariot.

verses.sanskrit:
वसिष्ठो वामदेवः च जाबालिः च दृढ व्रतः |

अग्रतः प्रययुः सर्वे मन्त्रिणो मन्त्र पूजिताः || २-११३-२
verses.wordMeaning:
2. agrataH = before him; vasiSThaH = Vasishta;
vaasudevashcha = Vasudeva; dR^iDhavrataH = sarve = and all; mantriNaH =
the ministers; mantra puujitaaH = distinguished for their advices; prayayuH =
went ahead.

verses.sanskrit:
मन्दाकिनीम् नदीम् रम्याम् प्रान् मुखाः ते ययुः तदा |

प्रदक्षिणम् च कुर्वाणाः चित्र कूटम् महा गिरिम् || २-११३-३
verses.wordMeaning:
3. tadaa = then; te = they; yayuH = advanced;
kurvaaNaaH = making; pradakSiNam = a round of; chitrakuuTam mahaagirim =
Chitrakuta mountain; praaNmukhaaH = facing east towards; ramyaam = by the
charming; nadiim = River; mandaakiniim = Mandakini.

verses.sanskrit:
पश्यन् धातु सहस्राणि रम्याणि विविधानि च |

प्रययौ तस्य पार्श्वेन ससैन्यो भरतः तदा || २-११३-४

अदूराच् चित्र कूटस्य ददर्श भरतः तदा |

आश्रमम् यत्र स मुनिर् भरद्वाजः कृत आलयः || २-११३-५
verses.wordMeaning:
5. tadaa = then; bharataH = Bharata; dadarsha =
saw; aashramam = a hermitage; yatra = where; muniH = the sage; saH
bharadvaajaH = that Bharadwaja; kR^itaalayaH = made it as a residence;
aduuraat = in the vicinity; chitrakuuTasya = of Chitrakuta Mountain.

verses.sanskrit:
स तम् आश्रमम् आगम्य भरद्वाजस्य बुद्धिमान् |

अवतीर्य रथात् पादौ ववन्दे कुल नन्दनः || २-११३-६

ततो हृष्टो भरद्वाजो भरतम् वाक्यम् अब्रवीत् |

अपि कृत्यम् कृतम् तात रामेण च समागतम् || २-११३-७
verses.wordMeaning:
7. tataH = then; uktaH = spoken; evam = thus;
bharadvaajena = by Bharadvaja; dhiimataa = the learned sage; bharataH =
Bharata; bhraatR^ivatsalaH = affectionate towards his brothers; pratyuvaacha =
replied; bharadvaajam = to Bharadvaja (as follows)

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्तः तु भरतो भरद्वाजेन धीमता |

प्रत्युवाच भरद्वाजम् भरतो धर्म वत्सलः || २-११३-८
verses.wordMeaning:
8. tataH then; uktaH = spoken; evam = thus;
bharadvaajena = by Bharadvaja; dhiimataa = the learned sage; bharataH =
Bharat; bhraatR^ivatsalaH = affectionate towards his brothers; prayuvaacha =
replied; bharadvaajam = to Bharadvaja (as follows):

verses.sanskrit:
स याच्यमानो गुरुणा मया च दृढ विक्रमः |

राघवः परम प्रीतो वसिष्ठम् वाक्यम् अब्रवीत् || २-११३-९
verses.wordMeaning:
9. yaachyamaanaH = despite the entreaties; guruNaa = of his
preceptor; mayaa = and of mine; raaghavaH = Rama; dR^iDhavikramaH = is
unshakeable in his resolve; parama priitaH = and most cheerfully; abraviit =
spoke; vaakyam = ( the following) words; vasiSTham = to Vasishta.

verses.sanskrit:
पितुः प्रतिज्नाम् ताम् एव पालयिष्यामि तत्त्वतः |

चतुर्दश हि वर्षाणि य प्रतिज्ना पितुर् मम || २-११३-१०
verses.wordMeaning:
10. paalayiSyaami = I shall honour; taam pratijJNaam eva =
that promise alone; pituH = of my father; tattvataH = scruprulously; yaa
= which; pratiJJNaaH = I promised; mama pituH = to my father; chaturdasha
varSaaNi = for fourteen years.
verses.translation:
"I shall honour the promise of my father scrupulously and reside in the forest for
fourteen years as I promised him."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्तो महा प्राज्नो वसिष्ठः प्रत्युवाच ह |

वाक्यज्नो वाक्य कुशलम् राघवम् वचनम् महत् || २-११३-११
verses.wordMeaning:
11. uktaH = spoken; evam = in this manner (by Rama);
vasiSThaH = Vasishta; mahaapraajJNaH = the highly wise; vaakyajJNaH =
and the knower of the mode of expression; pratyuvaachaha = replied; mahat = in
great; vachanam = words; raaghavam = to Rama; vaakyakushalam = who is
the most skilful of orators.

verses.sanskrit:
एते प्रयग्च्छ सम्हृष्टः पादुके हेम भूषिते |

अयोध्यायाम् महा प्राज्न योग क्षेम करे तव || २-११३-१२
verses.translation:
"O, the extremely segacious prince! Bestow joyfully thse gold-encrusted sandals of
yours on us and ensure peace and harmony in Ayodhya."

verses.sanskrit:
एवम् उक्तो वसिष्ठेन राघवः प्रान् मुखः स्थितः |

पादुके हेम विकृते मम राज्याय ते ददौ || २-११३-१३
verses.wordMeaning:
13. evam = thus; uktaH = spoken; vasiSTena = by
Vasishta; raaghavaH = Rama; sthitaH = stood up; praaNmukhaH = facing the
EAst; adhiruhya = placed his feet; ete = in those; paaduke = sandals;
dadau = and gave; mama = to me; raajyaaya = as a sign of regency.
verses.translation:
"Hearing the words of Vasishta, Rama stood up and turning to the East, placed his
feet in those sandals and gave them to me as a sign of regency."

verses.sanskrit:
निवृत्तो अहम् अनुज्नातो रामेण सुमहात्मना |

अयोध्याम् एव गग्च्छामि गृहीत्वा पादुके शुभे || २-११३-१४
verses.translation:
"Having taken leave of the very high-souled Rama, I turned back after receiving the
auspicious sandals. Now, I am proceeding to Ayodhya."

verses.sanskrit:
एतत् श्रुत्वा शुभम् वाक्यम् भरतस्य महात्मनः |

भरद्वाजः शुभतरम् मुनिर् वाक्यम् उदाहरत् || २-११३-१५

न एतच् चित्रम् नर व्याघ्र शील वृत्तवताम् वर |

यद् आर्यम् त्वयि तिष्ठेत् तु निम्ने वृष्टिम् इव उदकम् || २-११३-१६
verses.translation:
"O Bharata the Tiger among men and excellent among those having virtue and good
conduct! There is no surprise that a noble trait prevails in you, as naturally as the water allowed
to go, always settles downwards."

verses.sanskrit:
अमृतः स महा बाहुः पिता दशरथः तव |

यस्य त्वम् ईदृशः पुत्रो धर्म आत्मा धर्म वत्सलः || २-११३-१७
verses.translation:
"Your valiant father, Dasaratha, is immortal in having such a son as you are, the
knower of righteousness and loving peity."

verses.sanskrit:
तम् ऋषिम् तु महात्मानम् उक्त वाक्यम् कृत अन्जलिः |

आमन्त्रयितुम् आरेभे चरणाउ उपगृह्य च || २-११३-१८

ततः प्रदक्षिणम् कृत्वा भरद्वाजम् पुनः पुनः |

भरतः तु ययौ श्रीमान् अयोध्याम् सह मन्त्रिभिः || २-११३-१९

यानैः च शकटैः चैव हयैः नागैः च सा चमूः |

पुनर् निवृत्ता विस्तीर्णा भरतस्य अनुयायिनी || २-११३-२०
verses.wordMeaning:
20. saa = that; vistiirNaa = extensive; chamuuH =
army; bharatasya = of Bharata; anuyaayinii = following him; yaanaishcha
= with vehicles; shakTaishcha = carts; hayaiH = horses; naagaishoha =
and elephants; nivR^ittaa = turned back; punaH = again (towards Ayodhya).

verses.sanskrit:
ततः ते यमुनाम् दिव्याम् नदीम् तीर्त्वा ऊर्मि मालिनीम् |

ददृशुः ताम् पुनः सर्वे गन्गाम् शिव जलाम् नदीम् || २-११३-२१

ताम् रम्य जल सम्पूर्णाम् सम्तीर्य सह बान्धवः |

शृन्ग वेर पुरम् रम्यम् प्रविवेश ससैनिकः || २-११३-२२

शृन्ग वेर पुराद् भूय अयोध्याम् संददर्श ह |
verses.wordMeaning:
22. saha baandhavaH = (Bharata) together with his relatives; sa
sainikaH = and his army; samtiirya = crossed; taam = that River Ganga; ramya
jalasmapuurNaam- full of charming waters; pravivesha = and entered; ramyam = the
beautiful; shR^iNgibherapuram = town of Shringibhera; shR^iNgibherapuraat = from
that Shringibhera; samdadarha = he saw; ayodhyaam = Ayodhya; bhuuyaH =
again.

verses.sanskrit:
अयोध्याम् च ततो दृष्ट्वा पित्रा भ्रात्रा विवर्जिताम् |

भरतो दुह्ख सम्तप्तः सारथिम् च इदम् अब्रवीत् || २-११३-२३
verses.wordMeaning:
23. tataH = then; dR^iSTvaa = beholding; ayodhyaam
= Ayodhya; vivarjitaam = bereft of; pitraa = his father; bhraatraa = and
brother; bharataH = Bharata; duHkhasantaptaH = tormented with grief;
abraviit = spoke; idam = these words; sarathiim = to the charioteer.

verses.sanskrit:
सारथे पश्य विध्वस्ता अयोध्या न प्रकाशते |

निराकारा निरानन्दा दीना प्रतिहत स्वना || २-११३-२४
verses.wordMeaning:
24. saarathe = O; charioteer!; pashya = see; saa
ayodhyaa = that Ayodhya; vidhvastaa = ruined; niraakaaraa = with a vacant
look; niraanandaa = joyless; diinaa = miserable; pratihatasvaraa = and
with choked voice.
verses.translation:
"O, Charioteer! See that Ayodhya city, ruined, with a vacant look, joyless,
miserable, and with an impeded voice."
verses.commentary:
